name: Internal Linking Depth Rule
then_action: Add contextual internal links from top-performing content; prioritize link anchor text that contains the target keyword phrase
if_condition: A target page has fewer than 3 internal links from high-authority pages

name: GEO Freshness Window Rule
then_action: Flag for refresh; update with most recent authoritative data point and cite the source explicitly with publication date
if_condition: A claim or statistic in content is older than 12 months in a fast-moving topic area

name: Schema Markup Coverage Rule
then_action: Implement the highest-priority schema type for the page; validate against Google Rich Results Test before deploying
if_condition: A page type has applicable schema markup (Article, Product, FAQ, HowTo) but it is not implemented

name: Lead with Direct Answer (Inverted Pyramid) for AI Citation
then_action: Then lead every page and every section with a direct, factual answer (state the conclusion first, then provide supporting detail) using the inverted pyramid structure, followed by supporting details, specific examples or data, and a FAQ section with question-style H2s
if_condition: If a page or section is being written with the goal of being cited by Perplexity AI
source_title: Perplexity Content Structure for Citation Optimisation

name: Use Specific Verifiable Facts to Enable Citation
then_action: Then the content is not citable by Perplexity AI; replace vague claims with specific, verifiable facts including numbers, percentages, dates, version numbers, prices, and measurements wherever possible
if_condition: If content contains vague claims, unverifiable promotional language (e.g., 'industry-leading', 'best-in-class'), or lacks specific numbers, percentages, dates, or measurements
source_title: Perplexity Content Structure for Citation Optimisation

name: Ensure page load under 3 seconds for Perplexity live result inclusion
then_action: The page may be skipped during answer generation — optimise page speed to under 3 seconds to avoid being excluded from real-time citation
if_condition: If pages load in 3 seconds or more during Perplexity's live web search answer generation
source_title: Perplexity How It Works and Citation Model

name: Use Question-Style Headings Matching User Query Patterns
then_action: Then use H2/H3 question-style headings that mirror how users ask Perplexity queries (e.g., 'What is X?', 'How do I Y?', 'What is the difference between X and Y?') and add id attributes to headings to enable deep-linking to specific passages
if_condition: If structuring content intended for AI extraction and citation by Perplexity
source_title: Perplexity Content Structure for Citation Optimisation

name: Add JSON-LD FAQ Schema and Semantic HTML5 to Aid AI Parsing
then_action: Then add JSON-LD FAQ schema to help Perplexity identify and extract individual Q&A pairs, use semantic HTML5 tags (header, nav, main, article, footer, time), and structure comparison content as HTML tables with defined criteria rows
if_condition: If a page contains question-based sections or comparison content intended for AI extraction by Perplexity
source_title: Perplexity Content Structure for Citation Optimisation

name: Avoid Repeated Recrawl Requests for the Same URL
then_action: Stop submitting repeated requests — Google does not crawl the URL faster due to multiple submissions, and doing so wastes the available quota
if_condition: If a site owner submits multiple recrawl requests for the same URL expecting faster crawling
source_title: Ask Google to Recrawl Your URLs

name: Signal Content Freshness with Visible Timestamps and Substantive Updates
then_action: Then avoid cosmetic timestamp churn (updating date without content changes); instead refresh content quarterly at minimum with updated statistics, new developments, revised recommendations, and a visible 'Last Updated' date stamp near the top of the page; add a changelog for guides and technical content
if_condition: If a page targets Perplexity AI citations and the 'last modified' date is updated without changing substantive content, or if content has not been refreshed in over 3 months
source_title: Perplexity Content Structure for Citation Optimisation

name: Allow PerplexityBot in robots.txt for citation eligibility
then_action: Content cannot be crawled or cited by Perplexity AI — remove the block to enable crawling and citation inclusion
if_condition: If PerplexityBot is blocked in robots.txt
source_title: Perplexity How It Works and Citation Model

name: Associate Content with Named Credentialed Author to Boost Citation Likelihood
then_action: Then associate the content with a named, credentialed author and link to their bio or professional profile, as anonymous/unattributed content is less likely to be cited by Perplexity AI than content with visible author credentials
if_condition: If content is published without authorship attribution or author credentials
source_title: Perplexity Content Structure for Citation Optimisation

name: Cite Primary Authoritative Sources Inline for AI Trust Signals
then_action: Then cite primary, authoritative sources (official documentation, research papers, government sources) directly inline within the text rather than only in a footnotes section, and include a tight References section with primary data and reputable third parties
if_condition: If content includes factual claims, statistics, or technical guidance intended to be cited by Perplexity AI
source_title: Perplexity Content Structure for Citation Optimisation

name: Use Callout Boxes and Visually Distinct Containers for Key Facts
then_action: Then place those key facts or answers in visually distinct containers (callout boxes, info panels), as content highlighted this way has a 2.3x higher chance of being cited by AI engines due to these visual cues signalling importance to extraction algorithms
if_condition: If a page contains key factual claims or direct answers intended for Perplexity AI citation
source_title: Perplexity Content Structure for Citation Optimisation

name: Serve content in raw crawlable HTML for Perplexity visibility
then_action: Perplexity's crawler cannot access that content — serve it as raw HTML in the initial page response to make it eligible for citation
if_condition: If key content such as FAQ answers, pricing, or specifications is rendered via JavaScript, hidden in accordions, or displayed in carousels/tabs
source_title: Perplexity How It Works and Citation Model

name: Implement structured data to increase Perplexity citation probability
then_action: Add appropriate structured data schema to clarify content intent and increase the likelihood of being selected as one of the 3–8 cited sources per Perplexity answer
if_condition: If informational pages intended for AI citation lack structured data markup (Article, FAQ, HowTo, Organization, or Product schema)
source_title: Perplexity How It Works and Citation Model

name: Use single-intent URLs for Perplexity citation eligibility
then_action: Perplexity deprioritises multi-intent pages — restructure content into separate single-intent URLs with clean, descriptive slugs to increase citation odds
if_condition: If a page mixes multiple content intents such as definitions, tutorials, and promotional content on a single URL
source_title: Perplexity How It Works and Citation Model

name: Allow BingPreview in robots.txt as prerequisite for Perplexity inclusion
then_action: Content will be excluded from Bing's index, which is a primary data source for Perplexity, preventing Perplexity citation — allow BingPreview to restore eligibility
if_condition: If BingPreview is blocked in robots.txt
source_title: Perplexity How It Works and Citation Model

name: Submit sitemaps with accurate lastmod values to prioritise Perplexity crawling
then_action: Perplexity may deprioritise crawling that content — update sitemaps with correct lastmod values and submit via IndexNow to signal freshness to Bing in near real-time
if_condition: If XML sitemaps are outdated or missing accurate lastmod timestamps for recently updated content
source_title: Perplexity How It Works and Citation Model

name: Enforce HTTPS across entire domain for AI citation trust
then_action: Activate and enforce HTTPS sitewide — insecure pages receive lower trust scores from AI citation systems including Perplexity
if_condition: If any pages on the domain are served over HTTP rather than HTTPS
source_title: Perplexity Technical Setup and robots txt Configuration

name: Prioritise branded web mentions over backlinks for AI citation visibility
then_action: Shift focus toward generating branded web mentions — Ahrefs 2025 data shows branded mentions correlate more strongly with AI Overviews visibility (Spearman r = 0.664) than backlink count
if_condition: If an SEO strategy focuses primarily on building backlinks to improve AI Overview and Perplexity citation visibility
source_title: Perplexity How It Works and Citation Model

name: Achieve page load times under 3 seconds for Perplexity live retrieval
then_action: Optimise page speed to load under 3 seconds — Perplexity uses live web results and slow pages may time out during answer generation, reducing citation likelihood
if_condition: If page load times exceed 3 seconds on content pages targeted for Perplexity citation
source_title: Perplexity Technical Setup and robots txt Configuration

name: Allow PerplexityBot and BingPreview in robots.txt
then_action: Add 'User-agent: PerplexityBot / Allow: /' and 'User-agent: BingPreview / Allow: /' to robots.txt — blocking these bots is the most common reason for Perplexity invisibility and citation ineligibility
if_condition: If PerplexityBot or BingPreview is blocked or not explicitly allowed in robots.txt
source_title: Perplexity Technical Setup and robots txt Configuration

name: Submit XML sitemap with accurate lastmod timestamps for Perplexity freshness
then_action: Submit a complete XML sitemap with accurate lastmod timestamps — Perplexity uses sitemaps to prioritise fresh content for crawling
if_condition: If a site lacks an XML sitemap or sitemap lastmod timestamps are missing or inaccurate
source_title: Perplexity Technical Setup and robots txt Configuration

name: Render key content in static HTML for Perplexity crawlability
then_action: Rewrite or restructure content to be present in static HTML at page load — JavaScript-rendered content is invisible to PerplexityBot and will not be cited
if_condition: If important content such as answers, data, or key text is rendered exclusively via client-side JavaScript, or placed inside accordions, tabs, or modals requiring user interaction
source_title: Perplexity Technical Setup and robots txt Configuration

name: Implement structured data in JSON-LD format for Perplexity citation relevance
then_action: Implement JSON-LD structured data using Article, FAQ, HowTo, Organization, or Person schema types — these are the most relevant schema types for improving Perplexity citation eligibility
if_condition: If content pages targeting Perplexity citations lack structured data markup
source_title: Perplexity Technical Setup and robots txt Configuration

name: Implement IndexNow for near real-time Bing index updates
then_action: Implement IndexNow to push new and updated URLs to Bing immediately — since Bing feeds Perplexity's index, faster Bing indexing improves Perplexity citation eligibility; IndexNow also simultaneously notifies Yandex, Naver, and Seznam
if_condition: If new or updated URLs are published and not yet submitted to Bing's index in near real-time
source_title: Perplexity Technical Setup and robots txt Configuration

name: Use stable semantic URL slugs to preserve Perplexity citation integrity
then_action: Replace with stable, semantic URL slugs — broken or relocated URLs in prior Perplexity citations cannot be corrected retroactively, permanently losing citation value
if_condition: If content pages use dynamic URL parameters, session IDs, or URLs that change frequently
source_title: Perplexity Technical Setup and robots txt Configuration

name: Target Concise Factual Sections for Speakable Markup
then_action: SHOULD target lead paragraphs, key facts, and summary sections that contain important, self-contained factual content suitable for voice readout
if_condition: If selecting content regions to mark as speakable on an Article or NewsArticle page
source_title: SpeakableSpecification

name: SpeakableSpecification Requires CSS Selector or XPath
then_action: MUST provide either cssSelector (Text) or xpath (Text) property identifying the speakable section(s); omitting both makes the markup invalid
if_condition: If SpeakableSpecification schema is implemented on an Article or WebPage
source_title: SpeakableSpecification

name: Avoid Marking Entire Page Body as Speakable
then_action: AVOID this pattern — only concise, information-dense sections should be marked speakable; broad targeting reduces voice response quality
if_condition: If cssSelector or xpath in SpeakableSpecification targets the entire page body or large non-specific content regions
source_title: SpeakableSpecification

name: Exclude Navigation, Footer, and Promotional Text from Speakable
then_action: AVOID marking these sections as speakable — they add no value for voice responses and may degrade Google Assistant answer quality
if_condition: If navigation menus, footer content, or promotional/advertising text are present on a page with speakable markup
source_title: SpeakableSpecification

name: Use Array of CSS Selectors for Multiple Speakable Regions
then_action: Provide cssSelector as an array of selector strings within the SpeakableSpecification JSON-LD, e.g., ".article-headline", ".article-body p:first-of-type"
if_condition: If multiple distinct content sections (e.g., headline and first paragraph) need to be marked as speakable on a single page
source_title: SpeakableSpecification

name: Apply Speakable Markup Primarily to Article and NewsArticle Types
then_action: SHOULD implement the speakable property with SpeakableSpecification on Article and NewsArticle schema types, as these are the primary supported contexts for voice readout
if_condition: If a publisher wants content to appear in Google Assistant voice responses
source_title: SpeakableSpecification

name: Monitor Re-indexing Progress via Search Console Reports
then_action: Monitor progress using the Index Status report or the URL Inspection tool in Search Console
if_condition: If a recrawl request has been submitted and the site owner needs to track whether Google has re-indexed the URLs
source_title: Ask Google to Recrawl Your URLs

name: Use hreflang annotations for multilingual/multiregional targeting
then_action: Implement hreflang annotations in page HTML, HTTP headers, or sitemaps to signal to Google which language and region each page targets
if_condition: If a site serves pages targeting different languages or regions
source_title: Managing Multi Regional and Multilingual Sites

name: Avoid automatic locale-based redirects for multilingual sites
then_action: This prevents Google from crawling alternate language versions; instead provide explicit links or language/region selection mechanisms visible on the page
if_condition: If a multilingual site automatically redirects users from one language version to another based on browser or IP signals
source_title: Managing Multi Regional and Multilingual Sites

name: Do not use IP-based content adaptation for locale targeting
then_action: Do not use IP-based adaptation — IP location analysis is unreliable and most Google crawls originate from US IP addresses without Accept-Language headers, meaning Google will not see the correct localized content
if_condition: If a site uses IP-based geolocation to serve different content or redirect users to locale-specific versions
source_title: Managing Multi Regional and Multilingual Sites

name: Avoid HTML lang attribute for language detection
then_action: This will not work — Google determines language from visible page content only, not from HTML lang attributes or URL paths; use a single language per page for both content and navigation instead
if_condition: If a multilingual site relies on HTML lang attribute or URL path to signal page language to Google
source_title: Managing Multi Regional and Multilingual Sites

name: Prefer ccTLD over URL parameters for geotargeting
then_action: Use ccTLD as the strongest geotargeting signal; avoid URL parameters as they are not recommended for geotargeting; subdomains and subdirectories are acceptable alternatives
if_condition: If a site needs to implement geotargeting and is choosing between URL structure options (ccTLD, subdomain, subdirectory, URL parameters)
source_title: Managing Multi Regional and Multilingual Sites

name: Treat certain ccTLDs as gTLDs for geotargeting
then_action: Google treats some ccTLDs (e.g., .tv, .me) as generic top-level domains rather than country-specific signals, so geotargeting effect will not apply; use alternative URL structures or hreflang for targeting
if_condition: If a site uses a domain with a ccTLD such as .tv or .me and expects it to serve as a strong geotargeting signal
source_title: Managing Multi Regional and Multilingual Sites

name: URL Fragment Content Change Warning
then_action: Use the History API instead, because Google Search generally does not support URL fragments and will not crawl the content change
if_condition: If a URL fragment (portion after # hash symbol) is used to dynamically change page content
source_title: URL Structure Best Practices for Google Search

name: URL Case Sensitivity Duplication Risk
then_action: Google treats them as distinct URLs, which can cause duplicate content issues and wasted crawl budget — standardize URL casing across the site
if_condition: If the same page content is accessible via URLs that differ only in letter casing (e.g., /APPLE and /apple)
source_title: URL Structure Best Practices for Google Search

name: Use Hyphens Not Underscores in URLs
then_action: Use hyphens (-) to separate words, not underscores (_), because underscores signal concepts that should be kept together and may confuse Google's word parsing
if_condition: If words in a URL need to be separated
source_title: URL Structure Best Practices for Google Search

name: URL Must Follow IETF STD 66 Standard
then_action: Google Search may crawl the site inefficiently, including extremely high crawl rates or failing to crawl at all
if_condition: If a URL does not conform to IETF STD 66 standard or reserved characters are not percent-encoded
source_title: URL Structure Best Practices for Google Search

name: Minimize URL Parameters to Prevent Crawl Bloat
then_action: Reduce or eliminate unnecessary URL parameters, because they generate large numbers of URLs pointing to identical or similar content, causing crawl inefficiency and index bloat
if_condition: If URLs contain multiple parameters such as session IDs, tracking codes, additive filters, or dynamically generated date ranges with no restrictions
source_title: URL Structure Best Practices for Google Search

name: Use Descriptive Words in URLs
then_action: Replace with descriptive words in the audience's language, as this improves both Google's understanding of page content and user comprehension
if_condition: If a URL uses long ID numbers or non-descriptive strings instead of meaningful words
source_title: URL Structure Best Practices for Google Search

name: Percent-Encode Non-ASCII Characters in URLs
then_action: Apply percent-encoding to those characters to ensure proper crawlability and compliance with IETF STD 66
if_condition: If a URL contains non-ASCII characters when linking to pages on the site
source_title: URL Structure Best Practices for Google Search

name: AI Search Optimization Goal Shift
then_action: Must reframe the optimization goal from winning a ranking position to becoming trusted source material that AI systems extract and cite, requiring broader brand validation across the ecosystem and strong authorship signals
if_condition: If optimizing content for AI search contexts where answer engines synthesize responses rather than returning ranked result lists
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT AI Visibility vs Tr

name: Article Schema Type Selection by Content Format
then_action: Use NewsArticle schema type for news content, BlogPosting for blog posts, and Article as the general fallback — all must be based on schema.org Article, NewsArticle, or BlogPosting types
if_condition: If a page contains news content, blog posts, or sports articles and Article structured data is being implemented
source_title: Article Structured Data – News, Blog, and Sports Articles

name: Canonical URL Only in Sitemap
then_action: Include only the canonical URL in the sitemap, not all alternate URLs
if_condition: If the same content is accessible through multiple URLs
source_title: Build and Submit a Sitemap to Google

name: Heavy JavaScript Usage Prevents AI Crawling
then_action: Most AI crawlers cannot render JavaScript and will miss the content, reducing AI citation potential — migrate critical content to server-rendered or static HTML
if_condition: If key content (brand claims, product info, FAQs) is rendered via JavaScript rather than served as static HTML
source_title: Generative Engine Optimization Growth Strategies and Metrics For the AI Era

name: Multi-Part Article Canonical Tag Requirement
then_action: The rel=canonical element must point to either each individual page or a single view-all page containing the full article — it must NOT point to page 1 of the series
if_condition: If an article is split across multiple pages (multi-part series)
source_title: Article Structured Data – News, Blog, and Sports Articles

name: High-Intent Keyword Identification
then_action: Identify and prioritize keywords that demonstrate high purchase or action intent for your specific offerings rather than broad or ambiguous terms
if_condition: If conducting keyword research for a product or service with a specific use case
source_title: Growth for startups YC Startup Library Transcript Part 19

name: Prioritize Intent-Driven Outbound for Revenue Impact
then_action: Prioritize intent-driven outbound automation — 79% of companies report increased revenue from this use case, making it the highest-impact AI application in GTM
if_condition: If allocating AI investment across GTM use cases and revenue impact is the primary KPI
source_title: 2025 B2B GTM Channel Benchmarks Winning Motions AI Adoption in GTM From What Can I Do to Show Me ROI

name: Paywalled or Registration-Gated Article Structured Data
then_action: Add structured data for subscription and paywalled content alongside the Article structured data to properly signal access restrictions to Google
if_condition: If a page with Article structured data requires a subscription or user registration to access its content
source_title: Article Structured Data – News, Blog, and Sports Articles

name: High Rankings Insufficient for AI Visibility
then_action: AI visibility will likely be weak because on-page relevance and backlinks are necessary but insufficient — AI systems narrow the decision set before users click, requiring additional trust and extractability signals
if_condition: If an SEO strategy relies solely on high organic rankings and backlink profiles without addressing content parsability, authorship strength, and cross-web brand mentions
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT AI Visibility vs Tr

name: Entity Relationships and Source Credibility for AI Citation
then_action: Must establish clear entity relationships and source credibility, maintain consistent authority signals across the web ecosystem, and ensure content is easily parseable by AI systems to increase citation likelihood
if_condition: If content is being evaluated for inclusion as source material in AI-generated answers
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT AI Visibility vs Tr

name: Answer-First Section Opening for AEO Citations
then_action: Rewrite the opening to deliver a 40-60 word direct answer within the first 1-2 sentences, because AI engines extract only the first 1-2 sentences to determine citation eligibility and will move to a competitor if the opening is vague
if_condition: If an H2 section opens with vague context-setting language rather than a direct answer to the question implied by the heading
source_title: Answer Engine Optimization Complete AEO Guide 2026 How to Optimize for Answer Engines Step by Step

name: Statistic Density Requirement for AEO Content
then_action: Insert a statistic sourced from an authoritative domain every 150-200 words, and replace vague phrases like 'significant growth' with specific numbers to maintain AI engine citation eligibility
if_condition: If a content section spans more than 200 words without including a specific statistic or quantified data point
source_title: Answer Engine Optimization Complete AEO Guide 2026 How to Optimize for Answer Engines Step by Step

name: Primary Source Citation Over Secondary Summaries
then_action: Replace the citation with a direct link to the original source, prioritizing .gov, .edu, peer-reviewed research, or major industry reports to maximize AI engine authority signals
if_condition: If a statistic or factual claim is cited using a secondary summary source (e.g., a blog summarizing a study) rather than the original publication
source_title: Answer Engine Optimization Complete AEO Guide 2026 How to Optimize for Answer Engines Step by Step

name: Semantic Chunking: One Concept Per Section
then_action: Split the section so each heading covers exactly one concept and is a self-contained unit that can be understood and cited independently by AI engines parsing content by section rather than by page
if_condition: If a content section mixes definitions with how-to instructions, or covers more than one distinct concept within the same H2 or H3 block
source_title: Answer Engine Optimization Complete AEO Guide 2026 How to Optimize for Answer Engines Step by Step

name: Topic Cluster Requirement for Competitive AEO
then_action: Build a complete topic cluster with pillar content and supporting articles, because a single optimized article will not outperform a site with comprehensive topical coverage across multiple angles
if_condition: If a site publishes a single optimized article on a subject without surrounding supporting cluster articles covering related subtopics
source_title: Answer Engine Optimization Complete AEO Guide 2026 How to Optimize for Answer Engines Step by Step

name: Increase Investment in AI Search and AEO
then_action: Allocate budget toward AI search and Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) — 51% of companies plan to increase investment in this channel, signaling growing strategic importance
if_condition: If planning 2025 GTM budget allocation and AI search visibility has not yet been prioritized
source_title: 2025 B2B GTM Channel Benchmarks Winning Motions AI Adoption in GTM From What Can I Do to Show Me ROI

name: Author Markup Type by Entity Kind
then_action: Use schema.org Person type for individual authors and schema.org Organization type for organisations; include the author's URL pointing to a page with more information about that author
if_condition: If the author field is being populated in Article structured data
source_title: Article Structured Data – News, Blog, and Sports Articles

name: Article Structured Data Recommended Properties for Rich Results
then_action: Include recommended properties: headline (title), datePublished, dateModified, author (with name and URL), publisher (organisation name), and image (representative image) to maximise eligibility for enhanced display in Google Search, Google News, and Google Assistant
if_condition: If Article structured data is implemented on a news, blog, or sports article page
source_title: Article Structured Data – News, Blog, and Sports Articles

name: Do Not Set Date in the Future
then_action: Do not set the date to a future date, as this violates Google's byline date consistency requirements
if_condition: If setting or updating the publication or modification date on a page
source_title: Byline Dates – Adding Publication Dates to Search Results

name: Heavy JavaScript Usage Prevents AI Crawling
then_action: Refactor critical content to be server-side rendered or statically available in HTML, as most AI crawlers do not execute JavaScript and will miss dynamically loaded content
if_condition: If site content is rendered primarily through JavaScript (e.g., React/Vue SPA with client-side rendering) rather than server-side HTML
source_title: Generative Engine Optimization Growth Strategies and Metrics For the AI Era

name: Avoid Full AI SDR Automation
then_action: Avoid full AI SDR automation — survey data shows multiple companies tried AI SDRs for six months and generated zero opportunities; maintain manual oversight for authentic engagement
if_condition: If planning to deploy fully automated AI SDR workflows with no human oversight for outbound prospecting
source_title: 2025 B2B GTM Channel Benchmarks Winning Motions AI Adoption in GTM From What Can I Do to Show Me ROI

name: AEO as Core B2B Content Strategy
then_action: Focus content efforts on Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) to capture intent-driven discovery through AI and search answer engines
if_condition: If developing or revising a B2B content strategy for 2025 GTM execution
source_title: 2025 B2B GTM Channel Benchmarks Winning Motions FAQ

name: Audit AI Presence Across Multiple Platforms
then_action: Then conduct a systematic audit recording which brands appear, which pages get cited, and what patterns repeat — focusing on identifying gaps in prompt coverage, not just confirming existing wins
if_condition: If you are trying to improve AI citation visibility and have not yet searched your most important prompts in Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Claude
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT A Practical Workflo

name: Use AI for Data Enrichment to Improve ICP Targeting
then_action: Deploy AI-driven data enrichment tools (60%+ adoption rate) to improve ICP targeting precision and signal quality for outbound campaigns
if_condition: If ICP targeting accuracy is low or prospect data quality is insufficient for effective segmentation
source_title: 2025 B2B GTM Channel Benchmarks Winning Motions AI Adoption in GTM From What Can I Do to Show Me ROI

name: Map Content to Prompt Intent Gaps
then_action: Then map existing content against prompt intent and identify coverage gaps — many sites have plenty of content but weak prompt coverage, requiring new or restructured pages targeting unmet prompt needs
if_condition: If a site has substantial existing content but is not appearing in AI-generated citations for relevant prompts
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT A Practical Workflo

name: Build Topic Cluster Depth for AI Authority
then_action: Then build out the full topic cluster including glossaries, comparisons, FAQs, service pages, use cases, case studies, and troubleshooting content to establish the depth AI models favor when sourcing citations
if_condition: If a site has only a flagship article on a topic but lacks supporting content such as definitions, comparisons, FAQs, use cases, or troubleshooting pages
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT A Practical Workflo

name: noindex blocked by robots.txt becomes ineffective
then_action: the crawler cannot see the noindex rule, and the page may still appear in search results if other pages link to it
if_condition: if a page has a noindex directive but is also blocked by a robots.txt file
source_title: Block Search Indexing with noindex

name: Reinforce Off-Site Brand Signals for AI Citation
then_action: Then reinforce brand presence off-site by getting listed where buyers research, contributing expert commentary, earning mentions in relevant publications, and encouraging reviews on category-relevant platforms
if_condition: If a brand is not appearing in AI citations despite having quality on-site content
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT A Practical Workflo

name: Schema Helps But Does Not Guarantee AI Citations
then_action: Then recognize that schema alone will not win citations — it helps clarify page type and content elements, but must be combined with content quality, prompt coverage, and authority-building efforts
if_condition: If structured data and schema markup are being implemented as the primary or sole strategy to earn AI citations
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT A Practical Workflo

name: Treat AI Visibility as Ongoing Operations
then_action: Then shift to ongoing monitoring and refresh operations, because competitors continuously publish new material, AI models change sourcing patterns, and fast-moving topics age quickly — making periodic-only updates insufficient
if_condition: If AI citation optimization is being treated as a one-time project rather than a continuous process
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT A Practical Workflo

name: Submit Sitemap After Site Launch or Site Move
then_action: Submit a sitemap to Google Search Console immediately to help Google discover all URLs on the site quickly
if_condition: If a new site has just been launched or a site migration has been completed
source_title: Ask Google to Recrawl Your URLs

name: Use NewsArticle schema type for news content
then_action: Use the NewsArticle schema.org type specifically for that content
if_condition: If a page contains news content and Article structured data is being implemented
source_title: Article Structured Data – News, Blog, and Sports Articles

name: Third-Party Mentions Drive AI Brand Citations
then_action: Shift effort toward earning mentions on third-party sites through industry rankings, PR coverage, and customer reviews, since most AI brand mentions originate from external sources rather than owned properties
if_condition: If a brand is seeking increased visibility in AI-generated answers and currently focuses only on owned website content
source_title: Generative Engine Optimization Growth Strategies and Metrics For the AI Era

name: page still indexed after adding noindex tag
then_action: check whether Google has recrawled the page since the tag was added; use the URL Inspection tool in Search Console to request a recrawl, and verify that robots.txt is not blocking Googlebot from accessing the page
if_condition: if a page continues to appear in search results after a noindex tag has been added
source_title: Block Search Indexing with noindex

name: noindex for non-HTML resources requires HTTP header
then_action: use the X-Robots-Tag HTTP response header with a value of noindex or none instead of a meta tag
if_condition: if the resource to be de-indexed is a non-HTML file such as a PDF, video, or image
source_title: Block Search Indexing with noindex

name: noindex in robots.txt is not supported by Google
then_action: Google does not support this method; the noindex directive must appear in the HTML head section or as an HTTP response header to be effective
if_condition: if a noindex directive is placed inside the robots.txt file
source_title: Block Search Indexing with noindex

name: noindex meta tag placement for HTML pages
then_action: place a meta tag with name=robots and content=noindex in the head section of the page, ensuring the page is not blocked by robots.txt
if_condition: if a webmaster wants to prevent all supporting search engines from indexing an HTML page
source_title: Block Search Indexing with noindex

name: Verify Googlebot Access Before DNS Switch
then_action: Then use the URL Inspection Tool to verify Googlebot can access the new infrastructure and remove any temporary crawl blocks before updating DNS settings

source_title: Changing Your Web Hosting Without Changing URLs

name: Expect Temporary Crawl Rate Drop After Hosting Migration
then_action: Then treat this as a normal temporary fluctuation caused by changed hosting signals; expect crawl rate to increase steadily over the following weeks provided no serious access problems or slowdowns exist on the new server
if_condition: If Googlebot crawl rate drops immediately after launching new hosting infrastructure
source_title: Changing Your Web Hosting Without Changing URLs

name: Delay Old Server Shutdown Until Traffic Fully Migrated
then_action: Then keep the old hosting infrastructure running and only shut it down once all traffic has verifiably moved to the new infrastructure
if_condition: If a hosting migration has been completed and DNS has been updated to the new provider
source_title: Changing Your Web Hosting Without Changing URLs

name: Use Temporary Hostname for Pre-Launch Testing
then_action: Then allow public testing via a temporary hostname and create or verify Search Console accounts for both the main site and the temporary hostname before going live
if_condition: If new hosting infrastructure is set up but DNS has not yet been switched to point to it
source_title: Changing Your Web Hosting Without Changing URLs

name: Breadcrumb Markup Guidelines Violation Triggers Manual Action
then_action: The site may receive a manual action penalty; ensure breadcrumb markup strictly follows Google's structured data guidelines to maintain rich result eligibility
if_condition: If breadcrumb structured data uses implementation techniques that violate Google's general structured data guidelines

name: BreadcrumbList Minimum ListItems Requirement
then_action: The breadcrumb markup is invalid; at least two ListItems must be included to define a breadcrumb trail
if_condition: If implementing BreadcrumbList structured data with fewer than two ListItem objects
source_title: Breadcrumb Structured Data – BreadcrumbList for Site Navigation

name: BreadcrumbList Required Properties Per ListItem
then_action: Each ListItem must include: 'name' (display text), 'position' (order starting from 1), and 'item' (URL for that breadcrumb level)
if_condition: If adding a ListItem within a BreadcrumbList schema
source_title: Breadcrumb Structured Data – BreadcrumbList for Site Navigation

name: ISBN-13 as Primary Reconciliation Signal
then_action: Use ISBN-13 as the primary identifier, since Google Search prefers ISBN-13 as the main reconciliation signal to match feed data to its own data

source_title: Book Actions Structured Data – Enabling Buy and Borrow Actions

name: Breadcrumb Path Should Reflect User Journey Not URL Structure
then_action: Revise breadcrumbs to represent a typical user path to the page, as Google recommends user-journey-based breadcrumbs over URL-mirrored ones
if_condition: If breadcrumb trail is defined by mirroring the URL path structure rather than a typical user navigation path
source_title: Breadcrumb Structured Data – BreadcrumbList for Site Navigation

name: LibrarySystem Requires at Least One Library Member
then_action: Ensure the LibrarySystem entity includes at least one Library member entity, and every Library member entity belongs to at least one LibrarySystem entity
if_condition: If implementing Book Actions for library collections and defining a LibrarySystem entity in the feed
source_title: Book Actions Structured Data – Enabling Buy and Borrow Actions

name: Book Actions Requires Partner Registration
then_action: Submit an interest form to Google, as participation is limited to partners only and cannot be self-implemented
if_condition: If a publisher or library wants to implement Book Actions structured data for buy/borrow functionality in Google Search
source_title: Book Actions Structured Data – Enabling Buy and Borrow Actions

name: Data-Vocabulary.org Markup Ineligibility
then_action: Replace with schema.org BreadcrumbList markup immediately; data-vocabulary.org is no longer eligible for Google rich result features
if_condition: If a page uses data-vocabulary.org markup for breadcrumb structured data
source_title: Breadcrumb Structured Data – BreadcrumbList for Site Navigation

name: Feed Must Be Hosted Securely for Regular Google Fetching

if_condition: If a Book Actions feed file has been built and is ready for submission
source_title: Book Actions Structured Data – Enabling Buy and Borrow Actions

name: Deep Link Quality Review Before Go-Live
then_action: Expect a quality review of deep links in the feed by a Google support team before the content becomes available on Google Search
if_condition: If a Book Actions feed has been submitted and the partner expects content to appear in Google Search results
source_title: Book Actions Structured Data – Enabling Buy and Borrow Actions

name: Noindex Must Not Be Used for Intra-Site Canonical Preference
then_action: Do not use noindex for canonicalization purposes — it completely blocks the page from search results rather than just influencing canonical selection
if_condition: If a site owner uses the noindex directive on a page to prevent Google from selecting it as the canonical within a single site
source_title: Canonical URLs – How to Specify and Consolidate Duplicate Pages

name: No Conflicting Canonical Signals Across Methods
then_action: Do not specify different URLs as canonical for the same page using different canonicalization techniques — signals must be consistent across all methods
if_condition: If different canonicalization techniques (e.g., rel=canonical, sitemap, redirect) are applied to the same page but point to different URLs
source_title: Canonical URLs – How to Specify and Consolidate Duplicate Pages

name: URL Fragments Must Not Be Specified as Canonical
then_action: Do not specify URL fragments as canonical — Google generally does not support URL fragments for canonicalization
if_condition: If a canonical URL being specified contains a URL fragment (e.g., page.html#section)
source_title: Canonical URLs – How to Specify and Consolidate Duplicate Pages

name: Hreflang Pages Must Specify Language-Matching Canonical
then_action: Specify a canonical page in the same language as the hreflang-annotated page to ensure consistency between language signals and canonical signals
if_condition: If hreflang elements are used on a page to indicate language or regional variants
source_title: Canonical URLs – How to Specify and Consolidate Duplicate Pages

name: Internal Links Must Point to Canonical URLs
then_action: Update internal links to point to the canonical URL — linking to duplicate URLs weakens canonicalization signals and can cause Google to select a different canonical than intended
if_condition: If internal links within a site point to duplicate or non-canonical versions of a URL rather than the canonical URL
source_title: Canonical URLs – How to Specify and Consolidate Duplicate Pages

name: JavaScript Must Not Modify Canonical Link Element in Client-Side Rendering
then_action: Specify the canonical URL in the static HTML source code and do not use JavaScript to change the canonical link element — Google requires the canonical to be present in the original HTML for client-side rendered pages
if_condition: If a site uses client-side rendering with JavaScript and the canonical URL is set or modified dynamically via JavaScript rather than in the HTML source
source_title: Canonical URLs – How to Specify and Consolidate Duplicate Pages

name: Core Update Recovery Analysis Timing
then_action: Confirm the core update has finished rolling out via the Search Status Dashboard, wait at least one full week after it completes before analyzing in Search Console, then compare performance from the week after conclusion against a week before the update started, and review top pages and queries to assess the scale of the drop
if_condition: If a site experiences a drop in search rankings that correlates with the timing of a Google core update
source_title: Core Updates – What They Are and How to Respond

name: Course Detail Page Requires Description and Name

source_title: Course List Structured Data – Course Discoverability on Google

name: Site Owners Without Ad Networks Require No Action for Translated Results
then_action: No action is required — the translated results will work correctly without any intervention; the URL decoding guidance applies only to ad network operators who integrate with publisher pages
if_condition: If a site owner does not operate their own ad network and their pages appear in Google translated search results
source_title: Ad Networks and Translation Related Google Search Features

name: Google Translate Proxy URL Detection for Ad Networks
then_action: The page is being served through Google's translation proxy, and ad networks that depend on the original publisher URL must decode the proxied URL to ensure ads render and attribute correctly
if_condition: If a page is accessed through Google's translated search results and the URL hostname contains the .translate.goog suffix
source_title: Ad Networks and Translation Related Google Search Features

name: Google Translate URL Hostname Decoding Process
then_action: Decode the original hostname by: (1) extracting the domain prefix by removing .translate.goog suffix, (2) splitting _x_tr_enc parameter by commas to get encoding list, (3) prepending _x_tr_hp parameter value to domain prefix if that parameter exists, (4) removing 1- prefix if encoding list contains '1' and domain prefix begins with '1-', (5) replacing hyphens with dots in the resulting string, (6) adding punycode prefix 'xn--' if is_idn parameter is set to true
if_condition: If an ad network detects a URL served through translate.goog proxy (hostname contains .translate.goog suffix)
source_title: Ad Networks and Translation Related Google Search Features

name: A/B Test Cloaking Prohibition
then_action: This constitutes cloaking and violates Google's spam policies — do not implement regardless of whether a test is active
if_condition: If a website test shows different URLs or content to Googlebot versus human users (via server logic, robots.txt, or any other method)
source_title: AB Testing and Multivariate Testing Best Practices for Google Search

name: Reconstruct Original Publisher URL After Hostname Decoding
then_action: Reconstruct the original URL by replacing the proxied hostname with the decoded hostname and removing all _x_tr_* parameters from the URL
if_condition: If the hostname has been decoded from a translate.goog proxied URL
source_title: Ad Networks and Translation Related Google Search Features

name: Measure Non-Click AI Visibility Outcomes
then_action: Then AI visibility impact is being undervalued and underinvested in; add non-click outcome measurement (AI citations, brand mentions in AI responses, influenced demand) alongside traditional session metrics

name: Cookie-Based Test Visibility for Googlebot

name: rel=canonical on Alternate Test URLs

name: Full Post Text Required in DiscussionForumPosting Element

name: Hire SEO for Large or Complex Sites

name: AEO Manual Tracking Log Setup
then_action: Create a tracking log with columns for date, query, AI platform (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, Bing Chat), brand mentioned (yes/no), cited as primary source (yes/no), competitor mentioned, and notes; assign a team member to query 10-15 high-priority questions across multiple AI platforms weekly

name: Prefer Server-Side or Static Rendering Over Dynamic Rendering

then_action: Then Google considers it cloaking, which violates webmaster guidelines and can result in penalties
if_condition: If dynamic rendering serves completely different content to crawlers than what users see

name: AEO Answer-First Content Structure Requirement
then_action: Lead every section with a direct answer in 40-60 words, and add statistics with source citations every 150-200 words to meet minimum AEO structural requirements

name: AEO Content Prioritization Rule

name: Navigation Must Follow Logical Hierarchy for Crawlability

name: BreadcrumbList Structured Data Communicates Page Hierarchy

name: Design Ecommerce URL Structure Following Google Best Practices

name: Implement Ecommerce-Specific Structured Data Types

name: Paginated Pages Must Have Unique URLs

name: URL Case Consistency Requirement

name: Large Ecommerce Sites Must Submit Sitemaps

name: Fragment Identifiers Cause Indexing Gaps

name: AEO Citation Manual Tracking Rule

name: Faceted Navigation Must Be Managed to Prevent Crawl Waste

name: Avoid Internal Linking to Temporary URL Parameters

name: Product Variants Should Have Separate URLs with Canonical

name: Empty Category Pages Should Use Noindex

name: Answer-First Content Positioning for AI Visibility
then_action: Place the core answer or key message directly under the heading at the top of the page before any elaboration, using the inverted triangle structure (most important point first, then supporting detail)

then_action: Structure content with clear headers, bullet lists, bold text, and tight paragraphs to enable easy parsing and extraction by AI engines

name: Link to Third-Party Pages to Improve Entity Understanding
then_action: Add canonical reference links using itemprop="url" pointing to those third-party pages to help search engines better understand and validate the entity being described

name: Irrelevant Structured Data Penalty
then_action: The structured data is considered irrelevant and unacceptable, disqualifying the content from enriched search participation

then_action: The item is considered lower quality by the enriched search ranking algorithm, reducing its likelihood of appearing in enriched results
if_condition: If structured data markup for enriched search results is missing recommended (optional) properties

name: Enriched Search Filter Exclusion Rule
then_action: The listing cannot appear in enriched search results when that filter is applied

if_condition: If a site wants to appear in enriched search result types such as recipes, jobs, or events

name: Site-Wide Enriched Search Exclusion
then_action: The entire site may be excluded from all enriched search results

name: Online Event Attendance Mode Configuration

name: Use time tag with datetime attribute for unambiguous dates
then_action: wrap the text in a <time> tag and include a datetime attribute using ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD for dates, prefix T for times, prefix P for durations) to ensure machine-readable unambiguous temporal data
if_condition: if a web page contains human-readable date, time, or duration text that needs to be marked up with structured data
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3a Dates times and durations use the time tag with datetime

name: Schema Markup for Collection Pages
then_action: Mark up each item separately and add the url property to each item linking to its corresponding individual page

name: Schema Markup for Entity Reinforcement
then_action: Implement schema types (Organization, Product, Service, FAQ) to reinforce factual accuracy, improve content extraction, and increase voice search visibility

name: Event Status Update for Cancelled or Rescheduled Events

if_condition: If an event that has Event structured data is cancelled, postponed, or rescheduled

name: Image Licensable Badge Eligibility via Metadata
then_action: Then the image becomes eligible for the Licensable badge in Google Images, which displays a link to the licence and usage details

name: IPTC Metadata Embedded Once Per Image
then_action: Then the metadata only needs to be embedded once into the image file itself, as it travels with the image regardless of where it is used

name: Network or DNS Errors Trigger Immediate Crawl Slowdown and Index Removal
then_action: Crawling immediately slows down and URLs that remain unreachable will be removed from the index within days
if_condition: If Googlebot encounters network timeouts, connection reset errors, or DNS errors when attempting to reach URLs
source_title: HTTP Status Codes, Network Errors, and DNS Errors

name: Soft 404 Causes Indexing Confusion
then_action: Google identifies this as a soft 404 error, which generates a warning in Search Console and may negatively affect indexing decisions
if_condition: If a URL returns a 200 status code but the page content tells the user the page does not exist
source_title: HTTP Status Codes, Network Errors, and DNS Errors

name: 5xx Errors Slow Googlebot Crawl Rate
then_action: Googlebot will slow its crawl rate for the site; individual 5xx errors are treated as temporary and Googlebot will retry the URL
if_condition: If a site repeatedly returns 5xx server error status codes across multiple crawl attempts

name: Answer-Focused Content for AI Citation
then_action: Then content must directly and completely answer the user's question in natural language that an AI model can interpret, extract, and cite — moving away from keyword-dense formats toward structured, answer-first writing
if_condition: If content is being created or optimized for visibility on AI answer engines like Perplexity AI

name: 204 No Content Treated as No Indexable Content
then_action: Google treats this as no content available to index for that URL
if_condition: If a server returns a 204 No Content status code in response to a Googlebot request

name: ImageObject Schema Properties for Licensing Structured Data
then_action: Then use the ImageObject type with properties: contentUrl (image URL), licence (URL of licence page), acquireLicensePage (how to licence the image), and creditText (attribution text to display)

source_title: Image License Metadata – Licensable Badge in Google Images

name: Conversational Query Alignment for Answer Engine Ranking
then_action: Then content should be structured to match and answer full questions directly, improving the likelihood of being quoted or cited by Perplexity AI and similar answer engines

name: Block Images from Google Images Only via robots.txt
then_action: Add a Disallow rule in robots.txt targeting the Googlebot-Image user agent for the relevant image URLs or patterns
if_condition: If you want to remove images from Google Images specifically but not all Google search surfaces
source_title: Image Removals – Removing Images from Google Search Results

name: High-Quality Structure Increases Direct Citation Likelihood
then_action: Then Perplexity AI is more likely to directly quote or cite that content in its answers, driving referral traffic and brand visibility through the citation link mechanism
if_condition: If content is well-structured, clearly written, and authoritative in its subject matter
source_title: How to Rank on Perplexity AI Embracing the Power of Answer Engines What Is Perplexity AI and Why It

name: Block Images from All Google Search Surfaces via robots.txt
then_action: Use the Googlebot user agent (instead of Googlebot-Image) in the robots.txt Disallow rule to exclude images from all Google search surfaces
if_condition: If you want to remove images from both Google Search and Google Images
source_title: Image Removals – Removing Images from Google Search Results

name: noimageindex Page Tag Does Not Guarantee Full Image Removal
then_action: Be aware that if the same images appear on other pages without the noimageindex tag, they may still be indexed through those other pages — use noindex X-Robots-Tag on the image resource for complete coverage
if_condition: If the noimageindex robots tag is added to a page to prevent its embedded images from being indexed
source_title: Image Removals – Removing Images from Google Search Results

name: Allow Crawling When Using noindex X-Robots-Tag for Image Removal
then_action: Ensure the image URLs are NOT blocked by robots.txt, so Googlebot can crawl the image and read the noindex header — blocking crawling will prevent the noindex rule from being processed
if_condition: If the noindex X-Robots-Tag HTTP response header is used to remove images from search results
source_title: Image Removals – Removing Images from Google Search Results

name: Use noindex X-Robots-Tag on Image Resource for Universal Image Blocking
then_action: Apply the noindex X-Robots-Tag HTTP response header directly on the image resource itself, rather than using noimageindex on individual pages
if_condition: If an image must be blocked from indexing regardless of which pages embed it
source_title: Image Removals – Removing Images from Google Search Results

name: Deprecated image sitemap tags must not be used
then_action: remove these tags as Google has deprecated and no longer supports them
if_condition: if an image sitemap includes image:caption, image:geo_location, image:title, or image:license tags
source_title: Image Sitemaps – Helping Google Find Site Images

name: robots.txt Image Removal Applies Cross-Engine but is Slower Than Removals Tool
then_action: Expect slower removal from search results but gain broader control (wildcards, path-based blocking) and cross-search-engine applicability, whereas the Removals tool only affects Google
if_condition: If robots.txt is chosen over the Google Removals tool to block images
source_title: Image Removals – Removing Images from Google Search Results

name: image:loc is the only required image sitemap tag
then_action: always include the image:loc tag specifying the image URL; all other tags (caption, geo_location, title, license) are deprecated and should not be used
if_condition: if adding image details within an image:image element in a sitemap
source_title: Image Sitemaps – Helping Google Find Site Images

name: PPC Ad Headline Continuous Testing
then_action: Continually test different versions of PPC ad headlines to maximize click-through rates; Bing saw a 12% revenue increase from headline A/B testing
if_condition: If PPC ad headlines are static and have not been tested against alternative variants
source_title: CRO Statistics 34 Vital Conversion Rate Optimization Stats 2025   Shopify Landing page CRO statistic

name: Specify Primary Image via Schema or OG Tag
then_action: Use the schema.org primaryImageOfPage property or og:image meta tag with a relevant, representative image (not a generic image like the site logo)
if_condition: If a site owner wants to control which image Google selects as the primary image for a page
source_title: Image SEO Best Practices for Google Search

name: Special Offers Surface for Coupon and Deal Queries
then_action: Then special offers and deals from that brand can appear in Google Search results
if_condition: If a user searches for coupons, discount codes, or sales for a specific brand

then_action: Then products may appear as standard web results, rich results, or product-specific formats depending on the specific intent of the query
if_condition: If Google interprets a search query as having ecommerce or product intent

name: Use Browser-Native Mechanisms for App Install Banners
then_action: Replace it with browser-supported banner mechanisms such as Smart App Banners for Safari or the in-app install experience for Chrome
if_condition: If a page prompts users to install a mobile app using a custom full-page or large interstitial overlay
source_title: Interstitials and Dialogs – Avoiding Intrusive Page Overlays

name: Block Google Images Inline Linking via HTTP Referrer
then_action: Detect the HTTP referrer header on image requests; if the request originates from a Google domain, respond with HTTP 200 or 204 with no content to block inline display
if_condition: If a site owner wants to opt out of inline linking in Google Images search results

name: Use Bubble Chart for Long-Tail Query Analysis
then_action: Use a Search Console bubble chart in Looker Studio with y-axis as average position, x-axis as CTR, bubble size as total clicks, and bubble color as device category to simultaneously analyze multiple performance dimensions
if_condition: If a site has many long-tail queries that are difficult to visualize and understand in standard tabular or line chart formats

name: High Position Low CTR Requires Snippet Optimization
then_action: Optimize the search result snippet or title link to improve CTR, as the page ranks well but is not attracting proportional clicks
if_condition: If a query appears in the top position quadrant (high ranking) but has low click-through rate in the Search Console bubble chart

name: Multilingual Site Language Matching
then_action: Google Search will try to find and serve pages that match the language of the searcher, so each language version should be properly signalled to Google
if_condition: If a website offers content in more than one language (multilingual site)
source_title: International and Multilingual Sites – Overview

name: Locale-Adaptive Pages Risk Warning
then_action: Google might not crawl, index, or rank all content for different locales — use separate locale URL configurations with hreflang annotations instead
if_condition: If a site returns different content based on the visitor's perceived country or language (locale-adaptive pages) using a single URL
source_title: International and Multilingual Sites – Overview

name: Low Position High CTR Requires Ranking Improvement
then_action: Prioritize improving the ranking position for this query, as it is clearly relevant to users and a position improvement could have significant impact on overall performance
if_condition: If a query appears in the low position quadrant but has high click-through rate in the Search Console bubble chart
source_title: Improve SEO with a Search Console Bubble Chart in Looker Studio

name: Low Position Low CTR Queries Need Comprehensive Action
then_action: Flag the query as underperforming on both ranking and click-through metrics, requiring deeper investigation and likely both content and SEO improvements
if_condition: If a query appears in the low position and low CTR quadrant of the Search Console bubble chart
source_title: Improve SEO with a Search Console Bubble Chart in Looker Studio

name: Hreflang Annotation Requirement for Multilingual/Multi-Regional Sites
then_action: Use hreflang annotations and sitemaps to explicitly tell Google about the different language and regional versions so they are correctly crawled, indexed, and ranked
if_condition: If a site has different language or regional versions of pages across separate URLs

name: Multi-Regional Site Locale Matching
then_action: Google Search will try to find the right locale page for the searcher, so geotargeting signals and URL structure options must be configured correctly
if_condition: If a website explicitly targets users in different countries (multi-regional site)
source_title: International and Multilingual Sites – Overview

name: Avoid Redirecting Users to Separate Consent Pages
then_action: Replace the redirect with an on-page mechanism so both users and search engines can access content without being diverted
if_condition: If users are redirected to a separate page to provide consent or input before accessing the main page content
source_title: Interstitials and Dialogs – Avoiding Intrusive Page Overlays

name: Avoid Full-Page Interstitials Blocking Content
then_action: Remove the full-page interstitial as it prevents Google from understanding page content and may lead to poor search performance
if_condition: If a page displays a full-page interstitial or overlay that obscures all content from users and search engines upon landing
source_title: Interstitials and Dialogs – Avoiding Intrusive Page Overlays

name: Use Small Banners Instead of Intrusive Overlays for Promotions
then_action: Use a banner that takes up only a small fraction of the screen, ensuring users and search engines can access content immediately upon landing
if_condition: If a page needs to display a promotional message or call-to-action that would otherwise require a full-page interstitial or large dialog
source_title: Interstitials and Dialogs – Avoiding Intrusive Page Overlays

name: Mandatory Interstitials Must Still Allow Partial Content Indexing
then_action: Ensure the interstitial overlays the content rather than replacing it entirely, so Google can still index at least some of the underlying page content
if_condition: If a site is legally required to show an interstitial (e.g., an age gate on a casino site) and the interstitial covers the entire page
source_title: Interstitials and Dialogs – Avoiding Intrusive Page Overlays

name: High Position High CTR Queries Require No Action
then_action: No significant action is needed as the query is performing well across both ranking and click-through dimensions
if_condition: If a query appears in the top position and high CTR quadrant of the Search Console bubble chart
source_title: Improve SEO with a Search Console Bubble Chart in Looker Studio

name: Meet Search Essentials Requirements for Google Indexing Eligibility
then_action: The website will not be eligible to appear in Google Search results
if_condition: If a website does not meet the technical requirements and spam policies outlined in Google Search Essentials
source_title: Introduction – Google Search Central Documentation

name: Use Search Console and Search Operators for Performance Monitoring
then_action: Use Search Console, search operators, and other tools as covered in the Monitoring and Debugging section of Google Search Central
if_condition: If a site owner needs to monitor search performance or diagnose indexing and ranking problems
source_title: Introduction – Google Search Central Documentation

name: Address Google Crawling Difficulties to Avoid Traffic Loss
then_action: The site owner may be missing out on an important source of traffic and should investigate crawling and indexing issues using tools like Search Console, sitemaps, and robots.txt
if_condition: If Google Search has trouble understanding or crawling a page
source_title: Introduction – Google Search Central Documentation

name: Robots.txt Compliance Is Not Guaranteed Across All Crawlers
then_action: Robots.txt rules may not be supported or enforced by all crawlers, as compliance is voluntary and syntax interpretation may differ between crawlers
if_condition: If robots.txt rules are used to control crawler access across multiple search engines
source_title: Introduction to robots.txt

name: Use Structured Data to Influence Search Appearance
then_action: Implement structured data and optimize page experience as covered under the Ranking and Search Appearance section of Google Search Central documentation
if_condition: If a site owner wants to influence how their site appears in Google Search results including rich results and search features
source_title: Introduction – Google Search Central Documentation

name: Use Noindex or Password Protection to Prevent Indexing
then_action: Use the noindex meta tag or response header, password-protect the page on the server, or remove the page entirely — do NOT rely solely on robots.txt disallow rules
if_condition: If the goal is to prevent a specific web page from appearing in Google Search results
source_title: Introduction to robots.txt

name: Scope Google Search Central Guidance to Public Web Content Only
then_action: The guidance does not apply — Google Search Central documentation only covers organic web content (pages, images, videos) that Google finds on the public web
if_condition: If applying Google Search Central documentation guidance to paid advertising, organic social media, email marketing, or non-public web content
source_title: Introduction – Google Search Central Documentation

name: Robots.txt for Media Files Blocks Search Appearance
then_action: Those media files will not appear in Google Search results, even if the pages linking to them remain indexable
if_condition: If media files (images, videos, audio) are blocked via robots.txt
source_title: Introduction to robots.txt

name: Robots.txt for Resource Files Reduces Unnecessary Crawling
then_action: Block them in robots.txt to prevent unnecessary crawl budget consumption without impacting indexing of content pages
if_condition: If resource files such as scripts, stylesheets, or image files do not need to appear in search results
source_title: Introduction to robots.txt

name: URL Fragments Are Not Crawlable for Client-Side Routing
then_action: The content loaded via fragments will not be crawlable by Googlebot; use the History API instead for client-side routing
if_condition: If a single-page application uses URL fragments (e.g., example.com/#/page) to load different content for client-side routing
source_title: JavaScript SEO Basics – How Google Crawls and Renders JavaScript

name: Noindex Tag Present Before JavaScript Execution Skips Rendering
then_action: Google may skip rendering and JavaScript execution entirely for that page, preventing any JavaScript-dependent content or tag changes from being processed
if_condition: If a noindex robots meta tag is present in the initial HTML source before JavaScript executes
source_title: JavaScript SEO Basics – How Google Crawls and Renders JavaScript

name: Shadow DOM Content Must Be Visible in Rendered HTML for Indexing
then_action: Google will not be able to index that content; use slot elements or equivalent mechanisms to ensure content appears in the rendered HTML
if_condition: If a web component uses shadow DOM to encapsulate content that is not exposed via slot elements or other mechanisms in the rendered HTML
source_title: JavaScript SEO Basics – How Google Crawls and Renders JavaScript

name: Server-Side or Pre-Rendering Recommended for JavaScript Sites
then_action: Implement server-side rendering or pre-rendering to improve crawlability for all crawlers (not just Google), improve page speed for users, and reduce dependency on Google's JavaScript rendering queue
if_condition: If a site relies primarily on client-side JavaScript rendering to generate page content
source_title: JavaScript SEO Basics – How Google Crawls and Renders JavaScript

name: Canonical URLs Should Be in HTML Source for JavaScript Applications
then_action: Define canonical URLs in the HTML source code where possible rather than injecting them dynamically with JavaScript, to ensure Google reliably processes them
if_condition: If a JavaScript application needs to specify canonical URLs for its pages
source_title: JavaScript SEO Basics – How Google Crawls and Renders JavaScript

name: Use JSON-LD for Structured Data in JavaScript Applications
then_action: Use JSON-LD format as it is Google's preferred structured data format for JavaScript applications
if_condition: If structured data needs to be added to a JavaScript-rendered page
source_title: JavaScript SEO Basics – How Google Crawls and Renders JavaScript

name: Use Meaningful HTTP Status Codes to Communicate Page Status to Googlebot
then_action: Return a 404 HTTP status code for pages that do not exist and a 401 HTTP status code for pages behind a login, so Googlebot correctly interprets page availability
if_condition: If a JavaScript application serves pages that are missing (not found) or restricted (behind authentication)
source_title: JavaScript SEO Basics – How Google Crawls and Renders JavaScript

name: Keywords Meta Tag Not Used for Ranking
then_action: The keywords meta tag is ignored by Google and has no effect on rankings; remove or deprioritize it to avoid wasted effort
if_condition: If a page includes a <meta name='keywords'> tag intended to influence Google search rankings
source_title: Meta Tags and HTML Attributes that Google Supports

name: JavaScript Links Must Use Anchor Elements with href for Crawlability
then_action: Googlebot will not be able to crawl those links, and linked pages may not be discovered or indexed
if_condition: If a JavaScript application uses non-anchor elements (e.g., div, span, button) or JavaScript event handlers instead of <a href='...'> tags for navigation links
source_title: JavaScript SEO Basics – How Google Crawls and Renders JavaScript

name: Document Text Must Be Removed Before Publishing
then_action: Remove all non-public text before generating the final document, convert to a public format that strips change history, and do not rely on black rectangle overlays for redaction
if_condition: If a document containing draft or private text is being prepared for public release
source_title: Keep Redacted Information Out of Google Search

name: Visual Redaction Methods Do Not Prevent Search Indexing
then_action: The content is NOT redacted from search engines and may be indexed by Google; proper redaction tools must be used instead
if_condition: If text is hidden using tiny fonts, color-matching to background, or covered with an image overlay in a web document
source_title: Keep Redacted Information Out of Google Search

name: Image Redaction Requires Flattened Non-Vector Export
then_action: Crop or fully remove sensitive content, strip metadata, and export as a flattened non-vector format such as PNG or WEBP before publishing
if_condition: If an image containing sensitive or unwanted information is being embedded in a public document
source_title: Keep Redacted Information Out of Google Search

name: Remove Already-Published Redacted Documents Using GSC Removals Tool
then_action: Verify the site in Google Search Console, remove the live document from the website, then use the GSC Removals tool to request removal of the document from search results
if_condition: If sensitive or improperly redacted information has already been published and indexed by Google
source_title: Keep Redacted Information Out of Google Search

name: robots.txt Block Does Not Prevent URL Indexing
then_action: The URL itself may still be indexed in Google Search even if its content is not crawled; sensitive information in the URL or filename must also be considered and addressed
if_condition: If a URL or file containing sensitive information is blocked via robots.txt
source_title: Keep Redacted Information Out of Google Search

name: Place JobPosting Structured Data on Leaf Pages
then_action: Place JobPosting structured data on the most detailed leaf page (individual job detail page) rather than on listing or index pages
if_condition: If a site has both listing/index pages and individual detail pages for job postings
source_title: Job Posting Structured Data – JobPosting for Job Search Experience

name: Remove Expired Job Postings Properly
then_action: Take one of the following actions: set validThrough to a past date, remove the page so it returns a 404 or 410 status code, or remove the JobPosting structured data from the page
if_condition: If a job posting is no longer available or has expired
source_title: Job Posting Structured Data – JobPosting for Job Search Experience

name: Include Required JobPosting Properties
then_action: Ensure the following required properties are present: title, description, hiringOrganization, and jobLocation to be eligible for job search rich results
if_condition: If JobPosting structured data is being implemented on a job listing page
source_title: Job Posting Structured Data – JobPosting for Job Search Experience

name: Specify Custom Logo in hiringOrganization
then_action: Use the logo property within the hiringOrganization schema to suggest or specify the desired logo
if_condition: If an organisation wants a different logo displayed in job search results than the one in its Google Knowledge Graph
source_title: Job Posting Structured Data – JobPosting for Job Search Experience

name: URL Fragments Not Crawlable — Use History API Instead
then_action: That content is not crawlable by Googlebot; replace fragment-based routing with the History API (pushState) for client-side routing so distinct URLs are indexable
if_condition: If a single-page application uses URL fragments (hash-based routing, e.g. /#/page) to load different content
source_title: JavaScript SEO Basics – How Google Crawls and Renders JavaScript

name: Mark Remote Jobs with TELECOMMUTE jobLocationType
then_action: Set jobLocationType property to TELECOMMUTE and optionally use applicantLocationRequirements to specify geographic restrictions
if_condition: If a job posting is for a work-from-home or remote opportunity
source_title: Job Posting Structured Data – JobPosting for Job Search Experience

name: Use Indexing API for Job Posting URLs
then_action: Use the Indexing API instead of sitemaps to notify Google, as it prompts Googlebot to crawl job posting pages sooner
if_condition: If a website publishes job posting pages with JobPosting structured data
source_title: Job Posting Structured Data – JobPosting for Job Search Experience

name: Crawl Budget Increase Requires Content Value, Not Speed Alone
then_action: Then this will NOT increase crawl budget; only improving content value to searchers and increasing server capacity will increase crawl budget
if_condition: If a site owner improves page load speed on low-quality pages expecting increased crawl budget
source_title: Large Site Owner's Guide to Managing Your Crawl Budget

name: Use Meaningful HTTP Status Codes in JavaScript Apps
then_action: Return the correct HTTP status code (404 for not-found pages, 401 for login-gated pages) so Googlebot correctly interprets page status instead of queuing soft-404 or protected pages for indexing
if_condition: If a JavaScript application returns a 200 HTTP status code for pages that are not found or are behind authentication
source_title: JavaScript SEO Basics – How Google Crawls and Renders JavaScript

name: Required Property: Name Must Be Present
then_action: The 'name' property must be included as it is a required property for LocalBusiness structured data
if_condition: If LocalBusiness structured data is implemented on any page
source_title: Local Business Structured Data – LocalBusiness for Knowledge Panels and Maps

name: Do Not Use Noindex to Reduce Crawl Consumption
then_action: Then Googlebot will still crawl those pages to read the noindex tag, making noindex ineffective as a crawl reduction method; use robots.txt to block crawling instead
if_condition: If a site owner applies noindex tags to pages with the goal of reducing crawl budget consumption
source_title: Large Site Owner's Guide to Managing Your Crawl Budget

name: Canonical URLs Must Be in HTML Source in JavaScript Apps
then_action: Specify canonical URLs in the HTML source code where possible so they are reliably read before rendering; JavaScript-injected canonicals may not be processed if rendering is skipped
if_condition: If a JavaScript application sets canonical URLs dynamically via JavaScript after page load
source_title: JavaScript SEO Basics – How Google Crawls and Renders JavaScript

name: Use Most Specific LocalBusiness Subtype
then_action: Use the most specific applicable LocalBusiness subtype (e.g., Restaurant, DaySpa, HealthClub) rather than the generic LocalBusiness type
if_condition: If implementing LocalBusiness structured data for a business that falls into a specific category (e.g., restaurant, spa, gym)
source_title: Local Business Structured Data – LocalBusiness for Knowledge Panels and Maps

name: Add Recommended LocalBusiness Properties for Rich Results
then_action: Include recommended properties: address, telephone, url, openingHoursSpecification, geo, image, priceRange, and aggregateRating
if_condition: If LocalBusiness structured data is implemented and the goal is to maximize knowledge panel and Maps visibility
