source_title: Local Business Structured Data – LocalBusiness for Knowledge Panels and Maps

name: Include Organisation Properties with LocalBusiness
then_action: Also follow Organisation properties since LocalBusiness is a subtype of Organisation
if_condition: If adding LocalBusiness structured data to any business page
source_title: Local Business Structured Data – LocalBusiness for Knowledge Panels and Maps

name: Separate LocalBusiness Entity Per Physical Location
then_action: Define each physical location as a separate LocalBusiness entity with its own structured data
if_condition: If a business has multiple physical locations
source_title: Local Business Structured Data – LocalBusiness for Knowledge Panels and Maps

name: Use Maps Booking API for Transactional Search Actions
then_action: Implement the Maps Booking API in conjunction with LocalBusiness structured data to support these capabilities
if_condition: If a business wants to enable bookings, payments, or other direct actions from search results
source_title: Local Business Structured Data – LocalBusiness for Knowledge Panels and Maps

name: Do Not Use Robots.txt Temporarily to Reallocate Crawl Budget
then_action: Then this practice is not recommended and should not be used as a crawl budget management strategy
if_condition: If a site owner temporarily blocks URLs via robots.txt with the intent of reallocating crawl budget to other sections
source_title: Large Site Owner's Guide to Managing Your Crawl Budget

name: JavaScript-Inserted Links Must Render as Standard Anchors
then_action: Ensure the final rendered HTML markup uses a standard anchor element with an href attribute; otherwise Google may not crawl those links
if_condition: If links are dynamically inserted into a page using JavaScript
source_title: Link Best Practices – Crawlability and Anchor Text

name: User-Generated Links Must Use rel='ugc'
then_action: Apply the rel='ugc' attribute to that link to signal its origin to Google
if_condition: If a link was created by users in comments, forums, or other user-generated content areas
source_title: Link Best Practices – Crawlability and Anchor Text

name: Crawl Demand Is Higher for Popular, Unique, and Recently Updated Content
then_action: Then Google's crawl demand for those pages is higher, resulting in more frequent crawling even if crawl capacity limit is not reached
if_condition: If a site's pages are popular among users, contain unique content, or have been recently updated
source_title: Large Site Owner's Guide to Managing Your Crawl Budget

name: Paid Links Must Use rel='sponsored'
then_action: Apply the rel='sponsored' attribute to that link to comply with Google's link attribute guidelines
if_condition: If a link was placed or influenced by payment, sponsorship, or commercial arrangement
source_title: Link Best Practices – Crawlability and Anchor Text

name: Do Not Apply nofollow Universally to Trusted External Links
then_action: Remove blanket nofollow from trusted external links; reserve nofollow only for links where the source or destination is not trusted
if_condition: If nofollow is applied broadly to all external outbound links, including those pointing to trusted sources
source_title: Link Best Practices – Crawlability and Anchor Text

name: Use UnitPriceSpecification for Product-Level Loyalty Benefits
then_action: Then add UnitPriceSpecification markup under the Offer structured data markup, using validForMemberTier and MembershipPointsEarned to define benefits per tier
if_condition: If loyalty prices or points earned need to be specified for individual products
source_title: Loyalty Program Structured Data – MemberProgram for Membership Benefits

name: Merchant Center Settings Override Structured Data for Loyalty Programmes
then_action: Then Google will use the Merchant Center settings, ignoring the structured data markup for loyalty programme information
if_condition: If both MemberProgram structured data markup and Merchant Center loyalty programme configurations are provided simultaneously
source_title: Loyalty Program Structured Data – MemberProgram for Membership Benefits

name: Malware Detection Triggers Search Console Security Warning
then_action: Google flags the site as hosting malware and lists suspected files in the Security Issues report in Search Console
if_condition: If a website hosts downloadable binaries or runs web-based applications that install software without user consent or contain viruses
source_title: Malware and Unwanted Software – What They Are and Google's Guidelines

name: Unwanted Software Warning for False Service Claims
then_action: Google classifies it as unwanted software and triggers browser or search warnings for the site
if_condition: If a software or website claims to provide a service or functionality it does not or cannot actually provide
source_title: Malware and Unwanted Software – What They Are and Google's Guidelines

name: Consolidate Duplicate Content to Improve Crawl Efficiency
then_action: Then consolidate duplicate content so Googlebot focuses crawl budget on unique, indexable content rather than redundant pages
if_condition: If a large site has duplicate or near-duplicate content across multiple URLs
source_title: Large Site Owner's Guide to Managing Your Crawl Budget

name: Image Links Require Descriptive Alt Text as Anchor Text Substitute
then_action: Add descriptive alt text to the img element, as Google uses the alt attribute as the anchor text for that link
if_condition: If an image element is used as a hyperlink (wrapped in an anchor tag)
source_title: Link Best Practices – Crawlability and Anchor Text

name: Link Crawlability: HTML Anchor with href Required
then_action: Google cannot reliably crawl or extract the URL; restructure the link as an HTML anchor element with a valid href attribute to ensure crawlability
if_condition: If a link is implemented using a non-anchor HTML element, an anchor without an href attribute, or a script-event-only tag (e.g., onclick without href)
source_title: Link Best Practices – Crawlability and Anchor Text

name: Every Important Page Must Have At Least One Internal Link
then_action: Add at least one internal link from another page on the site to ensure Google can discover and understand the page's role in the site structure
if_condition: If a page that matters to the site has no internal links pointing to it from any other page on the same site
source_title: Link Best Practices – Crawlability and Anchor Text

name: Unwanted Software Warning for Unauthorized Browser Setting Changes
then_action: Google classifies the behavior as unwanted software and the site or app will display warnings to users
if_condition: If a software or application changes the user's default search settings, startup page, or new tab page without using the Chrome Settings Override API
source_title: Malware and Unwanted Software – What They Are and Google's Guidelines

name: Unwanted Software Warning for SSL/TLS Degradation
then_action: Google classifies it as unwanted software and the site will be flagged in Security Issues report
if_condition: If an application or website degrades TLS/SSL connection security or intercepts SSL/TLS connections by installing root certificate-authority certificates
source_title: Malware and Unwanted Software – What They Are and Google's Guidelines

name: Unwanted Software Warning for Non-Disclosed Private Data Transmission
then_action: Google classifies the behavior as unwanted software and triggers warnings for the site or application
if_condition: If an application transmits private user data to servers in ways unrelated to app functionality, without disclosing this to users, or without encrypting the data
source_title: Malware and Unwanted Software – What They Are and Google's Guidelines

name: MemberProgram Must Be Nested Under Organisation Type
then_action: Then it must be nested under the Organisation structured data type on the page where administrative details and policies of the business are specified
if_condition: If MemberProgram structured data is implemented on a site for loyalty programme information
source_title: Loyalty Program Structured Data – MemberProgram for Membership Benefits

name: Loyalty Programme Structured Data Geographic Eligibility
then_action: Then loyalty programme information will not appear in Google Search results, as the feature is only available in those listed countries
if_condition: If a merchant implements MemberProgram structured data and their business operates outside of Australia, Brazil, Canada, France, Germany, Mexico, the UK, or the US
source_title: Loyalty Program Structured Data – MemberProgram for Membership Benefits

name: Monitor and Fix Technical Site Health Issues
then_action: Then monitor for crawl errors, broken links, redirect chains, mobile and desktop performance issues, and security vulnerabilities as part of ongoing technical maintenance
if_condition: If a site is growing in size or complexity, or has undergone recent changes such as redirects or structural updates
source_title: Maintaining Your Website's SEO

name: Monitor Search Console Performance Regularly
then_action: Then regularly monitor Google Search Console Performance report (clicks, impressions, CTR, average position), Page Indexing report, and Core Web Vitals report to identify trends and catch problems early
if_condition: If a website is live and receiving search traffic, especially after algorithm updates or site changes
source_title: Maintaining Your Website's SEO

name: Set Up Search Console Email Alerts
then_action: Then set up email alerts in Google Search Console to receive notifications of critical issues without requiring daily manual log-in
if_condition: If a website owner cannot log into Search Console daily to check for critical issues
source_title: Maintaining Your Website's SEO

name: Disavow Harmful or Illegitimate Backlinks
then_action: Then disavow those harmful links using Google's disavow tool to protect the site against negative SEO impact
if_condition: If a link profile review reveals links pointing to the site that were not legitimately acquired and appear harmful or spammy
source_title: Maintaining Your Website's SEO

name: Block Faceted Navigation URLs When Not Needed in Search
then_action: Use robots.txt to disallow crawling of those faceted navigation URLs to prevent overcrawling and preserve crawl budget for useful URLs
if_condition: If faceted navigation URLs do not need to appear in Google Search results
source_title: Managing Crawling of Faceted Navigation URLs

name: Return 404 for Empty Faceted Filter Combinations
then_action: Return an HTTP 404 status code so crawlers do not waste crawl budget indexing empty or useless pages
if_condition: If a faceted navigation filter combination returns no results on the page
source_title: Managing Crawling of Faceted Navigation URLs

name: Malware or Unwanted Software Site Review Request Process
then_action: The site owner must follow Google's malware/unwanted software guidelines to resolve the issue, then request a review through the Security Issues report in Search Console
if_condition: If a site or application is found to host malware or unwanted software as flagged in the Security Issues report
source_title: Malware and Unwanted Software – What They Are and Google's Guidelines

name: Use Ampersand as URL Parameter Separator in Faceted Navigation
then_action: Use the industry-standard ampersand (&) as the URL parameter separator; avoid characters like commas, semicolons, and brackets as they are hard for crawlers to detect as separators
if_condition: If faceted navigation URLs need to be crawled and indexed by Google
source_title: Managing Crawling of Faceted Navigation URLs

name: Maintain Consistent Filter Order in URL Paths
then_action: Ensure the logical order of filters always stays the same to avoid creating duplicate URLs with different orderings of the same filter combination
if_condition: If faceted navigation filters are encoded in the URL path rather than as query parameters
source_title: Managing Crawling of Faceted Navigation URLs

name: Validate Structured Data with Rich Results Test After Implementation
then_action: Use Google's Rich Results Test to verify that the structured data can be correctly parsed before and after deployment, then monitor Rich Result status reports in Search Console
if_condition: If structured data has been added or modified on ecommerce pages
source_title: Structured Data for Ecommerce Sites – Relevant Schema Types

name: Recognize High Purchase Intent of AI Search Visitors
then_action: Treat these visitors as high-intent prospects who are already educated about the solution and closer to a purchase decision, and optimize conversion paths accordingly
if_condition: If traffic analytics show visitors arriving from AI search citations
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 Why AI Visibility Is the Next SEO Frontier

name: Large Site Migrations Should Be Executed in Sections
then_action: Then move the site in sections, starting with sections that change less frequently, to reduce risk and manage indexing progressively
if_condition: If a large site undergoes a URL migration
source_title: Move a Site with URL Changes – Migrations and Domain Changes

name: JavaScript-Generated Product Markup Reduces Shopping Crawl Reliability
then_action: Google's Shopping crawls may become less frequent and less reliable; place Product structured data in the initial HTML to optimise for all shopping result types
if_condition: If Product structured data markup is generated dynamically via JavaScript rather than included in the initial HTML
source_title: Merchant Listing Structured Data – Products for Sale

name: MathSolver Structured Data: Homepage Placement Only
then_action: Move MathSolver structured data to the home page only; it must not be added to individual problem pages
if_condition: If MathSolver structured data is being implemented on individual problem pages instead of the site's home page
source_title: Math Solver Structured Data – MathSolver for Educational Tools

name: MathSolver Eligibility: No Full Paywall or Login Gate
then_action: The tool is not eligible for MathSolver structured data and will not appear in the Google Search math solver feature
if_condition: If the math solver tool is entirely hidden behind a login or paywall with no public access
source_title: Math Solver Structured Data – MathSolver for Educational Tools

name: Merchant Listing Eligibility: Direct Purchase Pages Only
then_action: The page is NOT eligible for merchant listing experiences in Google Search
if_condition: If a page contains links to other sites that sell the product rather than enabling direct purchase on the page itself
source_title: Merchant Listing Structured Data – Products for Sale

name: MathSolver UX: Immediate Solution Accessibility After Click
then_action: The site fails MathSolver eligibility requirements; solutions and walkthroughs must be immediately accessible upon landing from the Google Search feature
if_condition: If a user navigates from Google Search's math solver feature to the site and the solution or step-by-step walkthrough for the initial problem is not immediately accessible
source_title: Math Solver Structured Data – MathSolver for Educational Tools

name: MathSolver Quality: Inaccurate Data Triggers Feature Removal
then_action: Google may remove the solver from the Search feature until accuracy issues are resolved; site owners are responsible for the accuracy and quality of their math solver
if_condition: If Google's quality review process finds a certain amount of inaccurate math solver data on the site
source_title: Math Solver Structured Data – MathSolver for Educational Tools

name: MathSolver Canonicalization: Multiple Copies Under Different URLs
then_action: Add canonical URLs on each copy of the page to help Google efficiently crawl the site and consolidate signals
if_condition: If multiple copies of the same math solver are hosted under different URLs
source_title: Math Solver Structured Data – MathSolver for Educational Tools

name: Product Rich Results: Single Product Pages Only
then_action: The page is NOT eligible for merchant listing rich results; only pages focused on a single product or multiple variants of the same product qualify
if_condition: If a page is a category page listing many different products
source_title: Merchant Listing Structured Data – Products for Sale

name: Multi-Currency Product Pages Require Distinct URLs
then_action: A distinct URL must be used for each currency to comply with Google's merchant listing structured data requirements
if_condition: If a merchant offers products for sale in multiple currencies on the same site
source_title: Merchant Listing Structured Data – Products for Sale

name: Speakable Markup Must Use Article or WebPage Object
then_action: Use the Article or WebPage schema.org object and identify speakable sections via CSS selectors or XPath selectors; the speakable property may be repeated for multiple sections
if_condition: If implementing speakable structured data on a webpage
source_title: Speakable Structured Data – Audio Playback for News Content

name: Add VideoObject Schema for Pages with Product Videos
then_action: Implement VideoObject structured data to help Google understand and index the video content correctly
if_condition: If an ecommerce product page includes one or more product videos
source_title: Structured Data for Ecommerce Sites – Relevant Schema Types

name: Validate Structured Data Markup for AI Visibility
then_action: Verify structured data markup works properly using Google's Rich Results Test before relying on it for AI visibility improvements
if_condition: If structured data markup has been added to pages intended for AI search optimization
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 Tools That Help You Optimize for AI Search

name: Rewrite Headings as Direct Questions with Immediate Answers
then_action: Rewrite 3-5 key headings as direct questions and ensure the first paragraph under each heading directly answers that question — this aligns content structure with AI answer extraction patterns
if_condition: If an article contains declarative or descriptive headings that do not match how users query AI search platforms
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 Tools That Help You Optimize for AI Search

name: Add Statistics with Sources to Increase Content Authority
then_action: Identify 2-3 relevant statistics and add them with their original sources — sourced data signals authority and increases the likelihood of being cited in AI-generated responses
if_condition: If an article lacks data points or cited statistics and needs to build authority for AI citation eligibility
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 Tools That Help You Optimize for AI Search

name: Benchmark Competitor AI Citations to Identify Content Gaps
then_action: Search your target topics on AI platforms, identify which competitors are cited most frequently, and isolate one specific content element from those sources (e.g., format, specificity, sourcing) to adapt into your own content
if_condition: If competitors are appearing in AI platform responses for your target topics and your brand is not being cited
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 Tools That Help You Optimize for AI Search

name: Use Platform Plugin for Automatic Structured Data on CMS-Based Ecommerce Sites
then_action: Use an integrated platform extension or plugin to add structured data automatically rather than implementing it manually
if_condition: If the ecommerce site is built on a third-party platform (e.g., Shopify, WooCommerce, Magento) that offers structured data extensions or plugins
source_title: Structured Data for Ecommerce Sites – Relevant Schema Types

name: Use Merchant Center for Complex or Frequently Changing Policies
then_action: Configure return policies in Google Merchant Center or Search Console account-level settings as an alternative to structured data
if_condition: If return policy details are complicated and frequently changing, making structured data maintenance difficult
source_title: Merchant Return Policy Structured Data – MerchantReturnPolicy

name: Prohibited Goods Content Ineligible for Merchant Listing Markup
then_action: The page violates Google's content policies and the merchant listing markup will not be eligible for merchant listing experiences in search results
if_condition: If a page promotes firearms, weapons, recreational drugs, tobacco, vaping products, or gambling-related products using merchant listing structured data markup
source_title: Merchant Listing Structured Data – Products for Sale

name: Product-Specific Non-Standard Return Policy Under Offer
then_action: Specify MerchantReturnPolicy under the Offer structured data type for that individual product
if_condition: If a specific product has a non-standard return policy that differs from the site-wide policy
source_title: Merchant Return Policy Structured Data – MerchantReturnPolicy

name: Search Console Report Selection for Product Structured Data
then_action: Use the Merchant Listings report (covers merchant listing experiences with Offer data), not the Product Snippets report (which covers non-merchant listing pages only)
if_condition: If a merchant listing page (with Offer structured data) needs to be audited for product snippet eligibility in Search Console
source_title: Merchant Listing Structured Data – Products for Sale

name: ShippingService Nested Under Organisation for Standard Policy
then_action: Specify ShippingService schema.org type nested under Organisation structured data using the hasShippingService property
if_condition: If a standard shipping policy applies to the entire business/site
source_title: Merchant Shipping Policy Structured Data – ShippingService

name: MerchantReturnPolicy Structured Data Enables Search Display
then_action: Google Search can display return policy information alongside products and in knowledge panels in search results
if_condition: If MerchantReturnPolicy structured data is added to a site
source_title: Merchant Return Policy Structured Data – MerchantReturnPolicy

name: Return Information Consolidated on Single Page
then_action: Return information should be placed on a single page on the site to ensure proper implementation
if_condition: If implementing MerchantReturnPolicy structured data on a site
source_title: Merchant Return Policy Structured Data – MerchantReturnPolicy

name: Standard Return Policy Nested Under Organisation
then_action: Specify MerchantReturnPolicy schema nested under the Organisation structured data type using the hasMerchantReturnPolicy property
if_condition: If a standard return policy applies to most or all products a business sells
source_title: Merchant Return Policy Structured Data – MerchantReturnPolicy

name: ShippingService Enables Rich Search Results for Shipping Info
then_action: Google Search may display shipping information alongside products and in knowledge panels in search results
if_condition: If ShippingService structured data is added to a site with valid shipping cost and delivery time properties
source_title: Merchant Shipping Policy Structured Data – ShippingService

name: Search Console Settings Override Structured Data Markup
then_action: Google will only use the information provided in Search Console, ignoring the structured data markup
if_condition: If both MerchantReturnPolicy structured data markup and return policy settings in Search Console are provided simultaneously
source_title: Merchant Return Policy Structured Data – MerchantReturnPolicy

name: Product-Specific Shipping Uses OfferShippingDetails
then_action: Specify OfferShippingDetails under the Offer structured data type using the shippingDetails property instead of the standard ShippingService
if_condition: If a specific product requires a different shipping policy than the business standard
source_title: Merchant Shipping Policy Structured Data – ShippingService

name: Member Shipping Benefits Require Non-Member Shipping Service
then_action: At least one regular non-member shipping service must also be provided alongside the member-tier shipping service
if_condition: If validForMemberTier property is used to designate member-specific shipping benefits in ShippingService structured data
source_title: Merchant Shipping Policy Structured Data – ShippingService

name: Permanent Redirects Preserve PageRank During URL Migration
then_action: Then link credit and PageRank are preserved through the redirects, and loss in PageRank should not occur
if_condition: If a site migration involves URL changes and 301 or other permanent server-side redirects are implemented from old URLs to new URLs
source_title: Move a Site with URL Changes – Migrations and Domain Changes

name: HTTPS Property Must Be Added Separately in Search Console
then_action: Then the HTTPS property must be added and verified separately in Search Console, since Search Console treats HTTP and HTTPS as distinct properties
if_condition: If a site migrates from HTTP to HTTPS
source_title: Move a Site with URL Changes – Migrations and Domain Changes

name: Mobile Content Parity Requirement
then_action: Update the mobile site so that primary content is equivalent to the desktop site, as Google uses the mobile version for indexing and ranking
if_condition: If the mobile version of a page has less content than the desktop version
source_title: Mobile First Indexing Best Practices

name: Responsive Design Preference
then_action: Use responsive design (same HTML, same URL, adapts via screen size) as it is Google's recommended approach over dynamic serving or separate URLs
if_condition: If choosing a mobile configuration approach for a website
source_title: Mobile First Indexing Best Practices

name: Robots Meta Tag Consistency
then_action: Align robots meta tags to be identical on mobile and desktop versions, otherwise Google may fail to crawl and index the page
if_condition: If the mobile site uses different robots meta tags than the desktop site, especially noindex or nofollow
source_title: Mobile First Indexing Best Practices

name: Mobile Image Quality and Stability
then_action: Provide high-quality images with stable, consistent URLs on the mobile site to ensure proper crawling and indexing
if_condition: If images on the mobile site are small, low resolution, or have URLs that change on every page load
source_title: Mobile First Indexing Best Practices

name: Image Alt Text Consistency
then_action: Ensure the same alt text is used on both mobile and desktop versions, as Google indexes the mobile version and inconsistency can harm accessibility and ranking signals
if_condition: If the alt text for images differs between the mobile and desktop versions of a page
source_title: Mobile First Indexing Best Practices

name: Every Old URL Must Redirect to Its Corresponding New URL
then_action: Then every old URL must be mapped to and redirect via permanent redirect to its corresponding new URL; gaps in redirect mapping risk loss of indexing and link equity
if_condition: If old site URLs are being replaced by new URLs during a migration
source_title: Move a Site with URL Changes – Migrations and Domain Changes

name: Search Console Settings Must Be Reviewed and Updated for New Site Property
then_action: Then review all configured settings (sitemaps, geotargeting, crawl rate, etc.) in Search Console for the old site and replicate or update them for the new site property
if_condition: If a site migration results in a new domain, subdomain, or protocol that creates a new Search Console property
source_title: Move a Site with URL Changes – Migrations and Domain Changes

name: Avoid JavaScript Injection of Meta Tags
then_action: Avoid using JavaScript to inject or change meta tags whenever possible, as Googlebot may not reliably process them; use static HTML meta tags in the head section instead
if_condition: If meta tags are being injected or modified via JavaScript on a page
source_title: Meta Tags and HTML Attributes that Google Supports

name: HTTPS Provides a Minor Ranking Boost but Content Quality Dominates
then_action: Then expect only a small ranking signal boost from HTTPS; high-quality site content has a greater impact on rankings than the HTTPS signal alone
if_condition: If evaluating whether to migrate to HTTPS primarily for ranking benefit
source_title: Move a Site with URL Changes – Migrations and Domain Changes

name: Use data-nosnippet to Prevent Snippet Content
then_action: Apply the data-nosnippet attribute to those HTML elements to prevent Google from using their content in snippets
if_condition: If specific HTML elements on a page contain content that should not appear in Google search result snippets
source_title: Meta Tags and HTML Attributes that Google Supports

name: No Interaction-Dependent Primary Content
then_action: Remove the interaction requirement so content loads automatically, as Google will not load content gated behind user interactions
if_condition: If primary content on the mobile site is lazy-loaded and requires user interactions such as swiping, clicking, or typing to appear
source_title: Mobile First Indexing Best Practices

name: HTML Lang Attribute Not Used for Language Detection by Google
then_action: Google does not support the HTML lang attribute for language detection; use alternative methods such as hreflang tags or page content signals to indicate language
if_condition: If a page relies solely on the HTML lang attribute to signal page language to Google
source_title: Meta Tags and HTML Attributes that Google Supports

name: Head Section Must Be Valid HTML for Meta Tag Recognition
then_action: Validate that the head section contains valid HTML; invalid HTML structure can prevent machine readability and cause meta tags to be ignored
if_condition: If meta tags in the head section of a page are not being recognized or processed correctly by Google
source_title: Meta Tags and HTML Attributes that Google Supports

name: rel=next and rel=prev Pagination Attributes Not Supported
then_action: Google does not support rel=next and rel=prev for pagination; remove these attributes and use alternative pagination handling strategies
if_condition: If a paginated series of pages uses rel=next and rel=prev link attributes to signal pagination structure to Google
source_title: Meta Tags and HTML Attributes that Google Supports

name: Alt Attribute Used as Anchor Text for Image Links
then_action: Provide a descriptive alt attribute on the image, as Google uses the alt text as the anchor text for image links
if_condition: If an image serves as a hyperlink and needs to communicate link context to Google
source_title: Meta Tags and HTML Attributes that Google Supports

name: Movie Carousel Mobile-Only Availability
then_action: The movie carousel rich result is only available on mobile devices, so desktop users will not see the carousel format
if_condition: If a site owner implements movie carousel structured data expecting it to appear on desktop search results
source_title: Movie Carousel Structured Data – Movie Rich Results on Mobile

name: Movie Carousel Schema Type Requirement
then_action: Use the ItemList schema.org type combined with the Movie type — not either type alone
if_condition: If a site owner wants to implement movie carousel structured data for a list of movies
source_title: Movie Carousel Structured Data – Movie Rich Results on Mobile

name: Movie Detail Page Structured Data Requirement
then_action: Add Movie structured data on the individual detail page including at minimum: name (movie title), image (movie image), director (director's name), and aggregateRating if ratings information is available
if_condition: If a movie detail page is linked from a movie carousel summary page
source_title: Movie Carousel Structured Data – Movie Rich Results on Mobile

name: Knowledge Panel Movie Claim Process
then_action: Use the Google knowledge panel claim process to get verified on Google
if_condition: If a site owner wants to claim a specific movie in a Google knowledge panel
source_title: Movie Carousel Structured Data – Movie Rich Results on Mobile

name: Watch Button Enablement via Media Actions
then_action: Implement the Media Actions feature to allow users to directly watch or rent a movie from search results
if_condition: If a site owner wants to enable a watch or rent button for a movie directly within Google Search results
source_title: Movie Carousel Structured Data – Movie Rich Results on Mobile

name: Place Organisation Structured Data on Homepage
then_action: Place the Organisation structured data on the homepage of the website, not on internal pages
if_condition: If adding Organisation structured data to a website
source_title: Organisation Structured Data – Helping Google Understand Your Business Entity

name: Include Core Recommended Organisation Properties
then_action: Include as many relevant properties as possible, prioritising: name, url, logo, contactPoint, sameAs (social profiles and canonical pages), address, and foundingDate
if_condition: If implementing Organisation structured data on the homepage
source_title: Organisation Structured Data – Helping Google Understand Your Business Entity

name: Add Disambiguation Properties for Common Organisation Names
then_action: Include iso6523 and naics properties in Organisation structured data to help Google disambiguate the entity from others with similar names
if_condition: If the organisation has a name similar to other organisations or operates in a space with many similarly-named entities
source_title: Organisation Structured Data – Helping Google Understand Your Business Entity

name: News Sitemap: Only Include Recent Articles (Last 2 Days)
then_action: Remove those URLs from the news sitemap entirely OR remove the news:news metadata from those older URL entries to keep the sitemap compliant
if_condition: If a news sitemap contains URLs for articles older than two days
source_title: News Sitemaps – Submitting News Articles to Google

name: Content Removal as Most Secure Universal Suppression Method
then_action: Remove or update the content on the page itself — this is the only method that prevents the information from appearing in search engines that do not respect the noindex tag
if_condition: If permanent removal of content from all search engines (not just Google) is required
source_title: Page Removals – How to Remove a Page from Google Search

name: Movie Carousel Summary Page Structure
then_action: Each movie description on the summary page must point to a separate detail page focused entirely on one movie, and the itemListElement must contain an array of ListItem objects each with a 'position' (starting from 1) and a 'url' property pointing to the individual movie detail page
if_condition: If implementing a movie carousel where a summary page lists multiple movies with brief descriptions
source_title: Movie Carousel Structured Data – Movie Rich Results on Mobile

name: News Sitemap: Update Existing Sitemap Instead of Creating New One
then_action: Update the existing sitemap with the fresh articles — do NOT create a new sitemap file with each update
if_condition: If new news articles are published and the news sitemap needs to be updated
source_title: News Sitemaps – Submitting News Articles to Google

name: Use Specific Organisation Subtype When Applicable
then_action: Use the specific Organisation subtype schema rather than the generic Organisation base type
if_condition: If the organisation fits a more specific category such as Corporation, LocalBusiness, GovernmentOrganisation, or EducationalOrganisation
source_title: Organisation Structured Data – Helping Google Understand Your Business Entity

name: News Sitemap: Required Tags Must Be Present in news:news Element
then_action: Ensure the following required tags are present: news:publication (with child elements news:name and news:language using ISO 639 two-letter code), news:publication_date, and news:title
if_condition: If a news sitemap entry contains a news:news element
source_title: News Sitemaps – Submitting News Articles to Google

name: News Sitemap: Create Separate Sitemap for Better Search Console Tracking
then_action: Create a dedicated news sitemap separate from the general sitemap rather than extending the existing one
if_condition: If a news publisher wants to track news article performance separately in Google Search Console
source_title: News Sitemaps – Submitting News Articles to Google

name: Include sameAs for Knowledge Panel and Entity Consolidation
then_action: Add sameAs property referencing URLs of all social profiles and canonical pages to support knowledge panel accuracy and entity disambiguation
if_condition: If the organisation has social media profiles, Wikipedia pages, or other canonical third-party pages
source_title: Organisation Structured Data – Helping Google Understand Your Business Entity

name: Use Person Schema for Individual Entities
then_action: Use the Person schema.org type instead of Organisation structured data
if_condition: If the entity being described is a person rather than an organisation
source_title: Organisation Structured Data – Helping Google Understand Your Business Entity

name: News Sitemap: Empty Sitemap Warning Is Non-Critical
then_action: Do not treat the warning as a critical error — it will not cause problems with Google Search indexing or ranking
if_condition: If no articles have been published in the last few days and the news sitemap becomes empty, triggering an Empty Sitemap warning in Search Console
source_title: News Sitemaps – Submitting News Articles to Google

name: Package Tracking Eligibility - Geographic Requirement
then_action: The company must either be based in India, Japan, or Brazil, OR be the sole authorised provider of package tracking information for a company servicing those areas
if_condition: If a delivery company wants to join Google's Package Tracking Early Adopters Programme
source_title: Package Tracking – Early Adopters Program for Delivery Companies

name: Package Tracking API - Personal Data Prohibition
then_action: The API is non-compliant with Google's Package Tracking requirements; such data must be excluded from all API responses
if_condition: If the delivery company's API response includes personal data about the recipient or sender, or geographical information about the recipient or sender
source_title: Package Tracking – Early Adopters Program for Delivery Companies

name: Package Tracking Programme - Application Requirement
then_action: The company must fill out and submit an interest form to be considered for participation
if_condition: If a delivery company meets eligibility requirements and wants to participate in the Package Tracking Early Adopters Programme
source_title: Package Tracking – Early Adopters Program for Delivery Companies

name: Package Tracking API Response Time - Average Threshold
then_action: The API does not meet Google's Package Tracking integration requirements and will fail compliance; average response time must be at or below 700ms
if_condition: If a delivery company's API average response time exceeds 700 milliseconds
source_title: Package Tracking – Early Adopters Program for Delivery Companies

name: Package Tracking API Response Time - 95th Percentile Threshold
then_action: The API does not meet Google's Package Tracking integration requirements; the 95th percentile response time must not exceed 1000ms
if_condition: If a delivery company's API 95th percentile response time exceeds 1000 milliseconds
source_title: Package Tracking – Early Adopters Program for Delivery Companies

name: Package Tracking API - Protocol Requirement
then_action: The company must expose a RESTful JSON API that accepts POST requests from Google to retrieve tracking information
if_condition: If a delivery company is integrating with Google's Package Tracking feature
source_title: Package Tracking – Early Adopters Program for Delivery Companies

name: Infinite Scroll Requires Paginated Fallback URLs
then_action: Then a paginated fallback with distinct URLs must be provided so Google crawlers can access all content without simulating scroll user actions
if_condition: If a site implements infinite scroll that automatically loads more content as the user scrolls
source_title: Pagination and Incremental Page Loading for Ecommerce

name: Removals Tool Duration Limit
then_action: The removal lasts approximately 6 months and then expires, so permanent removal requires additional lasting actions
if_condition: If a URL removal request is submitted via the Search Console Removals tool
source_title: Page Removals – How to Remove a Page from Google Search

name: Accelerate Noindex Processing via URL Inspection
then_action: Use the URL Inspection tool in Search Console to request a recrawl, which speeds up the removal process
if_condition: If a noindex tag has been added but the page continues appearing in Google search results
source_title: Page Removals – How to Remove a Page from Google Search

name: Protect All URL Variations Before Removal Request
then_action: All URL variations pointing to the same content must be protected or removed first, since different URLs can resolve to the same page
if_condition: If submitting a removal request via the Search Console Removals tool
source_title: Page Removals – How to Remove a Page from Google Search

name: Update URL via History API During Infinite Scroll
then_action: Then the displayed URL should be updated using the History API to reflect the current position in the paginated set
if_condition: If a new page chunk loads during infinite scroll and becomes the primary visible element in the viewport
source_title: Pagination and Incremental Page Loading for Ecommerce

name: Robots.txt Insufficient for Search Result Blocking
then_action: The URL can still be indexed and appear in search results even if crawling is blocked — do not use robots.txt as a page removal method
if_condition: If robots.txt is used to block crawling of a URL intended to be removed from search results
source_title: Page Removals – How to Remove a Page from Google Search

name: Noindex Tag Limited to Supporting Search Engines
then_action: The noindex tag only affects Google and search engines that support it; users and other search engines can still access the page, so this method does not universally suppress the content
if_condition: If a noindex tag is added to a page to prevent it from appearing in search results
source_title: Page Removals – How to Remove a Page from Google Search

name: Paginated URLs Must Return Consistent Content
then_action: Then it must display the same content every time it is loaded, and absolute page numbers must be used rather than relative parameters to ensure consistent and stable URLs
if_condition: If each URL in a paginated set is accessed by a browser or crawler at any point in time
source_title: Pagination and Incremental Page Loading for Ecommerce

name: Pagination Must Use Crawlable Links
then_action: Then pages must be linked sequentially using anchor elements with href attributes, and each paginated page must have a unique URL, so Google crawlers can discover all content without triggering JavaScript or clicking buttons
if_condition: If a paginated ecommerce site uses a Load More button or traditional numbered pages to display content across multiple pages
source_title: Pagination and Incremental Page Loading for Ecommerce

name: Control Indexing of Sorting and Filtering Variations
then_action: Then those variations should either be blocked with a noindex tag or discouraged via robots.txt to prevent indexing of duplicate content
if_condition: If a product listing page generates multiple URL variations through sorting or filtering parameters that produce near-duplicate versions of the same content list
source_title: Pagination and Incremental Page Loading for Ecommerce

name: rel=next and rel=prev Have No Effect on Google
then_action: Then these attributes will have no effect on Google indexing or ranking, though they may still be recognized by other search engines
if_condition: If a site uses rel=next and rel=prev link attributes to signal pagination relationships to search engines
source_title: Pagination and Incremental Page Loading for Ecommerce

name: Preferred Sources Domain Eligibility Requirement
then_action: Then the site is NOT eligible for Preferred Sources; only domain-level (https://www.example.com/) and subdomain-level (https://code.example.com/) sites qualify
if_condition: If a site uses a subdirectory structure (e.g., https://www.example.com/blog) and wants to appear in Google's Preferred Sources tool
source_title: Preferred Sources – Helping Your Audience Follow Your Publication

name: Verify Site Eligibility Before Promoting Preferred Sources
then_action: Then first verify the site appears in the source preferences tool by entering the site URL in the tool's search box before launching any promotion
if_condition: If a site owner plans to promote their publication using Preferred Sources buttons or deep links
source_title: Preferred Sources – Helping Your Audience Follow Your Publication

name: Preferred Sources Eligibility Is Determined by Google
then_action: Then note that eligibility and appearance in the source preferences tool are determined solely by Google's systems; these promotion methods do not guarantee inclusion
if_condition: If a site owner implements Preferred Sources deep links or buttons expecting guaranteed inclusion in the source preferences tool
source_title: Preferred Sources – Helping Your Audience Follow Your Publication

name: Monitor site: operator for unknown pages indicating hack
then_action: The site may have been hacked; investigate immediately and review Security Issues report in Search Console
if_condition: If unknown pages or off-topic content appear when using the site: search operator in Google Search that were not published by the site owner
source_title: Prevent a Malware Infection – Security Best Practices for Site Owners

name: Security Issues report shows malware or phishing detection
then_action: Act immediately to remediate the security issue and consult Google's Help for Hacked Sites documentation for identifying hack type and recovery steps
if_condition: If Google Search Console Security Issues report detects malware, phishing, or other security issues on the site
source_title: Prevent a Malware Infection – Security Best Practices for Site Owners

name: Third-party apps or ads sourced from unvetted providers
then_action: Replace or remove them; only use third-party content providers from trusted and legitimate sources to prevent malware injection
if_condition: If third-party apps or advertisements on the site come from unverified or unvetted sources
source_title: Prevent a Malware Infection – Security Best Practices for Site Owners

name: Preferred Sources Deep Link Promotion Format
then_action: Then use the deep link URL format https://google.com/preferences/source?q=Your_Website_URL to direct users to the source preferences tool
if_condition: If a site owner wants to help their audience add their publication as a preferred source via social posts or promotions
source_title: Preferred Sources – Helping Your Audience Follow Your Publication

name: Use reCAPTCHA or equivalent on sign-up forms
then_action: Implement reCAPTCHA or a similar human-verification tool on those forms to block automated bot submissions
if_condition: If a site has publicly accessible sign-up or comment submission forms
source_title: Prevent User Generated Spam on Your Site

name: Server software outdated or unpatched on sites with server access
then_action: Update server software with security patches, review server configuration for common weaknesses, and monitor server log files for unusual activity
if_condition: If the site owner has direct server access and server software has not been updated with the latest security patches
source_title: Prevent a Malware Infection – Security Best Practices for Site Owners

name: Workstation computers used for site management lack updated security software
then_action: Update all software, install and update anti-virus software, and remove any detected malware before continuing site management activities
if_condition: If computers used to manage or edit the website have outdated software, lack updated anti-virus tools, or are potentially infected with viruses or trojans
source_title: Prevent a Malware Infection – Security Best Practices for Site Owners

name: Apply noindex to new user profile pages by default
then_action: Add a noindex tag to new user profile pages by default to prevent unverified or spammy content from being indexed by Google
if_condition: If a website allows users to create profile pages or submit user-generated content
source_title: Prevent User Generated Spam on Your Site

name: Apply ugc or nofollow attribute to untrusted user-posted links
then_action: Automatically apply the ugc or nofollow rel attribute to those links to prevent passing PageRank to potentially spammy destinations
if_condition: If a user submits or posts a link on the site and the user is not verified as trusted
source_title: Prevent User Generated Spam on Your Site

name: Identify spam patterns in user submissions
then_action: Flag those submissions as likely spam and apply additional scrutiny or automatic rejection
if_condition: If user submissions show signals such as form completion times too fast for a human, suspicious IP addresses, similar content patterns across multiple submissions, or use of temporary/disposable email addresses
source_title: Prevent User Generated Spam on Your Site

name: Third-Party Resources Can Introduce Social Engineering Attacks
then_action: Then the site may inadvertently host social engineering attacks that trick visitors into revealing confidential information or downloading malware, triggering Google security warnings
if_condition: If a site uses third-party resources such as external ads or images that become compromised
source_title: Preventing and Monitoring Abuse on Your Site – Overview

name: Ban IP addresses associated with multiple spammy profiles
then_action: Remove all content and profiles from that source and add the IP address to a permanent ban list
if_condition: If multiple spammy user profiles or submissions are traced back to the same IP address
source_title: Prevent User Generated Spam on Your Site

name: Use Security Issues Report to Detect Threats
then_action: Then use the Security Issues report in Google Search Console to identify detected security threats and investigate them
if_condition: If a site owner suspects or wants to monitor for security threats including spam, malware, or social engineering attacks
source_title: Preventing and Monitoring Abuse on Your Site – Overview

name: Enable manual moderation for user-generated content
then_action: Implement manual approval or moderation workflows to prevent spammy content from being instantly published
if_condition: If a site allows users to submit comments, profiles, or other public-facing content without prior review
source_title: Prevent User Generated Spam on Your Site

name: Security Issues Trigger Search Warnings
then_action: Then Google may display warnings to users before they reach the site (e.g., Chrome's 'Deceptive site ahead' warning), which can significantly decrease search traffic
if_condition: If Google detects security threats on a site (user-generated spam, malware, unwanted software, or social engineering attacks)
source_title: Preventing and Monitoring Abuse on Your Site – Overview

name: Request Review After Fixing Security Issues
then_action: Then request a review through the Security Issues report so Google can reassess the site and potentially remove warnings
if_condition: If a security issue identified in the Search Console Security Issues report has been investigated and resolved
source_title: Preventing and Monitoring Abuse on Your Site – Overview

name: Use Product Snippet Markup for Non-Purchasable Product Pages
then_action: Use product snippet structured data (schema.org Product type) rather than merchant listing markup
if_condition: If a page features product information (reviews, comparisons, aggregations) but users cannot directly purchase the product from that page
source_title: Product Snippet Structured Data – Non Merchant Product Pages

name: Open User Input Forms Risk Spam Abuse
then_action: Then spammers may exploit these to add spammy content that violates Google's spam policies, risking manual actions or security warnings
if_condition: If a site has open comment forms or other unmoderated user-generated content inputs
source_title: Preventing and Monitoring Abuse on Your Site – Overview

name: Do Not Use AggregateOffer for Product Variants
then_action: Do not use AggregateOffer type; AggregateOffer is only for representing aggregations of offers from multiple merchants
if_condition: If marking up a set of product variants (e.g. different sizes, colors) using structured data
source_title: Product Snippet Structured Data – Non Merchant Product Pages

name: Restrict Pros and Cons Markup to Editorial Review Pages
then_action: Do not use pros/cons structured data (positiveNotes/negativeNotes properties); this markup is only eligible for editorial product review pages
if_condition: If the page is a merchant product page or contains customer product reviews (not an editorial review)
source_title: Product Snippet Structured Data – Non Merchant Product Pages

name: Ensure Each Product Variant Has a Unique Identifier and URL
then_action: Each variant must have a unique identifier and a unique URL; failure to follow unique identification guidelines will prevent Google from processing the structured data correctly
if_condition: If marking up individual product variants within a ProductGroup
source_title: Product Variants Structured Data – ProductGroup for Size and Color Variants

name: Include Both Car and Product Types When Attaching Ratings to Cars
then_action: Include both the Car type and the Product type together in the markup, since Car is not automatically supported as a subtype of Product for ratings purposes
if_condition: If marking up cars using Product structured data and needing to attach ratings
source_title: Product Snippet Structured Data – Non Merchant Product Pages

name: Use FAQPage Instead of QAPage for Single Definitive Answers
then_action: Use FAQPage markup instead of QAPage markup — using QAPage in this context is incorrect per Google guidelines
if_condition: If a question on the page has a single definitive answer provided by the site without user-submitted alternatives
source_title: Q&A Page Structured Data – QAPage for Questions and Answers

name: Monitor Non-Merchant Product Pages via Product Snippets Report
then_action: Monitor structured data performance using the Product Snippets report in Search Console, not the Merchant Listings report
if_condition: If a page uses product snippet structured data and is not a merchant listing page where shoppers can buy products
source_title: Product Snippet Structured Data – Non Merchant Product Pages

name: Support Either Single-Page or Multi-Page Variant Design
then_action: Use either a single-page design (all variants selectable on one page via query parameters) or a multi-page design (each variant on its own distinct URL); both approaches are valid for ProductGroup structured data
if_condition: If deciding how to structure variant URLs for a product with multiple options
source_title: Product Variants Structured Data – ProductGroup for Size and Color Variants

name: Use ProductGroup Schema for Product Variants
then_action: Use the ProductGroup schema.org type together with the Product type to help Google understand which products are variations of the same parent product, making them eligible for variant information in merchant listing experiences
if_condition: If a product is sold in multiple variations (e.g., different sizes, colors, materials, or patterns)
source_title: Product Variants Structured Data – ProductGroup for Size and Color Variants

name: Specify variesBy, hasVariant, and productGroupID on ProductGroup
then_action: Include the 'variesBy' property to specify the differentiating attribute (e.g., colour, size), 'hasVariant' to link to each individual Product variant, and 'productGroupID' to provide a unique identifier for the product group
if_condition: If implementing ProductGroup structured data for product variants
source_title: Product Variants Structured Data – ProductGroup for Size and Color Variants

name: Place Shared Product Properties at the Group Level
then_action: Specify those common properties once at the ProductGroup level rather than repeating them on each individual Product variant
if_condition: If product properties such as brand and review information apply to all variants of a product group
source_title: Product Variants Structured Data – ProductGroup for Size and Color Variants

name: Required Core Properties for ProfilePage Markup
then_action: Include key properties: name, image, description, and url for the creator's profile page as minimum required fields
if_condition: If implementing ProfilePage structured data for a Person or Organisation type
source_title: Profile Page Structured Data – ProfilePage for Creator Profiles

name: Linking Author Properties to ProfilePage
then_action: Ensure the linked author page uses ProfilePage markup so that cross-structured-data relationships are properly established for Google
if_condition: If Article, Recipe, or discussion/Q&A structured data includes author properties pointing to a creator's page
source_title: Profile Page Structured Data – ProfilePage for Creator Profiles

name: ProfilePage Markup for Creator Profile Pages
then_action: Apply ProfilePage structured data markup to help Google understand the creator and improve appearance in search results including Discussions and Forums feature
if_condition: If a page's primary focus is a single person or organisation affiliated with the website (e.g., author bio page, user profile page, creator page on a platform)
source_title: Profile Page Structured Data – ProfilePage for Creator Profiles

name: sameAs Property for Cross-Platform Profile Linking
then_action: Use the sameAs property within ProfilePage markup to link to those official external profiles
if_condition: If the creator has official profiles on other platforms (e.g., social media, other websites)
source_title: Profile Page Structured Data – ProfilePage for Creator Profiles

name: Use QAPage Markup Only When Users Can Submit Answers
then_action: Use QAPage structured data markup (schema.org QAPage, Question, and Answer types) to mark up the content and become eligible for rich results in Google Search
if_condition: If a page contains a single question with answers AND users are able to submit their own answers to that question
source_title: Q&A Page Structured Data – QAPage for Questions and Answers

name: Recent Activity Markup on ProfilePage
then_action: Include markup using URLs on those activity objects to reference the pages with full content and structured data markup
if_condition: If the profile page also displays the creator's recent activity such as latest posts or articles
source_title: Profile Page Structured Data – ProfilePage for Creator Profiles

name: Single Subject Requirement for ProfilePage
then_action: Do not apply ProfilePage markup — the primary focus must be a single person or organisation; use individual profile pages per subject instead
if_condition: If a page attempts to apply ProfilePage markup but features multiple people or organisations as co-equal subjects
source_title: Profile Page Structured Data – ProfilePage for Creator Profiles

name: Include Required QAPage Properties for Rich Result Eligibility
then_action: Include the required 'mainEntity' property containing a Question object with: (1) 'name' property for the question text, and (2) 'acceptedAnswer' property containing an Answer object with a 'text' property; optionally add 'suggestedAnswer' for non-accepted answers
if_condition: If implementing QAPage structured data markup on a Q&A page
source_title: Q&A Page Structured Data – QAPage for Questions and Answers

name: Recipe Structured Data: Valid Use Case Restriction
then_action: The content violates recipe structured data guidelines and may be prevented from appearing as a rich result, though it can still appear in regular search results
if_condition: If Recipe schema markup is applied to content that is NOT about preparing a specific dish (e.g., facial scrub instructions, party planning ideas)
source_title: Recipe Structured Data – Rich Results for Recipes

name: Structured Data Enables Rich Results in Google Search
then_action: Then the page may be eligible to appear in rich results and enhanced search features beyond standard blue links
if_condition: If a page includes structured data markup recognized by Google (e.g., articles, recipes, events, FAQs, job postings, local businesses, videos, etc.)
source_title: Ranking and Search Appearance – Overview

name: Recipe Instructions: Structured Step Formatting
then_action: Use HowToStep or HowToSection types within recipeInstructions to provide more structured step information, improving eligibility for enhanced rich result displays
if_condition: If the recipeInstructions property is being implemented in Recipe structured data
source_title: Recipe Structured Data – Rich Results for Recipes

name: Recipe Structured Data: Required Name Property
then_action: The structured data is invalid and the recipe will not be eligible for rich results; the 'name' property is a required field and must contain a valid dish name
if_condition: If Recipe structured data is implemented but the 'name' property is missing or does not contain a valid dish name
source_title: Recipe Structured Data – Rich Results for Recipes

name: QAPage Markup Improves Snippets Even Without Rich Results
then_action: The answer content from the markup may still appear in the basic search result snippet, providing SEO benefit regardless of rich result eligibility
if_condition: If a Q&A page is marked up with QAPage structured data but does not qualify for or receive a rich result display
source_title: Q&A Page Structured Data – QAPage for Questions and Answers

name: Recipe Carousel Eligibility: ItemList + Summary Page Required
then_action: The site must provide ItemList structured data summarising the recipes alongside individual recipe structured data, AND must have a summary page that lists all recipes in the collection
if_condition: If a site wants recipes to appear in a carousel or grid format in search results
source_title: Recipe Structured Data – Rich Results for Recipes

name: Recipe Rich Results: Recommended Properties for Maximum Eligibility
then_action: Include recommended properties: image, author, datePublished, description, prepTime, cookTime, totalTime, keywords, recipeYield, recipeCategory, recipeCuisine, nutrition, recipeIngredient, recipeInstructions, aggregateRating, and video
if_condition: If Recipe structured data is implemented and the goal is to maximise rich result eligibility and detail shown in search
source_title: Recipe Structured Data – Rich Results for Recipes

name: Paywalled Content Requires Flexible Sampling Markup
then_action: Then the site should implement flexible sampling and subscription/paywalled content structured data so Google can understand and correctly display the content
if_condition: If a site restricts content behind a paywall or subscription and wants Google to index and surface that content appropriately
source_title: Ranking and Search Appearance – Overview

name: Page Experience Signals Affect Search Ranking
then_action: Then these signals are factored into Google's ranking systems and can influence how the page appears in search results
if_condition: If a page is being evaluated for search ranking and has measurable page experience signals including Core Web Vitals, HTTPS status, mobile-friendliness, and interstitial usage
source_title: Ranking and Search Appearance – Overview

name: Images in Structured Data Must Be Crawlable and Relevant
then_action: The image must be relevant to the page it is on, and the image URL must be crawlable and indexable by Google
if_condition: If an image URL is specified as a structured data property
source_title: Structured Data General Guidelines

name: Missing or Incorrect Structured Data Limits Rich Result Eligibility
then_action: Then the page will not be eligible for that rich result feature in Google Search, losing potential enhanced visibility
if_condition: If a page targets a rich result type (e.g., recipes, FAQs, events, review snippets) but does not implement the corresponding structured data markup
source_title: Ranking and Search Appearance – Overview

name: Use rel=sponsored for Paid Links
then_action: Then apply rel=sponsored to that anchor element to signal to Google the commercial nature of the link
if_condition: If a link was created as part of a paid arrangement including advertising, sponsorships, or compensation in any form (money, goods, services)
source_title: rel Attributes – Qualifying Outbound Links for Google

name: Use rel=ugc for User-Generated Content Links
then_action: Then apply rel=ugc to that anchor element to designate it as user-generated content
if_condition: If a link appears in user-generated content such as comments or forum posts (inserted by users, not the site owner)
source_title: rel Attributes – Qualifying Outbound Links for Google

name: Completeness of Structured Data Improves Rich Result Quality
then_action: Provide as many recommended properties as possible; more complete structured data is considered higher quality and improves eligibility for enriched search results
if_condition: If structured data is implemented and additional recommended properties are available beyond required ones
source_title: Structured Data General Guidelines

name: Do Not Use rel Attributes to Prevent Indexing
then_action: Then do not rely on rel=nofollow or similar attributes; instead allow crawling of the page and apply a noindex robots rule on that page
if_condition: If the goal is to prevent Google from indexing a specific linked page
source_title: rel Attributes – Qualifying Outbound Links for Google

name: Temporary Redirect for Service Unavailability
then_action: Use a temporary redirect so Google preserves the original URL as canonical and does not replace it with the temporary destination in search results
if_condition: If a page or service is temporarily unavailable and needs to redirect users to an alternate URL
source_title: Redirects and Google Search – Types, Implementation, and Canonicalization

name: Permanent Redirect Signals Canonical to Google
then_action: Google will treat the redirect target as canonical and show the new redirect target URL in search results
if_condition: If a URL uses an HTTP 301 or 308 server-side redirect to point to a new URL
source_title: Redirects and Google Search – Types, Implementation, and Canonicalization

name: Employer Reviews Must Use EmployerAggregateRating
then_action: Then use EmployerAggregateRating structured data instead of the standard Review or AggregateRating types
if_condition: If a site provides reviews specifically about employers
source_title: Review Snippet Structured Data – Stars and Ratings in Search Results

name: Combine Multiple rel Values When Applicable
then_action: Then apply multiple rel values together on the same anchor element (e.g., rel='ugc nofollow')
if_condition: If a link qualifies for more than one rel attribute category simultaneously (e.g., user-generated content that the site owner also does not endorse)
source_title: rel Attributes – Qualifying Outbound Links for Google

name: Use rel=nofollow for Non-Endorsed Links
then_action: Then apply rel=nofollow to signal to Google to not associate the site with the linked page and generally not follow that link
if_condition: If a site owner does not endorse or trust the linked content and does not want Google to associate the site with the linked page
source_title: rel Attributes – Qualifying Outbound Links for Google

name: Temporary Redirect Preserves Source URL in Search
then_action: Google will treat the source (original) page as canonical and continue showing the source URL in search results
if_condition: If a URL uses a temporary redirect to point to another URL
source_title: Redirects and Google Search – Types, Implementation, and Canonicalization

name: Server-Side Redirects Are Most Reliable for Google Interpretation
then_action: Google has the highest chance of correctly interpreting the redirect signal, making server-side redirects the preferred implementation method
if_condition: If a redirect is implemented as a server-side HTTP response (301 or 308) rather than via JavaScript or meta refresh
source_title: Redirects and Google Search – Types, Implementation, and Canonicalization

name: Self-Serving Review Restriction for LocalBusiness/Organisation
then_action: Then the page is NOT eligible for the star review rich result feature in Google Search
if_condition: If a LocalBusiness or Organisation entity controls the reviews about itself (whether placed directly in structured data or via embedded third-party widget)
source_title: Review Snippet Structured Data – Stars and Ratings in Search Results

name: JavaScript and Meta Refresh Redirects Are Less Reliable
then_action: Google is less likely to correctly interpret the redirect, increasing the risk that canonicalization signals are misread or ignored
if_condition: If a redirect is implemented using JavaScript or a meta refresh tag instead of a server-side HTTP status code
source_title: Redirects and Google Search – Types, Implementation, and Canonicalization

name: No rel Attribute Needed for Trusted Links
then_action: Then apply no rel attribute to the anchor element
if_condition: If a link should be followed by Google unconditionally without any qualification
source_title: rel Attributes – Qualifying Outbound Links for Google

name: Pros and Cons Appearance for Editorial Product Reviews
then_action: Then use positiveNotes and negativeNotes properties within the Review type to enable the pros and cons rich result appearance
if_condition: If implementing an editorial product review page using Review type nested within a Product type structured data
source_title: Review Snippet Structured Data – Stars and Ratings in Search Results

name: Required Properties for Review Structured Data
then_action: Then the 'author' property (with reviewer's name) and 'reviewRating' (with ratingValue) are required; optionally include bestRating and worstRating to define the scale
if_condition: If implementing Review type structured data on a page
source_title: Review Snippet Structured Data – Stars and Ratings in Search Results

name: Review Snippet Eligibility Requires Valid Markup
then_action: Then Google may display a rich snippet with star ratings in search results or Knowledge Panels
if_condition: If a page contains valid Review or AggregateRating structured data for eligible content types (books, courses, events, how-to, local businesses, movies, products, recipes, software applications)
source_title: Review Snippet Structured Data – Stars and Ratings in Search Results

name: Conflicting Robots Rules: Apply Most Restrictive
then_action: Apply the more restrictive rule among the conflicting directives
if_condition: If multiple robots meta tags or X-Robots-Tag directives conflict with each other on the same page or resource
source_title: Robots Meta Tag, data nosnippet, and X Robots Tag Specifications

name: Spam Policy Violation Triggers Removal
then_action: The page may be removed from Google Search results entirely, not merely demoted
if_condition: If a page employs practices that manipulate ranking signals or deceive users (as defined by Google's Spam Policies)
source_title: Search Essentials – Overview

name: X-Robots-Tag for Non-HTML Resources
then_action: Use the X-Robots-Tag HTTP response header instead of robots meta tags, as meta tags cannot be placed in non-HTML files
if_condition: If crawl/index directives need to be applied to non-HTML resources such as PDFs, video files, or image files
source_title: Robots Meta Tag, data nosnippet, and X Robots Tag Specifications

name: Search Essentials Apply Universally
then_action: The page must comply with Google Search Essentials as the foundational minimum — no exemptions exist based on geography, language, or content category
if_condition: If a site owner wants any page to appear in Google Search, regardless of language, hosting country, or content type
source_title: Search Essentials – Overview

name: Key Best Practices Are Recommendations Not Requirements
then_action: The page improves its potential to perform well in Google Search, but non-compliance does not disqualify the page from indexing — these are advisory, not mandatory
if_condition: If a page follows Google's Key Best Practices (the third part of Search Essentials)
source_title: Search Essentials – Overview

name: Meeting Search Essentials Does Not Guarantee Indexing
then_action: The page is only eligible for consideration — indexing and ranking are not guaranteed, as many additional factors influence appearance in results
if_condition: If a page meets all three parts of Google Search Essentials (Technical Requirements, Spam Policies, Key Best Practices)
source_title: Search Essentials – Overview

name: data-nosnippet Must Not Be Dynamically Modified via JavaScript
then_action: Do not add or remove data-nosnippet via JavaScript on existing nodes, as this is explicitly unsupported and may not be honored by Google
if_condition: If the data-nosnippet attribute is being added to or removed from existing HTML nodes through JavaScript
source_title: Robots Meta Tag, data nosnippet, and X Robots Tag Specifications

name: Cloaking Detected as Spam Policy Violation
then_action: The page is classified as cloaking, treated as a spam policy violation, and subject to removal or demotion regardless of intent
if_condition: If the content served to Googlebot differs from the content served to regular users
source_title: Search Essentials – Technical Requirements

name: Googlebot Access Blocked by Robots.txt
then_action: The page cannot be crawled or indexed by Google; failing this baseline requirement guarantees exclusion from the index
if_condition: If a page is blocked by robots.txt, requires login, returns an HTTP error code, or is not accessible via HTTP/HTTPS
source_title: Search Essentials – Technical Requirements

name: Use unavailable_after to Expire Time-Sensitive Pages
then_action: Use the unavailable_after robots directive with the target date and time to instruct Google to stop showing the page after that point
if_condition: If a page contains time-sensitive content that should no longer appear in search results after a specific date and time
source_title: Robots Meta Tag, data nosnippet, and X Robots Tag Specifications

name: Blocked Resources Cause Incomplete Rendering
then_action: Googlebot may render the page incorrectly and fail to extract content fully, risking poor or incomplete indexing
if_condition: If a page blocks CSS, JavaScript, or image resources from Googlebot via robots.txt
source_title: Search Essentials – Technical Requirements

name: Noindex Directive Excludes Page from Google Index
then_action: Google will exclude the page from its index regardless of other signals or content quality
if_condition: If a page contains a noindex directive in a robots meta tag or HTTP response header
source_title: Search Essentials – Technical Requirements

name: Robots.txt Block Prevents Noindex Tag from Being Read
then_action: Google cannot read the noindex tag (because the page is blocked), but the page URL may still appear in search results due to external link signals
if_condition: If a page is blocked by robots.txt but has a noindex tag and is discoverable via links from other pages
source_title: Search Essentials – Technical Requirements

name: Unsupported Browser Features Prevent Content Extraction
then_action: Googlebot may not correctly extract the page's content, risking incomplete or failed indexing
if_condition: If a page requires specific browser features not supported by Googlebot's rendering engine
source_title: Search Essentials – Technical Requirements

name: Prohibited Content Triggers Removal Regardless of Other Signals
then_action: The page will be removed from Google's index regardless of any other positive SEO signals
if_condition: If a page contains content that violates Google's content policies (e.g., child sexual abuse material or other explicitly prohibited categories)
source_title: Search Essentials – Technical Requirements

name: Control Snippet Length with max-snippet
then_action: Use the max-snippet robots directive with a numeric character limit to cap the snippet length shown by Google
if_condition: If a page owner wants to limit the character length of the text snippet Google displays in search results
source_title: Robots Meta Tag, data nosnippet, and X Robots Tag Specifications

name: Technical Requirements Are Binary Eligibility Gate
then_action: The page is ineligible for Google Search results — no ranking or indexing will occur regardless of content quality or other factors
if_condition: If a page fails to meet Google's Technical Requirements (e.g., cannot be crawled or indexed by Google)
source_title: Search Essentials – Overview

name: Unique Descriptive Title Per Page
then_action: Add a unique, descriptive title to each page's HTML title element to help Google correctly identify and rank the page's content
if_condition: If a page is missing a unique, descriptive title in the HTML title element or shares a title with other pages on the site
source_title: SEO Starter Guide

name: Use Both Structured Data and Merchant Center for Maximum Product Visibility
then_action: Implement both structured data on product pages AND upload product feeds to Google Merchant Center, as some surfaces like the Shopping tab require Merchant Center participation and some experiences combine data from both sources
if_condition: If an ecommerce site aims to maximize product visibility across Google surfaces including Shopping tab
source_title: Share Product Data with Google – Structured Data and Merchant Center

name: Internal Linking and Sitemap for Crawlability
then_action: Add internal links and/or a sitemap to ensure Google can discover all important pages through crawling
if_condition: If important pages on the site are not reachable by following internal links from the homepage or other key pages
source_title: SEO Starter Guide

name: Merchant Center Feed Supplements Missing Structured Data Pricing
then_action: Then Google may pull pricing data from the Merchant Center feed to populate product snippets, so maintaining an accurate Merchant Center feed is critical even when structured data is present
if_condition: If a product page has structured data but does not include pricing data in that structured data
source_title: Share Product Data with Google – Structured Data and Merchant Center

name: Avoid robots.txt Blocking of Indexable Pages
then_action: Remove the robots.txt disallow rules for those pages, as blocking crawling prevents indexing and reduces search visibility
