if_condition: If robots.txt is used to block pages that are intended to appear in Google search results
source_title: SEO Starter Guide

name: Use data-nosnippet to Prevent Page Content from Forming Snippets
then_action: Then add the data-nosnippet attribute to those HTML elements to explicitly instruct Google not to use that content in snippets
if_condition: If specific HTML elements on a product page should not be used by Google to form search snippets
source_title: Share Product Data with Google – Structured Data and Merchant Center

name: Merchant Center is Mandatory for Google Shopping Tab
then_action: Then participation in Google Merchant Center is mandatory — structured data alone is insufficient for this surface
if_condition: If a retailer wants their products listed in the Google Shopping tab
source_title: Share Product Data with Google – Structured Data and Merchant Center

name: Site Name Scope Limited to Domain or Subdomain Level
then_action: Google does not support site names at the subdirectory level; site names are only supported at the domain or subdomain level, so structured data must be placed on the root home page
if_condition: If attempting to define a site name at a subdirectory level (e.g., example.com/section/)
source_title: Site Names in Google Search

name: Avoid Manipulative Link-Building Tactics
then_action: Stop those link-building practices immediately and focus on earning links through genuinely useful content, as policy-violating tactics risk Google penalties
if_condition: If a site is acquiring links through tactics that violate Google's spam policies (e.g., paid links, link schemes, or artificial link networks)
source_title: SEO Starter Guide

name: Googlebot Fallback When SXG Cannot Be Parsed
then_action: Googlebot will recrawl the URL without the 'application/signed-exchange;v=b3' Accept header value to retrieve the standard text/html variant instead
if_condition: If Googlebot cannot parse an SXG response (e.g., malformed or invalid signed exchange)
source_title: Signed Exchanges – Prefetching for Better Page Experience

name: Add WebSite Structured Data for Site Name Preference
then_action: Add WebSite structured data to the home page with a 'name' property for the preferred site name and an 'alternateName' property for an alternative name
if_condition: If a website owner wants to indicate a preferred site name to Google for display in search results
source_title: Site Names in Google Search

name: Heading Hierarchy for Content Structure
then_action: Implement a proper heading hierarchy to help both Google and users understand the structure and key topics of the page
if_condition: If a page lacks a clear heading hierarchy (e.g., missing H1, or headings used inconsistently without logical structure)
source_title: SEO Starter Guide

name: SXG Indexing Error Causes Fallback to Original URL
then_action: Google Search will link to the original URL without SXG, bypassing the prefetch performance benefit entirely
if_condition: If any SXG indexing error occurs during Google's processing of a signed exchange
source_title: Signed Exchanges – Prefetching for Better Page Experience

name: SXG Prefetch Enables LCP Improvement
then_action: Google may prefetch key resources (HTML, JS, CSS, images, fonts) before the user clicks, causing the page to render sooner and lowering Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) score
if_condition: If a website supports Signed Exchanges (SXG) and its pages appear in Google Search results
source_title: Signed Exchanges – Prefetching for Better Page Experience

name: SXG LCP Improvement Contributes to Page Experience Ranking Signal
then_action: The improved Core Web Vitals scores are used by Google's ranking systems as a page experience signal, indirectly benefiting rankings — SXG itself is not a direct ranking signal
if_condition: If SXG implementation reduces LCP and improves Core Web Vitals scores
source_title: Signed Exchanges – Prefetching for Better Page Experience

name: Use SXG Validator to Check Cache Compatibility
then_action: Use the SXG Validator Chrome extension to determine whether the signed exchange meets the necessary cache requirements
if_condition: If you have implemented SXG and need to verify whether it meets Google's cache requirements
source_title: Signed Exchanges – Prefetching for Better Page Experience

name: Site Name Must Be Unique, Accurate, and Concise
then_action: The name must be unique, accurately reflect the site's identity without being misleading, and be concise and commonly recognised; generic names are unlikely to be selected unless they represent an extremely well-recognised brand
if_condition: If specifying a site name via WebSite structured data
source_title: Site Names in Google Search

name: Monitor AMP SXG Errors via Search Console
then_action: Use the AMP status report in Google Search Console to track and diagnose SXG errors specific to AMP pages
if_condition: If SXG is implemented on AMP pages and you need to monitor for SXG-related errors
source_title: Signed Exchanges – Prefetching for Better Page Experience

name: Home Page Must Be Crawlable for Site Name Recognition
then_action: Google cannot recognise the site name structured data; ensure the home page is fully crawlable by Google
if_condition: If the home page is blocked from crawling by Googlebot (e.g., via robots.txt or noindex)
source_title: Site Names in Google Search

name: Repetitive Content Dilutes Sitelinks
then_action: The quality of sitelinks will be diluted, reducing the chances of high-quality sitelinks being shown
if_condition: If a website contains repetitive or duplicate content across multiple pages
source_title: Sitelinks – How Google Generates Navigation Links for Sites

name: Duplicate Home Pages Must All Carry Site Name Structured Data
then_action: Place the same WebSite structured data with site name properties on all duplicate home page versions to ensure consistent signalling to Google
if_condition: If a site has duplicate home pages such as both HTTP and HTTPS versions
source_title: Site Names in Google Search

name: Removing Unwanted Sitelink Pages
then_action: Either remove the page from the site entirely or add a noindex directive to that page to prevent it from appearing as a sitelink
if_condition: If a site owner wants to remove a specific page from appearing as a sitelink
source_title: Sitelinks – How Google Generates Navigation Links for Sites

name: Nest Site Name Properties in Existing WebSite Structured Data Block
then_action: Do not create a separate WebSite structured data block; instead, nest the site name 'name' and 'alternateName' properties within the existing WebSite structured data block
if_condition: If WebSite structured data already exists on the home page
source_title: Site Names in Google Search

name: Sitelinks Structural Requirements
then_action: Google's algorithms will be unable to identify good sitelinks, and sitelinks will not be shown for that site in search results
if_condition: If a website lacks a clear hierarchical structure with meaningful internal links and logical navigation
source_title: Sitelinks – How Google Generates Navigation Links for Sites

name: Sitelinks User Relevance Threshold
then_action: Sitelinks will not be displayed for that query, even if the site otherwise has a valid sitelinks structure
if_condition: If Google determines that sitelinks for a site are not relevant to the user's specific query
source_title: Sitelinks – How Google Generates Navigation Links for Sites

name: Use Site Name Consistently Across Home Page
then_action: Ensure the site name is used consistently across all elements of the home page to reinforce the preferred name signal to Google
if_condition: If a preferred site name is specified in WebSite structured data but differs from how the site name is presented in visible home page content
source_title: Site Names in Google Search

name: Sitelinks Title and Heading Quality
then_action: The quality of sitelinks generated by Google will improve, increasing the likelihood of sitelinks being displayed
if_condition: If page titles and headings are informative, relevant, and compact across the site
source_title: Sitelinks – How Google Generates Navigation Links for Sites

name: Sitemap Index File Size and URL Limits
then_action: The sitemap index file must be 50 megabytes or less uncompressed and contain no more than 50,000 sitemap URLs; up to 500 sitemap index files can be submitted per site
if_condition: If a sitemap index file is being created or submitted to Search Console
source_title: Sitemap Index Files – Managing Multiple Sitemaps

name: Sitemap Index Same-Site Hosting Requirement
then_action: The configuration is invalid; referenced sitemaps must be hosted on the same site as the sitemap index file unless cross-site submission is set up via Search Console
if_condition: If a sitemap index file references individual sitemaps hosted on a different site without cross-site submission configured in Search Console
source_title: Sitemap Index Files – Managing Multiple Sitemaps

name: Use Sitemap for Rich Media or Google News Sites
then_action: Then create a sitemap and include media-specific entries (video runtime/rating, image locations, news article titles/dates) to provide Google with valuable metadata for those content types
if_condition: If the site has a lot of rich media content such as video and images, or is shown in Google News
source_title: Sitemaps – What They Are and When to Use One

name: Sitemap Directory Hierarchy Requirement
then_action: The configuration is invalid; referenced sitemaps must be in the same directory as the sitemap index file or lower in the site hierarchy
if_condition: If sitemaps referenced in a sitemap index file are located in a directory higher in the site hierarchy than the sitemap index file itself
source_title: Sitemap Index Files – Managing Multiple Sitemaps

name: Use Sitemap for Large Sites with Crawl Risk
then_action: Then create and submit a sitemap to help Google discover and crawl those pages more efficiently
if_condition: If the site is large and Google might overlook some new or recently updated pages, or has a large archive of content pages that are isolated or not well linked to each other
source_title: Sitemaps – What They Are and When to Use One

name: Cross-Site Sitemap Verification Requirement
then_action: The cross-site sitemap will not work; all sites must be verified in Search Console OR the sitemaps must be listed in the robots.txt files of their individual hosts
if_condition: If a single sitemap includes URLs from multiple sites (cross-site sitemap) and some of those sites are not verified in Search Console and not listed in their respective robots.txt files
source_title: Sitemap Index Files – Managing Multiple Sitemaps

name: Meta Description Uniqueness Per Page
then_action: replace with unique, page-specific meta descriptions, as duplicate descriptions are not helpful when individual pages appear in search results
if_condition: if multiple pages on a site use identical or similar meta descriptions
source_title: Snippets and Meta Descriptions

name: Robots.txt Sitemap Reference Per Site
then_action: Each site's robots.txt file should reference the sitemap specific to that individual site
if_condition: If a site participates in a cross-site sitemap setup with multiple verified properties in Search Console
source_title: Sitemap Index Files – Managing Multiple Sitemaps

name: Use Sitemap for New Sites with Few External Links
then_action: Then create and submit a sitemap to ensure Google can discover and crawl the site's URLs
if_condition: If the site is new and has few external links pointing to it
source_title: Sitemaps – What They Are and When to Use One

name: Check CMS for Auto-Generated Sitemap Before Creating One
then_action: Then check whether the CMS has already automatically created and submitted a sitemap before manually creating one
if_condition: If the site is built on a CMS such as WordPress, Wix, or Blogger
source_title: Sitemaps – What They Are and When to Use One

name: Skip Sitemap for Small Well-Linked Sites
then_action: Then a sitemap is likely unnecessary and may not provide meaningful crawl or indexing benefit
if_condition: If the site has roughly 500 pages or fewer of content intended for search results AND is comprehensively linked internally AND has no significant media files or news pages
source_title: Sitemaps – What They Are and When to Use One

name: Snippet Length Control via Max-Snippet Tag
then_action: use the max-snippet:[number] meta tag to specify the maximum snippet length Google can display
if_condition: if a site owner wants to limit the maximum character length of snippets shown in search results
source_title: Snippets and Meta Descriptions

name: Snippet Source Priority
then_action: Google may use the meta description as the snippet source instead of extracting text directly from page content
if_condition: if a page has a meta description that provides a more accurate summary than the page's body content for a given query
source_title: Snippets and Meta Descriptions

name: Snippet Suppression via Nosnippet Tag
then_action: add the nosnippet meta tag to the page to block Google from creating and displaying snippets
if_condition: if a site owner wants to prevent any snippet from appearing for their pages in Google Search results
source_title: Snippets and Meta Descriptions

name: Do Not Assume Sitemap Guarantees Indexing
then_action: Then do not assume all URLs listed in the sitemap will be crawled and indexed — a sitemap aids discovery but does not guarantee indexing
if_condition: If a sitemap has been submitted to Google
source_title: Sitemaps – What They Are and When to Use One

name: Partial Content Exclusion from Snippets
then_action: apply the data-nosnippet attribute to those specific HTML elements to exclude them from snippet generation
if_condition: if specific HTML elements on a page should not be included in Google-generated snippets
source_title: Snippets and Meta Descriptions

name: Social Engineering Content Triggers Chrome Warning
then_action: Chrome browser will display a 'Deceptive site ahead' warning to visitors viewing the site
if_condition: If Google detects that a website contains social engineering content (phishing, deceptive content, or insufficiently labelled third-party services)
source_title: Social Engineering – Phishing and Deceptive Sites

name: Unlabelled Third-Party Services Flagged as Social Engineering
then_action: Google may flag this as social engineering content, risking a 'Deceptive site ahead' Chrome warning
if_condition: If a third party operates a site or service on behalf of another entity without clearly disclosing the relationship to users
source_title: Social Engineering – Phishing and Deceptive Sites

name: Hacked Site Hosting Social Engineering Content Requires Remediation
then_action: Consult Google's Help for Hacked Sites documentation to remediate the issue and remove the social engineering content
if_condition: If a site is suspected of being hacked and is hosting or distributing social engineering content through modified existing content or newly added deceptive pages
source_title: Social Engineering – Phishing and Deceptive Sites

name: SoftwareApplication Validation and Monitoring Rule
then_action: Use the Rich Results Test to validate the markup, then monitor ongoing implementation using the Software Apps status report in Google Search Console
if_condition: If SoftwareApplication structured data has been added to a page or deployed across a site
source_title: Software App Structured Data – SoftwareApplication Rich Results

name: SoftwareApplication Required Properties Rule
then_action: The markup is invalid and will not qualify for rich results; all three required property groups must be present: name, offers with price, and at least one of aggregateRating or review
if_condition: If SoftwareApplication structured data is implemented but missing 'name', 'offers' (with price), or both 'aggregateRating' and 'review' properties
source_title: Software App Structured Data – SoftwareApplication Rich Results

name: Embedded Third-Party Social Engineering Content Penalizes Host Site
then_action: The host site itself can be flagged for social engineering even if the deceptive content originates from third-party resources rather than the site's own pages
if_condition: If a host site contains embedded images, third-party components, or ads with social engineering content, or uses popups/redirects that lead users to social engineering pages
source_title: Social Engineering – Phishing and Deceptive Sites

name: SoftwareApplication Recommended Properties Rule
then_action: Include recommended properties: 'applicationCategory' (e.g., game, browser), 'operatingSystem' to specify supported OS, and additional pricing details within the 'offers' property to improve rich result completeness
if_condition: If SoftwareApplication structured data is being implemented for a software app
source_title: Software App Structured Data – SoftwareApplication Rich Results

name: Hidden Text and Links Warning
then_action: The page violates Google's hidden text and links spam policy and may be ranked lower or removed from search results
if_condition: If a page uses very small font sizes, text color matching the background, content placed behind images, or CSS to move content off-screen to hide text or links from users while keeping them visible to crawlers
source_title: Spam Policies – Cloaking Through Link Spam

name: SoftwareApplication Content Accuracy Rule
then_action: The markup violates Google's requirements; structured data must only represent apps users can actually download or use, and must reflect content visible and accessible on the page
if_condition: If SoftwareApplication structured data describes an app that is not actually downloadable or usable by users, or includes information not visible on the page
source_title: Software App Structured Data – SoftwareApplication Rich Results

name: Cloaking Detection Warning
then_action: The page violates Google's cloaking spam policy and may be ranked lower or removed from search results entirely
if_condition: If a page shows different content, redirects, or uses JavaScript/CSS to hide content from Googlebot compared to what human visitors see
source_title: Spam Policies – Cloaking Through Link Spam

name: Doorway Page Detection Warning
then_action: The pages qualify as doorway pages, violating Google's spam policies, and risk ranking demotion or removal from search results
if_condition: If a site contains multiple similar template-generated pages targeting specific regions, cities, or keywords that exist primarily to redirect traffic rather than provide standalone value
source_title: Spam Policies – Cloaking Through Link Spam

name: Hacked Content Risk Warning
then_action: The site is hosting hacked content, which violates Google's spam policies and may result in ranking demotion or removal from search results, harming both users and the legitimate site owner
if_condition: If a site contains content injected without the site owner's permission, particularly content designed to manipulate search rankings
source_title: Spam Policies – Cloaking Through Link Spam

name: Link Spam Detection Warning
then_action: The links qualify as link spam under Google's spam policies and may cause the site to be ranked lower or removed from search results
if_condition: If a site's backlink profile includes paid links passing PageRank, reciprocal link exchanges for ranking manipulation, automated link-building, forum or blog comment links placed primarily for SEO, or links from private blog networks
source_title: Spam Policies – Cloaking Through Link Spam

name: Sneaky Redirect Cloaking Detection
then_action: The practice is classified as a sneaky redirect and cloaking violation, and the page may be removed from search results
if_condition: If a page shown to Googlebot is optimised for a query but users are redirected to a page with unrelated or different content
source_title: Spam Policies – Machine Generated Content Through Scam and Fraud

name: Scam and Fraudulent Content Removal
then_action: The content is classified as scam or fraud, pages are removed from search results, and in severe cases the entire site may be penalised
if_condition: If a site impersonates legitimate businesses, operates fake review schemes, distributes fraudulent downloads, or harvests personal information under false pretences
source_title: Spam Policies – Machine Generated Content Through Scam and Fraud

name: Speakable Markup Should Exclude Voice-Confusing Content
then_action: Do NOT mark it with speakable structured data, as it may sound confusing in voice-only situations
if_condition: If a webpage element is a dateline, photo caption, or source attribution
source_title: Speakable Structured Data – Audio Playback for News Content

name: Speakable Content Should Be Concise Per Section
then_action: Limit speakable content to approximately 20 to 30 seconds of audio (roughly two to three sentences) per section to optimize for text-to-speech playback
if_condition: If speakable structured data is being added to a content section
source_title: Speakable Structured Data – Audio Playback for News Content

name: Spam Update Compliance Recovery Timeline
then_action: Expect recovery to take months, not days or weeks, as Google's automated systems need time to reassess compliance over an extended period
if_condition: If a site's rankings drop after a Google spam update and changes are made to bring the site into compliance with spam policies
source_title: Spam Updates – What They Are and How to Respond

name: ItemList Required for Carousel Beta Eligibility
then_action: Then ItemList structured data must be added in combination with at least one supported structured data content type (e.g., LocalBusiness or specific entity types)
if_condition: If a site wants to be eligible for the structured data carousels beta rich result
source_title: Structured Data Carousels Beta – New Host Carousel Format

name: Link Spam Update Permanent Ranking Loss
then_action: Then the ranking benefits those spammy links previously generated cannot be regained, even after cleaning up the link spam — rankings may not return to previous levels
if_condition: If a site was affected by a Google link spam update and previously benefited from spammy links that have since been removed or discounted by Google's systems
source_title: Spam Updates – What They Are and How to Respond

name: Use Most Specific Type Closest to Actual Content Scenario
then_action: Then use the type closest to the actual scenario; generic types like LocalBusiness are acceptable but specific types are preferred, especially when recommended properties require the specific type
if_condition: If choosing a structured data type for carousel markup and a specific type exists that matches the content scenario
source_title: Structured Data Carousels Beta – New Host Carousel Format

name: Post-Spam-Update Policy Review Requirement
then_action: Then review Google's spam policies to identify any violations, as sites violating spam policies may rank lower or be excluded from results entirely
if_condition: If a site experiences a ranking change following a Google spam update announcement
source_title: Spam Updates – What They Are and How to Respond

name: Use ProductGroup Schema for Multi-Variant Products
then_action: Use ProductGroup structured data instead of Product to correctly communicate variant relationships to Google
if_condition: If a product page represents a single product that exists in multiple variants (e.g., different sizes, colors, or configurations)
source_title: Structured Data for Ecommerce Sites – Relevant Schema Types

name: Summary Page Only Requires Structured Data for Beta Carousel
then_action: Then structured data only needs to be added to the summary page, not individual detail pages
if_condition: If implementing the beta host carousel using a summary page that links to individual detail pages
source_title: Structured Data Carousels Beta – New Host Carousel Format

name: Carousel Markup Must Follow Search Essentials and General Structured Data Guidelines
then_action: Then the markup must comply with Google Search Essentials and general structured data guidelines, regardless of which structural approach (summary page or all-in-one page) is used
if_condition: If adding structured data markup for the beta carousel rich result
source_title: Structured Data Carousels Beta – New Host Carousel Format

name: Add LocalBusiness Schema for Ecommerce Sites with Physical Stores
then_action: Implement LocalBusiness structured data to communicate physical location information to Google
if_condition: If the ecommerce site has one or more physical store locations
source_title: Structured Data for Ecommerce Sites – Relevant Schema Types

name: Implement Review and AggregateRating Schema to Enable Review Snippets
then_action: Add Review and/or AggregateRating structured data to enable review snippets in Google Search rich results
if_condition: If a product page or review page contains user reviews or aggregate ratings
source_title: Structured Data for Ecommerce Sites – Relevant Schema Types

name: Validate Structured Data Before Deployment
then_action: Use the Rich Results Test to identify and fix critical errors before deployment, as unresolved errors will prevent rich result eligibility
if_condition: If structured data has been added to a page and has not yet been validated
source_title: Structured Data Deployment Workflow – How to Build, Test, and Release

name: Ensure Pages Are Crawlable Before Structured Data Testing
then_action: Remove access restrictions so pages are accessible to Google; blocked pages cannot be properly indexed or validated via the URL Inspection Tool
if_condition: If pages with structured data are blocked by robots.txt, noindex tag, or login requirements
source_title: Structured Data Deployment Workflow – How to Build, Test, and Release

name: Manual Action Causes Structured Data to Be Ignored
then_action: The structured data on that page will be ignored by Google and will not generate rich results, though the page may still appear in regular search results
if_condition: If a manual action has been applied against a page that contains structured data
source_title: Structured Data Deployment Workflow – How to Build, Test, and Release

name: Monitor Rich Result Performance After Indexing
then_action: Check the relevant Rich Result status report in Google Search Console to monitor performance and detect any implementation issues
if_condition: If structured data pages have been deployed and sufficient time has passed for Google to index them
source_title: Structured Data Deployment Workflow – How to Build, Test, and Release

name: Submit Sitemap After Structured Data Deployment
then_action: Submit a sitemap to Google Search Console to ensure Google is informed of current and future page changes, supporting timely recrawling
if_condition: If structured data has been deployed across pages and future changes are anticipated
source_title: Structured Data Deployment Workflow – How to Build, Test, and Release

name: Correct Implementation Does Not Guarantee Rich Results
then_action: Do not assume rich results will appear in search; Google does not guarantee rich result display even for correctly implemented structured data
if_condition: If structured data is correctly implemented and validated on a page
source_title: Structured Data Deployment Workflow – How to Build, Test, and Release

name: Structured Data Must Appear on All Duplicate Pages
then_action: Place the same structured data on all duplicate pages, not only on the canonical page
if_condition: If a site has duplicate pages for the same content and structured data is implemented
source_title: Structured Data General Guidelines

name: Browser-Hidden Paywall Content Must Not Be Served to Browser
then_action: Choose a paywall implementation that does not supply the paywalled content to the browser at all, rather than hiding it with CSS or JavaScript
if_condition: If paywalled content should not be accessible to the browser at the time of page serving
source_title: Subscription and Paywalled Content Structured Data

name: Paywalled Indexable Content Requires Structured Data
then_action: Use schema.org structured data with isAccessibleForFree=false on the Article (or other creative work type), plus hasPart pointing to WebPageElement instances with cssSelector identifying paywalled CSS classes — to distinguish paywalled content from cloaking (a spam policy violation)
if_condition: If a site offers subscription-based or registration-gated content that is intended to be crawled and indexed by Google
source_title: Subscription and Paywalled Content Structured Data

name: Structured Data Policy Violation Triggers Manual Action
then_action: A manual action may be applied, removing eligibility for rich result appearance for the affected page (page ranking in standard web search is not affected)
if_condition: If structured data on a page violates Google Search content policies or spam policies
source_title: Structured Data General Guidelines

name: Include self-referencing hreflang link on every page variant
then_action: Each page variant must include a complete set of link elements in the head — one link for each page variant including itself — and the set of links must be identical across every version of the page
if_condition: If implementing hreflang annotations in the HTML head section
source_title: Tell Google About Localized Versions of Your Pages – hreflang Annotations

name: No Structured Data Needed for Non-Indexed Paywalled Content
then_action: Do not implement paywall structured data — it is only required for content intended to be indexed
if_condition: If paywalled content should NOT be crawled or indexed at all
source_title: Subscription and Paywalled Content Structured Data

name: Exclude Paywalled Sections from Search Snippets Using data-nosnippet
then_action: Add the data-nosnippet HTML attribute to the paywalled HTML elements to prevent those sections from being included in snippets
if_condition: If paywalled content sections should not appear in Google search result snippets
source_title: Subscription and Paywalled Content Structured Data

name: Use Only Class Selectors in cssSelector Property
then_action: Use only CSS class selectors (not ID or element selectors) in the cssSelector property, and add a class name around each paywalled section of the page; do not nest paywalled content sections
if_condition: If implementing paywall structured data using the cssSelector property on WebPageElement
source_title: Subscription and Paywalled Content Structured Data

name: Use Most Specific Schema.org Type and Properties
then_action: Use the most specific applicable type and property names defined by schema.org, and follow the additional guidelines for each specific rich result type
if_condition: If implementing structured data markup and multiple schema.org types or properties could apply
source_title: Structured Data General Guidelines

name: Missing Filtered Property Excludes Listing from Enriched Search Filters
then_action: The listing will not appear in enriched search results when users apply that filter, reducing visibility
if_condition: If a structured data listing is missing a property that users can filter by in enriched search (e.g., salary for job postings)
source_title: Structured Data General Guidelines

name: AMP Paywalled Pages Must Use amp-subscriptions and Grant Googlebot Access
then_action: Use amp-subscriptions where appropriate; the authorisation endpoint must grant access to appropriate Googlebot crawlers, and bot access policy must be consistent between AMP and non-AMP pages
if_condition: If AMP pages contain paywalled content
source_title: Subscription and Paywalled Content Structured Data

name: Site-Wide Low Quality Structured Data Triggers Enriched Search Exclusion
then_action: Google may exclude the entire site from enriched search results
if_condition: If the enriched search ranking algorithm determines that a large portion of a site's structured data does not meet the quality bar
source_title: Structured Data General Guidelines

name: Indicate hreflang for multi-language or multi-region page variants
then_action: Use hreflang annotations to tell Google about the different page variants, implemented via HTML head link elements, HTTP response headers, or XML sitemap entries
if_condition: If a site has multiple versions of a page for different languages or regions
source_title: Tell Google About Localized Versions of Your Pages – hreflang Annotations

name: Keep Site Online During Temporary Business Disruptions
then_action: Keep the site online and limit functionality (disable cart, display banners, adjust structured data) rather than taking it offline entirely to minimise negative effects on Google Search presence
if_condition: If a business temporarily cannot fulfil orders or has many products out of stock
source_title: Temporarily Pause or Disable a Website

name: Use 503 Status Code for Very Short Site Disabling (1-2 Days)
then_action: Return an informational error page with a 503 HTTP response status code instead of all content
if_condition: If the entire website must be urgently disabled for one to two days
source_title: Temporarily Pause or Disable a Website

name: Use 200 Status Placeholder Page for Longer Site Disabling
then_action: Provide an indexable home page as a placeholder returning a 200 HTTP status code so users can still find the site in search
if_condition: If the site needs to be disabled for a period longer than two days
source_title: Temporarily Pause or Disable a Website

name: Warn Against Complete Site Removal from Google Index
then_action: Avoid complete removal — there is no fixed recovery time and no mechanism to speed recovery, making it very difficult to regain previous search presence
if_condition: If considering completely removing a site from Google's index due to temporary business issues
source_title: Temporarily Pause or Disable a Website

name: Site Returning Errors Blocks Metadata Refresh by Google
then_action: Google's systems cannot refresh titles, descriptions, metadata, or structured data for that site, further degrading its search presence
if_condition: If a site returns error responses for all URLs
source_title: Temporarily Pause or Disable a Website

name: Top Places List Opt-Out Mechanism
then_action: The site owner must opt out of display in local search results and other Google properties; direct removal requests for specific lists are not supported
if_condition: If a site owner wants to prevent their curated lists from appearing in Top Places List rich results
source_title: Top Places List – Site Eligibility for Business Mentions

name: Use x-default hreflang value as language fallback
then_action: Specify the x-default hreflang value on the fallback page to handle users whose language settings do not match any localised version
if_condition: If a site has localised page versions but a user's browser language does not match any of the available localised variants
source_title: Tell Google About Localized Versions of Your Pages – hreflang Annotations

name: Avoid Flight Pricing in Title Elements
then_action: Then it should be removed, as rapidly changing flight prices make title links inaccurate and reduce quality
if_condition: If a page includes flight price information within the title HTML element
source_title: Title Links in Google Search – How They Are Generated and Best Practices

name: Use valid ISO language and region codes in hreflang attributes
then_action: These codes are invalid and will be ignored by Google Search; use ISO 639-1 for language codes and ISO 3166-1 Alpha 2 for region codes instead
if_condition: If hreflang annotations include region codes such as EU, UN, or UK
source_title: Tell Google About Localized Versions of Your Pages – hreflang Annotations

name: Top Places List Physical Location Requirement
then_action: The business is ineligible for Top Places List rich results, regardless of how many curated lists mention it
if_condition: If a business does not have a physical location
source_title: Top Places List – Site Eligibility for Business Mentions

name: Recommend hreflang when only page template elements are translated
then_action: Indicate alternate page variants using hreflang annotations so Google can direct users to the most appropriate version
if_condition: If main page content is in a single language but template elements such as navigation and footer are translated into other languages
source_title: Tell Google About Localized Versions of Your Pages – hreflang Annotations

name: Title Language Must Match Page Content Language
then_action: Then the title link quality is degraded; titles should match the same language and writing system as the primary page content
if_condition: If the language or writing system used in the title element differs from the primary content language on the page
source_title: Title Links in Google Search – How They Are Generated and Best Practices

name: Title Element as Primary Signal for Title Links
then_action: Google uses it as the primary signal for generating the title link in search results
if_condition: If a page has a title HTML element with descriptive text
source_title: Title Links in Google Search – How They Are Generated and Best Practices

name: Monitor Translated Result Performance in Search Console
then_action: Use the Search Appearance filter in the Performance report in Google Search Console to monitor translated result metrics separately
if_condition: If a publisher wants to track clicks and impressions specifically from translated search results
source_title: Translated Results – Cross Language Search Results

name: Every Page Must Have a Unique Title Element
then_action: Then title link quality is reduced; every page should have a descriptive, concise title specified in the title HTML element
if_condition: If a page is missing a title HTML element or uses vague descriptors like 'Home' or 'Profile'
source_title: Title Links in Google Search – How They Are Generated and Best Practices

name: Title Link Update Delay After Recrawl
then_action: Then expect Google to take a few days to a few weeks to reflect the change in search results after recrawling and reprocessing the page
if_condition: If a title element or other title link source has been updated on a page
source_title: Title Links in Google Search – How They Are Generated and Best Practices

name: Boilerplate Titles Reduce Title Link Quality
then_action: Then title link quality is reduced; each page should have a unique title that clearly describes its specific topic
if_condition: If multiple pages across a site use the same or very similar title text
source_title: Title Links in Google Search – How They Are Generated and Best Practices

name: Top Places List Curation Authenticity Requirement
then_action: The list is ineligible to appear as a Top Places List rich result in Google Search
if_condition: If a top places list is sponsored, not independently curated, or built from templated sentences using automated data or metrics
source_title: Top Places List – Site Eligibility for Business Mentions

name: Opt Out of Google Translated Results Using Meta Tag or HTTP Header
then_action: Apply the 'notranslate' meta tag to the page or use the X-Robots-Tag HTTP response header with the 'notranslate' value to opt out of translation-related features across all of Google Search
if_condition: If a publisher wants to prevent Google from translating their page title, snippet, or content in Search results
source_title: Translated Results – Cross Language Search Results

name: Top Places List Automatic Eligibility Determination
then_action: The request will have no effect; Google's systems automatically determine eligibility based on the defined criteria without manual intervention from site owners
if_condition: If a site owner attempts to directly request or guarantee their list appears as a Top Places List rich result
source_title: Top Places List – Site Eligibility for Business Mentions

name: Page-Level vs Site-Wide Ranking Independence
then_action: Do not assume all pages will rank uniformly; Google ranks at the page level, so strong site-wide signals do not guarantee all pages rank well, and weak site-wide signals do not doom all pages to rank poorly
if_condition: If a site has some high-quality pages but also contains low-quality pages, or vice versa
source_title: Understanding Google Search Ranking Systems

name: Query Freshness Signal for Recency-Sensitive Queries
then_action: Then Google's Query Deserves Freshness system will favor newer content, so publishing or updating content promptly improves ranking potential for time-sensitive topics
if_condition: If a search query implies recency expectations (e.g., news, current events, recent product releases)
source_title: Understanding Google Search Ranking Systems

name: Per-URL Crawl History Investigation via Server Logs
then_action: Examine server logs directly to determine whether specific URLs have been crawled by Googlebot, as Search Console does not offer per-URL crawl history filtering
if_condition: If you need to verify whether a specific URL or path has been crawled by Googlebot and Search Console does not provide filtered crawl history by URL
source_title: Troubleshoot Google Search Crawling Errors

name: Ad Network Compatibility Check Required for Translated Results
then_action: Take additional action to ensure ad networks display correctly after a user clicks a translated result, as default ad behavior may break under machine-translated pages
if_condition: If a site uses ad networks and Google translated results are active (user clicks a translated title link triggering machine translation)
source_title: Translated Results – Cross Language Search Results

name: Translated Results Eligibility by Target Language
then_action: Google may translate the title link and snippet of that result into the user's search language, enabling cross-language discovery and potentially expanding publisher audience reach
if_condition: If a user searches in one of the supported languages (Arabic, Bengali, English, French, German, Gujarati, Hindi, Indonesian, Kannada, Korean, Malayalam, Marathi, Persian, Portuguese, Spanish, Tamil, Telugu, Thai, Turkish, Urdu, or Vietnamese) and a relevant result exists in a different language
source_title: Translated Results – Cross Language Search Results

name: robots.txt Misuse for Crawl Budget Management
then_action: Stop this practice immediately — robots.txt should not be used as a temporary crawl budget mechanism, and rotating sitemaps or dynamically adding/removing pages from robots.txt is not recommended
if_condition: If robots.txt is being used temporarily to reallocate crawl budget, or if pages are being regularly added/removed from robots.txt or sitemaps rotated
source_title: Troubleshoot Google Search Crawling Errors

name: Soft 404 Detection and Diagnosis
then_action: Use the URL Inspection tool to examine the rendered content and returned HTTP status code to determine if the page failed to load properly, was missing critical resources, or displayed a prominent error message during rendering
if_condition: If a page that should be valid is flagged as a soft 404 (returns 200 HTTP status but displays a 'page not found' message)
source_title: Troubleshoot Google Search Crawling Errors

name: Crawl Budget Optimization for Large Sites
then_action: Increase server capacity, ensure no important pages are blocked by robots.txt, and reduce excessive duplicate content to improve crawl budget allocation
if_condition: If important URLs on a large site are not being crawled or updated frequently enough in Google Search Console Crawl Stats report
source_title: Troubleshoot Google Search Crawling Errors

name: Blocking Non-Indexable Pages from Crawling
then_action: Block those pages or directories from crawling using robots.txt to prevent Googlebot from accessing them
if_condition: If pages or directories exist that should not appear in Google search results
source_title: Troubleshoot Google Search Crawling Errors

name: New URL Crawl Timeline Expectation
then_action: This is expected behavior — new pages typically take several days at minimum to be noticed and crawled; monitor progress through the Crawl Stats report in Search Console rather than expecting same-day crawling
if_condition: If newly published URLs are not showing as crawled within 24 hours of publication
source_title: Troubleshoot Google Search Crawling Errors

name: Link Analysis as Topic and Authority Signal
then_action: Then Google's link analysis systems use those links to determine what the page is about and assess its helpfulness, making internal and external linking structure a relevant ranking signal
if_condition: If a page receives or gives links to other pages
source_title: Understanding Google Search Ranking Systems

name: Link Spam Detection via Penguin
then_action: Then Google's Penguin system will detect and discount or penalize those links, reducing their positive ranking impact and potentially harming site authority
if_condition: If a site's backlink profile includes manipulative or spammy links
source_title: Understanding Google Search Ranking Systems

name: Core Web Vitals as Primary Ranking Signal
then_action: Core Web Vitals are the most directly influential page experience signals; prioritize optimizing Core Web Vitals above other page experience factors
if_condition: If a page is being evaluated for page experience signals in Google Search ranking
source_title: Understanding Page Experience in Google Search

name: Core Web Vitals Do Not Guarantee Top Rankings
then_action: Do not assume top rankings are guaranteed; Core Web Vitals scores alone are insufficient for top placement in Google Search results
if_condition: If a page achieves good scores in Search Console's Core Web Vitals report or third-party tools
source_title: Understanding Page Experience in Google Search

name: Non-Core Web Vitals Page Experience Factors Are Indirect
then_action: Recognize these factors do not directly improve rankings but improve user satisfaction, which aligns with what Google's ranking systems seek to reward
if_condition: If optimizing page experience factors beyond Core Web Vitals (e.g., HTTPS, mobile-friendliness, interstitials, ad density)
source_title: Understanding Page Experience in Google Search

name: Satirical Content Must Not Use NewsArticle Type
then_action: replace NewsArticle schema type with SatiricalArticle subtype to avoid misrepresentation of content nature
if_condition: if content is clearly satirical and is marked up with NewsArticle schema type
source_title: NewsArticle

name: Holistic Page Experience Optimization Required
then_action: Expand optimization efforts to cover all page experience aspects including Core Web Vitals, HTTPS, mobile-friendliness, ad density, interstitials, and content distinguishability
if_condition: If a site owner is optimizing for Google Search success and focusing on only one or two page experience aspects
source_title: Understanding Page Experience in Google Search

name: Core Web Vitals Evaluated Per Page Not Site-Wide
then_action: Evaluate and optimize Core Web Vitals on a per-page basis, not as a site-wide average, since Google's ranking systems apply page experience on a page-specific basis
if_condition: If assessing Core Web Vitals performance for a website with mixed page quality
source_title: Understanding Page Experience in Google Search

name: Understand Platform Source Selection Criteria
then_action: Audit and understand how each AI platform (ChatGPT Search, Google AI Overviews, Perplexity, Copilot) selects sources, then align content structure and signals to match those platform-specific selection criteria
if_condition: If content is not being cited by AI platforms despite covering relevant topics
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 What Is AI Search Optimization

name: Ensure Consistent Preferred Citation Status
then_action: Prioritize content signals that drive consistent preferred citation status — inconsistent or lower-ranked AI citations indicate insufficient GEO optimization and risk losing visibility to competing sources that AI models reference first
if_condition: If content appears inconsistently or as a secondary source in AI-generated responses
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 What Is AI Search Optimization

name: Rankings Prerequisite for AI Overview Targeting
then_action: Prioritize improving organic ranking before optimizing for AI Overview inclusion, as top-10 presence is a prerequisite for consistent AI citation
if_condition: If a target page is ranking outside the top 10 organic results for a query
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 2

name: Do Not Expect Exact Data Match Between Search Console and GA
then_action: Expect data discrepancies — the two tools use different metrics and systems, so data will not match completely
if_condition: If comparing metrics between Search Console and Google Analytics
source_title: Using Search Console and Google Analytics Together for SEO

name: Blend Data for Per-Query Advanced Analysis
then_action: Blend or join the two data sources in Looker Studio using Country, Device, and Landing Page dimensions to enable advanced per-query analysis
if_condition: If you need per-query metrics combining Search Console and Google Analytics data
source_title: Using Search Console and Google Analytics Together for SEO

name: VacationRental Structured Data: Content Accuracy Requirement
then_action: The page violates Google's structured data guidelines and may receive a manual action penalty; ensure all structured data values mirror what users can see on the page
if_condition: If the VacationRental structured data on a page contains information that does not match or accurately reflect the visible on-page content
source_title: Vacation Rental Structured Data – VacationRental Listings

name: Invalid HTML Element in Head Causes Metadata Truncation
then_action: Google will ignore all elements appearing after the first invalid element in the <head>, causing meta descriptions, canonical tags, robots meta tags, and structured data to be unprocessed
if_condition: If an invalid HTML element (e.g., div, p, img) appears inside the <head> element before valid metadata elements
source_title: Use Valid HTML to Specify Page Metadata

name: VacationRental Structured Data: Page Indexability Requirement
then_action: The page is ineligible for rich results in Google Search; remove access restrictions so Googlebot can crawl and index the page
if_condition: If a vacation rental page with VacationRental structured data is blocked by robots.txt, has a noindex tag, or requires user login to access
source_title: Vacation Rental Structured Data – VacationRental Listings

name: Place Invalid Head Elements After Critical Metadata
then_action: Place those invalid elements after all valid metadata elements (title, meta, link, script, style, base, noscript, template) so Google can process the critical metadata before encountering the invalid element
if_condition: If invalid elements (div, p, img) must be placed in the <head> element for technical reasons
source_title: Use Valid HTML to Specify Page Metadata

name: Structured Data in Head Affected by Preceding Invalid HTML
then_action: Google may not process the structured data, reducing eligibility for rich results and knowledge graph integration
if_condition: If invalid HTML elements appear in the <head> before structured data markup (e.g., JSON-LD script blocks)
source_title: Use Valid HTML to Specify Page Metadata

name: Valid Head Elements Whitelist
then_action: Flag the element as invalid per HTML standard; treat it as a risk for metadata truncation and audit all metadata elements placed after it
if_condition: If any element other than title, meta, link, script, style, base, noscript, or template is found inside the <head> element
source_title: Use Valid HTML to Specify Page Metadata

name: Use Search Console + GA Together for Conversion Attribution
then_action: Compare Search Console performance data with Google Analytics organic traffic to accurately attribute conversions to Google Search
if_condition: If you need to attribute conversions (ecommerce transactions, newsletter signups, lead gen form completions) to Google Search traffic
source_title: Using Search Console and Google Analytics Together for SEO

name: Use Looker Studio for Unified Search Console and GA Visualization
then_action: Use Looker Studio with the Google Search Central dashboard template (orange = GA data, blue = Search Console data), filtering GA data to Google organic search sessions only
if_condition: If you need to view Search Console and Google Analytics data side by side in a single dashboard
source_title: Using Search Console and Google Analytics Together for SEO

name: VacationRental Structured Data: Required Name Property
then_action: The structured data is incomplete and will not meet Google's requirements; add 'name' as a required property to be eligible for enhanced search results
if_condition: If a vacation rental property page implements VacationRental schema.org structured data but omits the 'name' property
source_title: Vacation Rental Structured Data – VacationRental Listings

name: Deep Topical Authority Required for AI Overview Eligibility
then_action: Restructure content strategy to build deep topical authority across multiple aspects of the target topic, covering different facets that align with Google's fan-out sub-queries
if_condition: Content is being optimized for AI Overview inclusion and currently targets individual keywords or isolated citations
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 3

name: Ensure Googlebot Can Access Video Streaming URL
then_action: Unblock the streaming file URL so Googlebot can crawl it; otherwise the video will not be eligible for indexing and video features
if_condition: If Googlebot is blocked from fetching the video's streaming file URL (e.g., via robots.txt or server-level restrictions)
source_title: Video SEO Best Practices for Google Search

name: Use Clip Structured Data to Specify Key Moments
then_action: Implement Clip structured data specifying the exact start time, end time, and label for each segment to override Google's automatic detection
if_condition: If a video contains distinct segments or chapters and the site owner wants to control how Key Moments are displayed in Google Search
source_title: Video SEO Best Practices for Google Search

name: VacationRental Structured Data: Recommended Properties for Enhanced Listings
then_action: The listing may display with fewer enhanced details in search results; add recommended properties to maximize eligibility for rich result features
if_condition: If a vacation rental page implements VacationRental structured data with only the required 'name' property and omits recommended properties such as address, geo, image, description, numberOfRooms, petsAllowed, amenityFeature, or aggregateRating
source_title: Vacation Rental Structured Data – VacationRental Listings

name: Use max-video-preview Tag to Control Preview Duration
then_action: Set the max-video-preview robots meta tag to the desired maximum number of seconds for the video preview
if_condition: If a site owner wants to limit the duration of the moving video preview Google displays in search results
source_title: Video SEO Best Practices for Google Search

name: Video Sitemap Required Tags Must Be Present
then_action: The sitemap entry is invalid; all four required tag types must be present, including at least one of video:content_loc or video:player_loc
if_condition: If a video sitemap entry is missing video:thumbnail_loc, video:title, video:description, or both video:content_loc and video:player_loc
source_title: Video Sitemaps and Alternatives

name: Avoid URL Fragments for Video Loading
then_action: Do not use fragment identifiers to load the video, as Google Search generally does not support URL fragments and the video may not be indexed
if_condition: If a video is loaded via a URL that uses a fragment identifier (e.g., example.com/page#video)
source_title: Video SEO Best Practices for Google Search

name: Ensure Sufficient Server Capacity for Video Crawling
then_action: Scale up server capacity on both the watch page host and streaming server to support crawling, otherwise Google may fail to index the video
if_condition: If the video watch page host or the video streaming server has insufficient server capacity to handle Googlebot crawl requests
source_title: Video SEO Best Practices for Google Search

name: Unrelated Videos Must Not Be Listed in Video Sitemap
then_action: Remove that video from the sitemap; only videos directly relevant to the host page content should be listed
if_condition: If a video listed in the video sitemap is unrelated to the content of the host page it is associated with
source_title: Video Sitemaps and Alternatives

name: Video Watch Page Must Be Publicly Accessible
then_action: Remove the robots.txt block or noindex tag, as Google cannot index videos on blocked pages and they will not appear in search results
if_condition: If a video watch page is blocked by robots.txt or has a noindex robots meta tag
source_title: Video SEO Best Practices for Google Search

name: Deprecated Video Sitemap Tags Must Not Be Used
then_action: Remove those tags as Google has deprecated them and they will have no effect or may cause sitemap processing issues
if_condition: If a video sitemap includes video:category, video:gallery_loc, autoplay or allow_embed attributes on video:player_loc, video:price tags, or video:tvshow tags
source_title: Video Sitemaps and Alternatives

name: Create Dedicated Watch Page Per Video for Rich Features
then_action: Create a dedicated watch page for each video whose primary purpose is displaying that single video
if_condition: If a video needs to be eligible for rich features such as Video mode results, Key Moments, or Live Badge in Google Search
source_title: Video SEO Best Practices for Google Search

name: Video Sitemap Files Must Be Googlebot Accessible
then_action: Those files will not be accessible to Googlebot and will not be indexed; ensure all referenced files are publicly accessible via HTTP or FTP without restrictions
if_condition: If files referenced in a video sitemap are blocked by robots.txt, require login/metafiles, are behind a firewall, or use streaming protocols instead of HTTP/FTP
source_title: Video Sitemaps and Alternatives

name: VacationRental Structured Data: Guideline Violation Manual Action Risk
then_action: Google may issue a manual action against the page, removing it from rich result eligibility; audit and correct all structured data to comply with both guideline sets
if_condition: If a vacation rental page's structured data violates either the VacationRental-specific documentation guidelines or Google's general structured data guidelines
source_title: Vacation Rental Structured Data – VacationRental Listings

name: VacationRental Page Crawlability Requirement
then_action: Then the page is not eligible for rich results in Google Search, regardless of whether valid VacationRental structured data is present
if_condition: If a vacation rental page is blocked by robots.txt, a noindex tag, or requires login to access
source_title: Vacation Rental Structured Data – VacationRental Listings

name: BroadcastEvent Live Badge Rule
then_action: Then implement BroadcastEvent structured data with a publication property linking to the broadcast, startDate and endDate defining the live broadcast window, and isLiveBroadcast set to true
if_condition: If a video page is hosting a live stream and the goal is to display a LIVE badge in Google Search results
source_title: Video Structured Data – VideoObject, Clip, and BroadcastEvent

name: Use mRSS Feed as Alternative to Video Sitemap
then_action: Implement an mRSS feed using the mRSS module to supplement RSS 2.0 elements; it can be submitted, tested, and updated the same way as a video sitemap
if_condition: If a site already uses an RSS 2.0 feed and needs to provide video metadata to Google without creating a separate video sitemap
source_title: Video Sitemaps and Alternatives

name: VacationRental Guideline Violation Penalty
then_action: Then Google may apply a manual action against the page, removing its eligibility for rich results
if_condition: If a vacation rental page violates either the VacationRental-specific structured data guidelines or the general structured data guidelines
source_title: Vacation Rental Structured Data – VacationRental Listings

name: VacationRental Structured Data Accuracy Requirement
then_action: Then the structured data must accurately reflect the content visible on the page; inaccurate structured data may result in a manual action
if_condition: If VacationRental structured data is added to a vacation rental page
source_title: Vacation Rental Structured Data – VacationRental Listings

name: VacationRental Required Property: name
then_action: Then the 'name' property (property name) must be included as a required field in the structured data markup
if_condition: If a vacation rental property page uses VacationRental schema.org structured data
source_title: Vacation Rental Structured Data – VacationRental Listings

name: Clip Structured Data for Video Segments Rule
then_action: Then implement Clip structured data to manually mark those key segments, enabling users to jump to specific points in the video from the search result
if_condition: If a video page contains important segments that users should be able to navigate to directly from search results
source_title: Video Structured Data – VideoObject, Clip, and BroadcastEvent

name: ItemList for Video Host Carousel Rule
then_action: Then implement ItemList structured data in combination with VideoObject markup to enable the video host carousel feature
if_condition: If a page lists multiple videos and the goal is to appear as a video host carousel in Google Search
source_title: Video Structured Data – VideoObject, Clip, and BroadcastEvent

name: Video Page Must Be Publicly Accessible
then_action: Remove the robots.txt block or noindex tag to make the page publicly accessible; blocked pages are ineligible for any video search features
if_condition: If the video watch page is blocked by robots.txt or a noindex robots meta tag
source_title: Video SEO Best Practices for Google Search

name: VacationRental Recommended Properties for Enhanced Listings
then_action: Then include recommended properties: address, geo, image, description, numberOfRooms, petsAllowed, amenityFeature, and aggregateRating to maximize eligibility for enhanced search result features
if_condition: If a vacation rental page implements VacationRental structured data and aims for enhanced rich result details
source_title: Vacation Rental Structured Data – VacationRental Listings

name: ISO 8601 Duration Format Rule
then_action: Then the duration value will not be correctly interpreted by Google; format the duration value using ISO 8601 standard (e.g., PT1H30M for 1 hour 30 minutes)
if_condition: If the duration property is included in VideoObject structured data and is not formatted in ISO 8601 format
source_title: Video Structured Data – VideoObject, Clip, and BroadcastEvent

name: Web Story Canonical Tag Requirement
then_action: Then the Web Story must include a canonical tag pointing to itself; absence of canonicalization will cause indexing and eligibility issues
if_condition: If a Web Story page is published and submitted for Google indexing
source_title: Web Stories – Enable, Best Practices, and Content Policy

name: VideoObject Required Properties Rule
then_action: Then the video rich result eligibility is at risk; ensure all four required properties (name, description, thumbnailUrl, uploadDate) are present to qualify for video rich results in Google Search
if_condition: If a VideoObject structured data markup is implemented on a video page without one or more of the required properties: name, description, thumbnailUrl, or uploadDate
source_title: Video Structured Data – VideoObject, Clip, and BroadcastEvent

name: contentUrl or embedUrl Recommended for Video Indexing
then_action: Then Google may have difficulty locating and indexing the actual video file; including at least one of contentUrl or embedUrl is strongly recommended to improve video indexability
if_condition: If VideoObject structured data is implemented and neither contentUrl nor embedUrl is provided
source_title: Video Structured Data – VideoObject, Clip, and BroadcastEvent

name: Clip Structured Data for Video Segment Navigation
then_action: Then implement Clip structured data to manually mark those key segments, enabling direct timestamp navigation in Google Search
if_condition: If specific important segments within a video should be surfaced so users can navigate directly to those points from search results
source_title: Video Structured Data – VideoObject, Clip, and BroadcastEvent

name: Web Story AMP Validity Requirement
then_action: Then the Web Story must be valid AMP and include all required metadata fields: publisher-logo-src, poster-portrait-src, title, and publisher — otherwise it will not be eligible for rich Web Story experiences
if_condition: If a Web Story is created and intended to appear on Google Search or Discover feed
source_title: Web Stories – Enable, Best Practices, and Content Policy

name: Rich Results Require Structured Data
then_action: Then it becomes eligible for rich results in Google Search, which display additional visual elements beyond the standard text result format
if_condition: If a webpage includes structured data markup
source_title: Visual Elements Gallery – Google Search Result Building Blocks

name: Search Result Visual Appearance Varies by Context
then_action: Then they should account for the fact that visual elements can look different depending on device type (desktop vs mobile), country, and language of the search query
if_condition: If a site owner is optimising for a specific visual element appearance in Google Search results
source_title: Visual Elements Gallery – Google Search Result Building Blocks

name: ISO 8601 Format Required for Duration Property
then_action: Then the duration value will not be correctly parsed by Google, potentially causing structured data errors and loss of the duration display in rich results
if_condition: If the duration property is included in VideoObject structured data but is not formatted according to ISO 8601 standard
source_title: Video Structured Data – VideoObject, Clip, and BroadcastEvent

name: Text Result Attribution Components
then_action: Then its attribution may display the site name, favicon, and URL as child visual elements alongside the title link and snippet
if_condition: If a page appears as a text result in Google Search
source_title: Visual Elements Gallery – Google Search Result Building Blocks

name: Image and Video Results Require Thumbnail Optimisation
then_action: Then they should optimise for thumbnail display and metadata, as image results show thumbnail images with attribution and video results show thumbnails with additional metadata
if_condition: If a site owner wants their image or video content to appear in Google Search image or video result types
source_title: Visual Elements Gallery – Google Search Result Building Blocks

name: SafeSearch Review Request Timing
then_action: Wait at least two to three months before requesting a review, as classifiers can take up to that long to re-process updated content
if_condition: If changes have been made to a site to correct incorrect SafeSearch flagging
source_title: What to Do If Your Site Is Incorrectly Flagged as Explicit

name: Web Story Egregious Violation Penalty
then_action: Then Google may permanently remove the site from rich Web Story experiences in Google Search
if_condition: If a site repeatedly or severely violates Web Story content policies (e.g., copyright infringement, promotional-only stories, excessive external link dependency)
source_title: Web Stories – Enable, Best Practices, and Content Policy

name: Multilingual Pages Not Treated as Duplicates When Primary Language Differs
then_action: Google does not treat them as duplicates — no canonicalization deduplication occurs across language variants with different primary content languages
if_condition: If different language versions of a page exist and the primary content is in different languages
source_title: What is URL Canonicalization

name: HTTPS Preferred for Canonicalization
then_action: Google uses HTTPS as a positive signal when determining the canonical URL, so ensure HTTPS version is the preferred/canonical version
if_condition: If a site has both HTTP and HTTPS versions of the same page
source_title: What is URL Canonicalization

name: SafeSearch Incorrect Flagging: Page-Level Check
then_action: Search for that specific page in Google Search with SafeSearch enabled and confirm whether the page appears or is filtered
if_condition: If a specific page on a nuanced or suggestive site (e.g. lingerie, massage, sex education) is suspected of being incorrectly flagged by SafeSearch
source_title: What to Do If Your Site Is Incorrectly Flagged as Explicit

name: Sitemap Inclusion Signals Canonical Preference
then_action: Google treats sitemap inclusion as a canonicalization hint, favoring the sitemapped URL as the canonical version
if_condition: If a URL is included in a sitemap while duplicate versions are not
source_title: What is URL Canonicalization

name: Faceted/Filtered URLs Risk Duplicate Content
then_action: Google may identify these as duplicate pages and select one canonical URL, potentially reducing crawl frequency of filtered/sorted variants
if_condition: If a category page generates multiple URLs through sorting or filtering functions
source_title: What is URL Canonicalization

name: rel=canonical Is a Hint Not a Directive
then_action: Treat rel=canonical as a hint Google may or may not follow, not a guaranteed rule — verify canonicalization outcome via Google Search Console
if_condition: If a rel=canonical tag is added to a page to indicate the preferred URL
source_title: What is URL Canonicalization

name: Explicit Classification Blocks Rich Results and Featured Snippets
then_action: That page is not eligible for rich results, featured snippets, or video previews regardless of the user's SafeSearch settings
if_condition: If a page or site is classified as explicit by SafeSearch
source_title: What to Do If Your Site Is Incorrectly Flagged as Explicit

name: Canonical Pages Receive Higher Crawl Frequency
then_action: That canonical URL will be crawled more regularly while duplicate pages are crawled less frequently — prioritize canonical signal accuracy to ensure important pages are crawled
if_condition: If Google designates a URL as the canonical page among a set of duplicates
source_title: What is URL Canonicalization

name: SafeSearch Incorrect Flagging: Site-Level Check
then_action: Use the site: search operator in Google Search with SafeSearch set to Filter to identify how broadly the site is affected
if_condition: If the scope of SafeSearch incorrect flagging needs to be assessed across an entire site
source_title: What to Do If Your Site Is Incorrectly Flagged as Explicit

name: Significant Explicit Content Triggers Whole-Site Classification
then_action: The entire site may be classified as explicit by SafeSearch, not just the individual pages containing that content
if_condition: If a website contains a significant amount of nudity, sexually explicit content (including computer-generated), or graphic violence
source_title: What to Do If Your Site Is Incorrectly Flagged as Explicit

name: Blurred Explicit Images Still Classified as Explicit
then_action: The page may still be classified as explicit by SafeSearch despite the blurring
if_condition: If explicit images on a page are blurred but can be unblurred by the user, or the page links to an unblurred version
source_title: What to Do If Your Site Is Incorrectly Flagged as Explicit

name: Product Catalogue Descriptions Drive Broad Search Visibility
then_action: Then the product catalogue gains broad search visibility across Google surfaces
if_condition: If an ecommerce site publishes product descriptions that match the search terms shoppers use when searching for products
source_title: Where Ecommerce Content Can Appear on Google

name: Google Shopping Tab Requires Merchant Center
then_action: Then the site must participate in Google Merchant Center, as the Shopping tab specifically requires it
if_condition: If an ecommerce site wants its products to appear on the Google Shopping tab
source_title: Where Ecommerce Content Can Appear on Google

name: IndexNow Submission for Fresh Content AI Citation
then_action: Implement IndexNow protocol and submit URLs immediately on publication or meaningful content change — do not wait for standard crawl cycles — to accelerate Bing indexing and improve speed of citation by Microsoft Copilot and ChatGPT Browse mode
if_condition: If a website publishes or updates time-sensitive content (news, product pricing, regulatory updates, seasonal information) where freshness determines citation eligibility in AI answer engines
source_title: Bing IndexNow Real Time Indexing for AI Visibility

name: XML Sitemap lastmod Accuracy for AI Discoverability
then_action: Update lastmod values to accurately reflect the last meaningful content change, and ensure the sitemap is referenced in robots.txt to support automatic discovery alongside real-time IndexNow submissions
if_condition: If an XML sitemap exists and includes lastmod values that do not reflect the most recent substantive content update date
source_title: Bing IndexNow Real Time Indexing for AI Visibility

name: Use Structured Data for Star Ratings on Product Pages
then_action: Mark up the content with Review and AggregateRating structured data to enable rich results eligibility in Google Search
if_condition: If genuine star ratings and actual user reviews are displayed on a product page
source_title: Write High Quality Reviews for Ecommerce

name: Avoid IndexNow Submission for Cosmetic-Only Changes
then_action: Do not submit the URL via IndexNow — only signal genuine content changes to avoid wasting crawl budget and misusing the protocol
if_condition: If the only changes made to a URL are cosmetic (reformatting, design updates, metadata changes) with no substantive content update
source_title: Bing IndexNow Real Time Indexing for AI Visibility

name: IndexNow Does Not Affect Google Indexing
then_action: Recognize that IndexNow only notifies Bing, Yandex, Naver, and Seznam — Google does not participate; use Google Search Console URL Inspection 'Request Indexing' feature separately for Google-specific fast indexing
if_condition: If a site operator submits URLs via IndexNow expecting faster Google indexing
source_title: Bing IndexNow Real Time Indexing for AI Visibility

name: IndexNow API Key Hosting Requirement
then_action: Generate an IndexNow API key and host the key file at https://yourdomain.com/{key}.txt before making any API submissions, then POST updated URLs to https://api.indexnow.org/indexnow with host, key, and urlList parameters
if_condition: If implementing IndexNow protocol for a domain
source_title: Bing IndexNow Real Time Indexing for AI Visibility

name: Self-Contained Block Structure for Copilot Citation
then_action: Structure content in self-contained blocks where each block addresses exactly one specific question or topic and is independently understandable without surrounding context; keep blocks to 150-200 words maximum
if_condition: If content is being structured for Bing Copilot or ChatGPT citation and grounding
source_title: Bing Copilot Content Structure for AI Grounding

name: Descriptive Heading Labels Required for Block Extraction
then_action: Replace generic headings with descriptive, specific labels that reflect the exact content of the block (e.g. 'How to Set Up IndexNow', 'What is the IndexNow rate limit?'); use question-style H2/H3 headings that reflect actual user queries
if_condition: If headings use generic labels such as 'Introduction', 'Overview', or 'More Info' on content targeting Copilot visibility
source_title: Bing Copilot Content Structure for AI Grounding

name: Use data-snippet Attribute for Granular Copilot Grounding Control
then_action: Add a data-snippet attribute (data-snippet="text") to the relevant HTML element containing the preferred grounding text; this enables granular control over which content Copilot cites
if_condition: If a publisher wants to specify exactly which text Bing displays or cites from a page in Copilot responses
source_title: Bing Copilot Content Structure for AI Grounding

name: Answer-First Block Formatting for AI Grounding
then_action: Place the key answer or fact at the very start of the block; do not bury conclusions after lengthy preamble; include specific data points such as numbers, dates, percentages, version numbers, prices, or measurements within every block
if_condition: If writing a content block intended for AI grounding by Copilot or ChatGPT
source_title: Bing Copilot Content Structure for AI Grounding

name: Critical Content Must Be in Crawlable HTML
then_action: Move all critical information into crawlable HTML text; content not in HTML is invisible to Bing's crawler and therefore excluded from Copilot grounding and citation
if_condition: If key facts, data, or answers are placed inside images (JPEG, PNG, SVG), infographics, rendered charts, or PDFs
source_title: Bing Copilot Content Structure for AI Grounding

name: Consistent Entity Terminology for Copilot Entity Resolution
then_action: Standardise terminology for all products, concepts, and entities throughout the content; inconsistent naming confuses Copilot's entity resolution and reduces citation accuracy
if_condition: If product names, concepts, or entities are referred to using inconsistent terminology across content blocks targeting Copilot
source_title: Bing Copilot Content Structure for AI Grounding

name: Avoid Unverifiable Promotional Language in AI-Targeted Content
then_action: Remove or replace unverifiable promotional language with specific, factual claims; Copilot will not ground responses in non-verifiable promotional assertions
if_condition: If content intended for Copilot grounding contains vague promotional phrases such as 'industry-leading', 'best-in-class', or 'cutting edge'
source_title: Bing Copilot Content Structure for AI Grounding

name: Single-Intent URLs for Higher Copilot Selection Probability
then_action: Split content into separate single-intent pages; single-intent pages have a higher probability of being selected by Copilot for a specific query
if_condition: If a single URL covers multiple distinct user intents or topics
source_title: Bing Copilot Content Structure for AI Grounding

name: Vague or Implied Claims Excluded from Copilot Grounding
then_action: The content is unsuitable for Copilot grounding — rewrite to state facts directly, include precise data, dates, and measurements to improve citation selection likelihood
