if_condition: If page content uses vague, implied, or unverifiable claims rather than directly stated, independently verifiable facts
source_title: Bing Webmaster Guidelines AI and Copilot Grounding

name: NOARCHIVE Blocks Copilot Citation
then_action: Remove the NOARCHIVE meta tag — it prevents the page's content from being used in Copilot responses and grounding results, making citation impossible regardless of content quality
if_condition: If a page has the NOARCHIVE robots meta tag applied and is intended to appear in Copilot or AI-generated answers
source_title: Bing Webmaster Guidelines AI and Copilot Grounding

name: Bing Index Is Prerequisite for AI Citation
then_action: The page cannot be cited by Microsoft Copilot or ChatGPT Browse mode — register in Bing Webmaster Tools, submit an XML sitemap, and explicitly allow Bingbot in robots.txt
if_condition: If a page is not indexed in Bing (not submitted via sitemap, Bingbot is blocked in robots.txt, or URL Inspection shows no index status)
source_title: Bing Webmaster Guidelines AI and Copilot Grounding

name: IndexNow Reduces Citation Lag for Updated Content
then_action: Submit the URL via IndexNow API immediately upon publishing or updating — this notifies Bing in near real-time and reduces the lag between content changes and AI citation of the updated version
if_condition: If a page is published or updated and near real-time citation of current information in Copilot is required
source_title: Bing Webmaster Guidelines AI and Copilot Grounding

name: JavaScript-Only Content Excluded from Bing Index
then_action: The content will not be crawled or indexed by Bingbot — move all essential content into static HTML so it is accessible without any user interaction or script execution
if_condition: If key page content is hidden behind interactive elements such as accordions, tabs, carousels, or requires JavaScript execution to render
source_title: Bing Webmaster Guidelines AI and Copilot Grounding

name: AI Mode Indexing Eligibility Requirement
then_action: The page must meet the same indexing and snippet eligibility requirements as standard Google Search and AI Overviews — no additional requirements exist beyond standard eligibility
if_condition: If a page is being considered for inclusion in Google AI Mode responses
source_title: Google AI Mode How It Works and Optimisation

name: NOCACHE Degrades Copilot Citation Quality
then_action: Remove NOCACHE — it limits Copilot to using only the URL, title, and snippet, severely reducing citation quality and usefulness in AI-generated answers
if_condition: If a page intended for Copilot citation has the NOCACHE robots meta tag applied
source_title: Bing Webmaster Guidelines AI and Copilot Grounding

name: AI Mode Topic Cluster Coverage
then_action: Build topic clusters that cover the entire decision-making or research path across multiple related subtopics, not just individual keywords, since AI Mode fan-out queries span multiple related subtopics simultaneously
if_condition: If a site is targeting visibility in Google AI Mode for research or decision-making queries
source_title: Google AI Mode How It Works and Optimisation

name: Structured Data Aids Copilot Content Understanding
then_action: Implement the corresponding JSON-LD structured data schema — this aids Copilot's understanding of content structure and improves selection probability in the AI grounding stage
if_condition: If a page covers Organization, Person, FAQ, HowTo, Article, or Product content and is intended to be cited in Copilot answers
source_title: Bing Webmaster Guidelines AI and Copilot Grounding

name: AI Mode Comparative Content Optimisation
then_action: Include comparative content formats such as X vs Y comparisons, pros and cons breakdowns, and decision frameworks, as AI Mode is specifically designed for users comparing options
if_condition: If content is intended to appear in AI Mode responses for users evaluating options or trade-offs
source_title: Google AI Mode How It Works and Optimisation

name: AI Overviews Snippet Eligibility Requirement
then_action: The page is excluded from AI Overviews selection — remove these directives from pages targeted for AI Overviews appearance
if_condition: If a page has nosnippet, max-snippet:0, or data-nosnippet directives applied to content intended for AI Overviews inclusion
source_title: Google AI Overviews Eligibility and Technical Requirements

name: AI Mode Entity Naming Consistency
then_action: Use consistent entity naming throughout all content so AI Mode systems can unambiguously identify and correctly attribute information to the right entities
if_condition: If content references entities (products, brands, concepts, people) across multiple pages or sections targeting AI Mode
source_title: Google AI Mode How It Works and Optimisation

name: AI Mode Long-Tail Query Targeting
then_action: Avoid targeting only short head queries; AI Mode is most active for longer, complex queries that previously required multiple searches, so content should address multi-part and nuanced questions
if_condition: If an SEO or AEO strategy focuses exclusively on short, high-volume head queries to capture AI Mode visibility
source_title: Google AI Mode How It Works and Optimisation

name: AI Mode Factual Evidence Requirement
then_action: Provide supporting evidence including data, statistics, and specific examples, as AI Mode favours verifiable and factual content when synthesising information across multiple sources
if_condition: If content is being optimised for AI Mode citation and synthesis across sources
source_title: Google AI Mode How It Works and Optimisation

name: AI Overviews Indexing Requirement
then_action: The page cannot appear in AI Overviews or AI Mode — ensure the page is fully indexed and free of noindex directives
if_condition: If a page is not indexed by Google or has noindex directives applied
source_title: Google AI Overviews Eligibility and Technical Requirements

name: AI Mode vs AI Overviews Source Independence Warning
then_action: Do not assume AI Mode and AI Overviews will cite the same pages — they use different underlying models and cited source sets vary independently, requiring separate optimisation consideration
if_condition: If a page is cited by AI Overviews and the site assumes the same page will be cited by AI Mode
source_title: Google AI Mode How It Works and Optimisation

name: AI Mode Search Console Reporting
then_action: Look under the 'Web' search type in Search Console Performance reports, as AI Mode performance is reported under the same category as AI Overviews and classic Search with no separate segment
if_condition: If performance data for AI Mode traffic is being sought in Google Search Console
source_title: Google AI Mode How It Works and Optimisation

name: AI Overviews Visible Text Content Requirement
then_action: The page cannot be selected for AI Overviews — ensure indexable text content is visible and accessible to Googlebot without rendering dependencies or authentication
if_condition: If a page's primary content is hidden behind JavaScript rendering, login walls, or is otherwise inaccessible to the crawler
source_title: Google AI Overviews Eligibility and Technical Requirements

name: AI Overviews HTTP Status Requirement
then_action: The page is ineligible for AI Overviews — fix server errors and ensure the page returns HTTP 200 for Googlebot
if_condition: If a page returns an HTTP status code other than 200 (e.g. 404, 500, 301 without resolution)
source_title: Google AI Overviews Eligibility and Technical Requirements

name: AI Overviews Crawlability Requirement
then_action: The page is ineligible for AI Overviews — remove crawl blocks and ensure Googlebot can fully access and render the page
if_condition: If Googlebot is blocked via robots.txt, CDN configuration, hosting infrastructure, or JavaScript rendering barriers on a page
source_title: Google AI Overviews Eligibility and Technical Requirements

name: AI Overviews Appearance Cannot Be Guaranteed
then_action: Appearance in AI Overviews is still not guaranteed — Google's AI systems select from eligible pages at their discretion, so focus on eligibility rather than assuming inclusion
if_condition: If a page meets all technical eligibility requirements for AI Overviews (indexed, snippet-enabled, crawlable, HTTP 200, visible content)
source_title: Google AI Overviews Eligibility and Technical Requirements

name: AI Overviews No Custom Machine-Readable Files Needed
then_action: These efforts are unnecessary and unrecognised by Google — do not create AI-specific files or markup as Google has confirmed they have no effect on AI Overviews eligibility
if_condition: If a site operator creates new machine-readable files, AI text files, or custom markup specifically to influence AI Overviews inclusion
source_title: Google AI Overviews Eligibility and Technical Requirements

name: AI Overviews Performance Monitoring via Search Console
then_action: Use the Search Console Performance report under the 'Web' search type — AI Overviews data including clicks and impressions is tracked within this report
if_condition: If a site operator wants to monitor AI Overviews clicks and impressions
source_title: Google AI Overviews Eligibility and Technical Requirements

name: Answer-First Structure for AI Overview Citation
then_action: Restructure the page so the most direct answer to the implied query appears in the opening paragraph, as AI systems frequently select the best-answer passage from the first 1–3 paragraphs and will bypass pages that delay the answer
if_condition: If a page buries the key answer beyond the first 1–3 paragraphs or uses a long preamble before stating the main conclusion
source_title: Google AI Overviews Content Optimisation for Citation

name: Structured Content Formats for AI Grounding
then_action: Convert steps to numbered lists, features to bullet points, and comparisons to tables, as structured formats aid AI extraction and grounding when constructing AI Overview responses
if_condition: If content presents steps, feature comparisons, or lists in unformatted prose paragraphs
source_title: Google AI Overviews Content Optimisation for Citation

name: Avoid Hiding Content Behind Interactive Elements
then_action: Move that content into static, visible HTML on the page, as AI crawlers may not expand interactive elements and will fail to index or cite the hidden content
if_condition: If important informational content is placed inside tabs, accordions, or modals that require user interaction to expand
source_title: Google AI Overviews Content Optimisation for Citation

name: AI-Generated Content Must Demonstrate Genuine Expertise
then_action: Revise the content to incorporate demonstrable human expertise and experience, as Google's AI evaluation penalises AI-assisted content that lacks genuine insight and treats AI generation as a replacement rather than a tool for human experts
if_condition: If content was produced primarily through AI generation without layering in original human expertise, first-hand experience, or editorial review
source_title: Google AI Overviews Content Optimisation for Citation

name: E-E-A-T Signals Required for AI Citation Trustworthiness
then_action: Add clear author bylines with links to author pages showing credentials, implement Person schema markup (name, url, sameAs), and include citations to authoritative sources to strengthen E-E-A-T signals and improve AI Overview citation eligibility
if_condition: If article pages lack visible author bylines, author credential pages, publisher transparency information, or links to authoritative external sources
source_title: Google AI Overviews Content Optimisation for Citation

name: Commodity Content Exclusion Risk
then_action: Rewrite the content to include unique value such as proprietary data, expert analysis, or first-hand experience, as Google's AI systems are designed to reward original content and deprioritise commodity content from AI Overview citations
if_condition: If a page duplicates widely available information without adding unique insight, original data, or a distinct perspective
source_title: Google AI Overviews Content Optimisation for Citation

name: Mass-Produced AI Pages Without User Value Trigger Scaled Content Abuse Policy
then_action: Then the content may violate Google's scaled content abuse spam policy and be demoted or removed from Search results by SpamBrain
if_condition: If AI is used to generate many pages at scale without adding distinct value or original insight for users
source_title: Google AI Overviews Generative AI Content Policy

name: AI Content Must Meet E-E-A-T Standards Regardless of Production Method
then_action: Then the content must demonstrate Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness — the same E-E-A-T standards applied to human-written content — regardless of how it was produced
if_condition: If content is generated using AI or automation tools and is intended for Google Search or AI Overviews
source_title: Google AI Overviews Generative AI Content Policy

name: Comprehensive Topic Coverage for Query Fan-Out Matching
then_action: Expand the page to cover related subtopics in discrete self-contained sections, as AI Overviews and AI Mode issue multiple sub-searches (query fan-out) across subtopics — comprehensive coverage increases the probability that at least one sub-query matches and cites your page
if_condition: If a content page covers only the top-level topic without addressing related subtopics, sub-questions, or adjacent aspects of the query space
source_title: Google AI Overviews Content Optimisation for Citation

name: Ecommerce AI-Generated Product Fields Should Be Labeled in Google Merchant Center
then_action: Then label those fields as AI-generated within Google Merchant Center to maintain transparency and comply with Google's product data guidelines
if_condition: If an ecommerce site uses AI to generate product data fields such as titles or descriptions submitted to Google Merchant Center
source_title: Google AI Overviews Generative AI Content Policy

name: Data-Nosnippet for Granular AI Citation Exclusion
then_action: wrap those HTML elements with data-nosnippet="true" attribute to exclude only those sections from AI citation while preserving overall page eligibility
if_condition: if specific sections of a page (e.g., disclaimers, navigation, boilerplate) should be excluded from AI Overview citations while the rest of the page remains eligible
source_title: Google AI Overviews Robots Meta Tag Controls

name: Thin or Derivative AI Content Is Assessed by Core Ranking Systems Like Human Thin Content
then_action: Then it is evaluated by Google's core ranking systems (including the integrated helpful content system as of March 2024) using the same signals as human-generated thin content, resulting in lower rankings
if_condition: If AI-generated content is thin, derivative, or low-effort — lacking original analysis, unique data, or genuine user value
source_title: Google AI Overviews Generative AI Content Policy

name: Course Schema Must Include Name
then_action: Add a name (Text) property representing the course title — it is a MUST requirement for Google Rich Results eligibility
if_condition: If a Course schema markup is implemented on a page without a name property
source_title: Course

name: Nosnippet Removes AI Overview Eligibility
then_action: the page is ineligible for AI Overview citations — remove nosnippet and noindex directives to restore eligibility
if_condition: if a page has 'nosnippet' or 'noindex' robots meta tag applied and the publisher wants it to appear in AI Overviews or AI Mode citations
source_title: Google AI Overviews Robots Meta Tag Controls

name: Noarchive Does Not Suppress AI Overviews
then_action: this approach will not work — noarchive only removes cached copies; AI Overviews use the same snippet preview controls as Search, not cache directives
if_condition: if a publisher applies noarchive to a page with the intent of suppressing its appearance in AI Overviews
source_title: Google AI Overviews Robots Meta Tag Controls

name: Max-Snippet Unlimited for Full AI Citation Access
then_action: set max-snippet to -1 (unlimited) or a high value so AI systems can access the full passage rather than a truncated excerpt
if_condition: if content is intended to be cited in AI Overviews or AI Mode responses
source_title: Google AI Overviews Robots Meta Tag Controls

name: Revenue Per Visitor as Ecommerce Health Indicator
then_action: Then calculate revenue per visitor by dividing total revenue by total visitors during the same period, and optimize site for conversions to increase this metric
if_condition: If assessing overall ecommerce store health including the impact of non-converting visitors on business performance
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 25

name: Max-Snippet Zero Acts as Nosnippet for AI Citation
then_action: treat this as effectively equivalent to nosnippet — the page will be excluded from AI citation; increase max-snippet value or remove restriction
if_condition: if max-snippet:0 is applied to a page intended for AI Overview citation
source_title: Google AI Overviews Robots Meta Tag Controls

name: No Robots Tag Present Allows Full AI Citation
then_action: the page is fully eligible for AI Overview and AI Mode citations with no excerpt length limitations
if_condition: if no robots meta tag is present on a page, or if the page has 'index, follow' with no snippet restrictions
source_title: Google AI Overviews Robots Meta Tag Controls

name: Same Directives Govern Both Search Snippets and AI Overviews
then_action: no AI-specific directives exist — the same nosnippet, data-nosnippet, max-snippet, and noindex tags that control classic Search snippets also control AI Overview and AI Mode citation eligibility
if_condition: if evaluating whether a separate or new robots directive is needed specifically to control AI Overview appearance
source_title: Google AI Overviews Robots Meta Tag Controls

name: Course Schema Must Include Description
then_action: Add a description (Text) property covering course content and learning outcomes — it is a MUST requirement for Google Rich Results eligibility
if_condition: If a Course schema markup is implemented on a page without a description property
source_title: Course

name: Course Schema Must Include Provider
then_action: Add a provider (Organization) property identifying the institution or platform offering the course — it is a MUST requirement for Google Rich Results eligibility
if_condition: If a Course schema markup is implemented on a page without a provider property
source_title: Course

name: Informational Query Type Required for AI Overview Visibility
then_action: Then targeting it for AI Overview visibility will not work, as 99.9% of AI Overview keywords are informational queries
if_condition: If a keyword is non-informational (commercial, transactional, or navigational intent)
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 1

name: Question-Based 'Why' Keywords Maximize AI Overview Trigger Rate
then_action: Then it has a 59.8% probability of triggering an AI Overview, making it a high-priority target for AI visibility strategy
if_condition: If a keyword is phrased as a 'why' question
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 1

name: Long-Tail Keywords (7+ Words) Increase AI Overview Appearance
then_action: Then it has a 46.4% trigger rate for AI Overviews, making it a strategically valuable query length to target for AI Overview inclusion
if_condition: If a target keyword contains 7 or more words
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 1

name: Boolean and Definition Queries Are High-Value AI Overview Targets
then_action: Then it triggers AI Overviews at rates of 57.4% and 47.3% respectively, and should be prioritized in AI Overview content strategy
if_condition: If a keyword is classified as a 'Bool' (yes/no) query or a 'Definition' query
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 1

name: AI Overview Rankings Are Non-Deterministic — Do Not Treat as Fixed
then_action: Then its inclusion should not be treated as stable or permanent, as AI Overview citations are non-deterministic and change with every search refresh
if_condition: If a URL is currently cited in an AI Overview for a specific query
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 1

name: AI Overviews Suppress Traditional Click-Through Rates Significantly
then_action: Then expect CTR drops of 34.5% to 61% on traditional results, and traditional results receiving only 8% of visits — shifting strategy focus from traffic to awareness/citation
if_condition: If AI Overviews are present on a SERP for a target keyword
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 1

name: Fan-Out Query Coverage Increases AI Overview Citation Probability
then_action: The page is 161% more likely to be cited in AI Overviews, with a Spearman correlation of 0.77 between fan-out query ranking and AI Overview citations — prioritize multi-facet topical coverage over single-keyword targeting
if_condition: A page ranks across multiple fan-out sub-queries related to a target topic (not just the primary search term)
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 3

name: Course Schema Should Include Offers for Paid Courses
then_action: Add an offers (Offer) property to the Course schema to expose pricing and enrollment information in Rich Results
if_condition: If a Course has pricing or enrollment requirements defined on the page
source_title: Course

name: Hotel Schema Container Must Use Correct Schema.org Type
then_action: use itemscope itemtype='https://schema.org/Hotel' as the top-level container element before nesting name, description, address, coordinates, and contact properties
if_condition: if adding structured markup for a hotel property on a webpage
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Geo Position

name: Prioritize Brand Search and Fame Engineering for Organic Revenue
then_action: Focus on brand search optimization and 'fame engineering' to increase branded search demand, as brand search is becoming critically important for organic revenue growth and AI citation inclusion
if_condition: If brand search volume is low or brand mentions in AI outputs are absent
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 Why AI Visibility Is the Next SEO Frontier

name: Hotel Offer Must Use LeaseOut businessFunction
then_action: Include the businessFunction property with value 'http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#LeaseOut' within the Offer schema container
if_condition: If implementing Schema.org structured data markup for a hotel room or accommodation offer
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Fees for Optional Services

name: FAQPage Markup Requires Visible Content
then_action: Remove FAQPage markup — Google rejects structured data where marked-up content is not visible to users, and rich result eligibility will be denied
if_condition: If FAQPage structured data is applied to Q&A content that is hidden, dynamically loaded, or not visibly displayed to users on the page
source_title: Schema FAQPage AEO Deep Dive

name: FAQPage Markup Text Must Match Visible Page Content Exactly
then_action: Correct the markup so it exactly matches visible page content — Google rejects FAQPage markup where marked-up text diverges from what users see
if_condition: If the question name or answer text in FAQPage JSON-LD markup differs from the text visibly displayed to users on the same page
source_title: Schema FAQPage AEO Deep Dive

name: Use QAPage Instead of FAQPage for Crowdsourced Q&A
then_action: Use QAPage schema type instead of FAQPage — FAQPage is reserved for editorially controlled single-answer Q&A only
if_condition: If a page allows users to submit their own answers to questions (crowdsourced or community Q&A format)
source_title: Schema FAQPage AEO Deep Dive

name: HowTo Schema Requires Visible Step Content
then_action: Remove the HowTo markup or ensure all marked-up steps are visibly present in the page HTML, as hidden or dynamically loaded step content cannot be validly marked up and may cause rich result ineligibility
if_condition: If HowTo schema markup is applied to steps that are hidden, behind tabs, or dynamically loaded and not rendered in the DOM on page load
source_title: Schema HowTo AEO Application

name: Optimal FAQPage Q&A Pair Count for AEO Signal
then_action: Adjust Q&A count to 3–10 pairs — fewer than 3 provides limited AEO value and fewer citation opportunities; more than 10 may dilute the structured data signal
if_condition: If a page implements FAQPage schema with fewer than 3 or more than 10 Q&A pairs
source_title: Schema FAQPage AEO Deep Dive

name: Write Self-Contained FAQ Answers for AI Citation Eligibility
then_action: Rewrite answer text to be fully self-contained and complete — truncated answers reduce rich result eligibility and lower probability of AI Overview and Perplexity citation
if_condition: If FAQ answer text is truncated, vague, or requires surrounding page context to be understood
source_title: Schema FAQPage AEO Deep Dive

name: HowTo Schema Must Not Be Used for Tips or General Reference Content
then_action: Do not apply HowTo schema markup — HowTo is strictly for sequential instructional processes; use alternative schema types appropriate to the content
if_condition: If a page contains a list of tips, general advice, or evergreen reference information rather than a sequential task-completion process with defined steps
source_title: Schema HowTo AEO Application

name: Align FAQ Question Phrasing with Actual User Query Patterns
then_action: Rephrase question names to match real user query patterns — natural language alignment increases probability of FAQ content matching AI answer engine queries and being cited
if_condition: If FAQ question names are phrased in non-natural language or do not match how users search in Google autocomplete, People Also Ask, or Perplexity query suggestions
source_title: Schema FAQPage AEO Deep Dive

name: Exclude Promotional Content from FAQPage Answer Text
then_action: Remove promotional content from answer text — this violates Google's structured data policies and risks loss of rich result eligibility
if_condition: If answer text within FAQPage markup contains promotional, commercial, or advertising content
source_title: Schema FAQPage AEO Deep Dive

name: Avoid Duplicate FAQPage Questions Across Multiple Pages
then_action: Ensure each FAQ question is unique to its page — duplicating FAQ questions across pages reduces AEO signal quality and may trigger content duplication issues
if_condition: If the same FAQ question text appears in FAQPage markup on more than one page of a site
source_title: Schema FAQPage AEO Deep Dive

name: ItemList Should Include URL for Each Item
then_action: Add a URL property to each itemListElement, as it is required for carousel rich result display
if_condition: If an ItemList schema is implemented without itemListElement.url for each item
source_title: ItemList

name: ItemList Should Declare Total Number of Items
then_action: Add numberOfItems as an integer representing the total count of items in the list to improve schema completeness
if_condition: If an ItemList schema is implemented without a numberOfItems property
source_title: ItemList

name: Intent Dilution Penalty for AI Overviews
then_action: AI Overview coverage percentage is diluted without increasing content selection, harming visibility; restrict content strictly to dominant search intent
if_condition: If a page includes tangentially related topics or excess information beyond the primary search intent
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 2

name: Direct Early Answer Requirement for AI Citations
then_action: LLM semantic understanding is delayed, reducing AI Overview citation likelihood; rewrite to answer the query directly and early in the page
if_condition: If the primary answer to the user query is buried mid-page or after introductory content
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 2

name: Density Over Length for AI Relevance Scoring
then_action: Prioritize topical density and relevance concentration over total word count; being the most relevant source for a specific query outweighs being the longest page
if_condition: If evaluating whether to add more content to a page targeting AI Overviews
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 2

name: Fan-Out Query SERP Coverage for AI Visibility
then_action: Increase frequency of appearing in fan-out query SERPs (sub-queries AI systems use to ground responses), as broader SERP presence across related queries correlates with higher AI citation rates
if_condition: If a page needs to improve its AI Overview visibility beyond on-page optimization
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 2

name: Speakable Markup Directly Influences Google Assistant Voice Responses
then_action: SHOULD implement speakable markup on Article, NewsArticle, and WebPage schema types to signal the highest-value, most answerable passages to AI systems parsing the page
if_condition: If a publisher wants their content selected for Google Assistant voice responses or AI system citation and grounding
source_title: Schema Speakable Voice and AEO Optimisation

name: HowTo Step Text Must Be Complete and Independently Actionable
then_action: Rewrite the step.text as a complete, standalone actionable instruction that makes sense without requiring the user to read surrounding steps, to improve AI extractability and voice search usability
if_condition: If any HowToStep's 'text' field contains a single word, vague phrase, or instruction that requires reading other steps to be understood
source_title: Schema HowTo AEO Application

name: HowTo Step Names Should Be Concise Action Phrases for AI Overview Extraction
then_action: Rewrite step names as concise imperative action phrases (e.g., 'Install the plugin', 'Configure the settings') so they are self-explanatory as standalone numbered list items in AI Overviews and voice search responses
if_condition: If HowToStep 'name' fields are written as questions, descriptions, or non-action phrases rather than concise imperative action phrases
source_title: Schema HowTo AEO Application

name: Speakable Sections Must Use Supported Targeting Mechanisms
then_action: MUST use cssSelector (Text) or xpath (Text) as the only supported targeting mechanisms to identify speakable sections
if_condition: If implementing speakable markup on an Article or WebPage schema type
source_title: Schema Speakable Voice and AEO Optimisation

name: HowTo Schema Should Include totalTime in ISO 8601 Format
then_action: Add totalTime using ISO 8601 duration format (e.g., PT30M for 30 minutes, PT1H30M for 1.5 hours) to provide Google with complete task metadata
if_condition: If a HowTo schema implementation omits the totalTime property or expresses duration in a non-ISO 8601 format
source_title: Schema HowTo AEO Application

name: QAPage requires mainEntity with Question name
then_action: the markup is invalid and will not qualify for Q&A rich results; add a single Question object as mainEntity with a full question text in the name field
if_condition: if a page is marked up with QAPage schema and is missing the mainEntity Question object or the Question's name property
source_title: Schema QAPage AEO Application

name: Complex HowTo Guides Should Use HowToSection for Hierarchical Structure
then_action: Group related steps under HowToSection with a descriptive section name to create hierarchical structure, improving machine readability and AI extraction accuracy for complex multi-phase guides
if_condition: If a HowTo guide contains more than one distinct phase or category of steps that could logically be grouped together
source_title: Schema HowTo AEO Application

name: QAPage must not mark multiple answers as acceptedAnswer
then_action: reduce to a single acceptedAnswer representing the community- or asker-selected best answer; move remaining answers to suggestedAnswer array
if_condition: if a QAPage markup contains more than one answer tagged as acceptedAnswer
source_title: Schema QAPage AEO Application

name: Speakable Content Must Be Publicly Visible and Accessible
then_action: MUST NOT mark those sections as speakable — targeted content must be publicly accessible, visible on the page, and self-contained without requiring visual context
if_condition: If designating speakable sections that reference hidden, dynamically loaded, or visually-dependent content (e.g., content referencing 'the chart below' or 'click the button above')
source_title: Schema Speakable Voice and AEO Optimisation

name: QAPage must not be used when site owner writes all answers
then_action: replace QAPage with FAQPage schema, as QAPage is reserved for community-generated or crowdsourced Q&A content
if_condition: if a page uses QAPage schema but all answers are written by the site owner or editorial team rather than community or crowdsourced contributors
source_title: Schema QAPage AEO Application

name: HowTo Schema Should Include Images Per Step for Rich Result Eligibility
then_action: Add an illustrative screenshot or photograph as an ImageObject to each HowToStep to qualify for visual rich result display in Google Search
if_condition: If a page implements HowTo schema but omits step.image (ImageObject) from individual HowToStep entries
source_title: Schema HowTo AEO Application

name: Speakable Should Target Concise High-Information-Density Sections
then_action: SHOULD target article headlines, lead/summary paragraphs, key facts sections, and definition blocks containing specific factual claims (dates, names, statistics, definitions); AVOID targeting entire article bodies, navigation, footers, cookie banners, promotional CTAs, or related-article sections
if_condition: If selecting which page sections to mark as speakable on an Article, NewsArticle, or WebPage
source_title: Schema Speakable Voice and AEO Optimisation

name: QAPage acceptedAnswer text must not be truncated
then_action: replace with the full, complete answer text to ensure AI answer engines and Google rich results can surface the complete response
if_condition: if the acceptedAnswer.text field in QAPage schema contains a partial or shortened version of the actual answer
source_title: Schema QAPage AEO Application

name: Community Q&A pages with QAPage schema are prioritised by AI answer engines
then_action: the page is more likely to be cited by AI answer engines such as Perplexity as a source, because peer-verified and user-experience content is treated as authoritative by these systems
if_condition: if a page hosts community-generated Q&A content (e.g. forum threads, Stack Overflow-style posts) and is correctly marked up with QAPage schema including acceptedAnswer and upvoteCount
source_title: Schema QAPage AEO Application

name: QAPage should include upvoteCount, answerCount, author, and dateCreated
then_action: add these SHOULD-level properties to strengthen markup quality, improve eligibility for rich results, and increase likelihood of AI engine citation as peer-verified content
if_condition: if a QAPage schema implementation is missing upvoteCount on answers, answerCount on the Question, author, or dateCreated on the Question or answers
source_title: Schema QAPage AEO Application

name: QAPage must represent a single question thread
then_action: remove QAPage markup from that page; QAPage is only appropriate for single-question thread pages where one main question is answered by multiple contributors
if_condition: if QAPage schema is applied to a category page, listing page, or a page containing multiple unrelated question threads
source_title: Schema QAPage AEO Application

name: AggregateRating Must Have ratingValue and Count
then_action: MUST provide ratingValue (Number or Text) AND at least one of ratingCount or reviewCount (Integer); missing either will result in invalid structured data
if_condition: If AggregateRating schema is implemented on a page
source_title: AggregateRating

name: Speakable Implementation Should Be Validated via Voice Playback
then_action: SHOULD validate implementation by testing how Google Assistant reads the page aloud via Google Home or a mobile device to confirm correct section targeting
if_condition: If speakable markup has been implemented using cssSelector, xpath, or URL fragment reference on a page
source_title: Schema Speakable Voice and AEO Optimisation

name: Action Must Include Target Property
then_action: MUST provide the 'target' property with a valid EntryPoint or URL value — this is the required entry point for the action
if_condition: If an Action or any of its subtypes (ConsumeAction, TradeAction, InteractAction, SearchAction, CreateAction) is implemented in schema markup
source_title: Action

name: AggregateOffer Must Include lowPrice
then_action: Then lowPrice (Number) MUST be provided representing the lowest price across all included offers
if_condition: If schema markup uses AggregateOffer type to represent multiple offers for a product
source_title: AggregateOffer

name: AggregateOffer Must Include priceCurrency
then_action: Then priceCurrency (Text) MUST be provided as a valid ISO 4217 currency code
if_condition: If schema markup uses AggregateOffer type to represent multiple offers for a product
source_title: AggregateOffer

name: AggregateOffer Should Include Individual Offer Objects When Seller Details Matter
then_action: Then full Offer objects SHOULD be included within the offers property array of the AggregateOffer
if_condition: If using AggregateOffer and individual seller details (e.g. seller name, seller-specific price) are important to communicate
source_title: AggregateOffer

name: AggregateOffer Should Include highPrice and offerCount
then_action: Then highPrice (Number) and offerCount (Integer) SHOULD be provided to fully express the price range and total number of available offers
if_condition: If schema markup uses AggregateOffer type and multiple seller offers are being aggregated
source_title: AggregateOffer

name: AggregateRating ratingValue Must Fall Within worstRating–bestRating Range
then_action: Flag as invalid schema — ratingValue must always be within the worstRating–bestRating range; an out-of-range value will cause structured data errors and likely suppress rich results
if_condition: If bestRating is set lower than ratingValue, or ratingValue falls outside the declared worstRating–bestRating range
source_title: AggregateRating

name: Use SearchAction as potentialAction on WebSite for Sitelinks Searchbox
then_action: Add a SearchAction nested as a potentialAction property on the WebSite schema entity, with a valid target/EntryPoint defined
if_condition: If a website wants to enable Sitelinks Searchbox in Google Search results
source_title: Action

name: AggregateOffer Attached to Product via offers Property
then_action: Then AggregateOffer MUST be attached to the Product using the offers property, identical to how a regular Offer is attached
if_condition: If a Product schema entity needs to reference an AggregateOffer
source_title: AggregateOffer

name: Use Most Specific Action Subtype Available
then_action: Use the most specific action subtype rather than the bare Action type to improve structured data precision and eligibility for rich features
if_condition: If an action being marked up matches a known schema.org Action subtype (e.g., ConsumeAction, TradeAction, InteractAction, SearchAction, CreateAction)
source_title: Action

name: AggregateRating Fabricated or Inflated Ratings Penalty Risk
then_action: MUST NOT fabricate or artificially inflate ratings — Google may penalise pages with suspicious rating patterns, removing rich result eligibility
if_condition: If ratingValue or ratingCount appears artificially inflated or statistically suspicious (e.g. perfect 5.0 across thousands of reviews with no variance)
source_title: AggregateRating

name: Action Should Include actionStatus for State Clarity
then_action: SHOULD provide the 'actionStatus' property using a valid ActionStatusType value (ActiveActionStatus, CompletedActionStatus, FailedActionStatus, PotentialActionStatus) to communicate the action's current state
if_condition: If an Action schema entity is implemented and the current state of the action is known (active, completed, failed, or potential)
source_title: Action

name: AggregateRating Non-Default Scale Requires Explicit bestRating
then_action: MUST set bestRating explicitly to match the actual scale (e.g. bestRating=10 for a 10-point scale); omitting it causes Google to default to 5, misrepresenting scores like 7/10 as 7/5
if_condition: If the rating system uses a scale other than 1–5 (e.g. a 10-point scale) and bestRating is not explicitly declared
source_title: AggregateRating

name: potentialAction Describes Idealized Interaction on a Thing
then_action: Nest the Action inside the 'potentialAction' property of the relevant Thing entity to signal an idealized, available interaction
if_condition: If an Action is intended to describe a possible interaction a user can perform on a specific Thing (page, entity, or object)
source_title: Action

name: AggregateRating Requires Real User Reviews
then_action: MUST NOT use AggregateRating — Google requires the rating to reflect real user feedback; use on pages without genuine reviews risks a manual penalty
if_condition: If a page uses AggregateRating schema but does not have actual user-generated reviews or ratings on the page
source_title: AggregateRating

name: BlogPosting Must Include Headline, DatePublished, and Author
then_action: The schema is non-compliant; add all three required fields to be eligible for Google Rich Results for articles
if_condition: If a BlogPosting schema is implemented on a blog post page without headline, datePublished (ISO 8601), or author (with name and url/sameAs)
source_title: BlogPosting

name: BlogPosting Should Include dateModified, Image, and ArticleBody
then_action: Mark schema as incomplete best-practice implementation; add these SHOULD fields to improve rich result eligibility and signal freshness to Google
if_condition: If a BlogPosting schema is present but missing dateModified, image (>=1200px wide), or articleBody fields
source_title: BlogPosting

name: Book Edition and Illustrator Should Be Credited Separately
then_action: Use 'bookEdition' (Text) for the edition name/number and 'illustrator' (Person) for the illustrator, keeping these separate from the 'author' property
if_condition: If a book has a specific edition designation (e.g. '2nd Edition') or a credited illustrator distinct from the author
source_title: Book

name: Book Schema Must Include Name and Author
then_action: The markup is non-compliant — both 'name' and 'author' are MUST fields required for valid Book rich results eligibility
if_condition: If a Book schema type is implemented without 'name' (book title) or 'author' (Person or Organization) properties
source_title: Book

name: Article Schema: Author URL or sameAs Required for E-E-A-T
then_action: authorship becomes unverifiable, weakening E-E-A-T signals; populate author.url or author.sameAs
if_condition: if Article schema author entity omits both url and sameAs properties
source_title: Article

name: Article Schema: Image Minimum Width for Top Stories
then_action: article is ineligible for Top Stories rich result; provide at least one image at minimum 1200px wide
if_condition: if Article schema is implemented and image provided is less than 1200px wide
source_title: Article

name: Book Schema Should Include ISBN for Identification
then_action: Provide 'isbn' property using ISBN-13 format (preferred over ISBN-10) to enable precise book identification in search
if_condition: If a Book schema is being marked up for a published book with a known ISBN
source_title: Book

name: Audiobook Editions Require Dual Subtype Declaration
then_action: Use the Audiobook schema type (subtype of both Book and AudioObject) rather than Book alone, to correctly represent audio format properties
if_condition: If marking up an audiobook edition of a book
source_title: Book

name: Article Schema: Speakable Property for AEO in Google Assistant
then_action: add the speakable property to identify those content sections, enabling AEO eligibility in Google Assistant responses
if_condition: if Article content contains sections suitable for audio text-to-speech delivery via Google Assistant
source_title: Article

name: Book Schema Should Include Offers for Purchase Eligibility
then_action: Include 'offers' (Offer) with current price and availability to maximize rich result eligibility and enable shopping features in search
if_condition: If a book is available for purchase and Book schema markup is implemented
source_title: Book

name: Book Schema Should Include AggregateRating for Review Display
then_action: Include 'aggregateRating' (AggregateRating) property to enable star rating display in Google Rich Results
if_condition: If a book page aggregates user or critic reviews with a star rating
source_title: Book

name: Article Schema: NewsArticle Subtype for Top Stories Carousel
then_action: use NewsArticle subtype instead of base Article type to qualify for the Top Stories carousel
if_condition: if the publishing entity is a news publisher targeting Top Stories carousel eligibility
source_title: Article

name: Article Schema: Required Date Fields in ISO 8601
then_action: schema is non-compliant; provide datePublished as Date or DateTime and dateModified as DateTime both in ISO 8601 format
if_condition: if Article schema is implemented and either datePublished or dateModified is missing or not in ISO 8601 format
source_title: Article

name: Use LiveBlogPosting Instead of BlogPosting for Real-Time Content
then_action: Replace BlogPosting schema with LiveBlogPosting subtype to correctly signal real-time updating content to search engines
if_condition: If a blog post page is being updated in real time (e.g., live sports commentary, breaking news live blog)
source_title: BlogPosting

name: Article Schema: Publisher Organization with Logo Recommended
then_action: rich result quality and brand trust signals are reduced; add publisher Organization with name and logo ImageObject
if_condition: if Article schema omits the publisher property or publisher lacks a logo ImageObject
source_title: Article

name: BlogPosting Must Use enclosingBlog to Connect to Parent Blog
then_action: Use the enclosingBlog property to link the BlogPosting to its parent Blog object, establishing the hierarchical relationship
if_condition: If a BlogPosting schema is implemented on a page that belongs to a blog with its own Blog schema entity
source_title: BlogPosting

name: BlogPosting Should Include Publisher and Canonical URL
then_action: Add publisher and url fields to improve entity clarity and help Google associate the post with the correct site organization and canonical page
if_condition: If a BlogPosting schema is implemented without a publisher (Organization) or url (canonical permalink) property
source_title: BlogPosting

name: Article Schema: Headline Length Limit
then_action: Google may truncate or deprioritise the headline; keep headline under 110 characters
if_condition: if Article schema headline property exceeds 110 characters
source_title: Article

name: BreadcrumbList ListItem Must Have Name Property
then_action: Then each ListItem MUST have a name property (Text) representing the displayed label for that breadcrumb level
if_condition: If a ListItem is included in a BreadcrumbList itemListElement array
source_title: BreadcrumbList

name: BreadcrumbList Non-Final ListItems Must Include Item URL
then_action: Then it MUST include an item property (URL or Thing with @id) representing the URL of that breadcrumb level; only the last ListItem (current page) may omit item if the current page URL is canonical
if_condition: If a ListItem in a BreadcrumbList is not the last item in the breadcrumb trail
source_title: BreadcrumbList

name: Avoid Duplicate BreadcrumbList When WebPage.breadcrumb Reference Exists
then_action: Then avoid including the current page's breadcrumb in a separate standalone BreadcrumbList; reference it directly from WebPage.breadcrumb instead
if_condition: If a WebPage schema is present and already references a BreadcrumbList via the breadcrumb property
source_title: BreadcrumbList

name: Course Schema Should Include CourseInstance for Scheduled Runs
then_action: Add hasCourseInstance (CourseInstance) property to represent each scheduled run, including startDate, endDate, location, and instructor fields
if_condition: If a Course has specific scheduled runs with defined start dates, end dates, locations, or instructors
source_title: Course

name: Avoid Generic CreativeWork When Specific Subtype Exists
then_action: Use the specific subtype instead of the generic CreativeWork type to improve semantic precision and eligibility for rich results
if_condition: If content matches a specific Schema.org subtype such as Article, VideoObject, Recipe, Book, or SoftwareApplication
source_title: CreativeWork

name: Course Schema Should Declare Educational Level
then_action: Add educationalLevel (Text or URL) property to the Course schema with values like 'Beginner' or 'Advanced' to improve search relevance and entity clarity
if_condition: If a Course targets a specific audience skill level such as beginner, intermediate, or advanced
source_title: Course

name: Include dateModified on Frequently Updated Content
then_action: Provide the dateModified property in ISO 8601 DateTime format, as search engines use it to assess content freshness
if_condition: If a CreativeWork entity represents content that is updated regularly (e.g. news articles, product pages, documentation)
source_title: CreativeWork

name: Course Schema Should Include Teaches for Skill Signals
then_action: Add teaches (DefinedTerm or Text) property to the Course schema to signal learning outcomes to search engines and answer engines
if_condition: If a Course page explicitly lists skills or competencies that learners will gain upon completion
source_title: Course

name: Course Schema Should Specify Language of Instruction
then_action: Add inLanguage (Language or Text) property to the Course schema to support language-filtered search queries and international Rich Results
if_condition: If a Course is offered in a specific language or multiple languages are available
source_title: Course

name: BreadcrumbList Must Have itemListElement with ListItem Array
then_action: Then it MUST include an itemListElement property containing an array of ListItem objects, each representing one breadcrumb level
if_condition: If a BreadcrumbList schema is implemented on a page
source_title: BreadcrumbList

name: BreadcrumbList ListItem Must Have Sequential Position Starting at 1
then_action: Then each ListItem MUST have a position property as an integer starting at 1 and incrementing by 1 for each subsequent level; duplicate or non-sequential position values must be avoided
if_condition: If a ListItem is included in a BreadcrumbList itemListElement array
source_title: BreadcrumbList

name: Provide Core Authorship and Publication Metadata
then_action: Include author (Person or Organization), publisher (Person or Organization), datePublished (YYYY-MM-DD), and headline (Text) properties to satisfy standard authorship and publication metadata requirements
if_condition: If implementing Schema.org CreativeWork markup on any content entity
source_title: CreativeWork

name: Express Structural Relationships Using isPartOf and hasPart
then_action: Use isPartOf to reference the parent work and hasPart to reference child works, establishing explicit structural relationships between entities
if_condition: If a CreativeWork is a component of a larger work (e.g. a chapter in a book, an episode in a series) or contains sub-works
source_title: CreativeWork

name: Use speakable Property for Voice Search Optimisation
then_action: Mark those sections using the speakable property to signal their suitability for voice assistants and text-to-speech rendering
if_condition: If a CreativeWork contains sections suitable for text-to-speech consumption or is targeting voice search and AEO use cases
source_title: CreativeWork

name: Dataset Distribution Should Include ContentUrl and EncodingFormat
then_action: Add both 'contentUrl' and 'encodingFormat' to the DataDownload object to fully specify where and how the dataset file can be downloaded
if_condition: If a Dataset schema includes a 'distribution' (DataDownload) property but is missing 'contentUrl' (direct file download link) or 'encodingFormat' (MIME type such as 'text/csv' or 'application/json')
source_title: Dataset

name: Dataset Schema Must Have Name
then_action: Add a 'name' (Text) property providing the dataset title — this is a MUST requirement for Dataset schema eligibility in Google Dataset Search
if_condition: If a Dataset schema markup is implemented without a 'name' property (dataset title)
source_title: Dataset

name: Provide Language Tag via inLanguage
then_action: Include the inLanguage property using a valid BCP 47 language tag (e.g. 'en', 'fr') to enable correct language targeting and indexing
if_condition: If a CreativeWork is published in a specific language or targets a specific locale
source_title: CreativeWork

name: Provide aggregateRating When User Ratings Exist
then_action: Include the aggregateRating property with a valid AggregateRating object to enable star rating rich results in search
if_condition: If a CreativeWork has collected user ratings or reviews that can be aggregated
source_title: CreativeWork

name: Continuously Updated Datasets Should Use DataFeed Subtype
then_action: Use the 'DataFeed' schema type (a subtype of Dataset) instead of 'Dataset' to accurately represent the continuously updated nature of the data
if_condition: If a dataset is continuously updated as a live or streaming data feed rather than a static or periodically updated file
source_title: Dataset

name: Dataset Schema Must Have Description
then_action: Add a 'description' (Text) property that clearly describes the dataset contents, methodology, and intended use — this is a MUST requirement for Dataset schema
if_condition: If a Dataset schema markup is implemented without a 'description' property covering contents, methodology, and intended use
source_title: Dataset

name: Dataset Schema Should Include License
then_action: Add a 'license' (URL or CreativeWork) property to communicate usage rights — this improves discoverability and compliance signaling in Google Dataset Search
if_condition: If a Dataset schema markup is implemented without a 'license' property specifying the data license (e.g., a Creative Commons URL)
source_title: Dataset

name: Dataset Schema Should Include Temporal and Spatial Coverage
then_action: Add 'temporalCoverage' (Text or DateTime) to indicate the time period the data covers and 'spatialCoverage' (Place) to indicate the geographic area, improving dataset discoverability and relevance signals
if_condition: If a Dataset schema is implemented for geographically or temporally bounded data but is missing 'temporalCoverage' or 'spatialCoverage' properties
source_title: Dataset

name: Dataset Schema Should Specify Creator and Publication Dates
then_action: Add 'creator' (Person or Organization), 'datePublished' (Date), and 'dateModified' (DateTime) to provide provenance and freshness signals that improve trust and ranking in Google Dataset Search
if_condition: If a Dataset schema is implemented without 'creator', 'datePublished', or 'dateModified' properties
source_title: Dataset

name: SearchAction URL Template Must Contain search_term_string Variable
then_action: Then urlTemplate must contain the {search_term_string} variable, otherwise the SearchAction markup will be invalid
if_condition: If EntryPoint is used as part of a SearchAction for Sitelinks Searchbox
source_title: EntryPoint

name: EntryPoint Should Specify actionPlatform
then_action: Then provide actionPlatform (URL or Text) indicating valid platforms such as 'http://schema.org/DesktopWebPlatform' or 'http://schema.org/IOSPlatform'
if_condition: If an EntryPoint schema is defined and the action is platform-specific (e.g. desktop, iOS)
source_title: EntryPoint

name: EntryPoint Should Provide urlTemplate
then_action: Then provide urlTemplate (Text) specifying the URL or URL template for invoking the action
if_condition: If an EntryPoint schema is defined as the value of target on an Action object
source_title: EntryPoint

name: API EntryPoint Should Specify HTTP Method
then_action: Then provide httpMethod (Text) specifying either GET or POST to indicate how the API entry point is invoked
if_condition: If an EntryPoint is used to describe an API endpoint
source_title: EntryPoint

name: Event Schema: Cancelled or Rescheduled Event Must Declare Status
then_action: Add eventStatus with value EventCancelled or EventRescheduled immediately — omitting it misleads users and search engines about the event's actual status
if_condition: If an event has been cancelled or rescheduled and eventStatus is omitted from the Event schema
source_title: Event

name: Event Schema: Online Events Must Use VirtualLocation
then_action: Set location to a VirtualLocation object with a url property — do not use Place or PostalAddress for purely virtual events
if_condition: If an event takes place fully online with no physical venue
source_title: Event

name: Link WebPage mainEntity to Primary Described Thing
then_action: Set the mainEntity property on the WebPage to point to the primary Thing described by that page to reinforce entity association
if_condition: If a WebPage schema entity is used and the page has a single primary subject (e.g. a product, person, or article)
source_title: CreativeWork

name: HowTo Schema: Required Fields for Rich Results Eligibility
then_action: Then the markup does not meet minimum requirements for Google HowTo Rich Results and will not be eligible to display step titles, images, or duration in search results
if_condition: If a HowTo schema markup is implemented but is missing either the 'name' (Text) field or at least one 'step' (HowToStep) with 'name' and 'text' properties
source_title: HowTo

name: Event Schema: Required Fields
then_action: The Event markup is invalid for Google Rich Results — all three fields (name, startDate in ISO 8601, and location as Place/PostalAddress/VirtualLocation) are mandatory
if_condition: If an Event schema is implemented without name, startDate, or location properties
source_title: Event

name: Event Schema: Rescheduled Events Must Preserve Previous Start Date
then_action: Add previousStartDate (Date) to the Event schema to record the original date, and update startDate to the new date with eventStatus set to EventRescheduled
if_condition: If an event's startDate has changed from its originally announced date
source_title: Event

name: Event Schema: Avoid Marking Evergreen Content as Event
then_action: Remove the Event schema — Event type is only valid for time-bounded occurrences with a defined startDate; misuse may result in rich result ineligibility
if_condition: If a page describes ongoing, evergreen, or indefinitely available content (e.g. a permanent resource or service) and is being marked up as an Event
source_title: Event

name: Event Schema: Use eventSchedule for Recurring Events Instead of Multiple Objects
then_action: Use the eventSchedule (Schedule) property on a single Event object to represent the recurrence pattern instead of creating individual Event objects for each occurrence
if_condition: If an event recurs on a regular schedule (e.g. weekly class, monthly meetup) and is being represented as many separate Event objects
source_title: Event

name: Event Schema: Recommended Fields for Rich Result Eligibility
then_action: Add the SHOULD-level fields to improve rich result display completeness — endDate, eventStatus, eventAttendanceMode, offers, organizer, image, and description all enhance eligibility and search appearance
if_condition: If an Event schema includes only the three required fields (name, startDate, location) without endDate, eventStatus, eventAttendanceMode, offers, organizer, image, or description
source_title: Event

name: HowTo Schema: Misuse on Non-Instructional Content
then_action: Then the markup violates schema.org restrictions and may be treated as spam or ineligible for rich results by Google
if_condition: If HowTo schema markup is applied to a page whose content does not describe a genuine step-by-step task completion process (e.g. informational articles, listicles, or product pages dressed as guides)
source_title: HowTo

name: FAQPage Answer Text Must Not Be Truncated
then_action: Expand the answer text to include the full answer as shown on the page — truncated answers reduce rich result eligibility and AI extraction quality
if_condition: If an acceptedAnswer text property contains a short or incomplete answer that does not include all relevant information visible on the page
source_title: FAQPage

name: HowTo Schema: Step Image Markup for Enhanced Rich Result Display
then_action: Then Google may display illustrative images alongside each step in rich results, increasing visual prominence in search
if_condition: If a HowTo schema is implemented and each HowToStep includes a 'step.image' property as an ImageObject
source_title: HowTo

name: HowTo Schema: Grouping Steps with HowToSection
then_action: Then group those steps under a HowToSection entity using '@type': 'HowToSection' with a 'name' and a nested 'step' array to provide structured hierarchy in the markup
if_condition: If a HowTo guide contains multiple related steps that logically belong to a distinct phase or section
source_title: HowTo

name: FAQPage mainEntity and acceptedAnswer Required
then_action: The markup is invalid and will not qualify for FAQ rich results or AI answer engine extraction — add mainEntity array with Question objects each containing acceptedAnswer with text property
if_condition: If a page is marked up with @type FAQPage schema but is missing mainEntity Question array or any Question is missing acceptedAnswer with text property
source_title: FAQPage

name: HowTo Schema: Duration Formatting Requirement
then_action: Then the value MUST be expressed in ISO 8601 duration format (e.g. 'PT30M' for 30 minutes, 'PT1H30M' for 1 hour 30 minutes) to be valid and parseable by Google
if_condition: If 'totalTime' is provided in a HowTo schema markup
source_title: HowTo

name: FAQPage Implementation via JSON-LD in Head
then_action: Implement FAQPage schema as a JSON-LD script block in the page head with @type FAQPage, mainEntity array of Question objects each containing name (full question) and acceptedAnswer with text (full answer), then validate using Google Rich Results Test
if_condition: If a page presents one or more editorially controlled frequently asked questions with fixed answers visible to all users
source_title: FAQPage

name: HowTo Schema: Supplementary Tips vs Full Steps
then_action: Then mark those items using 'HowToTip' rather than 'HowToStep' to correctly distinguish supplementary content from actionable steps in the schema
if_condition: If a HowTo guide includes supplementary advice or tips that are not standalone instructional steps
source_title: HowTo

name: FAQPage Must Not Mark Up User-Submitted Q&A
then_action: Replace FAQPage markup with QAPage schema type — FAQPage is only valid for editorially controlled Q&A, not crowdsourced content
if_condition: If a page allows users to submit or vote on answers and is marked up with @type FAQPage
source_title: FAQPage

name: FAQPage Question name Must Be Complete Sentence
then_action: Replace the name value with the full question text as a complete sentence to meet schema requirements and improve rich result eligibility
if_condition: If a Question object in FAQPage schema has a name property that is not a complete question sentence (e.g. truncated, keyword-only, or incomplete phrase)
source_title: FAQPage

name: FAQPage Must Not Contain Promotional Content in Answers
then_action: Remove promotional content from all answer text fields — Google may demote or reject the FAQPage markup entirely if promotional content is detected
if_condition: If any acceptedAnswer text property contains promotional, advertising, or sales-oriented content
source_title: FAQPage

name: FAQPage Must Only Mark Up Visibly Rendered Questions
then_action: Remove those Question entries from the markup — only Q&A pairs visibly present on the page at render time should be included in FAQPage schema
if_condition: If FAQPage schema markup includes Question entries for Q&A content that is hidden, behind a tab, or loaded dynamically and not visible in the initial page render
source_title: FAQPage

name: Place Schema Must Include Name
then_action: Add a name (Text) property — it is a MUST requirement for Place schema markup
if_condition: If a Place schema entity is implemented without a name property
source_title: Place

name: Use LocalBusiness Instead of Bare Place for Commercial Entities
then_action: Use LocalBusiness schema type instead of bare Place, as LocalBusiness is a subtype of both Organization and Place and is more appropriate for commercial entities
if_condition: If the entity being marked up is a commercial establishment (e.g. shop, restaurant, service provider)
source_title: Place

name: Place Schema Should Include GeoCoordinates for Map Placement
then_action: Add geo (GeoCoordinates) with decimal-degree latitude (positive = north) and longitude (positive = east) to enable precise map placement and geographic entity recognition
if_condition: If a Place schema entity is implemented without a geo (GeoCoordinates) property containing latitude and longitude
source_title: Place

name: Place Schema Should Include PostalAddress
then_action: Add a full PostalAddress to the address property to improve local search markup completeness and geographic entity recognition
if_condition: If a Place schema entity is implemented without an address (PostalAddress) property
source_title: Place

name: Place Schema Should Include OpeningHoursSpecification When Applicable
then_action: Add openingHoursSpecification (OpeningHoursSpecification) to communicate operating hours in structured form for local search and maps integration
if_condition: If a Place schema entity represents a location with defined business or operating hours and lacks openingHoursSpecification
source_title: Place

name: FAQPage Required Properties Must Include Full Question and Answer Text
then_action: The structured data is invalid; mainEntity is required and each Question must have a 'name' with full question text and an 'acceptedAnswer' of type Answer with a 'text' property containing the full answer
if_condition: If a FAQPage implementation is missing the mainEntity property, or any Question item lacks a full question text in 'name', or any Answer item lacks full answer text in 'acceptedAnswer.text'
source_title: FAQ Structured Data – FAQPage for Government and Health Sites

name: Primary Page Image Should Set representativeOfPage
then_action: SHOULD set 'representativeOfPage' to Boolean true so Google can identify and prioritize it for image rich results
if_condition: If an ImageObject represents the primary image of the page (e.g., used in primaryImageOfPage)
source_title: ImageObject

name: FAQPage and HowTo Co-occurrence Markup
then_action: Mark up each section with its respective schema type independently — FAQPage for the Q&A section and HowTo for the instructional section — do not combine into a single type
if_condition: If a single page contains both FAQ content sections and HowTo instructional content sections
source_title: FAQPage

name: Use Enumeration Subtypes for Controlled Vocabularies
then_action: Use the specific Enumeration subtype (e.g., ItemAvailability, PaymentMethod, DeliveryMethod, EventStatusType) rather than generic Enumeration or Intangible
if_condition: If schema markup needs to express controlled vocabulary values such as item availability, payment methods, delivery methods, or event status types
source_title: Intangible

name: ImageObject Must Include URL
then_action: MUST provide the 'url' property containing the direct URL of the image file; omitting it makes the markup invalid and disqualifies rich result eligibility
if_condition: If an ImageObject is defined in structured data markup
source_title: ImageObject

name: ImageObject Should Include Licensing Metadata
then_action: SHOULD provide 'license' (URL), 'acquireLicensePage' (URL), 'creditText' (Text), and 'copyrightNotice' (Text) to enable Google Images licensing features and proper attribution display
if_condition: If an ImageObject is defined and the image has a known license or attribution requirement
source_title: ImageObject

name: ImageObject Should Include Dimensions
then_action: SHOULD provide both 'width' and 'height' properties (in pixels as QuantitativeValue or Distance) to support image entity recognition and rich result eligibility
if_condition: If an ImageObject is structured in schema markup
source_title: ImageObject

name: Use Specific Intangible Subtype Instead of Bare Intangible
then_action: Replace bare Intangible with the most specific applicable subtype — Offer for pricing, Reservation for bookings, Service for services, JobPosting for job listings, etc.
if_condition: If schema markup uses bare 'Intangible' type when a more specific subtype (e.g., Offer, Reservation, Service, JobPosting, Ticket, Language, Occupation, Permit, StructuredValue) is applicable
source_title: Intangible

name: Article Image Minimum Width Requirement
then_action: Image MUST be at least 1200px wide (minimum 696px for AMP); aspect ratio should be between 16:9 and 4:3 to qualify for Article rich results
if_condition: If an ImageObject is used as the image property of an Article schema
source_title: ImageObject

name: ImageObject Caption Should Be Provided
then_action: SHOULD include a 'caption' property with descriptive text to improve image entity recognition and contextual understanding by search engines
if_condition: If an ImageObject is used in structured data for any page or entity type
source_title: ImageObject

name: Organization Logo Height Constraint
then_action: Image height MUST NOT exceed 60px and a transparent background is preferred to ensure correct publisher logo display in Google Search
if_condition: If an ImageObject is used as the logo property of an Organization schema
source_title: ImageObject

name: Remote JobPosting Must Use jobLocationType TELECOMMUTE
then_action: Set jobLocationType to 'TELECOMMUTE' and optionally omit or make jobLocation flexible; add applicantLocationRequirements (AdministrativeArea) if geographic restrictions apply to remote eligibility
if_condition: If a job posting is for a fully remote role with no required physical office location
source_title: JobPosting

name: ItemList Must Include itemListElement
then_action: Add itemListElement as a required property containing ListItem, Text, or Thing array to meet schema requirements
if_condition: If an ItemList schema is implemented without an itemListElement property
source_title: ItemList

name: ItemList Must Include Position for Each Item
then_action: Add a 1-based integer position value to each itemListElement to satisfy the MUST requirement
if_condition: If an ItemList schema contains itemListElement entries missing the position property
source_title: ItemList

name: Carousel Eligibility Requires Uniform Item Types
then_action: Standardize all items in the ItemList to the same rich result type to maintain carousel eligibility
if_condition: If an ItemList intended for carousel rich results contains items of mixed rich result types (e.g., Recipe mixed with Article)
source_title: ItemList

name: Carousel Requires Item URL with Own Rich Result Markup
then_action: Ensure each ListItem's item property links to a URL that has its own valid rich result structured data markup
if_condition: If an ItemList is used for carousel rich results and each ListItem's item property does not point to a URL containing its own rich result markup (Article, Recipe, Course, etc.)
source_title: ItemList

name: JobPosting Must Include Core Required Fields
then_action: The JobPosting is non-compliant with Google Jobs rich result requirements and will likely be ineligible for display in Google Jobs search features
if_condition: If a JobPosting schema is implemented without one or more of: title, description, hiringOrganization (with name and sameAs), jobLocation (with PostalAddress), or datePosted in ISO 8601 format
source_title: JobPosting

name: JobPosting Must Not Have Expired validThrough Date
then_action: Remove or update the listing immediately, as Google will remove expired job postings from Google Jobs results
if_condition: If a JobPosting schema has a validThrough date that is earlier than the current date (i.e., the application deadline has passed)
source_title: JobPosting

name: Use ProductGroup Schema for Products with Multiple Variants
then_action: Use the 'ProductGroup' schema type for the parent product, and link each individual variant as a 'Product' via the 'isVariantOf' property rather than marking the entire group as a single Product
if_condition: If a product page represents a single product available in multiple variants such as different sizes or colors
source_title: Product

name: JobPosting Should Include Salary Information
then_action: Add baseSalary with currency, value or minValue/maxValue, and unitText (HOUR, DAY, WEEK, MONTH, or YEAR) to improve rich result completeness and user engagement in Google Jobs
if_condition: If a JobPosting schema is implemented but baseSalary is omitted
source_title: JobPosting

name: ListItem Should Reference Item Entity or URL
then_action: add the item property pointing to the entity or URL that the list item represents to satisfy the SHOULD requirement and enable proper entity linking
if_condition: if a ListItem is used in an ItemList or BreadcrumbList and the item property (Thing or URL) is absent
source_title: ListItem

name: LocalBusiness Schema: Use Most Specific Subtype
then_action: Use the most specific subtype instead of bare LocalBusiness to maximize GEO markup effectiveness and local pack eligibility
if_condition: If a LocalBusiness entity has a more specific applicable subtype available (e.g., Restaurant, Hotel, Dentist, AutoDealer)
source_title: LocalBusiness

name: Direct Apply Signal for JobPosting Application Links
then_action: Set directApply to Boolean true to signal to Google that the application flow is direct, which may improve click-through performance in Google Jobs
if_condition: If the apply button in a JobPosting links directly to an application form (not an intermediate job detail page)
source_title: JobPosting

name: BreadcrumbList Last Item May Omit item Property
then_action: the item property may be omitted since the current page URL is implicit; all other breadcrumb levels should still include item
if_condition: if a ListItem is the final entry in a BreadcrumbList representing the current page
source_title: ListItem

name: JobPosting Should Include Employment Type
then_action: Add employmentType using one of the allowed values: FULL_TIME, PART_TIME, CONTRACTOR, TEMPORARY, INTERN, VOLUNTEER, PER_DIEM, or OTHER to enhance listing completeness in Google Jobs
if_condition: If a JobPosting schema is implemented but employmentType is omitted
source_title: JobPosting

name: ListItem Must Have Sequential Position
then_action: assign sequential integer positions starting at 1 without gaps; missing or skipped position numbers violate the MUST requirement and may break rich result eligibility
if_condition: if a ListItem is defined within an ItemList or BreadcrumbList and position numbers are missing, non-integer, or have gaps in the sequence
source_title: ListItem

name: Carousel ItemList Must Point to Pages With Their Own Structured Data
then_action: add appropriate rich result markup (e.g., Recipe, Movie, Article) to the destination page, as carousel rich results require each linked page to carry its own structured data
if_condition: if a ListItem is used inside a carousel-style ItemList and the ListItem.item URL points to a page lacking its own structured data markup
source_title: ListItem

name: LocalBusiness Schema: Required Fields for Local Search Eligibility
then_action: MUST provide: name (exactly matching Google Business Profile), address (with streetAddress, addressLocality, addressRegion, postalCode, addressCountry), and url (official website URL) — absence of any MUST field risks local pack exclusion
if_condition: If implementing LocalBusiness schema markup on a business page
source_title: LocalBusiness

name: ListItem Should Include Display Name
then_action: add a name property with a human-readable label for the list item to improve rich result rendering and user-facing display
if_condition: if a ListItem is defined and the name property (display label) is absent
source_title: ListItem

name: Product Schema Must Not Be Applied to Category Listing Pages
then_action: Do not apply Product schema markup to that page; Product schema applies only to individual product pages, not category or listing pages
if_condition: If a page displays a list of multiple products (category listing page) and Product schema markup is being considered
source_title: Product

name: GTIN Values Must Pass GS1 Check-Digit Validation
then_action: The GTIN will be rejected by Google; only use all-numeric GTIN values that pass GS1 check-digit validation
if_condition: If a Product schema includes a 'gtin' property with a GTIN-8, GTIN-12, GTIN-13, or GTIN-14 value that does not pass GS1 check-digit validation
source_title: Product

name: LocalBusiness Schema: Opening Hours Format Compliance
then_action: Specify dayOfWeek as an array of Schema.org day URIs, and express opens and closes times in HH:MM 24-hour format to ensure correct parsing by search engines
if_condition: If openingHoursSpecification is included in LocalBusiness schema markup
source_title: LocalBusiness

name: LocalBusiness Schema: GBP Data Consistency Requirement
then_action: Correct the inconsistency immediately — mismatches between schema and GBP reduce local ranking confidence and can suppress knowledge panel display
if_condition: If schema markup fields (name, address, telephone, openingHours) differ from the corresponding Google Business Profile data for the same location
source_title: LocalBusiness

name: Movie Schema: Director and cast markup
then_action: Provide director as a Person entity and actor as a Person array to enable cast and director display in Google Rich Results for movies
if_condition: If a Movie schema is implemented and the page represents a film with known director(s) and cast members
source_title: Movie

name: LocalBusiness Schema: Recommended Signals for Rich Results
