then_action: Add SHOULD fields to strengthen local signals: telephone (E.164 format), openingHoursSpecification (dayOfWeek, opens/closes in HH:MM), geo (GeoCoordinates), aggregateRating, priceRange, and image — each additional SHOULD field increases eligibility for knowledge panel and local pack features
if_condition: If a LocalBusiness schema entity is implemented with only the MUST fields (name, address, url)
source_title: LocalBusiness

name: LocalBusiness Schema: Multi-Location Entity Separation
then_action: Create separate LocalBusiness schema entities per location, each with distinct streetAddress, addressLocality, postalCode, and telephone values
if_condition: If a business operates multiple physical locations under the same brand
source_title: LocalBusiness

name: Movie Schema: Required name property
then_action: Add a name property (Text) containing the film title — this is the only MUST field and its absence invalidates the Movie markup for Google Rich Results
if_condition: If a Movie schema type is implemented on a streaming platform, film database, review site, or cinema chain page without a name property
source_title: Movie

name: Movie Schema: Content rating and language metadata
then_action: Provide contentRating (e.g. 'PG-13', 'R', '12A') and inLanguage (language code) to give search engines accurate audience suitability and language signals
if_condition: If a Movie schema is implemented on a streaming platform or cinema page where content classification and language are known
source_title: Movie

name: Movie Schema: Image and description for rich results
then_action: Provide image as an ImageObject (movie poster or promotional still) and description as Text (plot synopsis) to enhance Google Rich Result appearance with visual and contextual information
if_condition: If a Movie schema is implemented and the page includes a movie poster or promotional image and a plot synopsis
source_title: Movie

name: Movie Schema: AggregateRating for rich result eligibility
then_action: Include aggregateRating (AggregateRating) to surface rating signals in Google search results, increasing rich result visibility
if_condition: If a Movie schema is implemented on a review site or film database page that displays critic or audience ratings
source_title: Movie

name: Movie Schema: Duration in ISO 8601 format
then_action: Correct the duration value to use ISO 8601 Duration format (e.g. PT1H30M for 1 hour 30 minutes) to ensure parsability by Google and other search engines
if_condition: If a Movie schema includes a duration property and the value is not formatted as ISO 8601 (e.g. PT2H15M)
source_title: Movie

name: Movie Schema: Use MovieSeries for film franchises
then_action: Switch to MovieSeries as the parent type to correctly represent the franchise relationship, using individual Movie entities as children
if_condition: If a page represents a film franchise or series of related films (e.g. a franchise hub page) and Movie schema is applied
source_title: Movie

name: MusicRecording Must Include Song Title
then_action: Add the 'name' property with the song title text, as it is a MUST requirement for valid MusicRecording markup and eligibility for Google Rich Results and Knowledge Panel integration
if_condition: If schema.org MusicRecording markup is implemented on a page without a 'name' (Text) property for the song title
source_title: MusicRecording

name: MusicRecording Should Include Artist, Duration, and Album
then_action: Add byArtist (MusicGroup or Person), duration (ISO 8601 format e.g. PT3M45S), and inAlbum (MusicAlbum) properties to improve metadata completeness and eligibility for Google Assistant music answers and rich results
if_condition: If MusicRecording schema markup is present but missing byArtist, duration, or inAlbum properties
source_title: MusicRecording

name: MusicRecording Duration Must Use ISO 8601 Format
then_action: Reformat the duration value to ISO 8601 duration format (e.g. PT3M45S) to ensure correct parsing by search engines and Google Assistant
if_condition: If a MusicRecording schema includes a duration property that is not formatted in ISO 8601 duration format (e.g. plain text like '3:45' instead of 'PT3M45S')
source_title: MusicRecording

name: MusicRecording vs MusicComposition Distinction
then_action: Switch the schema type to MusicRecording and optionally reference the underlying written work using the 'recordingOf' property pointing to a MusicComposition entity, since MusicComposition represents the written work and MusicRecording represents the specific performance
if_condition: If a page describes a specific audio recording or performance of a song and the schema type used is MusicComposition instead of MusicRecording
source_title: MusicRecording

name: Review Schema Must Include reviewRating with ratingValue
then_action: The markup is invalid for Rich Results eligibility; add reviewRating with ratingValue and include bestRating/worstRating if the scale differs from the default 1-5
if_condition: If a Review schema markup is implemented without a reviewRating property containing ratingValue (and bestRating/worstRating when using non-standard scales)
source_title: Review

name: Avoid Price Range String in Offer — Use AggregateOffer Instead
then_action: Then replace the single Offer with AggregateOffer using lowPrice, highPrice, and offerCount fields instead of a price range string
if_condition: If a product is offered at a variable or range price (e.g. '$10-$20') and a single schema.org Offer type is used
source_title: Offer

name: MusicRecording Should Reference Underlying Composition
then_action: Add a 'recordingOf' property linking to the MusicComposition entity to strengthen entity relationship signals and improve knowledge panel integration accuracy
if_condition: If MusicRecording markup is implemented for a track that has a distinct underlying musical composition (e.g. covers, classical recordings, licensed tracks) and the 'recordingOf' property is absent
source_title: MusicRecording

name: Offer MUST Include price, priceCurrency, and availability
then_action: Then price (numeric), priceCurrency (ISO 4217 code), and availability (ItemAvailability enum) MUST all be present or the markup may fail validation and lose rich result eligibility
if_condition: If a schema.org Offer is implemented on a product, service, or event page
source_title: Offer

name: MusicRecording Should Include Genre and Release Date
then_action: Add genre and datePublished fields to enrich the structured data signal, improving eligibility for Google search music metadata display and Google Assistant answers
if_condition: If MusicRecording schema markup is present but lacks 'genre' (Text) and 'datePublished' (Date) properties
source_title: MusicRecording

name: Include shippingDetails and hasMerchantReturnPolicy for Product Rich Results
then_action: Then SHOULD include shippingDetails (OfferShippingDetails) and hasMerchantReturnPolicy (MerchantReturnPolicy) to improve eligibility and data completeness signals
if_condition: If a product page uses schema.org Offer and targets Google Merchant Center or rich result eligibility
source_title: Offer

name: Add Offer.category for Event Ticket Tiers
then_action: Then add each Offer under event.offers and include a category (Text) field naming the ticket tier
if_condition: If an Event schema includes ticketed offers with multiple pricing tiers (e.g. VIP, General Admission)
source_title: Offer

name: Use AggregateOffer When Multiple Sellers Offer the Same Product
then_action: Then use schema.org AggregateOffer (not Offer) with lowPrice, highPrice, and offerCount to accurately represent the pricing landscape
if_condition: If the same product is offered by multiple sellers on a page
source_title: Offer

name: NewsArticle Author E-E-A-T Signal
then_action: add url or sameAs to the author entity to strengthen E-E-A-T signals for Google evaluation
if_condition: if a NewsArticle schema includes an author Person or Organization with only a name property and no url or sameAs
source_title: NewsArticle

name: NewsArticle Image Minimum Width Requirement
then_action: replace the image with one that is at least 1200px wide and declare it using ImageObject with url, width, and height properties
if_condition: if a NewsArticle schema includes an image property where the ImageObject width is less than 1200px
source_title: NewsArticle

name: Include priceValidUntil to Signal Price Guarantee Period
then_action: Then add priceValidUntil (Date) to indicate when the listed price expires, reducing ambiguity and supporting structured data validation
if_condition: If an Offer schema is present and price stability over a defined period is relevant (e.g. sale or promotional pricing)
source_title: Offer

name: NewsArticle Headline Character Limit
then_action: truncate or rewrite the headline to stay under 110 characters to comply with NewsArticle schema requirements
if_condition: if a NewsArticle schema markup includes a headline property exceeding 110 characters
source_title: NewsArticle

name: NewsArticle Publisher Logo Compliance
then_action: resize the logo to a maximum of 60px height and ensure it uses a transparent background to comply with publisher logo requirements
if_condition: if a NewsArticle schema publisher Organization includes a logo ImageObject with height exceeding 60px or a non-transparent background
source_title: NewsArticle

name: Promotional Content Must Not Use NewsArticle Type
then_action: replace NewsArticle schema type with AdvertiserContentArticle to avoid policy violation and potential rich result ineligibility
if_condition: if content is promotional, advertorial, or sponsored and is marked up with NewsArticle schema type
source_title: NewsArticle

name: NewsArticle Requires Both Schema and AMP for Top Stories
then_action: evaluate AMP implementation separately, as both NewsArticle schema markup and AMP technical requirements may be needed to qualify for Top Stories
if_condition: if a news publisher targets Google Top Stories carousel eligibility and has implemented NewsArticle schema markup but has not implemented AMP
source_title: NewsArticle

name: NewsArticle dateModified Must Be Present
then_action: add dateModified in ISO 8601 DateTime format to satisfy mandatory NewsArticle schema requirements
if_condition: if a NewsArticle schema includes datePublished but omits dateModified
source_title: NewsArticle

name: Use LocalBusiness Subtype for Physical Commercial Establishments
then_action: Then use the LocalBusiness subtype instead of the bare Organization type to ensure correct entity classification and eligible rich results
if_condition: If the entity being marked up is a physical commercial establishment such as a shop, restaurant, or retail location
source_title: Organization

name: Include sameAs for Entity Authority and Knowledge Graph Recognition
then_action: Then include sameAs URLs pointing to those authoritative sources to strengthen entity recognition in knowledge graphs and support E-E-A-T signals
if_condition: If Organization schema is implemented and the organization has profiles on Wikipedia, Wikidata, LinkedIn, or other authoritative identity references
source_title: Organization

name: Organization Schema Must Include Name and URL
then_action: Then the markup is non-compliant — add the legal or commonly known name and the official website URL as required fields before publishing
if_condition: If Organization schema markup is implemented without both 'name' (Text) and 'url' (URL) properties present
source_title: Organization

name: Person Affiliation and WorksFor for Authority Signals
then_action: Include affiliation and worksFor properties referencing the associated Organization schema to strengthen authority and E-E-A-T signals
if_condition: If a Person schema represents a professional or expert contributor on a website
source_title: Person

name: Add Logo, Address, and ContactPoint for Rich Result Eligibility
then_action: Then add these SHOULD-level properties to improve entity completeness, increase eligibility for rich results, and enhance local and institutional entity display
if_condition: If Organization schema is implemented but logo (ImageObject or URL), address (PostalAddress), or contactPoint (ContactPoint) properties are missing
source_title: Organization

name: Person Schema Must Include Name
then_action: Add the full name as a Text value to the name property — it is the only MUST-have field for Person schema
if_condition: If Person schema markup is implemented without a name property
source_title: Person

name: Restrict News Publisher Properties to NewsMediaOrganization Subtype
then_action: Then remove those properties or switch to the NewsMediaOrganization subtype, as these properties are only relevant and valid for news publishers
if_condition: If Organization schema includes properties such as correctionsPolicy, ethicsPolicy, or masthead on a non-news publisher entity
source_title: Organization

name: Use department Property to Model Organizational Sub-Units
then_action: Then use the 'department' property on the parent Organization to link to sub-unit entities, enabling accurate hierarchical entity modelling within the knowledge graph
if_condition: If an organization has distinct sub-units or departments that each have their own URLs and need to be represented separately in markup
source_title: Organization

name: Person sameAs for Entity Disambiguation
then_action: Include sameAs URLs pointing to those identity-verified sources to strengthen knowledge graph entity resolution and disambiguation
if_condition: If a Person entity is marked up in schema and has verifiable profiles on Wikipedia, Wikidata, LinkedIn, or ORCID
source_title: Person

name: Person hasCredential for Expertise Documentation
then_action: Add hasCredential property with an EducationalOccupationalCredential value to formally document qualifications and support E-E-A-T
if_condition: If a Person schema represents a credentialed professional (e.g., doctor, lawyer, certified expert) and qualifications are relevant to the content
source_title: Person

name: Person Author Property for E-E-A-T Signal
then_action: Use the author property on the Article/Review/CreativeWork schema to associate the Person entity — this is a key E-E-A-T signal for Google
if_condition: If a Person is the author of an Article, Review, or CreativeWork
source_title: Person

name: Person givenName and familyName for Disambiguation
then_action: Add givenName and familyName as separate Text properties to improve entity disambiguation in the knowledge graph
if_condition: If a Person schema includes only the name property and the individual shares a name with other known entities
source_title: Person

name: Person birthDate and deathDate Format
then_action: Ensure dates are formatted in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) to avoid validation errors and ensure correct parsing
if_condition: If birthDate or deathDate properties are included in Person schema markup
source_title: Person

name: PriceSpecification requires numeric price value
then_action: mark as invalid — 'price' (Number) is a MUST field for PriceSpecification and must be provided
if_condition: if a PriceSpecification entity is defined without a numeric 'price' property
source_title: PriceSpecification

name: PriceSpecification requires ISO 4217 currency code
then_action: mark as invalid — 'priceCurrency' (Text, ISO 4217 code) is a MUST field and must be a valid currency code (e.g. 'USD', 'EUR')
if_condition: if a PriceSpecification entity is defined without a 'priceCurrency' property or uses a non-ISO-4217 currency value
source_title: PriceSpecification

name: PriceSpecification should include validity period
then_action: flag as incomplete — both 'validFrom' and 'validThrough' are SHOULD fields that define the active price window and improve structured data reliability
if_condition: if a PriceSpecification entity is missing 'validFrom' or 'validThrough' DateTime properties
source_title: PriceSpecification

name: PriceSpecification should define price range bounds when applicable
then_action: flag as incomplete — 'minPrice' and 'maxPrice' are SHOULD fields for expressing price ranges and should be populated when price varies
if_condition: if a PriceSpecification represents a variable or range-based price but omits 'minPrice' or 'maxPrice' Number properties
source_title: PriceSpecification

name: UnitPriceSpecification requires referenceQuantity and unitCode for unit pricing
then_action: add 'referenceQuantity' (QuantitativeValue) and 'unitCode' (UN/CEFACT code) properties to correctly express unit-based pricing in structured data
if_condition: if pricing is expressed per unit (e.g. per kilogram, per liter) using UnitPriceSpecification subtype
source_title: PriceSpecification

name: PriceSpecification should specify eligibleQuantity for quantity-gated prices
then_action: flag as incomplete — 'eligibleQuantity' (QuantitativeValue) is a SHOULD field that must be included to accurately represent quantity-conditional pricing
if_condition: if a PriceSpecification applies only above a minimum or below a maximum purchase quantity but omits 'eligibleQuantity'
source_title: PriceSpecification

name: Use correct PriceSpecification subtype for complex pricing scenarios
then_action: use the appropriate PriceSpecification subtype — DeliveryChargeSpecification, PaymentChargeSpecification, CompoundPriceSpecification, or UnitPriceSpecification — rather than the base PriceSpecification type
if_condition: if pricing involves delivery charges, payment surcharges, compound pricing tiers, or unit-based pricing
source_title: PriceSpecification

name: Express Geographic Containment Using containedInPlace and containsPlace
then_action: Use containedInPlace to reference the parent Place and containsPlace to reference child Places to express hierarchical geographic containment in schema markup
if_condition: If a Place entity has a hierarchical geographic relationship with another Place (e.g. a room within a building, a city within a country)
source_title: Place

name: Place Schema Should Include AggregateRating When Reviews Exist
then_action: Add aggregateRating (AggregateRating) to the Place schema to surface review signals in local search and maps results
if_condition: If a Place entity has user reviews or ratings but lacks an aggregateRating property in its schema markup
source_title: Place

name: ProfilePage Image on mainEntity
then_action: Add an ImageObject to the mainEntity representing a photograph (for Person) or logo (for Organization) to complete the structured entity representation
if_condition: If a ProfilePage schema is implemented and the mainEntity (Person or Organization) lacks an image property
source_title: ProfilePage

name: Product Schema Should Include Brand, Image, SKU, and Description for Shopping Graph
then_action: Add the missing SHOULD properties — brand (Brand or Organization), image (ImageObject or URL), sku (merchant-specific identifier), and description (concise text) — to strengthen shopping graph inclusion and improve e-commerce search visibility
if_condition: If a product page uses Product schema but is missing one or more of: 'brand', 'image', 'sku', or 'description' properties
source_title: Product

name: ProfilePage sameAs for E-E-A-T Authority
then_action: Add sameAs array to mainEntity with links to authoritative identity references such as Wikipedia, Wikidata, LinkedIn, or verified social profiles to strengthen E-E-A-T signals and knowledge graph association
if_condition: If a ProfilePage schema is used for an author bio page or company about page and sameAs is missing from the mainEntity
source_title: ProfilePage

name: ProfilePage Should Include dateModified
then_action: Add dateModified (DateTime) to the ProfilePage schema to indicate when the profile was last updated, supporting freshness signals
if_condition: If a ProfilePage schema is present on an author or organization page but dateModified is not declared
source_title: ProfilePage

name: Product Schema: use ProductGroup for multi-variant products
then_action: use 'ProductGroup' as the schema type for the parent product, and link each individual variant as a 'Product' via the 'isVariantOf' property
if_condition: if a product has multiple variants such as different sizes or colors
source_title: Product

name: Product Schema: recommended properties for rich result eligibility
then_action: SHOULD provide: description, image, brand, aggregateRating, gtin, sku, review, color, and material to improve shopping graph inclusion and rich result quality
if_condition: if a page uses Product schema markup and aims to maximise rich result display
source_title: Product

name: ProfilePage Should Include Canonical URL
then_action: Add the url property to the ProfilePage schema with the canonical URL of the profile page to ensure proper page-to-entity association
if_condition: If a ProfilePage schema is implemented without a url property pointing to the canonical URL of the page
source_title: ProfilePage

name: QAPage Must Not Be Used for Site-Owner-Written Answers
then_action: Do not use QAPage schema; use FAQPage schema instead to correctly represent the authoritative answer pattern
if_condition: If all answers on the page are written by the site owner rather than crowdsourced or user-generated
source_title: QAPage

name: Product Schema: name and offers are mandatory
then_action: MUST provide 'name' (Text) and 'offers' (Offer or AggregateOffer); without these the product is ineligible for Google Rich Results and shopping graph inclusion
if_condition: if a webpage uses Product schema markup
source_title: Product

name: ProfilePage Should Include dateModified
then_action: Add dateModified (DateTime) and dateCreated (Date) to the ProfilePage schema to reflect freshness signals for crawlers
if_condition: If a ProfilePage schema is implemented without a dateModified property and the profile content is updated periodically
source_title: ProfilePage

name: ProfilePage mainEntity Should Include Image and Description
then_action: Add image and description to the mainEntity to provide a representative photo or logo and a brief biography, improving entity completeness for knowledge graph association
if_condition: If a ProfilePage mainEntity (Person or Organization) is missing image (ImageObject) or description (Text) properties
source_title: ProfilePage

name: Product Schema: GTIN must pass GS1 check-digit validation
then_action: the GTIN value MUST be all-numeric and pass GS1 check-digit validation; invalid GTINs will be rejected by Google
if_condition: if a Product schema includes a 'gtin' property value
source_title: Product

name: Product Schema Requires Name and Offers for Rich Results
then_action: The product is not eligible for Google product rich results; both 'name' and 'offers' are MUST fields and must be provided to qualify for price, availability, and review display in search
if_condition: If a webpage uses Product schema markup but is missing either the 'name' property or the 'offers' (Offer or AggregateOffer) property
source_title: Product

name: Product Schema: omitting offers blocks rich result eligibility
then_action: the product is NOT eligible for Google product rich results; add an Offer or AggregateOffer with pricing and availability to restore eligibility
if_condition: if a Product schema markup does not include an 'offers' property (Offer or AggregateOffer)
source_title: Product

name: QAPage acceptedAnswer Must Map to Visible Content
then_action: Remove or correct the markup so that only answers fully visible in the page content are marked up; do not mark up hidden or truncated text
if_condition: If a Question object includes an acceptedAnswer or suggestedAnswer that is hidden, truncated, or not rendered in the page content
source_title: QAPage

name: ProfilePage Must Include mainEntity
then_action: Add mainEntity with a Person or Organization type as the primary entity described by the profile page — this is a MUST requirement
if_condition: If a ProfilePage schema is implemented without a mainEntity property (Person or Organization)
source_title: ProfilePage

name: ProfilePage sameAs for E-E-A-T Signals
then_action: Include sameAs on the Person entity with links to authoritative identity references such as Wikipedia, Wikidata, and LinkedIn to strengthen E-E-A-T signals and knowledge graph association
if_condition: If a ProfilePage schema includes a Person as mainEntity and is used as an author bio or professional profile page
source_title: ProfilePage

name: Rating Should Include Author
then_action: Include the author property (Person or Organization) to attribute the rating to its source — improves structured data completeness and credibility signals
if_condition: If a Rating object is used directly on a Review object representing a single reviewer's evaluation
source_title: Rating

name: QAPage Should Include Answer Engagement Signals
then_action: Add upvoteCount (Integer) on each Answer, answerCount (Integer) on the Question, author (Person or Organization) on Question and each Answer, and dateCreated (Date or DateTime) on both Question and Answer objects to improve rich result eligibility and signal quality
if_condition: If a QAPage includes Answer objects but omits upvoteCount, answerCount, author, or dateCreated properties
source_title: QAPage

name: Recipe Schema: Required name Field
then_action: Add the 'name' property with the recipe's title — it is a MUST requirement and its absence will disqualify the markup from rich result eligibility
if_condition: If a Recipe schema markup is implemented without a 'name' (Text) property
source_title: Recipe

name: Rating Must Include ratingValue
then_action: Add ratingValue (as Number or Text) to the Rating object — it is a MUST requirement and its absence makes the rating invalid
if_condition: If a Rating schema object is implemented without a ratingValue property
source_title: Rating

name: Rating Should Include Explanation
then_action: Add ratingExplanation (Text) to provide brief context for the score — improves interpretability for both search engines and users
if_condition: If a Rating object is implemented without a ratingExplanation property
source_title: Rating

name: Rating Scale Ambiguity Risk
then_action: Add explicit bestRating and worstRating properties to define the scale — otherwise the score will be misinterpreted against the default 1–5 scale, causing score inflation or deflation
if_condition: If a Rating object uses a non-default scale (e.g. ratingValue of 7 out of 10) without explicitly declaring bestRating and worstRating
source_title: Rating

name: AggregateRating vs Rating Subtype Selection
then_action: Use AggregateRating subtype instead of base Rating — AggregateRating adds ratingCount and reviewCount properties required for collective evaluations
if_condition: If the rating being marked up represents an aggregation of multiple individual user or reviewer ratings
source_title: Rating

name: Recipe Schema: Required image Field
then_action: Add a valid 'image' property showing the completed recipe — it is a MUST requirement and omitting it disqualifies the page from recipe rich results
if_condition: If a Recipe schema markup is implemented without at least one 'image' (ImageObject or URL) of the finished dish
source_title: Recipe

name: Recipe Schema: Required author Field
then_action: Add the 'author' property identifying the recipe creator — it is a MUST requirement for rich result eligibility
if_condition: If a Recipe schema markup is implemented without an 'author' property (Person or Organization)
source_title: Recipe

name: Recipe Schema: Avoid Single-String Ingredients
then_action: Split each ingredient into its own separate Text value in the recipeIngredient array — combining them into one string violates schema best practices and reduces rich result eligibility
if_condition: If all recipe ingredients are provided as a single concatenated string within the 'recipeIngredient' property
source_title: Recipe

name: Recipe Schema: Avoid Missing recipeInstructions
then_action: Add step-by-step 'recipeInstructions' using HowToStep markup — recipes without instruction markup are less likely to appear in immersive recipe features such as the recipe panel and Google Assistant recipe mode
if_condition: If a Recipe schema markup is present but 'recipeInstructions' (HowToStep array or Text) is omitted
source_title: Recipe

name: Recipe Schema: Recommend aggregateRating for Star Display
then_action: Add 'aggregateRating' with ratingValue and reviewCount — its presence enables star rating display in Google Search rich results, increasing click-through rate
if_condition: If a Recipe schema markup is implemented without an 'aggregateRating' (AggregateRating) property and user reviews exist on the page
source_title: Recipe

name: Recipe Schema: Recommend nutrition with calories
then_action: Add a 'nutrition' object with at minimum a 'calories' (Text) field — this enables calorie display in recipe rich results and improves feature eligibility
if_condition: If a Recipe schema markup is implemented without a 'nutrition' (NutritionInformation) property
source_title: Recipe

name: Recipe Schema: Use suitableForDiet for Dietary Restriction Filtering
then_action: Add the 'suitableForDiet' property using the appropriate RestrictedDiet enum value (e.g. VeganDiet, GlutenFreeDiet) to enable dietary filter eligibility in Google Search and Google Images
if_condition: If a recipe is vegan, vegetarian, gluten-free, or matches another RestrictedDiet enum value and the schema markup does not include 'suitableForDiet'
source_title: Recipe

name: Review Schema Must Include itemReviewed with Type and Name
then_action: The markup is invalid and will not qualify for Google Rich Results review snippets; add itemReviewed with proper @type and name fields
if_condition: If a Review schema markup is implemented without an itemReviewed property that includes both @type and name of the reviewed item
source_title: Review

name: Use Specific Review Subtype When Applicable
then_action: Use the appropriate Review subtype schema (ClaimReview, CriticReview, EmployerReview, or UserReview) instead of the generic Review type to improve semantic accuracy and eligibility for specialised rich result formats
if_condition: If the review being marked up is specifically a fact-check, professional criticism, employer/workplace review, or consumer review rather than a generic review
source_title: Review

name: Review Schema Must Include Author Entity
then_action: The markup is incomplete and will not qualify for Rich Results; add an author field with @type set to Person or Organization and the reviewer's name
if_condition: If a Review schema markup is implemented without an author property specifying a Person or Organization entity
source_title: Review

name: Self-Reviews Violate Google Review Guidelines
then_action: Remove or correct the markup immediately; self-reviews violate Google review guidelines and can result in rich result penalties or manual actions
if_condition: If the author entity in Review schema markup is the same individual or business as the entity being reviewed in itemReviewed
source_title: Review

name: AggregateRating Must Only Include Genuine User Reviews
then_action: Remove non-genuine reviews from the aggregate count and score; including them violates Google guidelines and risks rich result ineligibility
if_condition: If AggregateRating schema aggregates reviews that were not submitted by genuine users (e.g., fabricated, incentivised, or imported from unverified sources)
source_title: Review

name: Missing reviewBody Reduces Rich Result Eligibility
then_action: Add the full text content of the review to reviewBody; reviews without text content are less likely to be displayed as rich results in Google Search
if_condition: If a Review schema markup is present but the reviewBody property is omitted or empty
source_title: Review

name: SearchAction Target URL Must Return Valid Results
then_action: AVOID using that URL pattern; Google validates search functionality and a broken or empty-result URL may disqualify the Sitelinks Searchbox from appearing
if_condition: If the urlTemplate in SearchAction target points to a search endpoint that returns errors or empty results
source_title: SearchAction

name: SearchAction Must Include Target URL Template
then_action: The implementation is invalid; MUST provide a target EntryPoint with a urlTemplate using {search_term_string} as the exact query placeholder
if_condition: If SearchAction schema is implemented without a 'target' EntryPoint containing {search_term_string} in the urlTemplate
source_title: SearchAction

name: SearchAction Must Declare query-input Parameter
then_action: The implementation is invalid; MUST include query-input declaration as 'required name=search_term_string'
if_condition: If SearchAction schema is implemented without a 'query-input' property set to 'required name=search_term_string'
source_title: SearchAction

name: SearchAction Should Be Placed on Homepage WebSite Object
then_action: Reposition SearchAction as a potentialAction on the WebSite JSON-LD block placed on the homepage for proper Sitelinks Searchbox eligibility
if_condition: If SearchAction is implemented as a potentialAction on a page other than the homepage or on a non-WebSite schema object
source_title: SearchAction

name: Leverage AI Citation Hunger for Smaller Sites
then_action: Prioritize AI visibility optimization, as AI platforms actively seek citations and this represents an untapped channel where smaller sites can gain disproportionate visibility compared to traditional search
if_condition: If a site is newer or smaller and struggling to compete in saturated traditional SERPs
source_title: How to Optimize for AI Search Results in 2026 Why AI Visibility Is the Next SEO Frontier

name: Sitelinks Searchbox Display Is Not Guaranteed
then_action: Note that display is at Google's discretion; valid markup is necessary but does not guarantee the feature will be shown
if_condition: If valid SearchAction markup is implemented and the site expects the Sitelinks Searchbox to appear in Google results
source_title: SearchAction

name: AI Overview Content Length Threshold
then_action: Expect diminishing returns for AI citation likelihood; grounding plateaus at ~540 words, so additional content beyond this threshold reduces density without increasing selection probability
if_condition: If a page targeting AI Overview visibility exceeds 2,000 words
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 2

name: SoftwareApplication Must Have Name
then_action: The schema is invalid for Google Rich Results eligibility; add a 'name' (Text) property with the application name
if_condition: If a SoftwareApplication schema is implemented without a 'name' property (the application name)
source_title: SoftwareApplication

name: Use WebApplication Subtype for Browser-Based Apps
then_action: Use the 'WebApplication' schema subtype instead of the generic 'SoftwareApplication' type to accurately represent the app's delivery mechanism
if_condition: If the software application being marked up runs exclusively in a web browser without a native desktop or mobile install
source_title: SoftwareApplication

name: SoftwareApplication Must Have operatingSystem
then_action: The schema is invalid for Google Rich Results eligibility; add an 'operatingSystem' (Text) property indicating all compatible operating systems
if_condition: If a SoftwareApplication schema is implemented without an 'operatingSystem' property specifying compatible OS (e.g. 'Windows 10', 'iOS 15+', 'Android 8.0+', 'macOS')
source_title: SoftwareApplication

name: SoftwareApplication Must Have applicationCategory
then_action: The schema is invalid for Google Rich Results eligibility; add an 'applicationCategory' (Text or URL) property with the appropriate category value
if_condition: If a SoftwareApplication schema is implemented without an 'applicationCategory' property (e.g. 'BusinessApplication', 'GameApplication', 'UtilitiesApplication')
source_title: SoftwareApplication

name: SoftwareApplication Should Include downloadUrl and softwareVersion
then_action: Add 'downloadUrl' (URL) pointing to the download or app store page and 'softwareVersion' (Text) with the current version number to improve search result completeness and user trust signals
if_condition: If a SoftwareApplication schema is implemented and both a direct download or app store link and a current version number are known
source_title: SoftwareApplication

name: SoftwareApplication Should Include aggregateRating
then_action: Add an 'aggregateRating' (AggregateRating) property to enable star rating display in Google Rich Results search snippets
if_condition: If a SoftwareApplication schema is present and user review data (star ratings, review count) is available on the page or platform
source_title: SoftwareApplication

name: SoftwareApplication Free App Pricing Declaration
then_action: Set 'price' to 0 and include 'priceCurrency' within the Offer object to correctly signal free pricing in search results
if_condition: If a SoftwareApplication schema represents a free app and the 'offers' property is being populated
source_title: SoftwareApplication

name: Use MobileApplication Subtype for Mobile-Specific Apps
then_action: Use the 'MobileApplication' schema subtype instead of the generic 'SoftwareApplication' type to accurately represent the app's scope
if_condition: If the software application being marked up is exclusively designed and distributed as a mobile app (iOS or Android only)
source_title: SoftwareApplication

name: SpecialAnnouncement Recommended Fields for Rich Result Quality
then_action: the rich result is technically valid but incomplete; adding category (schema.org COVID-19 URL or descriptive category), newsUpdatesAndGuidelines (URL or WebContent), and publisher (Organization) improves rich result completeness and authority signals
if_condition: if a SpecialAnnouncement schema is present but omits category, newsUpdatesAndGuidelines, or publisher properties
source_title: SpecialAnnouncement

name: SpecialAnnouncement Required Fields
then_action: the markup is non-compliant with schema.org requirements and will not qualify for Google Search rich results for special announcements
if_condition: if a SpecialAnnouncement schema is implemented without one or more of: name, text, datePosted, or announcementLocation
source_title: SpecialAnnouncement

name: TechArticle Must Include Author
then_action: Add an author property typed as Person or Organization — this is a MUST requirement for valid TechArticle markup
if_condition: If a page uses TechArticle schema markup and the author property is missing or not typed as Person or Organization
source_title: TechArticle

name: SpecialAnnouncement Missing Expires Property
then_action: the announcement may remain visible in Google Search results after the information becomes outdated, potentially misleading users; add an 'expires' property to define when the announcement is no longer valid
if_condition: if a SpecialAnnouncement is published without an 'expires' date or datetime value
source_title: SpecialAnnouncement

name: TechArticle Should Include dateModified for Accuracy Signalling
then_action: Add a dateModified (DateTime) property to signal technical accuracy and freshness to search engines — particularly critical for fast-evolving technical content
if_condition: If a TechArticle page has been updated since original publication and the dateModified property is absent from the schema markup
source_title: TechArticle

name: SpecialAnnouncement Use Case Scope
then_action: apply SpecialAnnouncement schema (subtype of CreativeWork) to enable the dedicated Google Search rich result displaying announcement text, date, source, and category directly in search results
if_condition: if a webpage contains a timely public announcement about an emergency situation, public health guidance, government advisory, or significant institutional communication
source_title: SpecialAnnouncement

name: TechArticle Must Include datePublished
then_action: Add a datePublished property (Date or DateTime format) — required for valid TechArticle structured data
if_condition: If a TechArticle schema markup block is present and datePublished is not provided
source_title: TechArticle

name: TechArticle Must Include Headline
then_action: Add a headline (Text) property representing the title of the technical document — this is a MUST requirement
if_condition: If a page is marked up with schema.org TechArticle type and the headline property is absent
source_title: TechArticle

name: TechArticle Should Declare Dependencies
then_action: Add a dependencies (Text) property listing software or hardware prerequisites (e.g. 'Node.js 18+, npm 9+') to improve precision of technical content categorisation
if_condition: If a TechArticle page covers software, programming tutorials, or how-to guides with technical prerequisites and the dependencies property is absent
source_title: TechArticle

name: TechArticle Should Declare Proficiency Level
then_action: Add proficiencyLevel (Text) to indicate expected reader expertise (e.g. 'Beginner', 'Expert'); note this is free-text with no controlled vocabulary enforced by schema.org
if_condition: If a TechArticle schema is implemented and the proficiencyLevel property is not specified
source_title: TechArticle

name: Schema.org Thing: name property required
then_action: add a 'name' (Text) property to the markup, as it is a MUST requirement for all schema.org types inheriting from Thing
if_condition: if a schema.org entity markup is implemented without a 'name' property
source_title: Thing

name: Use APIReference Subtype for Formal API Documentation
then_action: Switch the schema type to APIReference, which is the designated subtype for formal API documentation pages, rather than the generic TechArticle type
if_condition: If a page is a formal API reference documentation page and is currently marked up as TechArticle
source_title: TechArticle

name: Schema.org Thing: url property required
then_action: add a 'url' (URL) property with the canonical URL of the item, as it is a MUST requirement for all schema.org types inheriting from Thing
if_condition: if a schema.org entity markup is implemented without a 'url' property pointing to the canonical URL
source_title: Thing

name: Schema.org Thing: sameAs recommended for entity disambiguation
then_action: add a 'sameAs' (URL) property referencing an authoritative external page that unambiguously identifies the entity, to improve entity disambiguation and knowledge graph association
if_condition: if a schema.org entity markup exists but lacks a 'sameAs' property linking to an authoritative external source (e.g. Wikipedia, Wikidata)
source_title: Thing

name: Schema.org Thing: description recommended for all entity types
then_action: add a concise 'description' (Text or TextObject) property to the markup, as it is a SHOULD requirement inherited by all schema.org types
if_condition: if a schema.org entity markup is present but the 'description' property is missing or empty
source_title: Thing

name: Schema.org Thing: image recommended for rich result eligibility
then_action: add an 'image' property with a representative image to improve eligibility for rich results and visual entity representation
if_condition: if a schema.org entity markup is present but no 'image' property (ImageObject or URL) is included
source_title: Thing

name: VideoObject MUST fields for Google Rich Results
then_action: Then MUST include: name (video title), description (video content description), thumbnailUrl (URL of thumbnail image), and uploadDate (ISO 8601 date of first publication) — omitting any of these disqualifies the markup from Rich Results eligibility
if_condition: If a VideoObject schema is implemented on a page hosting video content intended for Google Rich Results
source_title: VideoObject

name: Schema.org Thing: identifier recommended for well-known entities
then_action: add an 'identifier' property (PropertyValue, Text, or URL) referencing the known identifier to strengthen entity disambiguation and structured data precision
if_condition: if a schema.org entity has a well-known external identifier (e.g. ISBN, GTIN, UUID) but the 'identifier' property is absent from the markup
source_title: Thing

name: Use Most Specific WebPage Subtype
then_action: Use the most specific WebPage subtype available rather than bare WebPage schema markup
if_condition: If a web page has a specific content type matching FAQPage, QAPage, AboutPage, ContactPage, CheckoutPage, or ProfilePage
source_title: WebPage

name: Schema.org Thing: additionalType should be used sparingly
then_action: restrict 'additionalType' usage to URI-identified RDF classes or schema.org-style text labels, and use sparingly to avoid over-typing entities
if_condition: if 'additionalType' is applied to multiple schema.org entities across a page or used with non-URI text values not following schema.org style guidelines
source_title: Thing

name: WebPage Should Declare Speakable Sections
then_action: Provide the 'speakable' property as a SpeakableSpecification or URL identifying those sections
if_condition: If a WebPage contains sections intended or suitable for text-to-speech rendering or voice assistant delivery
source_title: WebPage

name: WebPage Should Include Canonical URL
then_action: Provide the 'url' property containing the canonical URL of the page
if_condition: If implementing WebPage or any WebPage subtype schema markup
source_title: WebPage

name: WebPage Should Include mainEntity Relationship
then_action: Provide the 'mainEntity' property linking to the Thing that is the primary subject of the page
if_condition: If a WebPage schema is implemented and the page has a clearly identifiable primary subject or topic
source_title: WebPage

name: WebPage Should Include Breadcrumb
then_action: Provide the 'breadcrumb' property as a BreadcrumbList or Text representing the navigation path to the page
if_condition: If implementing WebPage schema markup and the page has a navigational hierarchy
source_title: WebPage

name: VideoObject thumbnail must be accurate and high-resolution
then_action: Then the markup violates Google's VideoObject requirements — replace with an accurate, high-resolution thumbnail that genuinely represents the video content
if_condition: If thumbnailUrl in VideoObject schema points to a placeholder image or a low-resolution thumbnail
source_title: VideoObject

name: YMYL Pages Should Include reviewedBy
then_action: Include the 'reviewedBy' property (Organization or Person) and 'lastReviewed' (Date) to identify who reviewed the page for accuracy and when
if_condition: If a WebPage covers YMYL (Your Money or Your Life) content such as health, finance, or safety topics
source_title: WebPage

name: YouTube-hosted videos must use standard embedUrl pattern
then_action: Then embedUrl must follow the pattern https://www.youtube.com/embed/<video_id> to be valid for Google indexing
if_condition: If the video is hosted on YouTube and embedUrl is provided in VideoObject schema
source_title: VideoObject

name: VideoObject requires contentUrl or embedUrl (not both omitted)
then_action: Then Google cannot access the video, disqualifying it from video search indexing — provide at least one of contentUrl (direct video file URL) or embedUrl (player iframe URL)
if_condition: If a VideoObject schema is implemented but both contentUrl and embedUrl are absent
source_title: VideoObject

name: VideoObject regionsAllowed should be omitted for global availability
then_action: Then omit the regionsAllowed property entirely — only include it when the video has specific geographic access restrictions
if_condition: If a video in VideoObject schema is available to all geographic regions without restriction
source_title: VideoObject

name: VideoObject Key Moments via hasPart Clip
then_action: Then implement hasPart with Clip objects, each specifying startOffset, endOffset (in seconds), url (deep-link to that moment), and name (chapter label)
if_condition: If a video has distinct chapters or key moments that should appear as jump links in Google Search results
source_title: VideoObject

name: VideoObject transcript improves indexing
then_action: Then SHOULD include transcript (Text) field with the full video transcript to enable Google to index the video's spoken content and improve discoverability
if_condition: If a VideoObject schema is implemented and the video has spoken content or captions available
source_title: VideoObject

name: WebSite Schema Placement Restriction
then_action: Remove it — WebSite schema must only appear on the homepage as it represents the site as a whole, not individual pages
if_condition: If WebSite schema markup is placed on any page other than the homepage
source_title: WebSite

name: WebSite Schema Required Fields
then_action: Flag as invalid implementation — both 'url' and 'name' are required fields and must be present for compliant WebSite schema
if_condition: If WebSite schema is implemented on the homepage but is missing either the 'url' (canonical homepage URL) or 'name' (site name) properties
source_title: WebSite

name: Sitelinks Searchbox potentialAction Requirement
then_action: Add a 'potentialAction' property of type SearchAction to the WebSite schema, including a 'target.urlTemplate' with a '{search_term_string}' query-input parameter formatted as: target.urlTemplate = 'https://example.com/search?q={search_term_string}' and 'query-input': 'required name=search_term_string'
if_condition: If the goal is to enable Google Sitelinks Searchbox for a site
source_title: WebSite

name: Sitelinks Searchbox Not Guaranteed
then_action: Do not assume Sitelinks Searchbox will appear — Google may suppress it if the site does not meet undisclosed quality thresholds, regardless of markup validity
if_condition: If valid WebSite schema with potentialAction SearchAction is implemented and validated in Google Search Console
source_title: WebSite

name: WebSite vs WebPage Schema Distinction
then_action: Use WebPage schema (or a subtype) instead of WebSite schema — WebSite represents the entire site, WebPage represents individual pages
if_condition: If schema markup is intended to describe a single page rather than the entire website
source_title: WebSite

name: WebSite Schema Language Declaration
then_action: Add the 'inLanguage' property using a BCP 47 language code (e.g. 'en-US') to improve language signal clarity for search engines
if_condition: If a WebSite schema is implemented and the site serves content in a specific language
source_title: WebSite

name: AEO Content Freshness for Time-Sensitive Topics
then_action: SHOULD update existing content regularly, as AI answer engines prioritize newer content for time-sensitive queries
if_condition: If content covers fast-moving topics such as tech, health, or news
source_title: How to Rank on Perplexity AI Embracing the Power of Answer Engines Key Ranking Factors for Perplexit

name: AEO Semantic Relevance Over Keyword Matching
then_action: SHOULD restructure using natural language, related terms, and Q&A formats — Perplexity AI prioritizes semantic relevance based on context and intent, not keyword matching alone
if_condition: If content is structured primarily around keyword repetition rather than contextual language and question-answer formats
source_title: How to Rank on Perplexity AI Embracing the Power of Answer Engines Key Ranking Factors for Perplexit

name: Content Freshness for AI Engine Preference
then_action: Update the content to achieve at least 25.7% greater freshness than the average competing page, as AI engines demonstrably prefer fresher content for citations
if_condition: If existing published content has not been updated and is performing below average in AI engine citations compared to competitor pages
source_title: Answer Engine Optimization Complete AEO Guide 2026 How to Optimize for Answer Engines Step by Step

name: AI Visibility: Open With Direct Answer
then_action: Restructure the page to open with a clear, direct answer to the primary question before elaborating, as AI systems prioritize content that immediately resolves the query
if_condition: If a content page targeting AI citation does not begin with a direct answer to the main question it addresses
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT Building AI Visible

name: AI Visibility: Single Purpose Per Page
then_action: Split the page so each URL has one clear, single purpose, ensuring AI systems can reliably cite it as an authoritative source for a specific query
if_condition: If a content page attempts to cover multiple distinct topics or intents within a single URL
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT Building AI Visible

name: AI Visibility: Question-Based Heading Structure
then_action: Rewrite headings to reflect real user sub-questions, and structure body content to answer each heading directly, improving AI extractability and citation likelihood
if_condition: If headings on a page do not mirror the sub-questions users actually ask about the topic
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT Building AI Visible

name: AI Visibility: Avoid Thin Definitional Content
then_action: Expand the page to include practical examples, evidence, differences from related concepts, tradeoffs, and comprehensive coverage of the topic to qualify as a citable reference source for AI systems
if_condition: If a page only defines a term and lists basic tips without covering full user intent, practical examples, tradeoffs, or contextual depth
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT Building AI Visible

name: AI Visibility: Avoid Frequency-Driven Publishing
then_action: Halt frequency-driven content production and redirect resources toward creating fewer, high-utility pages that AI systems can dependably cite as authoritative answers
if_condition: If content is being created primarily to meet a publication schedule rather than to serve as a reliable reference source
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT Building AI Visible

name: AI Visibility: Reference Document Structure Implementation
then_action: Implement the full 9-step reference document structure (define, explain why, differentiate, detail AI requirements, explain measurement, provide improvement methods, document mistakes, specify metrics, outline operationalization) to maximize AI source utility
if_condition: If a page is being optimized for AI citation and lacks structured coverage of: concept definition, importance, differentiation, what AI systems look for, measurement, improvement methods, common mistakes, and tracking metrics
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT Building AI Visible

name: Single-Keyword Focus Reduces AI Overview Visibility
then_action: Avoid this approach — pages ranking only for the primary search term are significantly less likely to be cited in AI Overviews; expand content to address multiple related aspects of the topic
if_condition: Content strategy over-focuses on individual keywords or isolated citations without covering related sub-topics
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 3

name: High-Growth Customer Acquisition Loss Tolerance
then_action: Then target breaking even on the first purchase (BEROAS threshold) while planning for future purchases to generate positive contribution margin, making upfront acquisition losses acceptable
if_condition: If operating a high-growth ecommerce business and evaluating whether to acquire customers at a loss on the first purchase
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 25

name: Fan-Out Query Gap Analysis for Content Planning
then_action: Map extracted fan-out sub-queries against existing content to identify topic gaps; create or expand content to cover missing sub-query facets and build authority across the full query cluster
if_condition: Existing content pages are not appearing in AI Overview citations and fan-out queries have been extracted for target topics
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 3

name: Use Automated Fan-Out Extraction Pipeline for Content Optimization
then_action: Use Screaming Frog to extract H1s, pass them to Gemini API via Dan Hinkley's Python script or Qforia tool, extract AI Overview responses and fan-out queries, download CSV, then optimize content around the identified sub-queries
if_condition: A site has a crawlable content subfolder (e.g. /blog/) and the goal is to identify fan-out query opportunities at scale
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 3

name: Export Non-Branded AI Overview Keywords to Identify Entity and Topic Associations
then_action: Export non-branded AI Overview keywords and associated Entities from Ahrefs to identify which topics and entities Google associates with the site in AI Overviews, then use this to guide fan-out query optimization priorities
if_condition: A brand has been added to Brand Radar and AI Overview keyword data is available in Ahrefs Organic Keywords report
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 3

name: Meal Information Must Use FoodService Attached to Offer
then_action: Attach meal information using a FoodService element within a TypeAndQuantityNode under the includesObject property of the Offer
if_condition: If a hotel offer includes meal plans (e.g., breakfast included, half-board) as part of the booking
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Fees for Optional Services

name: Internal Linking for Entity Association
then_action: Use the Internal Link Opportunities report in Site Audit to identify mentions of top 10 keywords on other pages and implement ready-made internal link recommendations to strengthen entity associations
if_condition: If pages covering related entities or themes lack strong internal linking connections
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 4

name: Entity Cluster Over Single Keyword Targeting
then_action: Restructure content using Parent Topics in Ahrefs Keywords Explorer to build clusters around related themes, as AI Overviews favor entity clusters over single keywords
if_condition: If content strategy targets single keywords instead of grouped thematic clusters
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 4

name: Surround Sound SEO for High-Intent SERPs
then_action: Implement surround sound SEO to maximize brand real estate across multiple content types and platforms on high-intent SERPs, increasing brand recognition as the top correlating factor for AI Overview visibility
if_condition: If brand presence is limited to owned properties on high-intent search results pages
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 4

name: Authentic Brand Mention Acquisition for AI Overview Rankings
then_action: Avoid inauthentic placements; instead target authoritative sites that have not yet written similar best-X content, offer keyword research, pitch traffic opportunity, and provide demos or trials with full disclosure for honest reviews
if_condition: If brand mention strategy involves bargaining with writers to update existing best-list posts or includes inauthentic product recommendations
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 4

name: Grey Entity Cluster Remediation for AI Overview Visibility
then_action: Conduct an entity audit targeting those grey clusters and build or optimize content to convert them to green (AI Overview-cited) clusters
if_condition: If network graph analysis shows entity clusters appearing as grey circles (not cited in AI Overviews)
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation None Part 4

name: Use UnitPriceSpecification for Hotel Pricing
then_action: Structure price information using UnitPriceSpecification with price, priceCurrency, and unitCode properties
if_condition: If implementing structured data markup for hotel room or service pricing
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Fees for Optional Services

name: Implement Relevant Schema Types to Support AI Readability
then_action: Implement relevant schema types such as Article, HowTo, and FAQPage as a low-risk method to improve machine readability for AI systems and indirectly improve AI Overview visibility
if_condition: If a site lacks structured data markup or has incomplete schema implementation
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation Partner with YouTube c 2

name: JavaScript-Heavy Sites Must Render HTML for AI Crawlers
then_action: Convert JavaScript rendering so AI crawlers can read the content, using tools like prerender.io to serve bots a pre-rendered HTML version that includes schema, content, and links
if_condition: If a site relies heavily on JavaScript for rendering content
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation Partner with YouTube c 2

name: Audit Structured Data Issues Before Optimizing for AI Overviews
then_action: Use the Schema Validator or Google's Rich Results Test to check existing markup, then run an Ahrefs Site Audit and review the Issues report for structured data problems, expanding individual issue rows to identify exactly what is missing on affected pages
if_condition: If a site owner wants to improve AI Overview visibility and has not audited existing schema markup
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation Partner with YouTube c 2

name: AIO Prevalence Threshold for US Market
then_action: Then apply a ~16% AIO prevalence rate (24.9M out of 156M search terms) as the baseline assumption for how many queries trigger AI Overviews
if_condition: If analyzing search visibility in the US market and need to assess AI Overview exposure
source_title: Insights From 55 8M AI Overviews Across 590M Searches 2

name: Monitor AI Overview Citation Impact After Schema Fixes
then_action: Monitor affected URLs in Ahrefs Brand Radar using citation filters in the Cited Pages report to measure AI Overview visibility improvements over time
if_condition: If structured data issues have been fixed and the goal is to track improvements in AI Overview citations
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation Partner with YouTube c 2

name: AIO Traffic Impact for Top-Ranking Domains
then_action: Then reassess the attribution — top-ranking domains are disproportionately featured in AIOs and may actually gain visibility rather than lose clicks
if_condition: If a domain consistently ranks in top positions and reports traffic loss attributed to AI Overviews
source_title: Insights From 55 8M AI Overviews Across 590M Searches 2

name: Country-Level AIO Rate Variation
then_action: Then use country-specific AIO rates: India 16.5%, Brazil 15.5%, UK 12.5%, accessed via Brand Radar's country dropdown
if_condition: If comparing AI Overview exposure across India, Brazil, or UK markets
source_title: Insights From 55 8M AI Overviews Across 590M Searches 2

name: Treat Structured Data as Indirect Rather Than Direct AI Ranking Signal
then_action: Adjust expectations: structured data has an indirect rather than direct impact on AI search visibility, as AI crawlers do not fully process structured data the way Google's search engine bots do, though both Google and Microsoft confirm it helps AI systems interpret content correctly
if_condition: If structured data is being implemented with the expectation that it will directly cause ranking in AI Overviews
source_title: How to Rank in AI Overviews What Actually Works Based on Data Not Speculation Partner with YouTube c 2

name: AIO Percentage Calculation Method
then_action: Then divide the AIO mentions data by the total search number from the Big Data page to derive the AIO percentage
if_condition: If calculating what percentage of a site's tracked searches trigger AI Overviews
source_title: Insights From 55 8M AI Overviews Across 590M Searches 2

name: AIO Domain Monitoring Requirement
then_action: Then use Brand Radar's domains tab to monitor AIO domain mentions longitudinally; note that filter functionality for domain-level AIO analysis is not yet available
if_condition: If tracking whether a specific domain appears in AI Overviews over time
source_title: Insights From 55 8M AI Overviews Across 590M Searches 2

name: Informational Intent Dominates AI Overviews
then_action: Expect a significantly higher probability of triggering an AI Overview, as 97.70% of AIOs appear for informational intent queries across US keywords
if_condition: If a keyword has informational search intent
source_title: Insights From 55 8M AI Overviews Across 590M Searches 4

name: Q&A and Community Platforms Prioritized in AI Overviews
then_action: Expect higher likelihood of AI Overview mentions, as this category represents 5.9% of top-50 source mentions across 55.8M AI Overviews — the highest category share observed
if_condition: If a domain belongs to Q&A or community discussion platforms (e.g., Reddit)
source_title: Insights From 55 8M AI Overviews Across 590M Searches 3

name: Branded Searches Suppress AI Overviews
then_action: Expect lower AI Overview appearance rates, as only 19.97% of AIO-triggering searches are branded versus 80.03% non-branded; prioritize non-branded informational content for AIO visibility
if_condition: If a keyword is branded (contains a brand name)
source_title: Insights From 55 8M AI Overviews Across 590M Searches 4

name: Filter for AI Overview Keywords in Ahrefs
then_action: Filter by 'SERP features' > 'On SERP' > 'AI Overview' to isolate AIO-triggering keywords, then combine with Brand Radar for comprehensive competitive AIO analysis
if_condition: If conducting AI Overview (AIO) keyword analysis in Ahrefs Keywords Explorer
source_title: Insights From 55 8M AI Overviews Across 590M Searches 4

name: Health and Medical Sites Show Strong AI Overview Representation
then_action: Expect strong performance in both AI Overview mentions and impressions, with the category representing 5.8% of top-50 source mentions — prioritize authoritative, medically accurate content to compete in this space
if_condition: If a domain is classified as Health & Medical (e.g., mayoclinic.org, clevelandclinic.org, nih.gov)
source_title: Insights From 55 8M AI Overviews Across 590M Searches 3

name: Local Searches Suppress AI Overviews
then_action: Expect significantly lower AI Overview appearance rates, as only 6.85% of AIO-triggering searches are local versus 93.15% non-local; do not prioritize local keyword strategy for AIO capture
if_condition: If a keyword has local search intent (geo-specific queries)
source_title: Insights From 55 8M AI Overviews Across 590M Searches 4

name: Wikipedia-Style Reference Content Drives Disproportionate Impressions

if_condition: If auditing which keyword rankings are exposed to AI Overview impact in Google Search Console or Ahrefs Site Explorer
source_title: Update AI Overviews Reduce Clicks by 58

name: Hotel Offer Container Must Use Correct Schema.org Offer itemtype
then_action: Use itemscope itemtype='https://schema.org/Offer' as the main offer container to ensure correct schema recognition by search engines
if_condition: If implementing structured data for hotel room pricing or booking offers on a hotel website or booking platform
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Fees for Optional Services

name: AEO and SEO Must Be Combined for Maximum Visibility
then_action: Invest in both AEO and SEO as complementary strategies, since SEO determines whether AI systems discover content in the first place and strong SEO fundamentals are prerequisites for AEO success
if_condition: If a marketing team is operating in an AI-augmented search landscape
source_title: Best practices for answer engine optimization AEO marketing teams can t ignore How can we track AI c

name: AEO Brand Authority as Direct Traffic Driver
then_action: Treat AEO as a direct traffic channel, because AI tools can prompt ideal audiences to navigate directly to the website when the brand is surfaced as a top choice
if_condition: If a brand is consistently returned by AI tools as a top recommendation for relevant queries in its niche
source_title: What Is Answer Engine Optimization And How to Do It Why Does AEO Matter

name: AEO Content Formatting for AI Inclusion
then_action: Optimize content structure and formatting specifically for how AI tools find, parse, and format answers — not just for traditional search ranking signals
if_condition: If content is being created or optimized and the goal is to appear in AI-generated responses (ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, etc.)
source_title: What Is Answer Engine Optimization And How to Do It Why Does AEO Matter

name: AI Traffic Value Prioritization
then_action: Prioritize AEO efforts because AI search visitors convert at 4.4x the rate of traditional organic search visitors, making each AI-referred visitor significantly more valuable
if_condition: If comparing the strategic value of AI search traffic versus traditional organic search traffic when allocating optimization resources
source_title: What Is Answer Engine Optimization And How to Do It Why Does AEO Matter

name: Brand Visibility Warning for AI Zero-Click Risk
then_action: Flag risk of traffic loss because users are increasingly getting answers without clicking through to original sources, and trust is shifting from websites to AI tools themselves
if_condition: If a website relies primarily on traditional organic search clicks and has not optimized for AI answer engines, and AI tools like ChatGPT are answering queries without linking to sources
source_title: What Is Answer Engine Optimization And How to Do It Why Does AEO Matter

name: Hotel Schema: Main Container Type Requirement
then_action: Use itemscope itemtype='https://schema.org/Hotel' for the main hotel container element
if_condition: If marking up a hotel or lodging business using Schema.org structured data
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Minimal Example

name: Translate Product for International User Bases
then_action: Translate the product and website into the relevant languages to reduce friction and improve conversion for international audiences
if_condition: If the website or product is targeting users in multiple countries or non-English-speaking markets
source_title: Growth for startups YC Startup Library Transcript Part 10

name: Room Occupancy Markup Requirement
then_action: MUST use the occupancy property with QuantitativeValue type, and MUST include unitCode='IE' meta tag when specifying people occupancy; use maxValue for maximum capacity or minValue/maxValue pair for ranges
if_condition: If room capacity or occupancy is being specified in hotel structured data
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Information about Rooms

name: Room Feature Fee Modeling Warning
then_action: amenityFeature does NOT indicate whether a feature is free or for an additional charge; to model optional feature fees, link the room offer to feature offers using the addOn property instead
if_condition: If amenityFeature is used to mark up optional room features that carry an additional charge
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Information about Rooms

name: Pinterest Idea Pins: Enable Saving and Encourage Engagement
then_action: Enable saving of Idea Pins to Pinterest Boards and actively encourage comments and conversations to maximize organic reach and audience interaction
if_condition: If publishing Pinterest Idea Pins for a business or creator account
source_title: Pinterest Idea Pins The complete guide to inspiring your followers What are Pinterest Idea Pins

name: Hotel Schema Address Must Use PostalAddress Type
then_action: Structure address using itemscope itemtype='https://schema.org/PostalAddress' and include streetAddress, postalCode, addressLocality, addressRegion, and addressCountry properties
if_condition: If adding address information to a Schema.org Hotel markup
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Minimal Example

name: Use Specific Lodging Subtypes for Non-Hotel Businesses
then_action: Replace Schema.org/Hotel with more specific types such as Campground or MeetingRoom to accurately represent the lodging business type
if_condition: If the lodging business is not a standard hotel (e.g., campground or meeting room)
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Minimal Example

name: Hotel Schema Minimal Implementation Must Include Price Range
then_action: Add itemprop='priceRange' to indicate the general price range of the hotel, as a substitute for detailed room-level pricing
if_condition: If implementing a minimal Schema.org Hotel markup without individual room rates or availability data
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Minimal Example

name: Prices Belong to Offer Not Room or Hotel
then_action: Attach price and terms and conditions properties to the Offer object, NOT to the hotel (LodgingBusiness) or hotel room (Accommodation) objects
if_condition: If marking up pricing or terms and conditions for a hotel room in schema.org
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Overview

name: includesObject for Multi-Item Offers
then_action: Use the includesObject property with one or more TypeAndQuantityNode nodes to represent all included items within the offer
if_condition: If a schema.org hotel offer includes more than just the bare room (e.g., multiple nights, breakfast, services bundled together)
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Overview

name: Minimum Stay Duration Markup
then_action: Use eligibleDuration with minValue and unitCode='DAY' to specify the minimum stay requirement in the structured markup
if_condition: If a hotel offer enforces a minimum number of nights stay
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Validity of an Offer

name: Eligible Stay Dates Markup
then_action: Use availabilityStarts and availabilityEnds properties to define the eligible stay dates in the structured markup
if_condition: If a hotel offer is restricted to specific check-in or stay date ranges
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Validity of an Offer

name: Multi-Component Pricing Specification
then_action: Use CompoundPriceSpecification type with priceComponent to represent each pricing element in the structured markup
if_condition: If a hotel offer includes multiple pricing components (e.g., room rate plus fees or taxes listed separately)
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Validity of an Offer

name: Occupancy Limit Markup
then_action: Use occupancy with QuantitativeValue and maxValue to specify the occupancy limit in the structured markup
if_condition: If a hotel offer applies only up to a maximum number of guests
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Validity of an Offer

name: Cancellation Policy Placement
then_action: Place cancellation policy and terms and conditions text into the description property of the Offer schema, not in separate custom properties
if_condition: If a hotel offer includes cancellation policy information or terms and conditions
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Validity of an Offer

name: Map Search Behavior to SEO and SEM Channels
then_action: Then prioritize SEO and SEM as primary acquisition channels to capture high-intent demand at the point of search
if_condition: If customers actively search online for solutions to the problem the product solves
source_title: How to set up hire and scale a growth strategy and team YC Startup Library 2 Identify growth channel

name: Hotel Amenity Availability Must Use meta value True
then_action: Include a meta tag with itemprop='value' and content='True' to indicate the amenity is available; optionally include hoursAvailable with OpeningHoursSpecification for amenity-specific hours
if_condition: If marking up an available amenity feature using amenityFeature with LocationFeatureSpecification in hotel schema
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Star Rating

name: Hotel Amenities Must Use amenityFeature with LocationFeatureSpecification
then_action: Use amenityFeature property with LocationFeatureSpecification itemtype, and set meta itemprop='value' content='True' for each available amenity; optionally include hoursAvailable with OpeningHoursSpecification for amenity-specific hours
if_condition: If marking up hotel features or amenities (e.g. pool, gym, WiFi) in schema.org structured data
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Star Rating

name: Star Rating Source Should Be Attributed to Known Organization
then_action: Use the author property with https://schema.org/Organization itemtype nested inside the starRating markup to specify the rating source organization
if_condition: If the hotel star rating originates from a known rating authority (e.g., HOTREC, DEHOGA, WHR, Hotelstars)
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Star Rating

name: Account for Transitional Credibility Gap in Audience Trust
then_action: Acknowledge the transitional credibility gap: institutional signals still influence mass audience decisions even as their underlying reliability erodes, so factor in institutional association as a short-term distribution lever while building independent authority signals
if_condition: If targeting an audience that still primarily discovers and trusts content through institutional channels (legacy media, accredited publications) despite individual creator alternatives existing
source_title: Don t Rely on Credibility Stamps

name: Negative Mobile Experience Brand Damage
then_action: Flag as high-risk: users with negative mobile experiences are 62% less likely to purchase from that brand in the future

source_title: CRO Statistics 34 Vital Conversion Rate Optimization Stats 2025   Shopify Mobile CRO statistics

name: Avoid Premature Pivot from Sales to Marketing/SEO

if_condition: If a startup has not yet collected sufficient conversion rate data across a full outbound sales cycle and is considering switching to marketing or SEO instead
source_title: How to get your first customers YC Startup Library Chapters Part 9

name: Focus AI SEO Training on Visibility-Connected Skills

source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 1 When to Build Develop Internally

name: Find Non-Search User Identification Before Scaling SEO/SEM
then_action: Find another online platform or community where target users actively identify themselves (e.g., forums, Slack groups, Reddit, LinkedIn communities) before investing in SEM/SEO, because SEM is expensive due to competition and SEO takes too long to show early results
if_condition: If an early-stage startup is planning to launch SEM or SEO as its primary customer acquisition channel before reaching scale
