name: D2C Ad Targeting Cost Reduction
then_action: Then use on-site customer identification tools to generate high-converting customer segments, keywords, and ad scripts to narrow targeting, which can reduce customer acquisition cost by up to 50% within the first 10 days
if_condition: If a D2C brand has a limited advertising budget and is running broad audience targeting for paid ads

name: Hire for Specialized Strategic Skills Over Short-Term AI Fixes
then_action: Then prioritize roles that are both specialized and strategic (e.g., data/visibility analysts measuring AI citations, technical SEO specialists modeling entities and structured data, AI content strategists aligning content with AI retrieval patterns) — and avoid hiring for short-term fixes or chasing unproven 'AI SEO' job titles

source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 2 When to Buy Hire New Talent

name: Define Hiring Outcome Before Recruiting for AI SEO Roles

name: Renovation Information Must Use additionalProperty with PropertyValue

source_title: Hotels   Schema org Star Rating

name: Room Count Should Use QuantitativeValue With unitText
then_action: Use the numberOfRooms property with QuantitativeValue itemtype, including both value and unitText properties to describe the quantity and room category

source_title: Hotels   Schema org Star Rating

name: Competitive Citation Gap Analysis for AI Retrieval

if_condition: If AI-generated answers for your top target keywords consistently cite competitor content instead of yours

name: External Hire Trigger: Specialized Skill Needed Faster Than Internal Training Allows

name: Role-Specific AI SEO Skill Mapping

name: Internal Build Strategy Suitability Check

name: Do Not Default to Outsourcing as a General Fix

name: Internalize Skills That Become Core to SEO Strategy

name: Outsource for Niche Expertise or Short-Term AI-SEO Needs

name: CLV Tracking Mandatory for Business Viability

name: Use Subfolder Over Subdomain for Analytics Attribution

name: Reassess Strategy After Two Quarters Without Results

name: Use Semrush AI Visibility Toolkit for Tracking

name: Eliminate Frameworks That Don't Consistently Improve Visibility

name: Use Enterprise AIO for Large Team Collaboration

name: Prioritize Retention Budget Over Acquisition Based on CLV

name: Convert Team Learnings Into Reusable Training Artifacts

name: AI Search Visibility Requires Team Skill and System Upgrades

name: Backlink and Referring Domain Monitoring for Content Strategy
then_action: Monitor total backlinks and referring domains as performance metrics, since backlink growth improves the search performance of the entire website, particularly valuable for new blogs with low domain authority

name: Blog Subfolder vs Subdomain Requirement

name: Focused Priority Selection Rule

name: Pilot Before Scaling Requirement
then_action: MUST run a small pilot on representative pages first, then recheck whether those updates improve brand appearance in AI answers before scaling to broader site

name: Structured Data for AI Content Parsing

name: Evaluate Growth Rate Not Just Final Score

name: Standardize Workflows for AI-Driven Discovery

name: Skill Gap Resolution via External Talent

name: Monthly Review Cadence for Adaptation Gaps
then_action: Run monthly reviews to identify where individuals need additional support, training, or outsourced help to adapt to new systems

name: Scale High-Performing Low-CAC Campaigns

name: Benchmark CTR Against Industry Standards

name: AI Visibility Baseline Requirement

name: Content Brief Specificity Requirement for AI Visibility
then_action: Content briefs MUST address specific questions and scenarios derived from documented AI prompt patterns, not generic keyword targets — failure to do so reduces likelihood of AI citation
if_condition: If building content briefs intended to surface in AI platform responses
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Query Intent Mapping

name: Problem-Solving Intent Shift for AEO
then_action: Shift research frame from 'what keywords do people use?' to 'what problems are people trying to solve?', analyzing prompt patterns in AI platforms to document longer, more specific conversational queries
if_condition: If conducting keyword research or content planning for AI-ready SEO
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Query Intent Mapping

name: Platform Source Preference Mapping for Content Targeting
then_action: Target content placement and authority-building on the source types each platform prefers: Perplexity — real-time indexed web sources; ChatGPT — YouTube videos and Reddit threads; Google AI Mode — Google search index and Gemini training data; Claude — Quora and domain-specific authority sites like Android Authority
if_condition: When a brand needs to increase citations on a specific AI platform (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, or Google AI Mode)
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Platform Specific Optimization

name: Unified AI Visibility Strategy Structure
then_action: Structure a single unified approach spanning content, technical implementation, and off-site presence rather than creating separate strategies per platform; coordinate across content, technical, and other teams to adjust overall strategy
if_condition: When building or adjusting an AI visibility strategy after identifying platform-specific citation gaps
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Platform Specific Optimization

name: Investigate Sudden CPC Spikes as Ad Saturation Signal
then_action: Treat the spike as a signal that the ad is saturated or the audience targeting is misaligned; audit audience segmentation and adjust or rotate ad creative and targeting accordingly
if_condition: If cost-per-click (CPC) increases suddenly without a corresponding increase in conversions
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 33

name: Accept Higher CPC When Audience Quality Justifies Cost
then_action: Accept the elevated CPC if conversion data shows that the audience generates proportionally more conversions, validating the higher spend per click
if_condition: If targeting a premium or harder-to-reach audience segment results in a higher CPC
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 33

name: Quarterly Audit of External Skill Dependencies
then_action: Review your team skill mix every quarter to identify which capabilities rely too heavily on outside help and adjust the balance accordingly
if_condition: If your SEO team is using a mix of built, bought, and borrowed skills across internal and external resources
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Hybrid Reality

name: Escalate Recurring Borrowed Skills to Internal Build
then_action: Begin building that skill internally rather than continuing to borrow it, as recurring reliance on external parties for the same capability signals it has become a core need
if_condition: If a skill that was initially borrowed from an outside expert is being used repeatedly or continuously over time
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Hybrid Reality

name: Rebalance Team Structure Based on Delivered Impact
then_action: Adjust your team structure and skill mix based on what is actually delivering the most SEO impact, not what is reflected on the org chart
if_condition: If your current SEO team structure is based on org chart design or historical norms rather than measured performance
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Hybrid Reality

name: SEO-Optimized Team Composition for Organic Growth
then_action: Staff the team with SEO marketers, data analysts, and full-stack web engineers; exclude mobile engineers and likely designers since their skills are not mission-critical for this problem
if_condition: When the growth team mission is SEO optimization and organic web traffic growth
source_title: How to build a growth team lessons from Uber Hubspot and others 50 slides at andrewchen 3

name: Performance Marketing Function Placement Decision
then_action: Either Marketing or Growth teams are valid homes — align placement with where the team's core KPIs and execution mandate sit; Growth ownership (like Facebook) works when performance marketing is central to growth loops, Marketing ownership (like Uber) works when brand and demand generation are centralized
if_condition: When deciding where to place performance marketing functions (SEO, Paid Marketing, Email) within the organization
source_title: How to build a growth team lessons from Uber Hubspot and others 50 slides at andrewchen 4

name: Target Notifications Within the Core Engagement Loop
then_action: Focus on highly-relevant notifications sent within the core engagement loop rather than generic or broad outreach campaigns
if_condition: When designing or evaluating a notification or messaging strategy for growth
source_title: How to build a growth team lessons from Uber Hubspot and others 50 slides at andrewchen 6

name: Digital Footprint Breadth Affects AI Inclusion
then_action: Audit the full digital footprint beyond the website — AI pulls from external mentions, third-party sources, and off-site content, not just on-site pages
if_condition: If a brand is underrepresented in AI-generated answers despite having strong website content
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Skills Gap Between Traditional and AI

name: Expand Performance Tracking to AI Platforms
then_action: Measure brand citations and mentions across AI platforms (e.g., ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini) in addition to traditional rank tracking and organic traffic metrics
if_condition: If a brand is measuring SEO performance and AI search is part of their visibility strategy
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Skills Gap Between Traditional and AI

name: Monitor Citation Frequency in AI Responses
then_action: Then monitor citation frequency — how often that content is cited as a source in AI-generated responses — as a required performance metric
if_condition: If content is being produced as part of an AI SEO strategy
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Tracking AI Performance

name: Zero-Click Risk Warning for AI-Visible Content
then_action: Recognize that zero-click behavior means traffic is no longer guaranteed even with strong visibility — reframe success metrics to include citation share and brand mention frequency, not just clicks
if_condition: If a site achieves strong AI search visibility or traditional search visibility but traffic metrics are declining
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Skills Gap Between Traditional and AI

name: Register Brand in Entity Databases Only After Core AI SEO Structure Is Established
then_action: Then defer entity database registration until after the core AI SEO structure is established, as early registration is not needed and should be explored only once the foundation is complete
if_condition: If core AI SEO structure is not yet in place and entity database registration (Wikipedia, Wikidata, Crunchbase) is being considered
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Tracking AI Performance

name: Track ROAS for Every Campaign
then_action: Track return on ad spend (ROAS) for every campaign to gauge efficiency and make adjustments; a lower ROAS signals campaigns need optimization
if_condition: If a marketing campaign is running and ROAS is not being monitored
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 32

name: Track Brand Mention Rate and Sentiment in AI Answers
then_action: Then measure both mention rate (how often the brand appears in AI-generated answers or recommendations) and mention sentiment (whether those mentions are positive, neutral, or negative)
if_condition: If tracking AI SEO performance for a brand
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Tracking AI Performance

name: Avoid Ad Spend Exceeding Revenue
then_action: Stop or reduce that advertising spend immediately; spending more on advertising than you make in sales is unsustainable
if_condition: If advertising spend equals or exceeds revenue generated from those ads
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 32

name: CPM Calculation Rule
then_action: divide total spend for that ad campaign by total impressions generated to get CPM
if_condition: if calculating cost per thousand impressions for an ad campaign
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 34

name: CPA Calculation Rule
then_action: divide total cost of the ad campaign by the number of acquisitions it generates to get CPA
if_condition: if calculating cost per acquisition for an ad campaign
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 34

name: Use Specialized AI Visibility Tools for Monthly Monitoring
then_action: Then use those tools to monitor visibility score and citation trends on a monthly basis to track AI search performance systematically
if_condition: If specialized AI visibility tools are available and an AI SEO strategy is active
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Tracking AI Performance

name: MER Calculation Rule
then_action: divide total revenue by total spend; aim for a high MER number as it reflects overall marketing efficiency regardless of attribution source
if_condition: if calculating Marketing Efficiency Ratio (MER) also known as blended ROAS
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 34

name: CPA Profitability Context Rule
then_action: analyze CPA alongside AOV and CLV to ensure acquired customers are profitable; do not optimize for lower CPA in isolation without confirming overall profitability
if_condition: if evaluating CPA to assess campaign performance
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 34

name: Mobile Experience Optimization Rule
then_action: ensure a positive mobile experience is in place, including mobile-optimized pages and checkout flows, to prevent conversion loss from poor mobile UX
if_condition: if traffic dashboard data shows a large percentage of website visitors are using mobile devices
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 34

name: Accept Higher CPC for High-Converting Expensive Audiences
then_action: Should accept higher CPC rather than optimizing purely for cost-per-click reduction, as long as the conversion volume justifies the spend
if_condition: If serving ads to a premium or higher-cost audience segment where conversion rates are demonstrably higher
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 24

name: Do Not Equate Traffic with Revenue
then_action: Track revenue and profit independently from traffic; high website traffic does not directly correlate with revenue or profit
if_condition: If website traffic is increasing but revenue or profit metrics are not being separately tracked
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 32

name: Schema.org Term Deletion Requires SupersededBy Marker
then_action: Expect it to be marked as supersededBy another term rather than deleted outright; outright deletion without a supersededBy marker is exceptionally rare and should be flagged as anomalous
if_condition: If a schema.org property, type, or enumerated value appears to be removed or deprecated from the vocabulary
source_title: How we work   schema org Schema definitions

name: Mix Independent Vocabularies with Schema.org Using JSON-LD or RDFa
then_action: Mix other independent schemas (e.g. Dublin Core via 'dc' prefix) into schema.org-based descriptions using JSON-LD context definitions or RDFa 1.1 prefix declarations to extend coverage without waiting for schema.org community consensus
if_condition: If structured data requires expressivity beyond schema.org definitions and is implemented using JSON-LD or RDFa syntax
source_title: How we work   schema org Extensibility Mechanisms

name: Use Role Mechanism for Extra Annotation on Schema.org Entities
then_action: Apply the Role mechanism to arbitrarily annotate any piece of schema.org data with extra information, independent of schema.org versioning or release planning
if_condition: If a schema.org entity or property requires additional contextual annotation that cannot be expressed through standard schema.org properties
source_title: How we work   schema org Extensibility Mechanisms

name: Schema.org Does Not Define Mandatory Properties
then_action: Do not expect schema.org to define any notion of mandatory properties; schema.org does not dictate what an ideal description of any type ought to contain
if_condition: If implementing schema.org markup and assuming certain properties are required or mandatory by schema.org specification
source_title: How we work   schema org Schema definitions

name: Schema.org Definitions Must Be Human and Machine Readable Across All Use Cases
then_action: Ensure both human-readable and machine-readable definitions are appropriate to all situations where a property might be used, and that textual definitions work across all different combinations of types a property can be used with
if_condition: If creating or validating schema.org property definitions for implementation
source_title: How we work   schema org Schema definitions

name: Schema.org Property Names Must Be Global Across All Vocabularies
then_action: Use schema.org property names as global identifiers across the entire project including core and all hosted vocabularies to ensure consistency
if_condition: If defining or implementing schema.org properties across core and hosted vocabularies within a project
source_title: How we work   schema org Schema definitions

name: Schema.org Terms Tracked as Core or Single Extension
then_action: Verify the term is marked as partOf either the core or exactly one extension; a term cannot simultaneously belong to multiple extensions
if_condition: If a schema.org term is moved between core and a hosted extension during implementation or auditing
source_title: How we work   schema org Schema definitions

name: Schema.org Fast-Track Eligibility for Minor Fixes
then_action: The change qualifies for fast-tracking as an 'Early Access Fix' by the project steering group, bypassing the full release process
if_condition: If a schema.org change involves supporting documentation, examples, or software bug fixes (not vocabulary changes)
source_title: How we work   schema org Overview

name: Pending Vocabulary Immediate Update Rule
then_action: The project webmaster may update it immediately without waiting for a full official release, but note it may not reflect wider community or steering group consensus
if_condition: If a new vocabulary proposal is designated as pending at pending.staging.schema.org
source_title: How we work   schema org Overview

name: Schema.org Definitions Evolve Gradually Over Time
then_action: Account for gradual evolution of definitions over time; properties may become applicable to previously unrelated types or expect values of new types, requiring periodic review of implemented markup
if_condition: If relying on a previously documented schema.org term definition for ongoing implementation without re-validation
source_title: How we work   schema org Schema definitions

name: Schema.org Release Candidate Approval Requirement
then_action: MUST submit to steering group for review and obtain approval before proceeding to the 10-business-day waiting period and site update
if_condition: If a periodic snapshot of community consensus is ready to be designated as a release candidate
source_title: How we work   schema org Overview

name: Use PropertyValue for Non-Standard Product Specifications
then_action: Use PropertyValue to expose those arbitrary property/value data pairs within the larger schema.org description to ensure structured data completeness without requiring custom vocabulary approval
if_condition: If a product or entity has key/value specifications or attributes that do not map easily into standard schema.org properties
source_title: How we work   schema org Extensibility Mechanisms

name: Monitor Bounce Rate as a Proxy for Content-Audience Fit
then_action: Investigate and address root causes such as poor content relevance, broken links, slow page load times, or mismatched audience targeting to improve engagement and reduce single-page exits
if_condition: If bounce rate is high (visitors consistently leave after viewing only one page)
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 33

name: Schema.org Release Requires Steering Committee Approval
then_action: MUST obtain steering committee approval before implementing the release candidate or site update
if_condition: If a release candidate or site update for Schema.org is prepared and ready for publication
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 3

name: Classify Early Access Fixes as Part of Subsequent Full Release
then_action: Then Early Access Fixes must be classified as part of the subsequent full release for versioning purposes, not as standalone releases
if_condition: If versioning or release tracking is applied to Schema.org Early Access Fixes
source_title: How we work   schema org Early Access Fixes and Pending Releases

name: Schema.org Community Discussion Ethics Requirement
then_action: MUST respect the W3C code of ethics and professional conduct in all interactions
if_condition: If participating in Schema.org community group or GitHub discussions
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 3

name: Share Early Implementation Feedback When Using Pending Terms
then_action: Then they are strongly encouraged to share early implementation feedback with the wider Schema.org community to contribute to consensus-building
if_condition: If a consumer or publisher encourages the use of Schema.org pending terms in their implementations or documentation
source_title: How we work   schema org Early Access Fixes and Pending Releases

name: Avoid Uncautious Use of Schema.org Pending Terms
then_action: Then AVOID using pending terms without caution, as they are subject to change, may lack consensus, and their definitions could still change significantly
if_condition: If a developer or publisher is selecting Schema.org vocabulary terms and pending terms are available for the concept being marked up
source_title: How we work   schema org Early Access Fixes and Pending Releases

name: Develop All Hosted Extensions Within Main Repository
then_action: Develop all hosted extensions within the main schema.org project repository rather than in separate external repositories to ensure consistency and proper integration
if_condition: If developing hosted schema.org extensions such as 'bib', 'auto', or 'meta'
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 1

name: Schema.org Term Definition Completeness Requirement
then_action: It MUST include: identifier, short textual HTML definition, cross-references, links, and factual metadata
if_condition: If a schema.org term definition page (type, property, or enumerated value) is being created or validated
source_title: How we work   schema org Schema structure

name: JSON-LD Context File Auto-Generation Requirement
then_action: It MUST be generated automatically by software (not manually authored) and made available via the homepage in application/ld+json format
if_condition: If a JSON-LD context file is being produced for schema.org
source_title: How we work   schema org Schema structure

name: Stage New Schema.org Terms Before Release
then_action: Stage the work-in-progress on staging.schema.org, which provides a 1:1 representation of the main development branch, before publishing to the official schema.org release
if_condition: If new schema.org vocabulary terms or proposals are being developed and are not yet officially released
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 1

name: Submit Schema.org Proposals via GitHub Issues
then_action: Use the GitHub issue list as the required channel for day-to-day project management; attach appropriate labels (e.g., cleanup) and reference high-level entry points: issue #1 (planning), #2 (vocab changes), #3 (tooling/infrastructure)
if_condition: If submitting a new vocabulary proposal, improvement, fix, or tooling issue to schema.org
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 1

name: W3C RDFS Base Approach Requirement
then_action: W3C RDFS MUST be used as the base approach for term definitions, with schema.org-specific customizations applied on top
if_condition: If schema.org term definitions are being structured or validated for technical compliance
source_title: How we work   schema org Schema structure

name: Prioritize Proposals Mapped to Widely Published Web Content
then_action: Assign higher priority to proposals that map easily onto already widely published structured web content and are likely to be used in large-scale public web projects; de-prioritize proposals with narrow or niche applicability
if_condition: If a schema.org vocabulary proposal is being evaluated for prioritization by the steering group or community
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 1

name: Hosted Extension File Location Requirement
then_action: Extension files MUST be located in data/ext/* in the GitHub repository, following the same file structure as core schema files
if_condition: If schema.org hosted extensions are being created or stored
source_title: How we work   schema org Schema structure

name: Track Evolving Changes When Using Pending Schema.org Terms
then_action: Then implementations and documentation MUST be updated to track any evolving changes to those pending terms, as terminology and definitions may change significantly after community and steering group review
if_condition: If an implementation or documentation encourages the use of Schema.org pending terms
source_title: How we work   schema org Early Access Fixes and Pending Releases

name: Use Pending Extension for Self-Contained New Term Proposals
then_action: Place the proposal within the 'pending' extension, which is designated for self-contained proposals adding new terms to schema.org prior to full integration
if_condition: If proposing entirely new terms to be added to schema.org that are self-contained and not yet integrated into the core vocabulary
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 1

name: Expand SEO Team Skills for AI Search Visibility
then_action: Then expand the team's skill set to cover AI search requirements, including passage extraction optimization, information synthesis, and answer engine visibility, as traditional SEO skills alone are no longer sufficient
if_condition: If an SEO team is optimizing solely for traditional Google rankings without addressing AI-powered search and answer engines
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide

name: Schema.org Release Mandatory Waiting Period
then_action: MUST wait 10 business days for concerns before updating schema.org site with the new release
if_condition: If steering group has reviewed and approved a schema.org release candidate
source_title: How we work   schema org Overview

name: Declare schemaVersion Property for Version-Specific Documents
then_action: Use the schemaVersion property within the document to explicitly indicate the intended schema.org version being referenced
if_condition: If a document's structured data relies on a specific version of schema.org definitions rather than the latest release
source_title: How we work   schema org Versioning and change control

name: Use Non-Versioned Schema.org URLs for Structured Data
then_action: Use simple non-versioned schema.org URLs such as 'https://schema.org/Place' rather than versioned or snapshot URLs
if_condition: If implementing structured data markup using schema.org vocabulary for general web publishing purposes
source_title: How we work   schema org Versioning and change control

name: CPM Value Interpretation Warning
then_action: do not assume lower CPM is always better; a higher CPM may indicate reaching a more valuable audience that converts at higher rates and generates higher AOV, making it more profitable
if_condition: if a higher CPM is observed on a particular ad campaign or audience segment
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 34

name: Use Dated Snapshots When Precise Versioning Is Required
then_action: Access and reference dated snapshots from the schema.org releases page instead of simple non-versioned URLs
if_condition: If the application or document requires precise, reproducible schema.org definitions (e.g., archival, contractual, or system-to-system data exchange contexts)
source_title: How we work   schema org Versioning and change control

name: Use Pending Extension Only for Additive Vocabulary Changes
then_action: Submit the proposal to pending.schema.org for community review using the pending extension mechanism
if_condition: If a proposed schema.org change adds new types or properties (additive change) without modifying existing definitions or supertypes
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 2

name: Treat External Schema.org Extensions as Independently Governed
then_action: Do not assume schema.org versioning, change control, or Steering Group governance applies; follow the external extension's own processes, versioning systems, and collaboration mechanisms
if_condition: If implementing or consuming an external schema.org extension (not hosted under a schema.org subdomain)
source_title: How we work   schema org Versioning and change control

name: Do Not Use Pending Extension to Change Existing Type Definitions
then_action: Do NOT submit via the pending extension mechanism; use GitHub issues and community discussion instead to avoid violating schema.org contribution policy
if_condition: If a proposed schema.org change alters the textual definition of an existing type or changes the supertype relationships of existing types
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 2

name: Use GitHub for Non-Additive Schema Changes
then_action: Propose the change directly via GitHub issues and pull requests rather than using the pending extension mechanism
if_condition: If a proposed schema.org change modifies textual definitions of existing types, alters supertypes of existing types, or is otherwise non-additive
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 2

name: Evolve Existing Team Before Adding New Roles
then_action: Then evolve and upskill the existing team to cover AI search requirements rather than adding unnecessary new roles
if_condition: If an organization needs to address AI search coverage gaps in their SEO team
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide

name: Flag Traditional-Only SEO Strategy as Insufficient
then_action: Then flag the strategy as incomplete and at risk of reduced visibility, requiring immediate expansion to include AI search optimization capabilities
if_condition: If a brand's SEO strategy does not account for AI-powered search engines that extract passages, synthesize information, and present instant answers from multiple sources
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide

name: Warn Against Waiting for Schema.org Approval When Custom Properties Needed
then_action: Use schema.org extensibility mechanisms (PropertyValue, Role, or mixed vocabularies) immediately rather than waiting for community approval, agreement, or steering group consensus, as these mechanisms operate independently of schema.org definitions
if_condition: If a structured data implementation requires properties or vocabularies not yet defined or approved in the schema.org community process
source_title: How we work   schema org Extensibility Mechanisms

name: Mobile Experience Optimization for High Mobile Traffic
then_action: Then ensure the mobile experience is positive and optimized, as poor mobile UX will negatively impact conversion rates and revenue metrics
if_condition: If a large percentage of ecommerce site visitors are coming from mobile devices
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 25

name: Underperforming Channel Strategy Rule
then_action: reexamine and revise the strategy for that underperforming channel, and tailor campaigns using traffic breakdown data including regional-specific offerings based on customer location
if_condition: if traffic dashboard reveals a declining trend in a specific channel (e.g., email traffic declining over recent months)
source_title: Understanding Ecommerce Metrics What to Track and Why It Matters 34

name: Schema.org Staging Environment Consensus Caveat
then_action: Treat with caution — staging.schema.org may contain changes that do not represent consensus of the wider community or steering group
if_condition: If referencing staging.schema.org content as authoritative schema definitions
source_title: How we work   schema org Overview

name: Coordinate Related Proposals Through Project Webmaster
then_action: Route proposals through the project webmaster, who will update pending.schema.org to display the complete designs alongside each other
if_condition: If multiple related additive vocabulary proposals exist and need to be synthesised or shared more widely with the community
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 2

name: Treat Staging Environment as Unstable
then_action: Treat staging.schema.org as potentially buggy, occasionally offline, or in an inconsistent state due to ongoing editorial work; do not depend on it as an authoritative source
if_condition: If referencing or relying on staging.schema.org for production or stable vocabulary information
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 2

name: Primary Keyword Placement Requirement
then_action: Then the primary keyword MUST appear in the title tag, meta description, H1 tag, and URL slug
if_condition: If a webpage is being optimized and a primary keyword has been selected to represent the page's overall topic
source_title: Irrelevant Keywords What They Are How to Avoid Them How to Avoid Irrelevant Keyword Rankings

name: Schema.org Term Naming Must Avoid Domain-Specific Clashes
then_action: MUST carefully assign term names to avoid clashes with existing terms or overly domain-specific definitions — process remains centralized due to schema overlap across topical scopes
if_condition: If proposing or assigning new term names in Schema.org hosted extensions or core vocabulary
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 3

name: Substantial Schema.org Contributors Must Join Community Group
then_action: The contributor MUST join the schema.org community group and review the project terms of service
if_condition: A contributor has made substantial textual contributions to schema.org schemas or examples
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 4

name: Schema.org Founding Search Engine Contributors Must Follow Specific Workflow
then_action: Then they must refer to the previous workflow FAQ question for specific guidance on how to get involved in Schema.org contributions
if_condition: If a contributor works for one of the Schema.org-founding search engines (Google, Bing, Yahoo, Yandex)
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 5

name: External Schema.org Extensions Require No Steering Group Permission
then_action: No permission or consensus from the schema.org steering group is required; external extensions can be published independently
if_condition: A third party (e.g., GS1) is creating an external extension to schema.org vocabulary outside the core schema.org project
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 4

name: Schema.org Release Requires Unanimous Steering Group Agreement
then_action: Unanimous agreement of all steering group members MUST be achieved before the release is published to the official site
if_condition: A release candidate for schema.org has been prepared and is ready for publication
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 4

name: Schema.org Contributors Should Reference W3C Community Group Materials
then_action: Then they should consult the 'how we work' materials in the W3C group, which lists all Schema.org-related W3C community groups
if_condition: If a contributor needs to understand Schema.org workflow processes and community structure
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 5

name: Deep Schema.org Understanding Requires External Reference Materials
then_action: Then they should reference the ACM Queue article, Richard Wallis's 'schema.org in practice' articles (parts 1, 2, and 3), and relevant conference presentations
if_condition: If a contributor or implementer needs deeper understanding of Schema.org background and practical implementation
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 5

name: Meta Extension Terms Are Not For General Web Use
then_action: Do not use 'meta' extension terms for general web use; these terms are intended solely for schema.org's internal schema definition purposes
if_condition: A developer or publisher is considering using terms from schema.org's 'meta' extension in general web markup
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 4

name: Schema.org Change Proposals Must Be Raised via GitHub Issue
then_action: Raise an issue at GitHub for the proposal; optionally accompany it with a thread on the public-schemaorg@w3.org mailing list
if_condition: A contributor wants to propose a change to schema.org vocabulary or examples
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 4

name: Match Keyword Intent to Page Purpose
then_action: Then stop targeting that keyword and remove or deprioritize it from your SEO strategy, as ranking for it signals a mismatch that reduces SEO effectiveness
if_condition: If a keyword your page ranks for has a SERP intent (shown by top-ranking results) that does not match the purpose or target audience of your page
source_title: Irrelevant Keywords What They Are How to Avoid Them How to Check for Irrelevant Keyword Rankings

name: Simple and Targeted Schema.org Proposals Reach Consensus Faster
then_action: The proposal is likely to reach rough consensus most easily and proceed through the steering group review process faster than complex topics
if_condition: A schema.org change proposal is simple, modestly sized, and clearly likely to be used by the community
source_title: How we work   schema org Workflow FAQ Part 4

name: Exact Match Campaign Keyword Precision
then_action: Then the keyword used must match the searcher's query meaning exactly — no semantic approximations or close variants that alter intent
if_condition: If setting up an exact match ad campaign targeting a specific keyword
source_title: Irrelevant Keywords What They Are How to Avoid Them How to Avoid Irrelevant Keyword Rankings

name: Advisory Scope Beyond SEO
then_action: They should know the advisory scope extends to hiring, org design, internationalization, M&A, and alternative channels like YouTube or Reddit, integrated with product strategy and business objectives
if_condition: If a client engages Kevin Indig's advisory services expecting only SEO support
source_title: Kevin Indig   Organic Growth Advisor

name: JSON-LD Property String Interpretation Recording
then_action: The definition MUST explicitly record whether the default interpretation of that string value is URL or text, and properties expecting URL values must be listed in JSON-LD definitions
if_condition: If a schema.org property is defined and may accept string values in JSON-LD
source_title: How we work   schema org Schema structure

name: Schema.org Governance Misattribution Warning

name: AggregateRating in Product Schema for Review Rich Results
then_action: Include aggregateRating attribute within Product schema to enable review rich results in search
if_condition: If a product detail page (PDP) has customer reviews and is using Product schema
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Organization

name: makesOffer vs hasOfferCatalog Based on Business Type
then_action: Use the makesOffer attribute to describe service offerings; if the business is an ecommerce website, use hasOfferCatalog to describe products instead
if_condition: If implementing Organization schema and the business is service-based
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Organization

name: Use Person Schema on All Profile Pages
then_action: Implement Person schema markup on that profile page to support entity validation
if_condition: If a page is a profile page for an individual
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Person

name: Use sameAs Attribute to Validate Person Entity with External Sources
then_action: Include the sameAs attribute pointing to those external social media profiles to validate the entity with authoritative external sources
if_condition: If Person schema is implemented and the individual has social media profiles such as LinkedIn
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Person

name: Product Schema on Product Detail Pages for Ecommerce
then_action: MUST use Product schema as the primary schema type on each individual product detail page
if_condition: If the website is an ecommerce retailer with individual product detail pages (PDPs)
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Organization

name: Use worksFor and jobTitle to Disambiguate People with Same Name
then_action: Add worksFor and jobTitle attributes to disambiguate between the individuals and help search engines correctly identify the unique entity
if_condition: If multiple people share the same name and occupation or job title in Person schema
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Person

name: Use knowsAbout to Connect Person Entity Credibility to Content Topics
then_action: Include the knowsAbout attribute describing the topics, subjects, and concepts the person is knowledgeable about to establish topical credibility
if_condition: If a Person schema entity is associated with specific content topics or subject matter
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Person

name: Use honorificPrefix for Medical or Academic Title Holders
then_action: Include the honorificPrefix attribute to declare their title, supporting E-E-A-T signals and entity credibility
if_condition: If the person referenced in Person schema holds a title such as Dr
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Person

name: Use alumniOf to Establish Educational Background for Credibility
then_action: Include the alumniOf attribute to declare their educational institutions, strengthening expertise signals for connected content
if_condition: If the person referenced in Person schema has a relevant educational background
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Person

name: Organization Schema Attributes for Validation
then_action: Include sameAs (pointing to social media, Crunchbase, LinkedIn), taxID/vatID (for government source validation), legalName (registered company name), and founder (for new businesses or startups) to establish entity validation signals
if_condition: If implementing Organization schema on a business website
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Organization

name: Product Schema Should Include AggregateRating and Review for Enhanced Visibility
then_action: Include both 'aggregateRating' (AggregateRating type) and 'review' (Review type) properties in the Product schema to enable star rating display in search rich results and improve shopping graph inclusion
if_condition: If a product page has user reviews or aggregate star ratings available
source_title: Product

name: Schema.org Attribute Reference Required
then_action: Then required and optional attributes may be missed or incorrectly structured; load the relevant Schema.org page and identify all desired attributes before prompting
if_condition: If generating schema markup via ChatGPT without first consulting Schema.org documentation for the target schema type
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Use ChatGPT for Schema Markup Generation

name: Schema Type Selection Before Prompting
then_action: Then output quality will be degraded; always identify and specify the exact schema type (e.g., Person, Legal Service, Dentist, OnlineBusiness) before constructing the prompt
if_condition: If prompting ChatGPT to generate JSON-LD schema markup without first selecting a specific schema type
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Use ChatGPT for Schema Markup Generation

name: Insufficient Prompt Causes Incorrect JSON-LD
then_action: Then ChatGPT will fill missing information with potentially incorrect assumptions, producing syntactically valid but semantically wrong JSON-LD
if_condition: If the ChatGPT prompt for JSON-LD schema generation omits specific attribute values or leaves information gaps
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Use ChatGPT for Schema Markup Generation

name: Include sameAs and alternateName for Entity Relationships
then_action: Then include sameAs and alternateName attributes in the prompt and schema output to create proper entity relationships and improve knowledge graph representation
if_condition: If generating schema markup for an entity where sameAs URLs (e.g., Wikidata, LinkedIn) or alternate names are available
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Use ChatGPT for Schema Markup Generation

name: Validate Generated JSON-LD Before Deployment
then_action: Then validate it using Schema.org's validator and optionally use Classy Schema's visualization tool to confirm entity relationships before implementing on the site
if_condition: If ChatGPT has generated JSON-LD schema markup that appears syntactically correct
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Use ChatGPT for Schema Markup Generation

name: Schema Markup Dual Role Entity Definition
then_action: Define the main entity in its dual roles — both as webpage content and as a website representation — and establish online presence through social media profile links and business register entries to create a complete entity profile
if_condition: If implementing schema markup for a business website
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Schema Markup is SEO

name: Schema Markup Operational Details Specification
then_action: Add structured schema markup specifying opening hours for each day and link services to their respective dedicated web pages to enable reliable machine-readable extraction without NLP interpretation
if_condition: If a business entity has defined operating hours and services but these are only present as natural language text on the page
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Schema Markup is SEO

name: Schema Markup Physical and Contact Identity Linking
then_action: Include complete postal address, telephone numbers, and Google Maps links in schema markup to connect the digital identity with a real-world location and improve entity disambiguation for search engines
if_condition: If a business entity has a physical location and contact information that is not declared in schema markup
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Schema Markup is SEO

name: Schema Markup Service Linking to Wikidata and Wikipedia
then_action: Link each service to broader concepts available on Wikipedia and Wikidata to provide authoritative context and help search engines and AI systems classify the services within a known knowledge graph
if_condition: If schema markup includes services offered by a business entity
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Schema Markup is SEO

name: Schemantra @id Property Assignment Requirement
then_action: Then an '@id' property must be assigned to every single entity without exception, using the template format #{schemaType} (e.g., #person, #organization, #event, #localbusiness, #article)
if_condition: If using Schemantra to generate schema markup for any entity
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Use Schema Markup Generators

name: Schema Markup Validation Step
then_action: Then validate the markup using Google Rich Results Test before adding it to individual pages to ensure correctness
if_condition: If schema markup has been generated and copied from a schema markup generator tool
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Use Schema Markup Generators

name: Merkle Generator Schema Type Limitation
then_action: Then Merkle's schema generator should not be used, as it is limited to only those five schema types with restricted attributes
if_condition: If a website requires schema types beyond Article, Breadcrumb, Event, FAQPage, or How-to, or needs extensive schema attributes
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Use Schema Markup Generators

name: Rank Ranger Parent Schema Attribute Limitation
then_action: Then Rank Ranger's schema tool should not be used, as it does not provide access to parent schema type attributes
if_condition: If a website requires access to parent schema type attributes (e.g., Organization attributes when the target schema type is Corporation)
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Use Schema Markup Generators

name: High-Intent Shopper Upsell Targeting
then_action: SHOULD focus upsells and cross-sells on these high-intent shoppers to maximize revenue per visitor
if_condition: If a visitor has items in their cart or has just completed a purchase
source_title: 28 Digital Marketing Tools for Small Businesses Budget Friendly and Free Options Conversion optimiza

name: Schema Markup Entity Declaration Requirement
then_action: Add schema markup using key-value pairs to explicitly declare the entity, its purpose, and authorship so search engines and AI systems (Google, ChatGPT, Perplexity) can interpret content without relying on imperfect NLP guesswork
if_condition: If a webpage contains content about a business, service, or entity without schema markup defining what the content is about, who it's for, and who stands behind it
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Schema Markup is SEO

name: @id Property as Cross-Reference Identifier
then_action: Then use the '@id' property as a unique identifier for each entity to enable cross-referencing between different schema parts on the same page
if_condition: If multiple schema markup blocks exist on the same page and need to reference each other
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Use Schema Markup Generators

name: SEO Diminishing Returns Pivot Trigger
then_action: Then initiate resource reallocation toward alternative growth levers such as performance marketing to sustain overall growth trajectory
if_condition: If SEO is the primary growth engine and incremental SEO efforts are producing measurably declining returns over time

name: SEO Engine Misalignment Warning
then_action: Then stop allocating resources to referral/invite mechanics — they do not accelerate the SEO engine and represent wasted effort; redirect focus to content and search ranking inputs instead
if_condition: If SEO/Content is identified as the primary Growth Engine and resources are being allocated to viral/referral mechanics such as user invite programs

name: Primary Growth Engine Focus Rule
then_action: Redirect resources away from viral loops and back to SEO-driving activities, as misaligned growth tactics waste time and dilute engine efficiency

then_action: Shift resources to alternative growth levers such as performance marketing to compensate for the reduced marginal impact of SEO

name: Enable Schema Markup via Rank Math Dashboard

name: Enable FAQPage Schema via Block Plugin Accordion
then_action: Enable the FAQ Schema option within the accordion block settings to automatically mark up questions and answers as FAQPage schema

name: Use WordPress SEO Plugins for Basic Schema Implementation
then_action: Install Yoast SEO or Rank Math, as they automatically generate schema markup (WebPage schema for pages, Article schema for posts) based on metadata like page title and published date
if_condition: If a WordPress site needs schema markup with minimal setup effort and customization is not a priority
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Use WordPress SEO Plugins

name: Warn Against Relying Solely on Plugins for Rich Connected Schema
then_action: Do not rely solely on automated WordPress SEO plugins (Yoast SEO, Rank Math), as they have limited customization and rarely convert on-page content into connected structured data; consider manual implementation
if_condition: If the goal is to create rich, interconnected structured data that reflects on-page content meaningfully
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Use WordPress SEO Plugins

name: Schema Syntax Error Detection Triggers Full Ignore by Googlebot
then_action: Googlebot will ignore the structured data entirely; validate and fix all syntax errors before deployment to ensure schema is recognized by search engines
if_condition: If JSON-LD schema markup contains syntax errors (missing commas, curly braces, or misplaced punctuation)
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Validate Syntax Errors With Schema org

name: Schema Validation Must Cover Three Distinct Aspects
then_action: Must check all three aspects: syntax errors, Google's requirements compliance, and proper entity connections — checking only one or two aspects is insufficient
if_condition: If validating schema markup for SEO implementation
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Validate Syntax Errors With Schema org

name: Use Schema.org Validator with Correct Input Method Based on Page Publication Status
then_action: Enter the full URL or paste the full JSON-LD script into the appropriate field, then click RUN TEST to retrieve validation results
if_condition: If testing schema markup for a published page, use FETCH URL input method; if testing schema for an unpublished page, use CODE SNIPPET input method in Schema.org validator
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Validate Syntax Errors With Schema org

name: Re-Validate JSON-LD After Every Correction Cycle
then_action: Paste the corrected JSON-LD back into Schema.org validator and run the test again, because the validator may only surface the first syntax error even when multiple errors exist
if_condition: If syntax errors are found and corrected in JSON-LD markup
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Validate Syntax Errors With Schema org

name: Use AI Assistance to Fix JSON-LD Syntax Errors When Validator Cannot Provide Fixes
then_action: Copy the full JSON-LD, submit it to ChatGPT with the prompt requesting silent correction output, then paste the corrected version back into the validator for re-testing
if_condition: If Schema.org validator identifies syntax errors in JSON-LD but does not provide correction guidance
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Validate Syntax Errors With Schema org

name: Growth Dashboard User Lifecycle Mapping

name: AI Travel Discovery Integration Rule
then_action: Then the OTA is likely losing ground in consumer discovery and booking conversion, as generative AI is actively reshaping how consumers discover and book travel

name: Monitor Incrementality of AI Platform Traffic vs Existing Channels
then_action: Track whether resulting traffic is incremental (not cannibalizing existing channels) and measure intent quality relative to Google Search, metasearch, and social media benchmarks to validate channel performance

source_title: Tripadvisor s Perplexity Deal AI for Customer Acquisition in Travel

name: Influencer-Led Booking Integration

then_action: Then conduct analysis of consumer trends driving social commerce uptake — including changing preferences of younger travelers — and incorporate findings into channel prioritization and content strategy to remain aligned with evolving booking behavior
if_condition: If a travel business is developing its annual marketing or distribution strategy and has not assessed current consumer preference shifts toward social commerce platforms for travel planning and purchasing
source_title: Social Commerce in Travel Opportunities and Consumer Trends   Skift Research

name: Things Not Strings Principle
then_action: Define entities as structured 'things' explicitly via schema markup rather than leaving machines to derive context from unstructured strings, as machines cannot derive contextual meaning as effectively as humans
if_condition: If content meaning depends on contextual interpretation beyond decoding raw strings of letters and numbers
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Why Is Structured Data Better Than Unstructured

name: Structured Data Application During Crawl-Render Phase
then_action: Add schema markup before indexing to ensure search engines receive structured data with explicit context during the parse and interpret stage, improving indexing accuracy
if_condition: If a webpage is being prepared for indexing and passes through crawling and rendering phases without schema markup
source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Why Is Structured Data Better Than Unstructured

name: High Uninstall Rates Negatively Impact App Visibility

if_condition: If an app experiences high uninstall rates among first-time installers during or after a Play Store experiment
source_title: A Guide to Google Play Store Listing Experiments Targeting the right metric

then_action: Select 'retained first-time installers' as the target metric, as Google recommends this for accurate A/B test results
if_condition: If an app has high brand recognition and receives many downloads in Google Play Store listing experiments
source_title: A Guide to Google Play Store Listing Experiments Targeting the right metric

name: Impression-to-Download Gap Signals Conversion Problem

if_condition: If a Google Play app receives high visibility or impressions but install and conversion rates remain low

name: Seasonality Insights from Store Listing Experiments
then_action: Then analyze store listing experiment data to identify seasonality trends and apply those takeaways to update the store listing page accordingly
if_condition: If a Google Play app experiences fluctuating install or conversion rates across different time periods

name: Higher Confidence Levels Require More Installers

name: Use Minimum 95% Confidence Level for A/B Tests

name: Use WebPage Schema Attributes to Establish Entity Relationships on Homepage

name: Schema Markup Required for Explicit Context

source_title: Schema Markup What It Is and Why It Matters in 2026 Why Is Structured Data Better Than Unstructured

name: Apply Person Schema for Individual Entities

name: Warn Against Relying Solely on Brand Building for Entity Recognition

name: JSON-LD Must Use Schema.org Vocabulary

name: Multi-Engine Schema Compatibility

name: Manual JSON-LD Preferred for Full Attribute Flexibility
then_action: Then writing JSON-LD manually is preferred over auto-generation tools, as it allows inclusion of any attribute for any schema type and enables cross-schema connections
if_condition: If schema markup quality and completeness are being optimized and specific or non-default attributes need to be included

name: Apply Five Key Schema Types for Entity Awareness
then_action: Use five specific schema types recommended for entity awareness rather than generic or excessive schema application — not every page requires schema markup simply because it is a webpage
if_condition: If an SEO professional is implementing schema markup to improve Google's awareness of entities on a website

name: Schema.org as Authoritative Source
then_action: Reference Schema.org as the authoritative body that creates and maintains schema markup rules — do not rely on unofficial or platform-specific definitions
if_condition: If creating or validating schema markup rules and vocabulary for structured data implementation

name: Do Not Add Schema Markup to Every Page by Default

name: Add Schema Markup for AI Discoverability

name: Increased Competitive Analysis Priority Due to Discoverability

name: Use Schema Markup to Accelerate Entity Recognition

if_condition: If a person, business, service, or product exists as a distinct entity in your content and lacks schema markup

name: Apply Organization Schema for Business Entities
then_action: Apply Organization schema markup to the business entity to help Google understand what the business is, accelerating knowledge panel eligibility and entity association

name: Restricted Elements on Custom Product Pages

name: Deprecated SEO Elements Persist Long After Replacement

name: Build Library Content Body to Compound Authority Over Time

name: Information Gain Requires Differentiation Not Just Depth

name: AI Visibility Pyramid Sequential Implementation
then_action: Then AI visibility efforts will be undermined, because AI tools frequently pull from search engine indexes — traditional SEO must be built first before adding AI-friendly content and credibility amplification layers

source_title: 17 Flagship Frameworks From 10 Years of Animalz Content   Animalz 1 Library vs Publication Part 3

name: BLUF Format Content Increases AI Citation Likelihood

if_condition: If content is structured to answer specific questions using Bottom Line Up Front (BLUF) format — with the direct answer stated immediately before supporting detail
source_title: 17 Flagship Frameworks From 10 Years of Animalz Content   Animalz 15 AI Visibility Pyramid

name: Third-Party Mentions Amplify AI Credibility Signals
then_action: Then AI models are more likely to treat that source as credible and authoritative, increasing the probability of citation in AI-powered search results

source_title: 17 Flagship Frameworks From 10 Years of Animalz Content   Animalz 15 AI Visibility Pyramid

name: Attic Area Terms Prohibition

name: AI Search Impact Analysis Caveat

name: Long-Tail Keyword Targeting Formula
then_action: Target long-tail keywords using the formula: [Problem or Task] + [Qualifier: Company Size, Role, or Vertical] + [Optional: Timeframe or Outcome] — e.g., 'project management software for remote engineering teams' (KD: 12) instead of broad terms like 'project management software' (KD: 78)

name: Embed Free Tools in Content to Maximize Backlinks

name: B2B SaaS SEO Blog Post Format Prioritization
then_action: Prioritize four formats: (1) how-to guides targeting 'how to [do what your product does]' keywords, (2) comparison pages for 'Best [Tool Category] for [ICP]' and '[Competitor] alternative' articles, (3) use-case pages for '[Your Product] for [Vertical or Role]', and (4) integration pages for '[Your Product] with [Popular Tool] integration guide'

name: Publish on Own Domain Before Syndicating

name: LinkedIn Algorithm Founder Content Preference

name: No Keyword Strategy Warning
then_action: Do not publish the content; first assign a long-tail keyword using the [Problem + Qualifier + Outcome] formula and validate keyword difficulty before proceeding

name: Internal Linking Requirement Per Article

name: New Blog Launch Sequence: Refresh Before Create

name: Organic Discovery Threshold Warning for Low-Traffic Sites
then_action: Implement active distribution via 3 core channels immediately, as organic-only discovery means new content will go unnoticed for months

name: Keyword Difficulty Ceiling for Low-Authority Domains

then_action: Allow keyword targeting up to KD 40; blogs with DA under 30 must stay at KD 20 or below, and the initial new content target should be KD 15 or below regardless of tier

name: Connect Google Search Console to CRM for Content Attribution

name: Meta Section General Usage Restriction

name: High Keyword Competition Requires Differentiated SEO Content Strategy

name: Ranking Drop Warning: Traffic Loss Is Non-Linear
