if_condition: If a brand's organic search strategy has not yet accounted for AI-generated answer surfaces (AI Overviews, AI Mode)
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 2

name: Monitor AI Visibility and Gap-Fill with New Content
then_action: Then create new content specifically targeting those uncovered topics and contact sites that are currently being cited to seek brand features, thereby improving AI mention and citation coverage
if_condition: If a brand is tracking AI visibility and identifies topics where it is not being cited or mentioned by AI indexes
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 3

name: Google Crawler 15MB File Size Limit
then_action: Content beyond the first 15MB will be ignored by Google's crawler by default; ensure critical content appears within the first 15MB of any file
if_condition: If a file served to Googlebot exceeds 15MB in size
source_title: Google Crawler User Agent Overview Technical properties of Google s crawlers and fetchers

name: User-Triggered Fetchers Are Not Automatic Crawlers
then_action: Classify the request as a user-triggered fetcher rather than an automatic crawler, meaning it acts on end-user requests and not automatic discovery — robots.txt blocking may not apply the same way
if_condition: If a Google fetcher makes a request to a website and the request appears to originate from a user action (e.g., Google Search rendering, link preview)
source_title: Google Crawler User Agent Overview

name: Last-Modified Header Date Format Compliance
then_action: Format the date strictly as 'Weekday, DD Mon YYYY HH:MM:SS Timezone' (e.g., 'Fri, 4 Sep 1998 19:15:56 GMT'); non-compliant formatting may cause caching failures
if_condition: If Last-Modified HTTP response header is implemented on pages crawled by Googlebot
source_title: Google Crawler User Agent Overview Technical properties of Google s crawlers and fetchers

name: AdsBot Ignores Global Wildcard robots.txt Rules
then_action: AdsBot will ignore those wildcard rules and continue crawling unless explicitly blocked with a specific 'AdsBot' user agent directive in robots.txt
if_condition: If a site uses only the global wildcard user agent (*) in robots.txt to block crawlers and AdsBot is among the crawlers
source_title: Google Crawler User Agent Overview

name: Build Multi-Layer Trust Signals Beyond Single Channel SEO
then_action: Diversify trust-building across multiple influence layers including event presence, editorial reputation, community engagement, and cross-platform content to reduce single-channel dependency
if_condition: If a brand's authority and trust signals are concentrated in a single channel such as Google organic rankings without presence in events, community platforms, or reputation-building content
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 4

name: Search Intent Alignment for AI and Traditional Search
then_action: Then content MUST be matched to what people are actually searching for, as intent-aligned content performs best across both AI systems and traditional search engines
if_condition: If content is being optimized for both AI-driven answer engines and traditional search engines simultaneously
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 5

name: LCP Must Load Within 2.5 Seconds
then_action: The Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) must load within 2.5 seconds to meet Google's 'Good' threshold and avoid negative impact on page experience ranking signals
if_condition: If a webpage is being evaluated for Core Web Vitals compliance
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part

name: MCP Integration for Advanced SEO Analysis
then_action: Then they SHOULD leverage Model Context Protocols (MCPs) with tools like Claude and Ahrefs to allow LLMs to directly access and analyze SEO data, shifting AI from assistant to analyst role
if_condition: If an SEO team needs to perform advanced data analysis tasks such as finding untargeted keywords, researching international SEO opportunities, or discovering competitor keyword strategies at scale
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 5

name: Build Brand Authority for LLM Referencing
then_action: Build brand equity through quality backlinks, media mentions, and citations from authoritative sources, since LLMs reference trusted and credible sources when generating answers
if_condition: If targeting visibility in AI-generated answers (LLM outputs) such as ChatGPT or AI Overviews
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 6

name: Use Keywords to Estimate Demand Despite AI Search Growth
then_action: Continue using keyword search volume as the primary demand estimation method, since reliable AI search volume data does not yet exist and Google Search still drives 40% of traffic vs 0.24% from ChatGPT
if_condition: If trying to measure topic demand or growth trends when AI search tools (e.g., ChatGPT) are part of the channel mix
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 6

name: Multi-Channel Resilience Requirement
then_action: Then the strategy carries unacceptable concentration risk and SHOULD be restructured to build multi-channel resilience, reducing dependency on any single platform
if_condition: If an SEO strategy relies solely on Google as the primary traffic and visibility channel
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 5

name: AI Tool Adoption Warning
then_action: Then they risk falling behind competitors who are leveraging AI tools effectively, and should adopt AI assistance for repetitive optimization tasks while reserving human effort for insight, strategy, and creativity
if_condition: If an SEO practitioner or content team is not currently using AI tools for content optimization tasks such as identifying search intent, finding topical gaps, or competitor coverage analysis
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 5

name: Group AI Prompts Under Shared Parent Topics for Monitoring
then_action: Group similar prompts under shared parent topics, since two completely different prompts can address the same underlying topic — enabling scalable content targeting and AI visibility tracking
if_condition: If monitoring AI-driven search activity where users submit long, highly specific prompts across different phrasings
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 6

name: Do Not Dynamically Inject Content Above the Fold
then_action: Avoid inserting dynamic content above the fold; place it below the fold instead to prevent shifting existing page content downward and degrading CLS score
if_condition: If dynamic content such as banners or notifications is being added to the page above the fold after initial load
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 3

name: Retroactive AI Visibility Through Good Structure
then_action: Audit and update content for readability and clear structural organization (headings, digestible sections, skimmable layout), as well-structured pre-AI content can still achieve high AI visibility without full rewrites
if_condition: If existing content was created before AI search considerations were factored in
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 6

name: Citable Expert Content Strategy
then_action: Then content must be built around authentic expertise that gets talked about and referenced throughout the industry, prioritizing original research and proprietary data over volume-based content production
if_condition: If the goal is to earn organic citations, backlinks, and shares from industry sources including AI-generated answers
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 5

name: Avoid Lazy Loading Above-the-Fold Images
then_action: Remove lazy loading from those images, as it delays their rendering and can increase LCP time rather than improve it
if_condition: If lazy loading is applied to images positioned above the fold
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part

name: Keyword-Only SEO Strategy Obsolescence
then_action: Flag the strategy as misaligned with Google's LLM-based query interpretation; update content to address inferred user intent and multi-constraint scenarios rather than surface-level keyword presence
if_condition: If an SEO strategy relies primarily on simple keyword matching and exact-match optimization without addressing semantic intent or topic depth
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same

name: Three Core Signals for AI Overviews Inclusion
then_action: Then prioritize branded web mentions (linked and unlinked), branded anchor texts in inbound links, and branded search volume as the three most important factors
if_condition: If optimizing a website for inclusion in AI Overviews
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 7

name: Bounce Rate Definition Boundary
then_action: Count this as a Bounce for that page; Bounce Rate only applies to the first page of a session and requires no interaction before leaving
if_condition: A visitor lands on a page and leaves the site without clicking on anything else on that page
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Bounce Rate vs Exit Rate

name: Unlinked Brand Mentions Count Toward AI Authority
then_action: Then those unlinked mentions contribute to branded web mention signals that influence AI citation likelihood and should be actively pursued as part of brand-building
if_condition: If a brand name is mentioned on any web page, even without a hyperlink
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 7

name: Unlinked Brand Mentions Signal Authority in AI Systems
then_action: Then those mentions carry significant weight in AI retrieval systems and should be actively pursued as part of a GEO strategy, tracked via share of voice and brand mention monitoring tools
if_condition: If a brand is mentioned across third-party sources without a hyperlink (unlinked brand mentions)
source_title: Generative Engine Optimization A Practical Guide How Do You Perform Generative Engine Optimization

name: Fan-Out Query Expansion Detection
then_action: Recognize that Google's Gemini LLM may decompose it into up to 10 related sub-searches behind the scenes; content must address multi-dimensional trade-offs to be eligible for inclusion in AI Overview responses
if_condition: If a user submits a complex query involving multiple constraints (e.g., processing power, portability, AND budget simultaneously)
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same

name: SEO Fundamentals Remain Required but Visibility Location Shifts
then_action: Then update success measurement approaches to account for AI Overview appearances and AI citations, as visibility now occurs in different places beyond traditional SERPs
if_condition: If a site has strong brand authority, high-quality content, and solid SEO fundamentals but is not measuring AI-driven visibility channels
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 7

name: AI Overview Source Eligibility Mapping
then_action: That content is more likely to be pulled as a source by AI Overviews, which aggregate insights from multiple sources to answer queries at the top of search results
if_condition: If content provides comprehensive answers that synthesize multiple related subtopics within a subject area
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same

name: No Bounce If Interaction Occurred
then_action: Do NOT count this as a bounce; a bounce requires the visitor to leave without any on-page interaction
if_condition: A visitor clicked on something on a page before leaving the site
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Bounce Rate vs Exit Rate

name: No Bounce If Not Entry Page
then_action: Do NOT count this as a bounce; record it only as an Exit for that page since Bounce Rate applies exclusively to session entry pages
if_condition: A visitor exits from a page that was NOT their initial landing page during the session
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Bounce Rate vs Exit Rate

name: Cache-Control Max-Age Configuration for Crawl Efficiency
then_action: Set the Cache-Control max-age field to the expected number of seconds the content will remain unchanged (e.g., Cache-Control: max-age=94043) to signal recrawl timing to Googlebot
if_condition: If implementing HTTP caching to optimize Googlebot re-crawl frequency
source_title: Google Crawler User Agent Overview Technical properties of Google s crawlers and fetchers

name: Maintain Content Structure for AI Visibility
then_action: Structure content for easy skimming with clear key details, organize into small digestible parts, and ensure readability — as LLMs favor structured, credible content when generating answers
if_condition: If content is being created or optimized for search visibility in an AI-driven search environment
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 6

name: Brand Authority Drives AI Citation Preference
then_action: Then it is significantly more likely to be cited by AI systems like ChatGPT, creating a reinforcing cycle of backlinks, domain rating growth, and further AI citations
if_condition: If a website has a Domain Rating above 80 and strong brand recognition (e.g., comparable to Forbes, Mayo Clinic, Harvard)
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 7

name: Early Mover Advantage for AI Visibility
then_action: Then begin building brand authority signals immediately to capture early-mover advantage before the space becomes competitive
if_condition: If a brand has not yet begun optimizing for AI visibility and most competitors are also inactive in this area
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 7

name: AI Overview Click Loss Warning
then_action: Expect approximately 34% reduction in click-through rate compared to historical #1 position benchmarks; do not assume traditional CTR performance holds
if_condition: If a page ranks #1 organically on Google and an AI Overview appears above it for that query
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same

name: Ad Landing Page Destination Match Rule
then_action: High bounce rate will likely result; send users to the specific product or category page that matches the ad to meet user expectations and reduce bounce
if_condition: If users click ads for specific products but are sent to a homepage instead of the relevant product page
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Why Do People Bounce

name: Single-Page Session Intent Satisfaction Rule
then_action: The bounce should be interpreted as a success signal rather than a failure, as the user found exactly what they needed without needing to navigate further
if_condition: If a page records a high bounce rate but the content is self-contained and fully answers user intent (e.g., a complete recipe, a direct answer page)
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Why Do People Bounce

name: GA4 Engagement Criteria to Avoid Bounce Classification
then_action: Then the session is classified as 'not engaged' (bounced), and it counts toward the bounce rate as the inverse of the engagement rate
if_condition: If a GA4 session fails to meet any of the three engagement criteria: duration greater than 10 seconds, at least one conversion event triggered, or at least two page/screen views generated
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It 2

name: Satisfy Search Intent to Prevent Pogo-Sticking
then_action: Then users will bounce back to search results (pogo-sticking), signaling poor relevance to Google and negatively impacting SEO rankings; rewrite or restructure content to align with the dominant search intent for those keywords
if_condition: If a page's content does not match what searchers expect for the keywords it targets (e.g., informational query landing on a product page, or vice versa)
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It How to Improve Your Bounce Rate

name: High Bounce Rate Content/UX Issue Signal
then_action: Then investigate potential issues with content quality, user experience, page layout, or copywriting as likely root causes
if_condition: If a site's bounce rate exceeds the upper threshold for its category benchmark (e.g., above 45% for ecommerce or above 51% average)
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It

name: Unsupported HTTP Caching Directives Warning
then_action: Those additional caching directives will not be supported by Google's crawlers and will be ignored; only ETag/If-None-Match and Last-Modified/If-Modified-Since are recognized
if_condition: If HTTP caching directives other than ETag/If-None-Match or Last-Modified/If-Modified-Since are implemented for Googlebot
source_title: Google Crawler User Agent Overview Technical properties of Google s crawlers and fetchers

name: Identify Low-Performing Pages via Google Analytics Engaged Session Filter
then_action: Then classify that page as a low-performing 'Donkey' page and prioritize it for bounce rate improvement efforts, aiming to convert it into a high-performing 'Unicorn' page
if_condition: If a page's Engaged Sessions per User and Average Engagement Time fall below the site average when filtered in Google Analytics Explore section
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It How to Improve Your Bounce Rate

name: Replace Intrusive Popups with Exit-Intent Popups
then_action: Then remove or disable those interruptive popups and replace them with exit-intent popups instead; exit-intent popups can reduce bounce rate by 10% when 10% of viewers convert, without degrading user experience
if_condition: If a site uses popups that trigger immediately upon page load or interrupt active user browsing
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It How to Improve Your Bounce Rate

name: Remove Unused Plugins and Scripts to Improve Page Load Speed
then_action: Then remove those unused plugins and scripts; slow page loading increases bounce rate, and Google PageSpeed Insights Opportunities section should be used to identify specific speed bottlenecks
if_condition: If a site has unused plugins or scripts installed that add to page load time without serving active functionality
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It How to Improve Your Bounce Rate

name: Open Internal and External Links in New Tabs
then_action: Then configure all internal and external links to open in new tabs (target='_blank') to prevent users from leaving the page entirely, thereby reducing bounce rate
if_condition: If a page contains internal or external hyperlinks that, when clicked, navigate users away from the current page within the same browser tab
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It How to Improve Your Bounce Rate

name: Implement Heatmap Tracking to Diagnose Bounce Causes
then_action: Then add JavaScript heatmap tracking code (e.g., Hotjar or similar tools) to capture real user behavior; without this data, identifying bounce rate causes remains guesswork
if_condition: If bounce rate is high on specific pages but the root cause of user drop-off is unknown
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It How to Improve Your Bounce Rate

name: Broken Link Building: Provide Broken Link List in Outreach
then_action: Include a specific list of broken links (404 pages) found on their site within the outreach email, then suggest your working link as a relevant replacement for one or more of those broken links
if_condition: If sending a broken link building outreach email to a target website
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide What Is Broken Link Building

name: High Exit Rate on Contact Pages Is Acceptable
then_action: Interpret this as likely positive — users completed their intended task; do not flag as a problem without further investigation
if_condition: A contact page or task-completion page shows a high Exit Rate
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Bounce Rate vs Exit Rate

name: Navigation and Readability UX Rule
then_action: Users will bounce due to bad UX; redesign navigation and reading experience to be intuitive and frictionless to reduce bounce rate
if_condition: If site navigation is complex, cluttered, or content is difficult to read due to poor formatting or layout
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Why Do People Bounce

name: Bounce Rate Benchmark Comparison Requirement
then_action: Then compare the site's bounce rate against benchmarks for its specific category (e.g., ecommerce: 20-45%, blogs: up to 90%) rather than using a universal standard
if_condition: If evaluating whether a site's bounce rate is good or bad
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It

name: Use Check My Links Extension to Validate Broken Links on Resource Pages
then_action: Run the Check My Links Chrome Extension on that page to quickly identify which external links are broken, then proceed with outreach offering replacement content
if_condition: If a resource page with many external links has been identified as a BLB candidate
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Target High External Link Count Pages for BLB
then_action: Prioritize that page as a BLB target, as pages with more external links are exponentially more likely to contain at least one broken link (a page with 187 external links is 38x more likely to have a dead link than a page with 5)
if_condition: If a page has a high number of external links (e.g., 100+ external links on resource or 'links' pages)
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Use Google Search Strings to Find Resource Pages
then_action: Use Google search operators: 'Keyword' + inurl:resources, 'Keyword' + intitle:links, 'Keyword' + 'helpful resources', or 'Keyword' + 'useful resources' to surface pages with many external links
if_condition: If conducting broken link building outreach and needing to identify candidate resource pages at scale
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Bounce Rate as Ranking Factor Risk
then_action: Then flag it as a potential Google ranking risk, since bounce rate correlates closely with first-page rankings and bounced visitors do not convert
if_condition: If a site's bounce rate is elevated relative to its category benchmark
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It

name: Googlebot IP Origin and Geo-Blocking Risk
then_action: Googlebot primarily egresses from US IP addresses; blocking US traffic will prevent normal crawling, causing Googlebot to attempt crawling from other countries as a fallback, which may produce inconsistent crawl behavior
if_condition: If a server blocks US-based IP addresses or applies geo-restrictions that affect United States traffic
source_title: Google Crawler User Agent Overview Technical properties of Google s crawlers and fetchers

name: Use Wikipedia Dead Link Technique for High-Authority BLB Targets
then_action: Apply the Wikipedia 'Dead Link' technique to identify 404 pages with many existing backlinks, making them high-value BLB targets with maximum outreach potential
if_condition: If seeking 404 pages that already have a large number of inbound links pointing to them
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Wikipedia Broken Link Search Requires Broad Keywords
then_action: Use broad keywords in the Google search string (e.g., 'marketing' instead of 'SEO') with format: site:wikipedia.org 'keyword' intext:'dead link' to surface any remotely related Wikipedia entries
if_condition: If conducting broken link building research using Wikipedia as a source
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 2

name: Scale BLB by Finding All Sites Linking to the Same Broken URL
then_action: Input the dead URL into Semrush or another link analysis tool to find all other sites linking to the same broken URL, enabling bulk outreach to multiple prospects from a single broken link discovery
if_condition: If a broken (dead) link has been identified on a resource page
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Do Not Attempt Direct Wikipedia Link Replacement
then_action: Do NOT attempt to replace the broken Wikipedia link with a link to your site, as Wikipedia editors will remove undeserving links within hours
if_condition: If a broken link is found on a Wikipedia page and the target site has relevant replacement content
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 2

name: Use Backlink Analysis to Amplify Broken Link Opportunities
then_action: Input the broken URL into a backlink analysis tool to discover all other sites linking to that broken page, expanding the pool of outreach targets beyond the original source
if_condition: If a dead/broken link is identified on Wikipedia or any other source
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 2

name: Use Semrush Site Audit to Discover Broken External Links
then_action: Navigate to Site Audit → Warnings → '# external links are broken' to identify broken external link opportunities
if_condition: If conducting broken link building using Semrush and looking for broken links on crawled domains
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 2

name: Broken Link Outreach: Verify Contact Has Link-Swap Authority
then_action: Then do not send the outreach email to that contact — only reach out to someone who has the power to swap the dead link with a link to your site, as contacting the wrong person wastes effort and reduces campaign effectiveness
if_condition: If the identified outreach contact cannot directly edit or update the page containing the broken link
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 4

name: Use Semrush Backlink Analytics to Find Competitor Broken Pages
then_action: Use Semrush Backlink Analytics → Indexed Pages tab → tick 'Broken Pages' option → click 'Backlinks' to identify all sites linking to competitor broken pages
if_condition: If conducting broken link building and targeting competitor domains for link opportunity discovery
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 2

name: Broken Link Outreach: Use Contact Form for Single-Author Blogs
then_action: Then use the site's contact form as the outreach method, since a single author is both the content owner and the decision-maker for link changes
if_condition: If the broken link target site is a single-author blog
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 4

name: GA4 vs UA Bounce Rate Definition Mismatch Warning
then_action: Then treat the metrics as non-comparable: UA bounce rate counted sessions with only one pageview and no further interaction, while GA4 counts sessions that are not engaged (missing all three engagement criteria), making direct comparisons misleading
if_condition: If comparing bounce rate metrics between Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and Universal Analytics (UA) reports for the same site or time period
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It 2

name: Traffic Source Bounce Rate Adjustment
then_action: Then expect significantly higher bounce rates as a baseline and adjust performance benchmarks accordingly, since these channels inherently produce higher bounce rates than email or referral traffic
if_condition: If a site's primary traffic sources include display ads or social media
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It

name: Use Gentle Non-Pushy Link Suggestion in Outreach
then_action: SHOULD gently nudge the recipient toward the replacement without directly asking for a link; AVOID being pushy as it reduces outreach conversion rates
if_condition: If suggesting a replacement link to a site owner in an outreach email
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 3

name: Offer Replacement Content for Dead Links
then_action: MUST provide a direct replacement resource for the dead link to make link swapping an easy decision for the site owner
if_condition: If a broken link is identified on a target page and outreach is being prepared
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 3

name: RDFa Lite Schema.org Vocab Declaration
then_action: Add the attribute value pair vocab="https://schema.org/" to the body or some other enclosing tag — this is a MUST requirement for valid RDFa Lite Schema.org implementation
if_condition: If implementing Schema.org markup using RDFa Lite syntax without a vocab attribute on the body or enclosing tag
source_title: Data model   Schema org Mapping to RDFa Lite

name: Skip Abandoned Sites in Broken Link Outreach
then_action: SHOULD skip outreach to that site entirely as abandoned sites almost never respond, and time is better spent on active sites
if_condition: If a target site shows signs of complete abandonment — no recent updates, no active owner, no engagement signals
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 3

name: Report All Broken Links on Target Page in Outreach Email
then_action: SHOULD identify and point out all broken links on the page with specific details about each dead link, not just the one being targeted for replacement
if_condition: If sending an outreach email to a site owner about a broken link on their page
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 3

name: Broken Link Building: Offer Help Before Requesting Link
then_action: Rewrite the outreach email to lead with help first — provide a specific list of broken links (404 pages) found on the target site before suggesting your working link as a replacement
if_condition: If conducting broken link building outreach and the email only requests a link, share, or favor without providing anything of value in return
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide What Is Broken Link Building

name: Content Gap: Target Weak First-Page Results
then_action: Prioritize creating content that addresses all identified gaps to maximize chances of outranking existing results on Google's first page
if_condition: If first page results for a target keyword contain content that is outdated, incomplete, hard to read, or unimpressive
source_title: Content Gap Analysis The Complete Guide

name: Broken Link Outreach: Target Decision-Maker Directly
then_action: Then go through the site's staff page to find a specific person with authority to fix broken links — look for titles like 'editor', 'blog manager', 'webmaster', or 'web admin' — rather than using generic contact@email or contact forms, to avoid emails ending up in trash or spam
if_condition: If conducting broken link building outreach and the target site has multiple authors or departments (100+ authors or 50+ departments)
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 4

name: RDFa Lite Attribute Mapping from Microdata
then_action: Replace itemprop with property, drop itemscope entirely, replace itemtype with typeof, and add vocab="https://schema.org/" to the body or enclosing tag
if_condition: If converting existing Schema.org Microdata markup to RDFa Lite syntax
source_title: Data model   Schema org Mapping to RDFa Lite

name: Use PropertyValue Pairs for Non-URI Identifier Types
then_action: Implement the identifier property using PropertyValue pairs with 'name' and 'identifier' fields to represent the identifier type and value
if_condition: If an identifier type (e.g., ISBN, product ID) does not have a standard URI form
source_title: Data model   Schema org Background notes

name: Research Dead Content via Archive.org Before Creating Replacement
then_action: MUST use Archive.org to research what the dead page originally contained before creating a replacement resource
if_condition: If a dead URL has been identified as a broken link building opportunity
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 3

name: Broken Link Building: Replace 404 Pages with Working Links
then_action: Identify those 404 pages, create or confirm working replacement content on your target website, and propose your URL as the replacement in outreach — this qualifies the opportunity for broken link building
if_condition: If a target website has outbound links pointing to 404 (dead) pages and your site has relevant working content that could serve as a replacement
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide What Is Broken Link Building

name: Only podcast feeds indexed in Google Search
then_action: note that only podcast feeds are indexed in Google Search; other RSS/Atom feeds are not indexed unless they fail to follow the Atom or RSS specification
if_condition: if a site publishes RSS or Atom feeds and expects them to appear in Google Search results
source_title: Google Feedfetcher

name: CLS Threshold for Visual Stability
then_action: CLS must be less than 0.1 to provide a good user experience and meet Google's Core Web Vitals standard
if_condition: If Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) is measured on a webpage
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance What Are Core Web Vitals

name: Core Web Vitals Audit via Google Search Console
then_action: Navigate to the 'Experience' section in Google Search Console, review individual Core Web Vital reports for details, and supplement with PageSpeed Insights or Semrush Site Audit for deeper performance analysis
if_condition: If a website owner wants to identify Core Web Vitals issues affecting their pages
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance What Are Core Web Vitals

name: INP Threshold for Responsive Experience
then_action: INP must be 200 milliseconds or less to qualify as a fast and responsive experience under Google's Core Web Vitals
if_condition: If Interaction to Next Paint (INP) is measured on a webpage
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance What Are Core Web Vitals

name: Competitor Keyword Gap: Use Tools to Find Untapped Opportunities
then_action: Use tools like Semrush to find competitor top-performing keywords, then check each first page result to identify content with holes where you can create significantly better content
if_condition: If you want to identify keywords where competitors rank but your site does not
source_title: Content Gap Analysis The Complete Guide

name: Core Web Vitals Failure Warning for Rankings
then_action: The page may be negatively impacted in Google search rankings due to poor page experience signals, and immediate performance optimization should be prioritized
if_condition: If any Core Web Vital metric (LCP, INP, or CLS) fails to meet its respective threshold (LCP ≥ 2.5s, INP > 200ms, CLS ≥ 0.1)
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance What Are Core Web Vitals

name: Third-Party Script Removal for High INP
then_action: Remove or defer non-critical third-party scripts to reduce INP time and restore page responsiveness
if_condition: If INP time exceeds 200ms and non-essential third-party scripts such as Google Analytics or heatmaps are present on the page
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 2

name: INP Priority by Page Type
then_action: Treat INP as a critical performance metric and prioritize optimization; for pure content pages such as blog posts where scrolling is the primary interaction, INP is lower priority
if_condition: If a page is a login page, sign-up page, or e-commerce page requiring add-to-cart interactions
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 2

name: JavaScript Deferral for INP Optimization
then_action: Minimize or defer JavaScript loading and break down long browser tasks into smaller chunks to allow user interaction during page load
if_condition: If a page has high INP scores and contains render-blocking or long-running JavaScript tasks
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 2

name: Set Explicit Size Attributes on All Media Elements
then_action: Set size attribute dimensions for all media elements so the browser reserves the correct space before content loads, preventing unexpected layout shifts
if_condition: If images or videos are included on a page without explicit width and height size attributes defined in HTML
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 3

name: Use mainEntity for Primary Entity Only
then_action: Use the 'about' property instead of 'mainEntity' for multiple entities; reserve mainEntity exclusively for the single primary entity of the page
if_condition: If a page has multiple topics or entities being described
source_title: Data model   Schema org Background notes

name: CLS Stability Requirement
then_action: Fix layout instability to maintain a low CLS score; ensure all elements remain stable in position throughout the loading process
if_condition: If page elements such as images, ads, or dynamic content blocks shift position during page loading
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 2

name: INP Threshold Compliance
then_action: Achieve INP time of 200ms or less; flag as poor performance if INP exceeds 500ms and requires immediate remediation
if_condition: If a page requires user interactions such as clicks on menus, sign-up buttons, or add-to-cart actions
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 2

name: Use sameAs for Well-Known Indirect Identifiers
then_action: Apply the 'sameAs' property to reference that well-known page, rather than using url or mainEntityOfPage
if_condition: If a well-known page (e.g., Wikipedia, Wikidata) indirectly identifies the entity but is not the entity's official page
source_title: Data model   Schema org Background notes

name: Do Not Treat URL-Valued Properties as identifier Specializations
then_action: Do not treat that property as a specialization of the identifier property; identifier should only be used for properties that uniquely identify individual entities
if_condition: If a property holds a URL value but does not specifically identify a single distinct individual entity
source_title: Data model   Schema org Background notes

name: Do Not Expect Unannounced JSON-LD Context File Changes
then_action: Then AVOID building logic that expects the context file to change without a long warning period, as unannounced changes would break deployed installations at scale
if_condition: If an application is deployed and relies on the Schema.org JSON-LD context file at https://schema.org/docs/jsonldcontext.json
source_title: Developers   Schema org

name: LCP Threshold for Good User Experience
then_action: LCP must be less than 2.5 seconds to provide a good user experience and meet Google's Core Web Vitals standard
if_condition: If Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) is measured on a webpage
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance What Are Core Web Vitals

name: Minify CSS to Reduce LCP Delays
then_action: Minify CSS files to reduce code size and eliminate unnecessary delays in LCP rendering time
if_condition: If a page has render-blocking or bulky CSS files contributing to a slow LCP score
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part

name: Remove Unnecessary Third-Party Scripts to Improve LCP
then_action: Remove unnecessary third-party scripts, as each script adds approximately 34 ms of load time and contributes to slower LCP
if_condition: If a page has a poor or needs-improvement LCP score and contains third-party scripts
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part

name: Use Google Search Console for Site-Wide LCP Analysis
then_action: Use Google Search Console to access site-wide LCP data, rather than analyzing random individual pages via PageSpeed Insights alone
if_condition: If auditing LCP performance across an entire site rather than individual pages
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part

name: Do Not Prioritize CWV Score Over Content Quality
then_action: Stop and reprioritize — chasing metric numbers at the expense of content quality and user experience is counterproductive to overall SEO performance
if_condition: If optimization efforts for Core Web Vitals begin to compromise content quality or user experience
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part

name: Feedfetcher robots.txt bypass
then_action: do not expect Feedfetcher to follow robots.txt rules, as it acts as a direct agent of human users and ignores robots.txt entirely
if_condition: if robots.txt rules are configured to block Feedfetcher-Google user agent
source_title: Google Feedfetcher

name: Reserve Dedicated Space for Ad Elements
then_action: Reserve dedicated space for ad elements before they load to prevent sudden appearance from causing layout shifts
if_condition: If ad elements are present on a page and no space is pre-reserved in the layout for them
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 3

name: Accept Text Strings for Structured Type Properties
then_action: Then accept plain text strings as valid values rather than rejecting them as errors
if_condition: If a schema.org property expects a structured type such as Person, Place, or Organization
source_title: Data model   Schema org Conformance

name: Schema.org Vocabulary Interoperability
then_action: Then it SHOULD be used alongside other vocabularies sharing the same data model and standards such as JSON-LD, Microdata, and RDFa
if_condition: If schema.org structured data is implemented on a webpage or data system
source_title: Data model   Schema org

name: Avoid Treating Unexpected Structures as Validation Errors
then_action: Then do not treat them as hard errors; treat type/property associations as guidelines rather than formal rules
if_condition: If a validator or checker encounters unexpected type/property combinations or structures in schema.org markup
source_title: Data model   Schema org Conformance

name: Search Engines Apply Best-Effort Interpretation to Non-Strict Markup
then_action: Then expect search engines to still attempt to interpret and use the markup under a 'some data is better than none' approach rather than ignoring it entirely
if_condition: If structured markup does not strictly follow schema.org schemas but still contains relevant data
source_title: Data model   Schema org Conformance

name: Combine Multiple Independent Types in Entity Descriptions
then_action: Then include all relevant types in the entity description and combine properties from multiple types rather than restricting to a single type
if_condition: If an entity logically belongs to multiple schema.org types simultaneously (e.g., both Book and Product)
source_title: Data model   Schema org Conformance

name: Allow Role and URL Types Across All Properties
then_action: Then allow these types to be used without restriction across all properties rather than limiting them to specific associations
if_condition: If schema.org markup uses Role or URL types in conjunction with any property
source_title: Data model   Schema org Conformance

name: Validate Underlying Format Compliance Over Schema Strictness
then_action: Then prioritize checking application-specific patterns and compliance with underlying formats (JSON-LD, Microdata, RDFa) rather than enforcing strict type/property associations
if_condition: If checking schema.org markup for compliance
source_title: Data model   Schema org Conformance

name: Schema.org Canonical Machine Representation
then_action: Then RDF/Turtle MUST be used as the canonical machine representation
if_condition: If schema.org structured data is being serialized in machine-readable format
source_title: Data model   Schema org

name: Schema.org Multiple Inheritance Hierarchy Requirement
then_action: Then it MUST be arranged within a multiple inheritance hierarchy where each type may be a sub-class of multiple parent types
if_condition: If a type is defined in schema.org structured data markup
source_title: Data model   Schema org

name: Artificial Type Creation Warning for Domain/Range
then_action: Then be aware this may create artificial types purely for domain/range purposes, which can introduce unintended complexity in the data model
if_condition: If multiple domains and ranges are assigned to a single property in schema.org
source_title: Data model   Schema org

name: Schema.org Property Domain and Range Definition
then_action: Then it MUST specify one or more domain types and one or more range types, and property values MUST be instances of at least one of the specified range types
if_condition: If a property is defined in schema.org using domainIncludes and rangeIncludes
source_title: Data model   Schema org

name: Avoid Treating Schema.org as Universal Ontology
then_action: Then AVOID this approach — schema.org is NOT a global or universal ontology; it is scoped for search engine optimization and structured data markup use cases only
if_condition: If schema.org vocabulary is being used to model all-encompassing real-world knowledge or as a global knowledge representation system
source_title: Data model   Schema org

name: Unique Content Required Per Page for Ranking
then_action: Then write 100% unique content for every page intended to rank in search engines; duplicate content prevents individual pages from ranking effectively
if_condition: If a site has multiple pages with highly similar or nearly identical content (e.g., location pages with same body text but different addresses)
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 2

name: CLS Score Must Be 0.1 or Below
then_action: The CLS score must be 0.1 or less to meet good performance requirements; scores between 0.1 and 0.25 need improvement, and scores above 0.25 are deemed poor
if_condition: If a page's Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) score is being evaluated for performance standards
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 3

name: Evaluate Pending Terms for Core Incorporation or Removal
then_action: Evaluate the term for either promotion to core schema.org status or removal based on usage, adoption, and discussions
if_condition: If a schema.org term has been in 'pending' status and has accumulated usage data, adoption signals, and community discussion
source_title: Extending Schemas   Schema org

name: Prioritize Vocabulary Likely to Be Consumed Over Merely Published
then_action: Place high emphasis on vocabulary likely to be consumed (i.e., used by search engines and data consumers) rather than vocabulary that is merely published by webmasters
if_condition: If evaluating which schema.org vocabulary terms to promote or expand
source_title: Extending Schemas   Schema org

name: Use mainEntityOfPage to Express Page-Entity Relationship
then_action: Use mainEntityOfPage on the entity to point to the page, even if the page is not recognized as the authoritative source for that entity
if_condition: If a page describes a primary entity and the relationship between the page and that entity needs to be explicitly declared in structured data
source_title: Data model   Schema org Background notes

name: Reserve url Property for Authoritative Pages
then_action: Use the url property only for official or authoritative web pages (e.g., the item's official website); do not use url as a generic identifier for non-authoritative pages
if_condition: If assigning a URL value to an entity in structured data markup
source_title: Data model   Schema org Background notes

name: Feedfetcher blocking via error status codes
then_action: configure your server to return a 404, 410, or other error status code specifically to the Feedfetcher-Google user agent, or work directly with your hosting service to restrict feed access
if_condition: if you need to prevent Feedfetcher from crawling your RSS or Atom feed
source_title: Google Feedfetcher

name: Duplicate Content Crawl Budget Waste
then_action: Crawl budget is wasted on duplicate pages that Google refuses to index, reducing the overall indexing efficiency of the site
if_condition: If a website (especially large ecommerce sites) contains large amounts of duplicate or near-duplicate pages
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide What Is Duplicate Content

name: Scraped or Copied External Content Penalty Risk
then_action: Google may apply a manual penalty or completely deindex the site; this is one of the few scenarios where Google issues an actual duplicate content penalty
if_condition: If a site purposely scrapes or copies content from other external websites
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide What Is Duplicate Content

name: In-House SEO Team Viability Condition
then_action: Then executing enterprise SEO with the existing in-house team is a viable implementation path
if_condition: If the organization already has an extensive multi-department internal team capable of handling technical SEO, content, and reporting
source_title: Enterprise SEO Strategies Tips for Large Scale Success How to Build a Winning Enterprise SEO Strateg

name: Internal Duplicate Content Traffic Loss
then_action: Those pages will receive less organic traffic because Google deprioritizes ranking pages with copied or recycled content
if_condition: If pages within the same website reuse content already present in Google's index (including from the site's own other pages)
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide What Is Duplicate Content

name: Feedfetcher does not discover links
then_action: stop assuming Feedfetcher behaves like normal web crawlers — it does not discover or follow links to crawl; it only fetches feeds explicitly requested by users of an app or service
if_condition: if treating Feedfetcher as a standard web crawler that follows links for crawl discovery
source_title: Google Feedfetcher

name: Agency Outsourcing Trade-off Warning
then_action: Then the organization should expect reduced control over task assignment and resource allocation, and should factor in higher costs as trade-offs for reduced internal pressure
if_condition: If enterprise SEO implementation is planned to be outsourced to an external agency
source_title: Enterprise SEO Strategies Tips for Large Scale Success How to Build a Winning Enterprise SEO Strateg

name: Same Context Served Regardless of HTTP or HTTPS Retrieval
then_action: Then expect the same context description to be served regardless of which protocol (http or https) was used for retrieval
if_condition: If a developer retrieves the Schema.org context description via either http or https protocol
source_title: Developers   Schema org

name: Enterprise SEO Platform Selection for Scalability
then_action: Then an all-in-one enterprise SEO platform should be selected as the implementation approach to leverage built-in automation, scalability, and expert support
if_condition: If the organization requires scalable SEO activities, automated tracking and reporting, and on-demand expert access without full agency dependency
source_title: Enterprise SEO Strategies Tips for Large Scale Success How to Build a Winning Enterprise SEO Strateg

name: Use schemaorg-current-https for Active Terms Only
then_action: Then use the 'schemaorg-current-https' file, which contains all current vocabulary terms; use 'schemaorg-all-https' only if retired/attic terms are also required
if_condition: If a developer needs only current, active Schema.org vocabulary terms for structured data implementation
source_title: Developers   Schema org

name: Do Not Treat OWL Mapping as Authoritative Schema.org Definition
then_action: Then treat the OWL mapping as an approximation only and do NOT consider it an authoritative definition for Schema.org terms
if_condition: If a developer or application uses the OWL definition files to interpret or validate Schema.org term definitions
source_title: Developers   Schema org

name: Distinct Content Requirement for Indexing
then_action: Google will be uncertain which page is the original, causing all similar pages to struggle to rank, and may refuse to index duplicate pages entirely — wasting crawl budget
if_condition: If a website has multiple pages with similar or identical content (word-for-word or slightly rewritten)
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide What Is Duplicate Content

name: Enterprise SEO Goal Definition Requirement
then_action: Then business goals and KPIs must be explicitly defined before implementation begins to establish measurable effectiveness criteria
if_condition: If an enterprise SEO strategy is being planned or initiated for a large-scale business
source_title: Enterprise SEO Strategies Tips for Large Scale Success How to Build a Winning Enterprise SEO Strateg

name: Redirect WWW/non-WWW and HTTP/HTTPS variants to canonical URL
then_action: Redirect all variants to a single canonical URL using 301 redirects to prevent duplicate content indexing and consolidate ranking signals
if_condition: If a site serves content on both WWW and non-WWW versions, or on both HTTP and HTTPS, creating multiple accessible URLs for the same content
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Use SEO Tools to Identify Duplicate Content at Scale
then_action: Then use tools such as Siteliner to scan the site and identify pages with high levels of duplicate content before deciding on a remediation strategy (delete, redirect, or canonicalize)
if_condition: If a site has a large number of pages and potential duplicate content issues are unknown or unaudited
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 2

name: Avoid generating unique URLs for product variations
then_action: Consolidate product variations under a single canonical URL rather than generating new URLs per variation to prevent mass duplicate content issues
if_condition: If an ecommerce site creates separate URLs for each product variation (e.g. size, color) resulting in thousands of near-identical pages
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Block search result pages from being indexed
then_action: Prevent search result pages from being indexed (via noindex or robots.txt disallow) to avoid large-scale duplicate content dilution
if_condition: If internal site search result pages are accessible to Googlebot and being indexed, adding 1,000+ thin or duplicate pages to the index
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Use 301 redirects to resolve duplicate page URLs
then_action: Apply 301 redirects from duplicate URLs back to the original page so Googlebot indexes only the original, consolidating ranking potential to that page
if_condition: If duplicate pages exist at different URLs serving substantially similar content
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Canonical Tag Preferred Over Blocking for Duplicate Pages
then_action: Then use the rel=canonical tag pointing to the original page rather than blocking it with robots.txt or a noindex tag, as Google has stated canonical is the preferred approach
if_condition: If a page contains duplicate content that cannot be immediately rewritten or removed
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 2

name: Enterprise Site Internal Linking Must Be Automated
then_action: Do not scale internal linking manually; instead set up an SEO-friendly site structure that automatically generates internal links or create a script/enterprise SEO platform to add internal links to important pages automatically
if_condition: If managing internal linking on an enterprise-level site with thousands or millions of pages
source_title: Enterprise SEO Strategies Tips for Large Scale Success What Is Enterprise SEO

name: Investigate auto-generated pages when indexed count exceeds manually created pages
then_action: Investigate automatically generated pages (e.g. faceted navigation, session IDs, search results) as likely sources of duplicate content and apply 301 redirects or noindex to resolve
if_condition: If the number of pages indexed in Google Search Console or via site:example.com search significantly exceeds the number of pages manually created on the site
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: WordPress Tag and Category Pages Should Be Noindexed
then_action: Then add a noindex tag to those pages so they remain accessible to users but are not indexed by search engines, preventing duplicate content issues from affecting rankings
if_condition: If a site runs on WordPress and automatically generates tag and category pages
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 2

name: Page Bloat Monitoring Required on Enterprise Sites
then_action: Investigate and address page bloat issues, as excess low-value pages can harm rankings of priority pages and deplete crawl budget
if_condition: If an enterprise site has thousands of pages and shows signs of crawl budget consumption or ranking drops on target pages
source_title: Enterprise SEO Strategies Tips for Large Scale Success What Is Enterprise SEO

name: Traditional SEO Tools Are Insufficient for Enterprise Complexity
then_action: Replace average SEO tools with enterprise-grade SEO platforms capable of handling the scale and complexity of the site
if_condition: If an enterprise business with complex SEO needs and thousands or millions of pages is using standard average SEO tools
source_title: Enterprise SEO Strategies Tips for Large Scale Success What Is Enterprise SEO

name: Accept Both HTTP and HTTPS Schema.org URIs
then_action: Then the application MUST accept and handle both 'http' and 'https' representations of Schema.org URIs without rejecting either form
if_condition: If a consuming application processes Schema.org URIs and receives either 'http://schema.org' or 'https://schema.org' prefixed identifiers
source_title: Developers   Schema org

name: Third-Party Broad Extensions Remain Independently Hosted
then_action: The external extension continues to be hosted independently by the third party rather than being folded into schema.org
if_condition: If a third party maintains a broadly applicable external extension vocabulary (e.g., http://gs1.org/voc)
source_title: Extending Schemas   Schema org

name: Do Not Rely on Automated Data Extraction Across Different Site Formats
then_action: Avoid this assumption — automated data extraction does not work reliably across different site formats and requires explicit markup
if_condition: If expecting automated structured data extraction to reliably work across sites with varying formats or implementations
source_title: FAQ   schema org 2

name: Use Consistent Schema.org Markup Format
then_action: Use consistent schema.org markup format to ensure computers can reliably parse and understand page data
if_condition: If a webpage requires structured data markup to help search engines understand its content
source_title: FAQ   schema org 2

name: Use Pending Label for Experimental Schema Terms
then_action: MUST use the 'pending' label to mark the term as experimental before full incorporation
if_condition: If introducing a new experimental term into schema.org vocabulary
source_title: Extending Schemas   Schema org

name: Retain Origin Labels for Folded Extension Terms
then_action: Retain the origin label corresponding to the term's originating extension, though labels may be dropped or simplified over time as usage becomes intermingled
if_condition: If a term from a hosted extension is folded into the main schema.org vocabulary
source_title: Extending Schemas   Schema org

name: Use Schema.org Markup for Richer Search Results
then_action: Add schema.org structured data markup to the page using schema.org vocabulary to help search engines understand page content and provide richer search results
if_condition: If a web page contains structured information that could benefit from enhanced search result presentation
source_title: FAQ   schema org

name: Featured Snippet Page 1 Prerequisite
then_action: Do not prioritize featured snippet optimization for that page — 99% of featured snippets come from page 1 results, making snippet capture practically impossible from lower-ranked pages
if_condition: If a page does not already rank on Google's first page for a target query
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part 3

name: Mark Up Multiple Components on Multi-Topic Pages
then_action: Apply schema.org markup separately to each distinct component or entity on the page, as schema.org supports multiple item types and properties within a single page
if_condition: If a single web page discusses or contains more than one distinct topic, entity, or component
source_title: FAQ   schema org

name: Supplement Facebook Open Graph with Schema.org
then_action: Add schema.org markup in addition to Open Graph, because Open Graph alone does not provide the detailed entity-level information search engines need to improve user experience
if_condition: If a web page uses only Facebook Open Graph markup and contains entities requiring detailed structured information for search engines
source_title: FAQ   schema org

name: Do Not Assume All Schema.org Types Surface in Search Results
then_action: Do not assume the markup will be surfaced — not every schema.org information type is displayed in search results by search engines
if_condition: If adding schema.org markup for a specific information type and expecting it to appear visibly in search result features
source_title: FAQ   schema org 2

name: Consult Search Engine Documentation for Specific Markup Uses
then_action: Refer to each search engine company's (Google, Microsoft, Yandex, Yahoo!) individual documentation to find supported markup types and display behaviors
if_condition: If implementing schema.org structured data and expecting specific search engine features or rich results
source_title: FAQ   schema org 2

name: Migrate to Schema.org for Cross-Company Standard Acceptance
then_action: Consider switching to schema.org format over time to benefit from cross-company standard acceptance across Google, Microsoft, Yandex, and Yahoo!
if_condition: If currently using legacy structured data formats such as microformats, RDFa, or data-vocabulary.org
source_title: FAQ   schema org 2

name: Use Schema.org as Single Markup Source
then_action: Stop grafting together schemas from different sources and instead learn and implement markup from schema.org as a single unified source to maximize benefit and consistency
if_condition: If implementing structured data markup by combining schemas from multiple different sources
source_title: FAQ   schema org

name: Mark Up Visible Content Only
then_action: Avoid that markup — only mark up content that is visibly presented to users visiting the web page
if_condition: If structured data markup is applied to content that is hidden from users (e.g., inside hidden divs or hidden page elements)
source_title: FAQ   schema org 3

name: Maximize Schema Markup Coverage
then_action: Mark up as much of that content as possible to maximize search engine understanding and useful presentation to users
if_condition: If a web page contains content that matches supported schema.org types (reviews, comments, images, breadcrumbs, etc.)
source_title: FAQ   schema org 3

name: Wrap Each Step or Item in H2/H3 Subheaders
then_action: Wrap every item or step in an H2 or H3 tag as a subheader and maintain consistent formatting throughout the entire content piece
if_condition: If content presents a list of items or sequential steps targeting a Featured Snippet
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part 2

name: Partial Markup Is Acceptable
then_action: Apply markup for the available properties — partial markup is valid and acceptable; full property coverage is not required
if_condition: If only some properties of a schema.org item type can be identified or are present on the page
source_title: FAQ   schema org 3

name: Use Numbered Step Format in Subheadings for Ordered Lists
then_action: Use 'Step #1' format (preferred over '1.') in each subheading to make the order crystal clear to both Google and users
if_condition: If content presents sequential steps targeting a Featured Snippet and subheadings need to indicate order
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part 2

name: HTTP Schema.org URLs Require No Urgent Migration
then_action: Do not treat migration to HTTPS schema URLs as urgent — both formats are acceptable and will remain widely understood; no immediate action is required
if_condition: If existing structured data markup uses 'http://schema.org' URLs instead of 'https://schema.org'
source_title: FAQ   schema org 3

name: Target Long-Tail Keyword Variations for Featured Snippets
then_action: Also optimize for long-tail keyword variations, since the vast majority of Featured Snippets trigger on long-tail queries — missing variations means missing significant snippet opportunities
if_condition: If optimizing content exclusively for high-volume Featured Snippet keywords without addressing long-tail variations
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part 2

name: Dual Dashboard Monitoring for AI vs Traditional Search
then_action: Then maintain two separate monitoring dashboards: one for website performance in traditional search (GA4, GSC) and one dedicated to brand mentions and citation frequency across AI search platforms
if_condition: If a brand is actively optimizing for both traditional search and AI search visibility
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 5 Visibility Is measured differently in AI

name: Use People Also Ask Boxes to Identify Long-Tail Featured Snippet Targets
then_action: Check the 'People Also Ask' boxes in Google SERPs for the seed keyword to identify additional long-tail questions and create mini FAQ-style content sections targeting those questions
if_condition: If researching long-tail keyword variations to target for Featured Snippets
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part 2

name: Use 'What is X' Header Above Definition
then_action: Place a 'What is X' heading directly above the definition text to signal to Google the definition's location and context
if_condition: If a definition paragraph is written to target a Definition Featured Snippet
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part

name: Use HTML Tables for Table Featured Snippets
then_action: Use an actual HTML table on the page containing the target data, because Google scrapes existing tables rather than assembling table data from scattered page content
if_condition: If the target SERP displays a Table-type Featured Snippet for a keyword
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part

name: Use Less-Specific Type for Unsupported Content
then_action: Use the closest less-specific parent type available (e.g., use 'Person' for professors) rather than omitting markup entirely
if_condition: If the specific content type (e.g., Professor, Recipe variation) does not exist in schema.org vocabulary
source_title: FAQ   schema org 3

name: Specify Included Room Services Using TypeAndQuantityNode
then_action: Create a product bundle using multiple TypeAndQuantityNode elements within includesObject to enumerate each included service type separately
if_condition: If a hotel offer bundles multiple service types (e.g., HotelRoom + FoodService) into a single package
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Fees for Optional Services

name: Target SERPs with Existing Featured Snippets
then_action: Only pursue keywords where a Featured Snippet already exists in the SERP, identified by searching manually or filtering via tools like Semrush or Ahrefs
if_condition: If you are researching keywords to target for Featured Snippet optimization
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part

name: Authority Pages Multi-Snippet Opportunity
then_action: Prioritize adding multiple definitions, questions, and answers on that single page — authority pages can rank for dozens of featured snippets from one URL
if_condition: If a high-authority page ranks on Google's first page across multiple related queries
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part 3

name: Use HTML Table Tags for Featured Snippet Data
then_action: Use proper HTML <tr> tags to structure the table so Google can easily parse and display the data in position zero
if_condition: If content includes tabular data intended to appear in a Google Featured Snippet
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part 2

name: Feedfetcher crawl frequency cap
then_action: consider that frequently updated sites may be refreshed more often than hourly, and brief spikes may occur due to network delays — this is expected behavior and not a misconfiguration
if_condition: if Feedfetcher appears to be retrieving your feed more than once per hour on average
source_title: Google Feedfetcher

name: Featured Snippet SERP Check Before Keyword Selection
then_action: Check the SERP to confirm whether a Featured Snippet exists before finalizing the keyword decision, as its presence affects expected click-through behavior
if_condition: If a keyword is being evaluated for targeting and no SERP check has been performed yet
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google What Are Featured Snippets

name: Use Personal Keyword Difficulty Over Generic Scores
then_action: Use Personal Keyword Difficulty (PKD) that incorporates your domain's relevance, search term competitiveness, and key metrics for your domain and competitors — not generic difficulty scores
if_condition: If analyzing keyword difficulty to assess ranking potential for a specific domain
source_title: Free Keyword Difficulty Checker See Who s Ranking Why

name: Table Format for Extractable Data
then_action: Ensure the table format allows Google to extract and restructure the data effectively; otherwise the snippet will not be selected
if_condition: If content is structured as a table intended to capture a Table Featured Snippet
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google Types of Featured Snippets

name: Incorporate Search Trends and CPC Data in Keyword Selection
then_action: Supplement difficulty scores with search volume, 12-month search trend data, CPC, and competitive density to make fully informed keyword prioritization decisions
if_condition: If selecting keywords for content creation or optimization based on difficulty scores alone
source_title: Free Keyword Difficulty Checker See Who s Ranking Why

name: Definition Box Format for 'What Is' Queries
then_action: Use a Definition Box format with short, direct text to match Google's snippet selection pattern for that query type
if_condition: If the target query begins with or implies 'what is' seeking a direct definition or description
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google Types of Featured Snippets

name: Do Not Auto-Reject Keywords With Featured Snippets
then_action: Do not automatically discard the keyword — Featured Snippets can still significantly boost organic click-through rate despite increasing no-click searches
if_condition: If a target keyword has an existing Featured Snippet in the SERP
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google What Are Featured Snippets

name: Include Featured Snippet Presence as a Keyword Scoring Factor
then_action: Factor in whether a Featured Snippet exists for that keyword, as it affects realistic click volume expectations and content format requirements
if_condition: If evaluating keyword difficulty or opportunity alongside competition and monthly search volume
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google What Are Featured Snippets

name: Use Domain View to Benchmark Against SERP Competitors
then_action: Use the Domain view to compare your site's authority, referring domains, and backlinks against the sites currently ranking to determine competitive gap
if_condition: If assessing your site's realistic chance of ranking for a keyword against current SERP incumbents
source_title: Free Keyword Difficulty Checker See Who s Ranking Why

name: Reduce Click Expectation for Featured Snippet SERPs
then_action: Adjust expected click volume downward compared to non-Featured Snippet SERPs, as snippets increase no-click searches and reduce overall organic clicks per Ahrefs data
if_condition: If a keyword SERP contains a Featured Snippet and click traffic is being projected
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google What Are Featured Snippets

name: Backlink Threshold Warning for Competitive Keywords
then_action: Expect that acquiring a comparable backlink profile will likely be required to rank for that term — deprioritize or resource-plan accordingly
if_condition: If the majority of top 10 ranking pages for a target keyword have high backlink counts and many referring domains
source_title: Free Keyword Difficulty Checker See Who s Ranking Why

name: Apply Location-Specific Keyword Difficulty for Local SEO
then_action: Use location-specific keyword difficulty insights rather than global difficulty data to accurately assess local ranking competitiveness
if_condition: If running a local SEO campaign targeting geographically specific search terms
source_title: Free Keyword Difficulty Checker See Who s Ranking Why

name: E-E-A-T Signals Influence AI Answer Framing
then_action: AI systems are more likely to contextualize, trust, and reference the content when assembling AI-generated answers
if_condition: If a page implements clear E-E-A-T signals including identifiable authorship, demonstrated experience, and expertise indicators
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 1 SEO fundamentals are the foundation

name: Definition Snippet Word Count Requirement
then_action: Keep the definition between 40-60 words long to optimize for Featured Snippet selection
if_condition: If content is written to target a Featured Snippet for a 'what is' definition query
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google Types of Featured Snippets

name: AI Crawlability Requires Technical Accessibility
then_action: AI crawlers will struggle to consistently process and extract the content, reducing the likelihood it will be used as a reference point in AI-generated answers
if_condition: If content relies on JavaScript-heavy rendering or client-side rendering for core information delivery
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 1 SEO fundamentals are the foundation

name: Page Performance Affects AI Source Dependability Perception
then_action: AI systems will perceive the source as less dependable when assembling answers, reducing citation likelihood even if the content is not blocked from indexing
if_condition: If a page has slow or unstable load performance
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 1 SEO fundamentals are the foundation

name: Use nopagereadaloud meta tag to opt out of Google Read Aloud
then_action: Add `<meta name="google" content="nopagereadaloud">` to the page's HTML head; Google Read Aloud will proactively render the page to detect this tag and reduce future requests automatically
if_condition: If a site owner wants to prevent Google Read Aloud from reading a specific page aloud
source_title: Google Read Aloud User Agent

name: Buffer Free Plan Analytics Restriction
then_action: block access to these analytics features; they are not available on the Free plan and require an upgrade to Essentials or Team
if_condition: if a user on Buffer's Free plan attempts to access performance overview, custom analytics, audience demographics, best time to post suggestions, or generate reports
source_title: Pricing Support

name: Product Page Element Disambiguation
then_action: Make each element unambiguously identifiable through explicit markup and structure so AI systems can correctly interpret what each piece of information represents
if_condition: If a product page contains multiple elements such as product name, description, price, attributes, availability, and variants without clear structural distinction between them
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 2 Entity clarity shapes AI understanding

name: GEO Multi-Platform Authority Requirement
then_action: Then AI systems will lack sufficient cross-platform signals to trust and cite your brand; must establish authority across all platforms AI tools reference (e.g., Wikipedia, Reddit, industry publications, social profiles)
if_condition: If optimizing content only on your own website without establishing presence on external platforms where AI tools pull information
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions A comprehensive guide to generative engine

name: GEO AI Crawler Accessibility Requirement
then_action: Then AI systems cannot access or process your content, eliminating any chance of being cited or recommended in AI-generated answers; audit and allow AI crawler access
if_condition: If your website blocks or restricts AI crawlers (e.g., GPTBot, Google-Extended, PerplexityBot) via robots.txt or server configuration
