source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions A comprehensive guide to generative engine

name: Schema Markup as Mirror of Visible Content
then_action: First establish clarity in visible page content, then mirror that exact structure in schema markup so machine-readable format reinforces human-readable content rather than diverging from it
if_condition: If schema markup (JSON-LD) is added to a page but does not mirror the visible page content structure and descriptions
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 2 Entity clarity shapes AI understanding

name: Structured Data Signal Consistency Across Page, Schema, and Feed
then_action: Ensure all three layers — visible page content, schema markup (JSON-LD), and product feed — describe the same entity in the same way to maintain AI system confidence and accurate categorization
if_condition: If the page content, schema markup, and commerce feed (e.g., Google Merchant Center) describe product or brand information differently or inconsistently
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 2 Entity clarity shapes AI understanding

name: Content Attributability Required for AI Surfacing
then_action: AI systems cannot reliably contextualize or trust the content, reducing the probability it will be surfaced or referenced in AI-generated responses
if_condition: If content lacks clear authorship, identifiable sourcing, or attributable structure
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 1 SEO fundamentals are the foundation

name: Consistent Crawling and Indexing Required for GEO Visibility
then_action: AI retrieval systems cannot reliably access the content, disqualifying it from being used as a reference in AI-generated answers regardless of content quality
if_condition: If a site has inconsistent crawl access, indexing failures, or rendering gaps
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 1 SEO fundamentals are the foundation

name: Front-Load Main Point in Paragraphs
then_action: Then place the main point at the start of the paragraph, not at the end, to increase likelihood of accurate extraction by LLM chunking systems
if_condition: If a paragraph is being written for a page that targets AI retrieval or answer engine visibility
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 3 Content must be easy to extract and reus

name: Conflicting Source Signals Reduce AI Brand Mention Probability
then_action: Resolve the conflicts by cross-referencing and aligning all sources, because AI confidence drops when signals conflict and the brand is less likely to be surfaced in AI-generated answers
if_condition: If signals about a brand's category, offerings, or authority conflict across multiple sources or formats
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 2 Entity clarity shapes AI understanding

name: Consistent Cross-Platform Brand Description Alignment
then_action: Align all brand descriptions to reinforce the same category, positioning, and value proposition across every platform, as conflicting signals reduce AI confidence and decrease likelihood of brand mentions
if_condition: If brand descriptions, category positioning, or value propositions differ across platforms such as LinkedIn, Crunchbase, review platforms, or industry directories
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 2 Entity clarity shapes AI understanding

name: Analyze Full SERP Metrics Before Targeting a Keyword
then_action: Analyze the top 10 ranking pages in Google for that keyword using metrics like Authority Score, referring domains, and backlinks to get a full picture of actual ranking difficulty
if_condition: If evaluating whether to target a specific keyword for new or existing content
source_title: Free Keyword Difficulty Checker See Who s Ranking Why

name: GEO Entity Clarity Requirement
then_action: Then AI systems cannot reliably identify, trust, or reference your brand in generated answers; establish unambiguous entity clarity through consistent structured information across all touchpoints
if_condition: If your brand, product, or content lacks clear entity definition (distinct name, consistent descriptions, structured identifying information) across platforms
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions A comprehensive guide to generative engine

name: Self-Contained Paragraph Rule for AI Extractability
then_action: Then write the paragraph as a self-contained unit that expresses one complete idea and makes sense when read in isolation, without relying on surrounding context
if_condition: If a paragraph contains a definition, explanation, comparison, or key fact intended to be surfaced in AI-generated answers
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 3 Content must be easy to extract and reus

name: Do not rely on user cookies in pages accessed by Google Read Aloud
then_action: Be aware that Google Read Aloud uses stateless rendering and does not send user cookies, so cookie-dependent content gating will not function as expected during Read Aloud access
if_condition: If a webpage relies on user cookies to serve personalized or access-controlled content and Google-Read-Aloud may render it
source_title: Google Read Aloud User Agent

name: Smaller Publishers Can Outrank Larger Brands in AI Answers
then_action: Then smaller publishers with clearer expertise and better-structured content will outperform them in AI-generated answers; do not rely on brand authority as a substitute for content quality
if_condition: If a brand assumes AI citation dominance based on brand size or recognition alone without investing in content clarity and structure
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions Frequently asked questions

name: Multi-Platform Content Presence for AI Visibility
then_action: Establish owned presence across YouTube (product demos), Reddit (subreddit participation), LinkedIn (executive newsletters), podcasts, webinars, and conference presentations to maximize surface area for AI system citations
if_condition: If a brand relies solely on its own website for content presence and AI-cited sources like Reddit, LinkedIn, and YouTube are absent from its strategy
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 4 AI visibility extends beyond your websit

name: Earned Mentions Validate Brand Credibility for AI
then_action: Actively build earned mentions from customers and industry sources so AI systems encounter multiple independent signals confirming brand credibility and relevance
if_condition: If a brand has owned content but lacks independent third-party mentions from customers or industry sources across multiple platforms
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 4 AI visibility extends beyond your websit

name: Combined Owned and Earned Signals for Comprehensive AI Understanding
then_action: AI systems will form an incomplete picture of the brand; combine owned presence with earned mentions so AI encounters both signal types and builds a comprehensive, accurate understanding of what the brand offers
if_condition: If a brand has either owned content OR earned mentions but not both simultaneously present across multiple platforms
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 4 AI visibility extends beyond your websit

name: Structure Content for Passage-Level Retrieval
then_action: Structure content so individual passages are self-contained and retrievable without surrounding context, including clear direct answers to specific questions and concise definitions
if_condition: If content is being created or optimized for AI-generated answer visibility (GEO)
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions Frequently asked questions

name: LLM Training Data Long-Term Brand Shaping
then_action: Recognize that this content may become part of future LLM training data, shaping how AI systems learn about and reference the brand long-term; ensure consistency and accuracy of brand messaging across all platforms
if_condition: If a brand is publishing owned content or generating earned mentions at scale across public platforms
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 4 AI visibility extends beyond your websit

name: Substantive Problem-Solving Content as AI Reference Requirement
then_action: AI systems will lack sufficient reference material to include the brand in generated answers; create detailed, helpful content that directly solves real problems to qualify as an AI-citable source
if_condition: If published content does not address real, specific industry problems with substantive depth
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 4 AI visibility extends beyond your websit

name: Avoid Context-Dependent References
then_action: Then rewrite the paragraph to remove those references and make the content independently interpretable, as AI systems retrieve isolated chunks without surrounding page context
if_condition: If a paragraph uses phrases like 'as mentioned above', 'this is why', or any reference that depends on reading other parts of the page for meaning
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 3 Content must be easy to extract and reus

name: Include Specific Facts Over Generalizations
then_action: Then include specific facts, statistics, and concrete details rather than vague generalizations, as specificity increases retrieval relevance and answer accuracy
if_condition: If content is being optimized for retrieval by AI systems that assemble generative answers from web passages
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 3 Content must be easy to extract and reus

name: Descriptive Headings for AI Section Signaling
then_action: Then use clear, descriptive headings that explicitly signal what the section covers, enabling AI retrieval systems to match the chunk to relevant user questions
if_condition: If a content section is intended to answer a specific user query or topic area within an AI-optimized page
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 3 Content must be easy to extract and reus

name: Use structured data for paywalled content with Google Read Aloud
then_action: MUST implement structured data with the `isAccessibleForFree` property set to `False` to prevent paywalled content from being read aloud to users
if_condition: If a webpage contains subscription-gated or paywalled content and Google-Read-Aloud user agent may access it
source_title: Google Read Aloud User Agent

name: Use Specific User Agent Tokens for Special-Case Crawlers in robots.txt
then_action: Use the specific user agent tokens for each crawler explicitly in robots.txt directives, as global wildcard rules will not apply to these crawlers
if_condition: If a site needs to control Google special-case crawlers (APIs-Google, AdsBot-Google-Mobile, AdsBot-Google, Mediapartners-Google) via robots.txt
source_title: Google Special Case Crawlers

name: Do Not Expect Stable AI Citation Results Over Time
then_action: Then adjust expectations — AI citation volatility means even well-optimized brands experience fluctuation as models update, signals are reweighted, and user context shifts
if_condition: If a brand has achieved strong AI citation performance and assumes results will remain consistent
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 5 Visibility Is measured differently in AI

name: Avoid Sole Reliance on Traditional Analytics for AI Visibility
then_action: Then stop — these platforms cannot track AI mention signals, creating measurement blind spots where high AI visibility registers as zero activity in standard dashboards
if_condition: If measuring brand visibility using only traditional analytics platforms such as GA4 or Google Search Console
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 5 Visibility Is measured differently in AI

name: Track Citation Frequency as Primary AI Visibility Metric
then_action: Then track citation frequency — the rate at which AI platforms mention the brand when answering relevant questions — as the primary GEO performance metric
if_condition: If evaluating how visible a brand is within AI-generated answers across platforms like ChatGPT, Perplexity, or Gemini
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 5 Visibility Is measured differently in AI

name: Do not use robots.txt to block Google Read Aloud
then_action: AVOID this approach — robots.txt rules will be ignored by Google Read Aloud because the service is user-initiated rather than an automated crawl
if_condition: If a site owner attempts to block Google-Read-Aloud by adding a disallow rule in robots.txt

name: Measure Share of Voice Against Competitors in AI Search
then_action: Then measure share of voice by comparing your brand's mention rate to competitors across AI-generated answers to reveal true competitive standing in the GEO landscape
if_condition: If assessing competitive positioning within AI search results
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 5 Visibility Is measured differently in AI

name: Conduct Context Tracking to Identify Citation-Triggering Prompts
then_action: Then conduct context tracking to identify which specific prompts or topics reliably trigger brand mentions, and optimize content to align with those high-citation contexts
if_condition: If a brand appears inconsistently or unpredictably in AI-generated answers
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 5 Visibility Is measured differently in AI

name: Include Sentiment Analysis in AI Mention Monitoring
then_action: Then also analyze sentiment of those mentions — classifying them as positive, neutral, or negative — to ensure high visibility translates to favorable brand representation
if_condition: If a brand's citation frequency in AI platforms is being monitored

name: Single Crawler Token Match Required for robots.txt Rule Application

name: GoogleOther Crawlers Do Not Affect Search Rankings
then_action: Do not assume these crawling preferences affect Google Search rankings or other Google products — these crawlers are independent of Search ranking signals

if_condition: If Ahrefs shows a competitor only briefly spent money on specific paid search keywords

name: Google-Extended Does Not Impact Search Inclusion or Rankings
then_action: Confirm that blocking Google-Extended does not affect the site's inclusion in Google Search index nor is it used as a ranking signal — it exclusively controls AI training data usage
if_condition: If a site owner blocks Google-Extended in robots.txt to restrict AI training data access
source_title: Google s common crawlers A note about Chrome W X Y Z in user agents Part 2

name: Prohibit Meta Content Tag for Directly Markable Information
then_action: Do NOT use <meta> with content attribute for that information — apply standard Schema.org markup directly to the existing visible content instead
if_condition: If structured data information is already present as visible, readable HTML text on the page and can be directly marked up using standard Schema.org markup
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3c Missing implicit information use the meta tag with content

name: Provide Name and Description for Each typeOfGood Element
then_action: Each typeOfGood element must include both name and description properties to ensure completeness and search engine interpretability of the structured data
if_condition: If TypeAndQuantityNode elements are used within a hotel offer's includesObject to list included services
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Fees for Optional Services

name: Microdata itemprop Required for Item Properties
then_action: MUST add itemprop attribute to that element to label the property so search engines can identify and parse it correctly
if_condition: if an HTML element contains a specific property value of a marked-up item (e.g., name, director, date)
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 1 How to mark up your content using microdata

name: Add Global Document Context Throughout Content
then_action: Include extra contextual metadata (such as company name, filing date, document title, or section identifier) repeatedly throughout the text so LLMs can correctly understand isolated chunks without losing context
if_condition: If content exists in long documents where individual chunks may be read in isolation by LLMs (e.g., financial reports, technical documentation, multi-section articles)
source_title: GEO LLMO AEO It s All Just SEO 4

name: Schema.org URL Required for itemtype Values
then_action: MUST use full schema.org URLs (e.g., https://schema.org/Movie) from the schema.org type hierarchy — shorthand names or non-URL values are not valid
if_condition: if specifying the type of a microdata item using the itemtype attribute
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 1 How to mark up your content using microdata

name: Microdata itemscope and itemtype Required Together
then_action: MUST add itemscope attribute to that element AND MUST specify itemtype attribute immediately after itemscope using a valid schema.org URL (e.g., https://schema.org/Movie)
if_condition: if an HTML element encloses information about a specific item intended for semantic markup
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 1 How to mark up your content using microdata

name: Use Meta Content Sparingly for Hidden Structured Data
then_action: Restrict use of meta with content attribute to only those specific properties where information is genuinely inaccessible via normal HTML markup due to presentation format (images, Flash, implied values); treat it as a last resort, not a default pattern
if_condition: If a developer is considering applying meta content tags broadly across multiple properties or as a default markup approach rather than only for presentation-constrained cases
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3c Missing implicit information use the meta tag with content

name: Nested Microdata Requires New itemscope After itemprop
then_action: MUST begin a new itemscope immediately after the corresponding itemprop, and MUST also add the appropriate itemtype URL to define the nested item type
if_condition: if the value of an itemprop is itself a complex item with its own properties (e.g., a Person nested inside a Movie)
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 1 How to mark up your content using microdata

name: Use Span Tags to Attach itemprop Without Altering Rendering
then_action: SHOULD wrap the text in an additional <span> tag and attach the itemprop attribute to it, preserving visual rendering while enabling structured data markup
if_condition: if relevant item property text exists in the page but is not enclosed in a semantic HTML element suitable for itemprop
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 1 How to mark up your content using microdata

name: Use Meta Tag with Content for Non-Markable Information
then_action: Use a <meta> tag with itemprop and content attributes to specify the hidden information within the appropriate itemscope container (e.g., <meta itemprop='ratingValue' content='4' />)
if_condition: If a web page contains valuable structured data information that is conveyed through images (e.g., star rating shown as image), Flash objects (e.g., video duration in Flash), or implied content (e.g., currency symbols) that cannot be directly marked up with HTML text
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3c Missing implicit information use the meta tag with content

name: Use robots.txt Tokens to Control Specialized Google Crawlers
then_action: Configure robots.txt using the appropriate user-agent tokens: GoogleOther for general R&D crawls, GoogleOther-Image for image crawls, GoogleOther-Video for video crawls, Google-CloudVertexBot for Vertex AI Agent crawls, and Google-Extended for AI training data control
if_condition: If a site owner wants to allow or restrict access for specific Google crawler types such as image crawlers, video crawlers, Vertex AI agents, or research crawlers
source_title: Google s common crawlers A note about Chrome W X Y Z in user agents Part 2

name: Use link element with href for invisible canonical references
then_action: Use <link itemprop="url" href="external-url" /> inside the itemscope block to provide the reference invisibly to search engines
if_condition: If you want to reference an external page discussing the same item without displaying a visible link to users
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3b Enumerations and canonical references use link with href

name: Use YYYY-MM-DD format in datetime attribute for dates
then_action: use YYYY-MM-DD format exclusively (e.g., datetime="2011-04-01") to avoid ambiguity across regional date formats
if_condition: if specifying a date value in the datetime attribute of a <time> tag
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3a Dates times and durations use the time tag with datetime

name: Prefix duration values with P in datetime attribute
then_action: prefix the duration value with P and use H for hours and M for minutes (e.g., datetime="PT1H30M" for 1 hour 30 minutes)
if_condition: if marking up a duration (e.g., cooking time, event length) in a datetime attribute
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3a Dates times and durations use the time tag with datetime

name: Schema Markup Testing Requirement
then_action: Test the markup using Google's rich snippets testing tool to verify correct implementation and identify any errors before publishing
if_condition: If schema.org markup has been implemented on a web page
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 2 Using the schema org vocabulary

name: Link to third-party sites to improve entity understanding
then_action: Add itemprop="url" links to those third-party pages to help search engines better understand and validate the entity being described
if_condition: If a schema.org entity (e.g., a product, organization, or person) is marked up and third-party authoritative pages exist that discuss the same item
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3b Enumerations and canonical references use link with href

name: Schema Markup Collection Items Separately
then_action: Mark up each item separately using its own schema.org item type, and add the url property to link each item to its corresponding dedicated page
if_condition: If a page contains a collection of multiple items (e.g., product listings, person profiles, places)
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 2 Using the schema org vocabulary

name: Warn against ambiguous date formats in structured data
then_action: search engines and automated systems cannot reliably interpret the date, risking incorrect parsing; always pair with an ISO 8601 datetime attribute in a <time> tag
if_condition: if a date is written in an ambiguous format such as 04/01/11 without a machine-readable datetime attribute
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3a Dates times and durations use the time tag with datetime

name: Prefix time component with T when combining date and time
then_action: separate the date and time with the letter T and express time in hh:mm or hh:mm:ss format (e.g., datetime="2011-05-08T19:30")
if_condition: if providing both a date and a time of day in a single datetime attribute value
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3a Dates times and durations use the time tag with datetime

name: Use URL-based enumeration values in schema markup
then_action: Replace plain text with a <link itemprop="propertyname" href="https://schema.org/EnumerationValue"/> element to specify the value unambiguously via URL
if_condition: If a schema.org property has a limited set of possible values (e.g., availability, condition) and is currently marked up with plain text
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3b Enumerations and canonical references use link with href

name: Schema Child Type Substitution Allowed
then_action: Use the more specific child type in the markup — child types are valid substitutes for their parent expected types
if_condition: If a schema.org property expects a specific item type (e.g., Place) but the content represents a more specific subtype (e.g., LocalBusiness)
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 2 Using the schema org vocabulary

name: Avoid JavaScript-Rendered Content for AI Crawler Visibility
then_action: Avoid relying on JavaScript rendering for critical content, as most AI crawlers do not execute JavaScript and will not index client-side rendered content; serve content as static HTML or use server-side rendering instead
if_condition: If website content is delivered via client-side JavaScript rendering (e.g., single-page apps, React/Vue/Angular without SSR) and the goal includes visibility in AI-generated answers
source_title: GEO LLMO AEO It s All Just SEO 4

name: Maximize Schema Markup Coverage
then_action: Mark up as many relevant properties as possible — greater markup coverage improves search engine understanding and display quality
if_condition: If a page has multiple eligible visible content properties that can be described using schema.org vocabulary
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 2 Using the schema org vocabulary

name: Use anchor tag for visible canonical references
then_action: Use link text instead of the link element so the reference is visible to users and parseable by search engines
if_condition: If you want to display a visible link to an external page discussing the same item
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3b Enumerations and canonical references use link with href

name: Schema.org Vocabulary Format Requirement
then_action: MUST use schema.org vocabulary combined with one of the approved formats: Microdata, RDFa, or JSON-LD to ensure major search engines (Google, Microsoft, Yandex, Yahoo) can interpret the markup
if_condition: If structured data is being added to web content for search engine understanding
source_title: Getting Started   schema org

name: Google Crawler robots.txt Compliance
then_action: The crawler MUST obey robots.txt rules; blocking via robots.txt will prevent that crawler from accessing the specified content
if_condition: If a Google common crawler (Googlebot, Googlebot-Image, Googlebot-Video, Storebot-Google, etc.) is crawling a site automatically
source_title: Google s common crawlers A note about Chrome W X Y Z in user agents Part 1

name: Schema Markup Visible Content Only
then_action: Remove the markup — schema.org structured data MUST NOT be applied to content hidden from users visiting the page
if_condition: If schema.org markup is applied to content inside hidden divs or hidden page elements
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 2 Using the schema org vocabulary

name: Use <time> tag with datetime attribute for unambiguous dates
then_action: wrap the text in a <time> tag and include a datetime attribute with an ISO 8601 machine-readable value (e.g., <time datetime="2011-04-01">04/01/11</time>)
if_condition: if a date, time, or duration is displayed in human-readable format on a webpage with structured data markup
source_title: Getting Started   schema org 3a Dates times and durations use the time tag with datetime

name: Hotel Schema Container Element Requirement
then_action: use itemscope itemtype='https://schema.org/Hotel' as the outermost container element, and mark up hotel name with itemprop='name' and description with itemprop='description'
if_condition: if building structured markup for a hotel property using Schema.org
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Geo Position

name: Hotel Room Count Markup Requirement
then_action: MUST use QuantitativeValue type and include both value and unitText properties within the numberOfRooms property
if_condition: If a hotel website uses numberOfRooms property in Schema.org structured data
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Information about Rooms

name: Optional Service Fees in Separate Offer with addOn Property
then_action: Create a separate Schema.org Offer for each optional service and link it to the base offer using the addOn property
if_condition: If a hotel offer includes optional services (e.g., breakfast, parking, spa) with separate pricing not included in the base room rate
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Fees for Optional Services

name: HTML Tags Alone Are Insufficient for Semantic Understanding
then_action: Search engines cannot intelligently determine what text strings mean, reducing ability to display relevant content in search results — add schema.org structured data to provide machine-understandable semantic meaning
if_condition: If web content relies only on HTML tags without structured data markup
source_title: Getting Started   schema org

name: Schema.org Types and Properties Apply Across All Formats
then_action: The core schema.org concepts — types and properties — remain consistent and transferable across all three formats, so knowledge of one format's vocabulary applies to the others
if_condition: If implementing schema.org structured data using any format (Microdata, RDFa, or JSON-LD)
source_title: Getting Started   schema org

name: Hotel Schema Must Use Exact Coordinate Values
then_action: provide exact decimal coordinate values in the content attribute (e.g., latitude content='47.2649990', longitude content='11.3428720') rather than rounded or approximate values
if_condition: if specifying latitude and longitude in Hotel Schema.org GeoCoordinates markup
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Geo Position

name: Hotel Schema Must Include Complete Postal Address Properties
then_action: include all five required address properties: streetAddress, postalCode, addressLocality, addressRegion, and addressCountry within itemscope itemtype='https://schema.org/PostalAddress'
if_condition: if implementing Schema.org Hotel markup with a PostalAddress component
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Geo Position

name: Hotel Schema GeoCoordinates Must Use Meta Elements
then_action: use meta elements with itemprop='latitude' and itemprop='longitude' inside itemscope itemtype='https://schema.org/GeoCoordinates' to provide coordinate values
if_condition: if marking up hotel geographical coordinates using Schema.org structured data
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Geo Position

name: Hotel Schema Must Include LeaseOut BusinessFunction
then_action: Set businessFunction to http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#LeaseOut to correctly describe the rental nature of hotel accommodation
if_condition: When specifying the businessFunction property within a Schema.org Offer for hotel room markup
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Basic Pattern

name: Attach Meal Information via FoodService and includesObject
then_action: Use includesObject with TypeAndQuantityNode and attach FoodService as the typeOfGood, providing name and description properties for each included service
if_condition: If a hotel offer includes meal plans (e.g., bed & breakfast, half board) as part of the room package
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Fees for Optional Services

name: Hotel Schema Use IE UnitCode for Per-Person Pricing
then_action: Set unitCode to 'IE' (UN/CEFACT Common Code for persons) and indicate occupancy as a single value using QuantitativeValue
if_condition: When the hotel room price is charged per person in Schema.org UnitPriceSpecification
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Basic Pattern

name: Hotel Schema UnitPriceSpecification Must Include Price, Currency, and UnitCode
then_action: Use itemtype https://schema.org/UnitPriceSpecification and include price (or minPrice/maxPrice for ranges), priceCurrency (e.g. 'USD'), and unitCode properties
if_condition: When creating a priceSpecification for a hotel room Schema.org Offer
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Basic Pattern

name: Hotel Schema Use DAY UnitCode for Per-Night Room Pricing
then_action: Set unitCode to 'DAY' in the UnitPriceSpecification
if_condition: When the hotel room price is charged per night or per room in Schema.org UnitPriceSpecification
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Basic Pattern

name: Hotel Schema Use minPrice and maxPrice for Price Ranges
then_action: Use minPrice and maxPrice properties instead of the price property in UnitPriceSpecification
if_condition: When a hotel room has a variable or range-based price rather than a fixed price in Schema.org markup
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Basic Pattern

name: Use LeaseOut BusinessFunction for Hotel Offers
then_action: Include the businessFunction property with value 'http://purl.org/goodrelations/v1#LeaseOut' to correctly classify the transactional nature of hotel room rentals
if_condition: If marking up a hotel room offer using Schema.org structured data
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Fees for Optional Services

name: Hotel Room Relationship Markup Requirement
then_action: MUST use containsPlace property to link the hotel entity to HotelRoom entities, or containedInPlace for the inverse relationship, and each room MUST use HotelRoom itemtype
if_condition: If a hotel website marks up individual room details in Schema.org structured data
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Information about Rooms

name: Googlebot Smartphone User Agent Identification
then_action: Identify the crawler as Googlebot Smartphone, which renders and indexes mobile versions of pages
if_condition: If a server log or request shows a user agent containing 'Nexus 5X Build/MMB29P' and 'Googlebot/2.1'
source_title: Google s common crawlers A note about Chrome W X Y Z in user agents Part 1

name: Room Occupancy Markup Requirement
then_action: MUST use the occupancy property with QuantitativeValue type and MUST include unitCode='IE' meta tag when specifying people occupancy; use maxValue for maximum capacity or minValue/maxValue for ranges
if_condition: If a hotel website specifies room occupancy capacity in Schema.org structured data
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Information about Rooms

name: Internal AI SEO Upskilling Priority Rule
then_action: Prioritize upskilling current team on AI SEO skills that directly connect to visibility outcomes (structure, clarity, retrievability) rather than hiring externally
if_condition: If team already knows brand workflows and audience, and budget is limited for new hires
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 1 When to Build Develop Internally

name: Room Feature Pricing Caveat Warning
then_action: amenityFeature does NOT convey pricing information; MUST use addOn property to link room offer to feature offers for modeling optional fees
if_condition: If a hotel website uses amenityFeature to list room features and needs to indicate whether the feature has an additional charge
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Information about Rooms

name: AI Visibility Metrics Tracking Rule
then_action: Expand tracking to include AI visibility metrics (citation frequency, answer inclusion) alongside conventional ranking data
if_condition: If analyst is currently tracking only traditional search rankings
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 1 When to Build Develop Internally

name: Room Feature Availability Markup Requirement
then_action: MUST set meta itemprop='value' content='True' to indicate the feature is available
if_condition: If a hotel room feature is available and being marked up using amenityFeature with LocationFeatureSpecification
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Information about Rooms

name: Theoretical AI Training Avoidance Rule
then_action: Restructure training to focus on practical, outcome-linked skills such as content structuring for AI extraction and understanding AI citation patterns
if_condition: If internal AI SEO training curriculum is heavy on conceptual or theoretical content without direct connection to structure, clarity, or retrievability outcomes
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 1 When to Build Develop Internally

name: Skill Concentration Bottleneck Warning
then_action: Redistribute skills by hiring or borrowing external expertise to avoid single-point bottleneck that slows AI SEO implementation
if_condition: If one team member (e.g., SEO lead) is assigned 3 or more new AI SEO skills simultaneously
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 1 When to Build Develop Internally

name: Competitor Citation Gap Analysis Rule
then_action: Compare competitor cited passages to your own content structure, then update one to two articles using the patterns observed in cited competitor content
if_condition: If competitors are being cited in AI-generated answers for your target keywords but your content is not
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 1 When to Build Develop Internally

name: Legacy Googlebot User Agent Recognition
then_action: Recognize these as legacy Googlebot user agents that are rarely used but still valid; do not block or treat them as suspicious crawlers
if_condition: If a server log shows user agents 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; Googlebot/2.1; +http://www.google.com/bot.html)' or 'Googlebot/2.1 (+http://www.google.com/bot.html)'
source_title: Google s common crawlers A note about Chrome W X Y Z in user agents Part 1

name: Use Wildcards for Chrome Version in User Agent Log Filtering
then_action: Use wildcards (Chrome/*W.X.Y.Z*) instead of exact version numbers, because Chrome version numbers in Googlebot user agents increase over time to match the latest Chromium release
if_condition: If searching server logs or filtering requests by Googlebot user agent string containing a specific Chrome version number like Chrome/W.X.Y.Z
source_title: Google s common crawlers A note about Chrome W X Y Z in user agents Part 2

name: Identify Google Messages as User-Triggered Fetcher
then_action: Classify this traffic as Google Messages, a user-triggered fetcher, not a standard Googlebot crawler, and reference the `/crawling/ipranges` user-triggered fetchers list to confirm IP ranges
if_condition: If you observe unfamiliar crawl traffic generating link previews for URLs shared in chat messages
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog February 2026

name: Apply File Size Limits to All Google Crawlers
then_action: Switch to the updated crawling documentation instead, as file size limits now apply universally to all of Google's crawlers and fetchers, not just Googlebot
if_condition: If you are referencing Googlebot-specific documentation to determine file size limits for crawling behavior
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog February 2026

name: Hotel Schema: Required Core Properties
then_action: Include itemprop='name' for hotel name, itemscope itemtype='https://schema.org/PostalAddress' for address, and itemscope itemtype='https://schema.org/Rating' with itemprop='ratingValue' content attribute for star rating — all are MUST requirements
if_condition: If implementing Schema.org Hotel markup and the hotel name, address, or star rating fields are missing or improperly attributed
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Minimal Example

name: Hotel Schema: PostalAddress Sub-Properties
then_action: Use PostalAddress schema and include streetAddress, postalCode, addressLocality, addressRegion, and addressCountry as itemprop attributes within the PostalAddress container
if_condition: If structuring address data within a Schema.org Hotel or lodging entity
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Minimal Example

name: Hotel Schema: Star Rating Markup with Meta Tag
then_action: Wrap the rating in itemscope itemtype='https://schema.org/Rating' and use a meta tag with itemprop='ratingValue' and a content attribute containing the numeric rating value
if_condition: If adding star rating data to a Schema.org Hotel entity
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Minimal Example

name: Hotel Schema: Alternative Lodging Business Types
then_action: Replace Schema.org/Hotel with a more specific type such as Campground or MeetingRoom while applying the same basic markup patterns
if_condition: If the lodging business is not a standard hotel (e.g., a campground or meeting room facility)
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Minimal Example

name: Use Google-Extended controls for AI training opt-out, not robots.txt
then_action: Apply Google-Extended specific controls rather than relying solely on Googlebot robots.txt directives, as robots.txt and snippet controls apply to Search crawling only
if_condition: If a site owner wants to control use of their content for AI training and grounding in Google systems beyond Search
source_title: AI Features and Your Website Controlling your content in AI features in Search

name: Use containedInPlace or containsPlace for Room-Hotel Relationship
then_action: Use containedInPlace property (from hotel room to hotel) OR containsPlace property (from hotel to hotel room) to express the relationship
if_condition: If representing the structural relationship between an accommodation unit (hotel room) and its parent lodging business (hotel) in schema.org
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Overview

name: Rental Accommodation Offer Requires LeaseOut businessFunction
then_action: MUST attach the businessFunction property with the value LeaseOut to the offer
if_condition: If creating a schema.org offer for a rental accommodation (not for property sale)
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Overview

name: Use additionalType for Specific Accommodation Sub-Classifications
then_action: Use the additionalType property with an external identifier URL to indicate the exact accommodation type
if_condition: If using generic LodgingBusiness or Accommodation types in schema.org markup but the exact accommodation type needs to be specified more precisely
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Overview

name: Prices and Terms Belong to Offer, Not Hotel or Room
then_action: Prices and terms and conditions MUST be properties of the Offer object, NOT of the LodgingBusiness or Accommodation objects
if_condition: If marking up hotel pricing, terms and conditions, or usage constraints in schema.org
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Overview

name: Advance Booking Requirement Schema
then_action: Use advanceBookingRequirement with minValue and unitCode='DAY' to specify the advance booking constraint in schema markup
if_condition: If a hotel offer requires booking a minimum number of days in advance
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Validity of an Offer

name: Use includesObject and TypeAndQuantityNode for Bundled Offers
then_action: Use the includesObject property with one or more TypeAndQuantityNode nodes to represent the additional bundled items or nights
if_condition: If a schema.org hotel offer includes more than just the bare room or covers multiple nights (e.g., breakfast included, multi-night packages)
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Overview

name: Booking Period Validity Constraints
then_action: Use validFrom and validThrough meta properties to define the booking period constraints in schema markup
if_condition: If a hotel offer is only valid for bookings made within a specific date window
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Validity of an Offer

name: Link Lodging Business to Its Offers via makesOffer or offeredBy
then_action: Use makesOffer property (from hotel to offer) OR offeredBy property (from offer to hotel) to express this relationship
if_condition: If representing the relationship between a lodging business (hotel) and its accommodation offers in schema.org
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Overview

name: Multi-Component Pricing Schema
then_action: Use CompoundPriceSpecification type with priceComponent to represent each pricing element separately in schema markup
if_condition: If a hotel offer includes multiple price components such as room rate plus fees or taxes
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Validity of an Offer

name: Minimum Stay Duration Schema
then_action: Use eligibleDuration with minValue and unitCode='DAY' to specify the minimum stay requirement in schema markup
if_condition: If a hotel offer requires a minimum length of stay in nights or days
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Validity of an Offer

name: Revisit Strategy If No Results After Two Quarters
then_action: Revisit skill-building priorities and adjust the build/buy/borrow mix, as the current approach may be focused on the wrong capabilities
if_condition: If AI Visibility Score or brand mention metrics show no meaningful improvement after two consecutive quarters
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Measuring AI SEO Team Success

name: Eligible Stay Dates Schema
then_action: Use availabilityStarts and availabilityEnds properties to define the eligible stay date range in schema markup
if_condition: If a hotel offer applies only to stays occurring within specific arrival and departure dates
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Validity of an Offer

name: Cancellation Policy in Offer Description
then_action: Place all cancellation policy information and terms and conditions into the description property of the Offer schema — do not use separate or custom properties for this content
if_condition: If a hotel offer has cancellation policies or terms and conditions to disclose
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Validity of an Offer

name: Occupancy Limit Schema
then_action: Use the occupancy property with QuantitativeValue type and maxValue to specify the occupancy limit in schema markup
if_condition: If a hotel offer is restricted to a maximum number of occupants
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Validity of an Offer

name: Hotel Room Schema Must Use Offer Type for Commercial Aspects
then_action: Use https://schema.org/Offer type for all commercial aspects; assign accommodation instances to both https://schema.org/HotelRoom and https://schema.org/Product types using Multiple Type Extensions (MTEs)
if_condition: When marking up hotel room pricing and commercial details with Schema.org structured data
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Basic Pattern

name: Hotel Room Offer Requires Multi-Typed Entity (MTE)
then_action: MUST assign the instance as both an Accommodation subclass AND a Product using the Multi-Typed Entity (MTE) technique
if_condition: If an accommodation (hotel room, suite, apartment, etc.) is being used as the subject of an offer in schema.org markup
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Overview

name: UN/CEFACT Unit Codes Required
then_action: Only use valid UN/CEFACT unit codes (DAY, WEE, C62, IE) as values for unitCode — do not use arbitrary or custom strings
if_condition: If unitCode is used in any hotel offer schema property such as advanceBookingRequirement, eligibleDuration, or occupancy
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Validity of an Offer

name: Flag Competitive Outpacing Even When Score Improves
then_action: Treat this as a warning signal — competitors are not only more visible but outpacing you; reassess strategy, skill priorities, or build/buy/borrow mix
if_condition: If your AI Visibility Score increases in absolute terms but competitors' scores grew at a faster rate over the same period
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Measuring AI SEO Team Success

name: Buffer Essentials Plan Collaboration Restrictions
then_action: deny access to these team collaboration and reporting features; they require the Team plan
if_condition: if a user on Buffer's Essentials plan attempts to use approval workflows, custom access and permissions, or create branded reports
source_title: Pricing Support

name: Outsource When Skill Is Specialized or Short-Term
then_action: Use outsourcing or external consultants to fill the capability gap without expanding headcount, structuring as one-off engagement or recurring partnership depending on strategic need
if_condition: If a required skill is highly specialized (e.g., AI visibility tracking, passage optimization, Reddit marketing) or only needed for a short-term project that would slow the internal team
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 3 When to Borrow Outsource or Consult

name: Hotel Opening Hours Must Follow Specific Time Format
then_action: Format opening hours using the pattern 'Mo-Fr 07:00-23:00' (day abbreviation range followed by 24-hour time range); for amenity-specific hours use hoursAvailable with OpeningHoursSpecification
if_condition: If adding reception hours or amenity hours to hotel schema.org markup using openingHours or openingHoursSpecification
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Star Rating

name: Hotel Star Rating Source Should Be Attributed
then_action: Include the author property with an Organization itemtype specifying the rating authority to provide provenance for the star rating
if_condition: If the hotel star rating originates from a known classification authority (e.g. HOTREC, DEHOGA, WHR, Hotelstars)
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Star Rating

name: Hire Before You Post: Define Outcome First
then_action: Then stop recruitment and document the exact business outcome needed (e.g., faster technical execution, deeper analytics, or AI visibility leadership) before writing the job description
if_condition: If a team is preparing to post a job listing for an AI-related SEO role without first defining the specific outcome the hire must achieve
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 2 When to Buy Hire New Talent

name: Hotel Schema Must Use Correct itemtype
then_action: Use https://schema.org/Hotel as the container itemtype with itemscope, then nest starRating with https://schema.org/Rating itemtype and include a meta tag with itemprop='ratingValue' containing the numerical star value
if_condition: If adding structured markup to a hotel property page, booking platform listing, or travel site hotel entry
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Star Rating

name: Buy vs Build: Use External Hiring When Speed Exceeds Internal Training Capacity
then_action: Then prioritize external hiring over internal upskilling, targeting specialists who bridge SEO and AI retrieval systems rather than generalists
if_condition: If a mid-sized or enterprise SEO team requires specialized AI-readiness skills (structured data at scale, AI retrieval alignment, citation measurement) and internal training timelines would delay execution by more than one planning cycle
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 2 When to Buy Hire New Talent

name: Prioritize Strategic Specialist Roles Over Trend-Based Titles
then_action: Then redirect hiring focus toward roles with demonstrable skills in data/visibility analysis (citations and brand mentions across AI platforms), entity modeling and structured data implementation at scale, or AI content strategy aligned with retrieval patterns
if_condition: If hiring for AI SEO capabilities and evaluating candidates or roles defined primarily by trendy 'AI SEO' job titles rather than specific technical competencies
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 2 When to Buy Hire New Talent

name: Chain Hotels Must Link to Parent Organization
then_action: Use the parentOrganization property to link the hotel entity to its operating company in the schema.org markup
if_condition: If the hotel being marked up belongs to a hotel chain or is operated by a parent company
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Star Rating

name: Avoid Short-Term Fix Hires for Long-Term AI Visibility Needs
then_action: Then flag the hire as misaligned with strategic intent and re-evaluate whether the role should be restructured as a long-term strategic position or handled via agency/contractor instead
if_condition: If a proposed hire is scoped to solve an immediate tactical AI SEO problem (e.g., a one-time audit or single campaign) rather than building sustained AI search visibility capability
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 2 When to Buy Hire New Talent

name: Hotel Renovation Details Must Separate Description from Year
then_action: Place renovation descriptive details in the description property and the renovation year in the value property — do not combine them in a single field
if_condition: If including renovation information in hotel schema.org markup using additionalProperty with PropertyValue itemtype
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Star Rating

name: Hotel Star Rating Must Use starRating Property Not Review Properties
then_action: Use starRating property with https://schema.org/Rating itemtype and ratingValue meta tag — NOT review/Review or aggregateRating/AggregateRating properties, which are reserved for customer reviews
if_condition: If marking up official hotel star ratings (e.g. 3-star, 5-star classifications) in schema.org structured data
source_title: Hotels   Schema org Star Rating

name: Bring Outsourced Skills In-House When They Become Core Strategy
then_action: Evaluate bringing the capability in-house rather than continuing to rely on external partners, to maintain control and deepen internal expertise
if_condition: If an outsourced skill (e.g., AI visibility tracking or content optimization) transitions from a niche need to a recurring, core component of the SEO strategy
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide 3 When to Borrow Outsource or Consult

name: AI SEO Team Tool: Specificity Requirement
then_action: The output plan will be generic rather than customized; require specific answers about team structure, budget, and capabilities to generate an actionable implementation plan
if_condition: If a user provides vague or generic answers during the AI SEO Team Building Assistant interactive session
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Get a Custom AI SEO Team Plan in 20 30 Mi

name: AI SEO Team Tool: Minimum Time Block Requirement
then_action: Do not proceed with the session; the interactive process requires a minimum 30-minute uninterrupted block to produce a complete and useful implementation plan
if_condition: If a user attempts to engage with the AI SEO Team Building Assistant without blocking at least 30 uninterrupted minutes
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Get a Custom AI SEO Team Plan in 20 30 Mi

name: Sparse Off-Site Brand Mentions Reduce AI Visibility
then_action: AI tools have less information to pull from when users search for the brand or ask about its product category, reducing AI-driven discoverability
if_condition: If brand mentions across G2, Reddit, forums, and news articles are sparse or outdated
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Optimizing Off Site Signals

name: SEO Lead Must Own Cross-Functional AI Visibility Coordination
then_action: An SEO lead or director MUST take the coordination role, ensuring all teams (PR, community, customer success) understand how their work affects AI visibility
if_condition: If an organization is optimizing for AI discoverability and off-site signals
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Optimizing Off Site Signals

name: Standalone Section Rule for AI Retrieval
then_action: Rewrite the section so it stands alone and makes sense when extracted out of context, as AI systems may fail to understand its relevance and skip citing it
if_condition: If a content section requires surrounding context (e.g., references like 'As we mentioned earlier...') to be understood
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Essential AI SEO Skills Your Team Needs

name: AI Platform Monitoring Cadence
then_action: Allocate 2-3 hours monthly to test how your brand appears across AI platforms, document citation patterns, and adjust content strategy based on findings
if_condition: If your team has a designated SEO lead or strategist responsible for AI search visibility
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Essential AI SEO Skills Your Team Needs

name: Track Core AI SEO Metrics for Brand Visibility
then_action: Track citation frequency, brand mention rate, platform coverage (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews), and sentiment analysis as core metrics to determine whether mentions align with brand positioning
if_condition: If measuring AI SEO team success or brand presence in AI-powered answers
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Measuring AI SEO Team Success

name: Structured Data for AI Content Comprehension
then_action: Implement schema markup, structured internal linking, and clear site hierarchy to create signals that help AI platforms understand site structure and content relationships
if_condition: If a website lacks schema markup, clear internal linking, or an explicit site hierarchy
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Essential AI SEO Skills Your Team Needs

name: Benchmark AI Visibility Score Against Five Competitors at Start
then_action: Benchmark your brand's AI Visibility Score alongside five competitors before tracking begins, to establish a relative competitive baseline
if_condition: If beginning to build team AI SEO skills or establishing a baseline measurement program
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Measuring AI SEO Team Success

name: AI SEO Team Tool: Pre-Session Preparation
then_action: Have current team structure, budget constraints, time limitations, and capability gaps clearly in mind before beginning the interactive conversation to maximize output quality
if_condition: If a user is preparing to start the AI SEO Team Building Assistant session
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Get a Custom AI SEO Team Plan in 20 30 Mi

name: Establish AI Visibility Baseline Before Optimization
then_action: Then search the brand's most important topics in ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews to establish a visibility baseline before selecting any optimization priorities
if_condition: If an SEO team is beginning AI search optimization efforts without knowing current brand visibility in AI tools
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 1 Foundation

name: Mobile-First Indexing Compliance
then_action: Ensure the site is easy to use on mobile devices, because Google's index is now mobile-first and non-mobile-friendly sites will be disadvantaged in rankings
if_condition: If a website is not optimized for mobile devices
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 4

name: AI SEO Readiness: Updated Systems and Skills Requirement
then_action: The team is not AI-ready; upgrade team skills and systems to ensure brand mentions and website citations appear in AI search results, as AI SEO requires additional capabilities beyond traditional SEO
if_condition: If an SEO team is operating with traditional SEO skills and systems without AI-specific layers
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Get a Custom AI SEO Team Plan in 20 30 Mi

name: Query Fan-Out Content Coverage Strategy
then_action: Expand content to cover related sub-queries and adjacent questions, since AI systems use query fan-out to surface content across multiple related sub-queries beyond the original prompt
if_condition: If content is optimized only for exact-match primary queries without covering semantically related sub-topics
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Essential AI SEO Skills Your Team Needs

name: Narrow AI-Era SEO Priorities to 2-3 Actionable Areas
then_action: Then select only 2-3 priority areas with the clearest opportunity to improve (e.g., content clarity, entity mapping, or off-site mention alignment) to avoid diffusion of effort
if_condition: If a team has completed capability assessment and AI visibility baseline tracking and is ready to act
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 1 Foundation

name: AI SEO Team Tool: Platform Compatibility
then_action: Upload the file into an AI platform of choice (Claude, ChatGPT, or Gemini) and follow the interactive conversation to receive the complete implementation plan including Skills Ownership Map, Phased Roadmap, KPI Tracking Framework, Leadership Brief, and 30-day checklist
if_condition: If a user has downloaded the AI SEO Team Building Assistant file and wants to run it
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Get a Custom AI SEO Team Plan in 20 30 Mi

name: Shared Dashboard Implementation for AI Metrics
then_action: Implement shared dashboards that consolidate AI visibility metrics in one place and review them as a team so all contributors see how their work impacts results
if_condition: If team is tracking AI visibility metrics across multiple disconnected tools or reports
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 2 Acceleration

name: Define AI Strategy Leadership Before Scaling
then_action: Then assign a named leader responsible for AI-related strategy, measurement, and experimentation, and clarify team responsibilities to maintain agility at scale
if_condition: If an SEO team is expanding AI-driven programs across full SEO and content operations without defined ownership
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 3 Scale

name: Retire Frameworks That Don't Improve Visibility
then_action: Then remove or replace that framework rather than continuing to maintain it, to prevent resource drain and preserve team focus on effective approaches
if_condition: If an AI SEO framework or workflow is not consistently improving search or AI visibility across evaluation cycles
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 3 Scale

name: Expand Only Proven Pilot Approaches
then_action: Then scale that approach across full SEO and content programs as the default execution method, rather than running continued limited tests
if_condition: If an AI SEO pilot has demonstrated consistent visibility improvements in earlier phases
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 3 Scale

name: Convert Team Learnings Into Onboarding Assets
then_action: Then document those learnings into onboarding sessions, playbooks, or process docs to institutionalize knowledge and reduce dependency on individual expertise
if_condition: If the team has accumulated validated AI SEO knowledge through experimentation and piloting
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 3 Scale

name: Require Quarterly AI Performance Reporting to Leadership
then_action: Then share quarterly reports on AI-driven results and learnings with senior stakeholders so leadership treats AI SEO as an integrated function rather than an experiment
if_condition: If AI SEO programs are operating at scale with defined roles and ongoing workflows
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 3 Scale

name: Assign Ownership for Mature AI SEO Functions
then_action: Then assign dedicated ownership to those functions — either by promoting an internal team member, assigning a specialist, or establishing a long-term external partnership
if_condition: If specific AI SEO tasks require consistent ongoing work as programs mature (e.g., AI visibility reporting, off-site signal monitoring)
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 3 Scale

name: Audience Identity Alignment for Click Intent
then_action: Ensure copy causes the target audience to see themselves reflected in the messaging, as audience self-identification increases click-through likelihood
if_condition: If targeting a specific audience segment in an ad
source_title: Making Ads 2

name: Migrated Crawling Documentation Has Unchanged Functionality
then_action: Then treat the migrated documentation as functionally identical to the original — only the location and minor wording have changed, no underlying crawling behavior has been modified
if_condition: If a site owner or SEO references previously bookmarked or linked Google crawling documentation URLs (e.g., for faceted navigation, crawl budget, HTTP status codes, DNS errors) and finds them redirected or relocated
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog December 2025

name: Tailor Content Strategy Per AI Platform's Citation Preferences
then_action: Customize the content and source-building strategy per platform: for ChatGPT prioritize Wikipedia and Forbes presence; for Google AI Overviews prioritize Reddit, YouTube, and Quora; for Perplexity prioritize Reddit above all other sources (46.7% citation share)
if_condition: If a brand is targeting a specific AI platform such as ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, or Perplexity for citation visibility
source_title: Answer Engine Optimization AEO Your Complete Guide to AI Search Visibility Frequently Asked Question

name: Customer Reviews on G2 or Trustpilot Support AI Signal Coverage
then_action: Work with customer success teams to actively encourage reviews on those platforms to expand AI-accessible brand signals
if_condition: If off-site review presence on platforms like G2 or Trustpilot is limited
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Optimizing Off Site Signals

name: Monitor Brand Mentions Across Forums and Social for AI Readiness
then_action: Implement ongoing monitoring of these channels to identify gaps in off-site signal coverage that could reduce AI discoverability
if_condition: If brand mentions across forums, social platforms, and community discussions are unmonitored
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Optimizing Off Site Signals

name: Long Section Segmentation for AI Parsing
then_action: Break it into three or more shorter subsections with specific, descriptive headers to improve AI parseability and standalone comprehension of each unit
if_condition: If a content section exceeds approximately 500 words under a single header
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Essential AI SEO Skills Your Team Needs

name: Platform-Specific Citation Pattern Awareness
then_action: Do not assume citation strategies that work on one platform will transfer to another — each platform retrieves and displays information differently (e.g., Perplexity uses real-time web search citations, ChatGPT cites YouTube and Reddit, Google AI Mode draws from search index and Gemini training data, Claude cites Quora and Android Authority)
if_condition: If optimizing content for AI visibility across multiple platforms (ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, Claude, Perplexity)
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Platform Specific Optimization

name: Add Google-Agent to Crawler Verification Processes
then_action: Add the Google-Agent user agent to your verification processes for Google crawlers and fetchers, as it is being rolled out for Google agents hosted on Google infrastructure that navigate the web upon user request
if_condition: If your site has crawler verification logic that checks for known Google user agents
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog March 2026

name: Include User-Triggered Agent IP Ranges in Verification Documentation
then_action: Include the IP ranges for user-triggered agents in your verification documentation to properly authenticate Google-Agent requests
if_condition: If you maintain documentation or logic for verifying requests from Google crawlers
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog March 2026

name: AI Visibility Core Metrics Tracking Requirement
then_action: MUST track all of the following: mentions (does brand appear at all), citations (does answer link or attribute site content), prominence (first, early, late, or incidental positioning), share of voice (frequency relative to key competitors), topic coverage (which prompt clusters include vs exclude the brand), sentiment and framing (how the brand is described), referral traffic (visits from AI platforms), branded search lift (rise in branded queries correlated with AI presence), and conversion quality (engagement differences of AI-influenced users)
if_condition: When measuring AI visibility for a brand or product
source_title: Primary meta title AI Visibility How to Get Cited in Google AI Overviews ChatGPT How to Measure AI V

name: Use Share of Voice for Competitive AI Visibility Benchmarking
then_action: Monitor Share of Voice percentage compared to competitors using Brand Performance reports to understand relative competitive standing in AI platforms
if_condition: If monitoring competitive position in AI-generated answers on an ongoing basis
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Measuring AI SEO Team Success

name: Validate AI Optimization Changes with Representative Pilot Pages
then_action: Then recheck those specific representative pages in AI tools to measure whether updates improved brand appearance frequency in AI-generated answers before scaling changes
if_condition: If content or entity updates have been made based on AI visibility learnings
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 1 Foundation

name: Strategic AI Search Role Requirement
then_action: Assign a lead or strategist (or content strategist in smaller teams) to develop AI retrieval knowledge, as absence of this role leaves the team unable to adapt when AI platforms change
if_condition: If an SEO team lacks at least one person with strategic understanding of how AI search retrieval and citation works
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Essential AI SEO Skills Your Team Needs

name: Unified Strategy with Platform-Aware Adjustments
then_action: Work with content, technical, and off-site teams to adjust the overall strategy holistically — a single unified strategy is sufficient, but it must account for platform-specific citation behaviors rather than requiring completely separate strategies per platform
if_condition: If brand coverage gaps are identified on specific AI platforms after cross-platform tracking
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Platform Specific Optimization

name: Measure Growth Rate Not Just Final Score
then_action: Compare growth rates over 3-12 months rather than comparing only final scores, to accurately assess team execution and competitive trajectory
if_condition: If evaluating AI SEO performance after 3-12 months of implementation
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Measuring AI SEO Team Success

name: Cross-Functional AI Visibility Alignment
then_action: Bring all functions together under one shared visibility goal and clarify ownership to prevent responsibility overlap
if_condition: If content, SEO, analytics, product, and brand teams are operating independently without a shared AI visibility goal
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 2 Acceleration

name: Function-Specific AI Readiness Definition
then_action: Define what AI-ready means within each function's specific area so each team understands its responsibilities in AI-driven discovery workflows
if_condition: If team members across different functions are unclear about what AI-ready means for their specific role
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 2 Acceleration

name: Google Read Aloud Requires Page Access for Meta Tag Detection
then_action: Then the page must be accessible to the Google Read Aloud user agent, as it must physically access the page to read the meta tags used on it
if_condition: If Google Read Aloud user agent is crawling a page and meta tags need to be identified or audited
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog December 2025

name: Pilot Expansion to Broader Pages After Phase 1 Learnings
then_action: Expand the pilot by applying Phase 1 learnings to more pages or campaigns and standardize those processes into repeatable workflows
if_condition: If Phase 1 pilot has produced learnings and validated initial AI SEO approaches
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Phase 2 Acceleration

name: Cross-Platform Brand Presence Tracking
then_action: Track how the brand appears across all major AI platforms (ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, Claude, Perplexity) and identify gaps in coverage on LLMs that matter to the brand
if_condition: If a brand is pursuing AI visibility optimization
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Platform Specific Optimization

name: Quarterly Audit of Skill Dependency on External Help
then_action: Audit which SEO skills rely too heavily on outside help and rebalance the build-borrow-buy mix accordingly to reduce over-dependency
if_condition: If a quarterly review period has been reached for the SEO team structure
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Hybrid Reality

name: Promote Borrowed Skill to Internal Build When Recurring
then_action: Transition that skill from borrowed to internally built — begin training or hiring internally rather than continuing to rely on external sources
if_condition: If a skill that was initially borrowed from outside experts is being used on a recurring basis (e.g., appearing consistently across quarters or projects)
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Hybrid Reality

name: Source Type Alignment by Platform
then_action: Prioritize content presence on the source types that platform preferentially cites — adjust off-site presence and content distribution channels to match each platform's known citation sources
if_condition: If targeting citation on a specific AI platform (e.g., ChatGPT cites YouTube and Reddit threads, Claude cites Quora and Android Authority, Perplexity cites real-time web sources with numbered citations, Google AI Mode draws from search index)
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Platform Specific Optimization

name: Conversational Query Pattern Documentation
then_action: Analyze and document longer, conversational prompt patterns from AI platforms and structure content briefs to address those specific questions and scenarios directly
if_condition: If building content briefs intended to rank or appear in AI platform responses
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Query Intent Mapping

name: Use External Resources for Speed and Experimentation
then_action: Borrow external expertise rather than building internally or hiring — prioritize speed and experimentation over long-term investment at this stage
if_condition: If the SEO team needs to move quickly on a new initiative or test an unproven AI SEO approach
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Hybrid Reality

name: Technical SEO Priority Shift for AI Crawlability
then_action: Technical SEO priorities MUST shift to emphasize AI crawlability, site performance, and schema implementation — alongside (not replacing) traditional ranking signals
if_condition: If technical SEO priorities are being set for a site that targets AI search visibility
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Skills Gap Between Traditional and AI

name: Hire Only When Role Becomes Strategically Essential
then_action: Only proceed with hiring when the role has become essential to the core SEO strategy — avoid premature full-time hires for skills that can still be borrowed or built
if_condition: If the team is considering purchasing (hiring) a new SEO role
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Hybrid Reality

name: Rebalance Team Structure Based on Impact, Not Org Chart
then_action: Adjust the build-borrow-buy ratio and role assignments based on what is actually delivering the most SEO impact, not on what titles or org chart positions suggest
if_condition: If the current team structure is based on org chart hierarchy rather than measured SEO impact
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Hybrid Reality

name: AI SEO Content Structuring Requirement
then_action: Content MUST be structured so AI can easily understand it and extract specific sections for AI-generated answers — formatting for section-level extraction, not just page-level ranking
if_condition: If content is being created or optimized for AI search visibility (AEO/GEO)
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Skills Gap Between Traditional and AI

name: Subscribe to Google Crawling Documentation Changelog
then_action: Add https://developers.google.com/crawling/docs/changelog to your feed reader, and use https://developers.google.com/crawling/docs/changelog/crawling_docs_updates.rss for direct RSS feed access
if_condition: If you need to stay current with Google's crawling behavior changes and new user agent introductions
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog March 2026

name: AI Platform Query Length Threshold
then_action: Target queries averaging 13+ words that are conversational and specific, rather than 3-4 word traditional keyword phrases
if_condition: If content is being optimized for AI platforms (e.g., ChatGPT, Perplexity) rather than traditional Google search
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Query Intent Mapping

name: Problem-Solving Intent Mapping Requirement
then_action: Shift research framing from 'what keywords do people use?' to 'what problems are people trying to solve?' and document the specific questions, context, and desired outcomes behind queries
if_condition: If conducting keyword research or content planning for AI-ready SEO
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Query Intent Mapping

name: AI Citation and Brand Mention Tracking Requirement
then_action: Measurement framework MUST include citations and brand mentions across AI platforms (e.g., ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity) in addition to traditional SEO metrics like rankings and organic traffic
if_condition: If performance tracking is being configured for a brand competing in AI search environments
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Skills Gap Between Traditional and AI

name: Internal Ownership of Outsourced SEO Work
then_action: Ensure someone on the internal team explicitly owns the outcome, reviews deliverables, and applies learnings — do not let external parties hold sole accountability for results
if_condition: If outside partners or contractors are executing SEO work on behalf of the team
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Hybrid Reality

name: Zero-Click Risk Warning for AI Search
then_action: Strategy must account for zero-click reality: strong AI visibility does not guarantee website traffic, so brand presence and citation-based authority must be tracked and optimized as independent KPIs
if_condition: If a brand's SEO strategy depends on organic traffic conversion from search visibility alone
