source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Skills Gap Between Traditional and AI

name: Delay Entity Database Registration Until Core AI SEO Structure Is Established
then_action: Do NOT register brand and products in entity databases like Wikipedia, Wikidata, or Crunchbase yet — entity registration should only be explored after core AI SEO structure is in place
if_condition: When an AI SEO strategy is in early stages and core AI SEO structure (content optimization, citations, structured data) has not yet been established
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Tracking AI Performance

name: AI Pulls From Full Digital Footprint, Not Just Website
then_action: Investigate the full digital footprint — including third-party mentions, social presence, reviews, and off-site citations — since AI systems pull from the entire web ecosystem, not just the brand's own website content
if_condition: If a brand is auditing why it is or is not appearing in AI-generated answers despite strong on-site SEO
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide The Skills Gap Between Traditional and AI

name: Use Manual Tracking When Specialized AI Visibility Tools Are Unavailable
then_action: Manually search key queries across AI platforms (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, etc.) and document when and how the brand appears, tracking changes over time in AI performance metrics
if_condition: When specialized AI visibility tools (e.g., Semrush's AI Visibility Toolkit) are not available for monitoring AI search performance
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Tracking AI Performance

name: Track Brand Mention Rate Across AI Systems
then_action: MUST track mention rate showing how often the brand appears in AI-generated answers or recommendations across AI systems
if_condition: When assessing brand presence in AI-generated answers and recommendations
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Tracking AI Performance

name: Front-Load Target Keyword in Title Tag
then_action: Start the title tag with the target keyword to maximize keyword prominence and SEO weight
if_condition: If a page has a target keyword and the title tag can begin with that keyword without appearing unnatural or awkward to users
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 2

name: Google Read Aloud Uses Stateless Rendering
then_action: Then that content will not be accessible to Google Read Aloud, since it uses stateless rendering and cannot maintain session or state between requests
if_condition: If a page relies on stateful rendering (e.g., session-based content, login state, persistent cookies) to serve its primary content
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog December 2025

name: Track Platform Breakdown for AI Search Visibility
then_action: MUST track platform breakdown showing where the brand is appearing across each specific AI platform, as traditional SEO metrics like rankings and organic traffic do not indicate AI search visibility
if_condition: When measuring AI search performance and brand visibility across AI platforms (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, etc.)
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Tracking AI Performance

name: Monitor Citation Frequency in AI Responses
then_action: MUST monitor citation frequency measuring how often your content gets cited as a source in AI responses, tracked monthly using specialized AI visibility tools or manual searches
if_condition: When evaluating whether content is being used as a source in AI-generated responses
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Tracking AI Performance

name: Monitor Mention Sentiment in AI Outputs
then_action: MUST monitor mention sentiment to determine whether AI-generated brand mentions are positive, neutral, or negative
if_condition: When a brand is appearing in AI-generated answers and the sentiment quality of those mentions is unknown
source_title: How to Build an AI Ready SEO Team in 2026 A Complete Guide Tracking AI Performance

name: Place Keyword Early When Front-Loading Is Unnatural
then_action: Include the target keyword as early in the title tag as possible while keeping the title readable and user-friendly
if_condition: If starting the title tag with the target keyword makes it look awkward, forced, or unnatural to users
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 2

name: Crawling Guidance Applies Beyond Google Search
then_action: Then broaden the scope of implementation to account for all Google products using Google's crawling infrastructure, as the guidance applies across multiple Google products not just Search
if_condition: If crawling optimization guidance (e.g., crawl budget, faceted navigation, HTTP status codes, DNS errors) is being applied assuming it only affects Google Search
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog December 2025

name: Poor UX Blocks Link Acquisition Despite Good Content
then_action: Then the site will fail to earn links and shares, indirectly suppressing SEO performance regardless of content quality
if_condition: If a site has high-quality content but also has poor user experience (intrusive popups, broken links, slow load times)
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 3

name: Avoid Bulky WordPress Themes for Speed
then_action: Then switch to a lightweight theme optimized for speed to improve Core Web Vitals and reduce load time
if_condition: If a WordPress site is using a bulky, unoptimized theme and site speed scores are low
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 3

name: Benchmark Speed Before Optimization
then_action: Then benchmark current loading speed using PageSpeed Insights and Web Page Test to get baseline metrics and Google-sourced improvement recommendations before making changes
if_condition: If beginning a site speed improvement initiative
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 3

name: Image Compression and CDN for Page Speed
then_action: Then compress images using tools like Kraken.io and implement a CDN to serve media from servers geographically close to users, reducing page size and load time
if_condition: If site pages are large due to unoptimized images or media served from a single origin server
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 3

name: Lazy Loading Trade-off Awareness
then_action: Then be aware of the UX trade-off: images only appear as users scroll, which may negatively impact perceived experience even while improving load speed metrics
if_condition: If implementing lazy loading to improve page load speed by 50% or more
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 3

name: Internal Link Authority Flow to New Pages
then_action: Link to that new page from older established pages to transfer link authority and improve its crawlability and ranking potential
if_condition: If a new page has been published with little or no inbound link authority
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 5

name: Backlink Building as a Ranking Priority
then_action: Prioritize acquiring backlinks to that page, as backlinks remain strongly correlated with higher Google rankings per SEMrush research
if_condition: If a page is underperforming in Google rankings despite good on-page optimization
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 5

name: Exclude Keywords with SERP Features Blocking Organic Clicks
then_action: Avoid targeting that keyword, as organic CTR will be suppressed; use Ahrefs 'Clicks' data or manual SERP inspection to confirm before exclusion
if_condition: If a keyword's SERP contains Featured Snippets, heavy ads, or other SERP features that occupy the visible results and reduce organic click opportunity
source_title: 6 Step Essential Guide to Choosing Keywords

name: Descriptive Image Filenames Required
then_action: Name images with descriptive filenames that reflect the image content, because Google cannot visually interpret images and uses filenames to understand image context
if_condition: If images are uploaded or added to a webpage
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 4

name: Avoid Keyword Difficulty Score as Sole Competition Metric
then_action: Do not rely on it exclusively, as keyword difficulty scores base competition entirely on backlinks and ignore other ranking factors; supplement with MozBar Page Authority and Domain Authority checks
if_condition: If keyword difficulty score is the only metric being used to assess competition for a keyword
source_title: 6 Step Essential Guide to Choosing Keywords

name: Assess Earning Potential Using Top of Page Bid and Product-Keyword Fit
then_action: Check the 'Top of page bid' in Google Keyword Planner to gauge commercial intent, and verify that the keyword has strong Product-Keyword Fit — meaning it reaches users likely to convert for your offering
if_condition: If a keyword has passed competition and search volume filters and is being evaluated for final selection
source_title: 6 Step Essential Guide to Choosing Keywords

name: Avoid Keywords With Heavy SERP Feature Saturation
then_action: Avoid targeting that keyword, as organic CTR will be severely suppressed and traffic potential is effectively nullified despite high search volume
if_condition: If a keyword's search results page contains Featured Snippets, multiple ads, or other SERP features that dominate above-the-fold visibility
source_title: 6 Step Essential Guide to Choosing Keywords

name: Defer Competition and CPC Filtering Until After Initial List Creation
then_action: Do not filter or discard keywords based on competition, CPC, or other metrics at this stage; focus solely on generating the broadest possible keyword list first
if_condition: If you are in the initial keyword brainstorming phase using tools like Google Keyword Planner, Semrush, or Google Suggest
source_title: 6 Step Essential Guide to Choosing Keywords

name: Select Only Trending or Stable Keywords Using Google Trends
then_action: Verify keyword trend direction using Google Trends set to 'Past 12 months'; only select keywords that are trending upward or remain stable — avoid declining keywords
if_condition: If a keyword has passed all prior filters (competition, volume, earning potential, CTR) and is being considered for final targeting
source_title: 6 Step Essential Guide to Choosing Keywords

name: Regular Search Console Report Monitoring
then_action: Regularly check the Performance, Coverage, and Enhancements reports to track impressions, clicks, indexed pages, crawling issues, and mobile usability
if_condition: If a site is actively targeting SEO rankings and Search Console is set up
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 4

name: Calibrate Search Volume Thresholds by Industry Context
then_action: Apply industry-specific thresholds rather than universal minimums — e.g., 25k/month may be high for B2B but insufficient for B2C fitness — and consider that targeting keywords not directly related to your product can still be valid if they reach your target demographic
if_condition: If evaluating whether a keyword's monthly search volume is sufficient to justify targeting
source_title: 6 Step Essential Guide to Choosing Keywords

name: Use Clickstream-Based Tools for Accurate Search Volume Estimates
then_action: Supplement or replace with clickstream-data tools such as Moz Pro or Ahrefs, as Google Keyword Planner provides estimated ranges rather than exact volumes, and different tools use varying data sources
if_condition: If precise search volume data is needed and Google Keyword Planner is the only tool being used
source_title: 6 Step Essential Guide to Choosing Keywords

name: Internal Linking for SEO Improvement
then_action: Add internal links from existing pages to other relevant pages on the site, as even random internal linking is better than none and it is one of the easiest SEO best practices to implement
if_condition: If pages on a site have no or minimal internal links pointing to other pages on the same site
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 4

name: SEO Strategy Reassessment When Metrics Stagnate
then_action: Reassess and try a different SEO approach, as flat or declining impressions and clicks indicate the current strategy is not working
if_condition: If impressions and clicks in Google Search Console Performance report are not increasing over time after implementing SEO best practices
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 4

name: Warn Against Passive Backlink Expectations from Format Alone
then_action: Do not assume backlinks will follow automatically; the content format increases odds but does not guarantee links without a compelling hook or unique element
if_condition: If a site publishes 'what' or 'why' posts or infographics without a hook or differentiated angle
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026 6

name: Cover Topic Comprehensively to Prevent Google Bounce Signal
then_action: Google interprets the bounce as a quality failure; content must cover everything readers are looking for, delivered concisely, to prevent this negative signal
if_condition: If a content page does not fully satisfy what readers are searching for and leaves them returning to Google to visit another site
source_title: Content Marketing

name: Use Google Trends to Target Breakout Keyword Opportunities
then_action: Use Google Trends to identify breakout (rapidly rising) keywords and publish content on those exploding topics early to capture emerging search demand
if_condition: If seeking high-growth content opportunities in your industry before topics become saturated
source_title: Content Marketing

name: Reference Updated Crawling Documentation Location
then_action: Update all references to point to the new dedicated Google crawling documentation site, as documentation has been migrated there from Google Search Central
if_condition: If referencing or linking to Google crawling documentation previously hosted on Google Search Central (covering crawlers overview, robots.txt specification, crawl rate, or verifying Googlebot)
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog November 2025

name: Apply Updated Google-Extended Token Description
then_action: Refer to the updated Google-Extended product token description which has been revised for additional specificity and clarity based on publisher feedback to ensure correct implementation
if_condition: If configuring robots.txt or access rules using the Google-Extended product token to control AI training data access
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog November 2025

name: Creator Economy Visibility Protection
then_action: Implement GEO optimization to restore content visibility and ensure creators are not disadvantaged as generative engines displace traditional search engines
if_condition: If content creators have little to no visibility or control over how their content is displayed in generative engine responses
source_title: 2311 09735 GEO Generative Engine Optimization

name: SEO Bulk Admin Limitation for Enterprise Teams
then_action: Do not rely solely on SEO Bulk Admin; prefer log file analysis instead, as SEO Bulk Admin is best suited for content-focused SEOs and marketers rather than enterprise teams needing deep technical detail
if_condition: If an enterprise SEO team requires granular AI crawler analysis with raw server log data
source_title: A Step By Step Guide For Growing AI Engine Engagement An Easier Way To Monitor AI Visits SEO Bulk Ad

name: GEO Visibility Optimization Framework
then_action: Apply GEO black-box optimization framework with domain-specific methods and measure visibility metrics using GEO-bench across diverse user queries and multiple domains to boost visibility by up to 40%
if_condition: If content is being optimized for generative engine responses (LLM-based search engines) rather than traditional search engines
source_title: 2311 09735 GEO Generative Engine Optimization

name: Unique Meta Elements for All Page Types
then_action: Write unique titles, meta descriptions, and on-page content for every individual page type including product, landing, category pages and image alt text
if_condition: If a site has title tags, meta descriptions, ecommerce product pages, landing pages, image alt text, or category pages sharing identical or template-duplicated metadata
source_title: 10 Best Practice to Improve Your SEO Rankings in 2026

name: Domain-Specific GEO Strategy Requirement
then_action: Switch to domain-specific optimization methods, as efficacy of GEO strategies varies significantly across domains and a one-size-fits-all approach will underperform
if_condition: If a single uniform GEO optimization strategy is being applied across multiple content domains without variation
source_title: 2311 09735 GEO Generative Engine Optimization

name: Non-WordPress Platform Incompatibility
then_action: Do not attempt to implement SEO Bulk Admin for AI crawler tracking, as the plugin requires WordPress and will not be available on other platforms
if_condition: If the website does not run on the WordPress platform
source_title: A Step By Step Guide For Growing AI Engine Engagement An Easier Way To Monitor AI Visits SEO Bulk Ad

name: Use Data-Driven Content Identification for AEO
then_action: Then identify which specific content pieces AI crawlers engage with most frequently to build a data-driven content optimization blueprint
if_condition: If developing an AI Engine Optimization strategy and unsure which content to prioritize
source_title: A Step By Step Guide For Growing AI Engine Engagement Take A Systematic Approach To AI Engine Optimi

name: Apply Systematic Approach to AI Engine Optimization
then_action: Take a systematic step-by-step approach to AI engine optimization, including understanding AI crawlers, tracking their activity, analyzing data, and applying targeted optimization strategies
if_condition: If a website owner wants to earn AI citations and improve visibility in AI engine results
source_title: A Step By Step Guide For Growing AI Engine Engagement

name: Use SEO Bulk Admin for AI Crawler Monitoring on WordPress
then_action: Install and use the SEO Bulk Admin WordPress plugin to track AI crawler visits, monitor which pages AI bots access most frequently, and make data-informed optimization decisions through its dashboard interface
if_condition: If the website runs on WordPress and the user needs to monitor AI crawler/bot activity without server log access or technical expertise
source_title: A Step By Step Guide For Growing AI Engine Engagement An Easier Way To Monitor AI Visits SEO Bulk Ad

name: AI Crawler Frequency Signals Training Data Representation
then_action: Those pages likely have higher representation in AI training data, increasing the probability your content is referenced or cited in AI-generated responses
if_condition: If certain pages on your site are crawled more frequently by AI crawlers (GPTBot, PerplexityBot, ClaudeBot, Googlebot AI)
source_title: A Step By Step Guide For Growing AI Engine Engagement What Are AI Crawlers How To Use Them To Your A

name: Warn Against Log File Analysis for Under-Resourced Teams
then_action: Be aware that log file analysis may be impractical due to barriers including large file sizes, complex parsing workflows, and the need for detailed user-agent knowledge — consider alternative crawler monitoring approaches
if_condition: If the team lacks dedicated technical resources for server access, log format understanding, and data parsing
source_title: A Step By Step Guide For Growing AI Engine Engagement How To Track AI Crawler Activity Find Use Log

name: Apply Clarity and Conciseness Formatting for AI-Favored Content
then_action: Refine content to use clear, unambiguous language with direct answers, brief paragraphs, and strong topic segmentation to match patterns observed in AI-favored content
if_condition: If a page receives low AI crawler attention despite covering a relevant topic
source_title: A Step By Step Guide For Growing AI Engine Engagement Using AI Crawler Data To Improve Content Strat

name: Monitor AI Crawler Activity to Identify High-Value Content
then_action: Use that data to identify what content AI systems find most valuable and prioritize optimizing or expanding that content to increase AI ecosystem visibility
if_condition: If you have access to server logs or crawler analytics showing which pages AI crawlers visit most frequently
source_title: A Step By Step Guide For Growing AI Engine Engagement What Are AI Crawlers How To Use Them To Your A

name: Track AI Visits Using SEO Bulk Admin Tools
then_action: Use easier methods such as SEO Bulk Admin tools to monitor and track AI engine visits to the website
if_condition: If log file analysis is too complex or unavailable for tracking AI crawler visits
source_title: A Step By Step Guide For Growing AI Engine Engagement

name: Web Light crawler ignores robots.txt entirely
then_action: Then those rules would have had no effect — Web Light ignored robots.txt entirely as it only served explicit human browse requests, not automated crawling
if_condition: If a site had robots.txt rules intended to block the Web Light crawler from accessing pages
source_title: Google Special Case Crawlers Retired special case crawlers

name: Web Light respects no-transform header
then_action: Then Web Light would check for the presence of the no-transform header and respect it, preventing content transformation
if_condition: If a page served a no-transform HTTP response header and a user clicked it in Search under appropriate conditions
source_title: Google Special Case Crawlers Retired special case crawlers

name: Adapt SEO Strategy to Include Generative Engine Optimization (GEO)
then_action: Evolve the strategy to include GEO practices — ensure the brand is visible, accurately cited, and correctly represented within AI-generated answers across search and LLM platforms
if_condition: If an existing SEO strategy was built solely around traditional search ranking signals and organic click-through rates
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 2

name: Schema.org Governance via W3C Community Group
then_action: Then join the W3C Schema.org Community Group as the main forum, and engage with the steering group (Google, Microsoft, Yahoo, Yandex, W3C representative, and substantial contributors) for schema decisions
if_condition: If seeking to contribute to or influence Schema.org schema decisions and operations after April 2015
source_title: About   Schema org People

name: Do Not Expect Discovery Traffic from Google AI Mode
then_action: Abandon that expectation — AI Mode has an almost 100% zero-click rate and consumes all top-of-funnel and mid-funnel activity, reserving clicks exclusively for transactional queries
if_condition: If a site depends on Google AI Mode for top-of-funnel or mid-funnel audience discovery
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 2

name: Schema.org Issue Tracking via GitHub
then_action: Then use GitHub to track Schema.org issues as the designated platform for issue management
if_condition: If reporting or tracking issues related to Schema.org schemas
source_title: About   Schema org People

name: Media Pickup Topic Selection
then_action: Prioritize current, high-interest topics that are likely to attract media and blogger coverage, as third-party coverage is the primary mechanism for driving significant traffic increases
if_condition: If selecting topics for data-driven blog posts intended to generate organic traffic
source_title: Customer Acquisition Maximizing your Funnel   For Entrepreneurs Example 2 Using Data to drive custom

name: Evaluate LLM Traffic by Conversion Quality, Not Volume
then_action: Do not dismiss LLM traffic based on volume alone — assess conversion quality separately, as LLM traffic has been observed to drive outsized outcomes (e.g., 12% of signups from 0.5% of clicks in one case), though this ratio is not universally extrapolatable
if_condition: If LLM-referred traffic appears low in volume (e.g., 0.5% of total clicks) but shows disproportionately high conversion signals
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 2

name: Use Keyword Search Volume as Demand Proxy Until AI Volume Data Exists
then_action: Use traditional keyword search volume as the best available proxy for overall demand, since reliable AI search volume data does not yet exist
if_condition: If trying to estimate demand or growth trends for topics targeted in AI-driven search
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 6

name: Prioritize Brand Credibility to Influence LLM Citations
then_action: Build brand equity through quality backlinks, media mentions, and citations from authoritative sources, as LLMs preferentially reference trusted and credible sources
if_condition: If a brand wants to be referenced or cited by LLMs when generating answers
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 6

name: Include TikTok as a Search Channel When Targeting Gen Z Audiences
then_action: Incorporate TikTok into the search optimization strategy, as Gen Z increasingly uses TikTok like a search engine and often prefers it over Google
if_condition: If the target audience includes Gen Z users and the current strategy does not account for TikTok as a primary search and discovery platform
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 4

name: Do Not Abandon Traditional SEO Despite AI Search Growth
then_action: Maintain traditional SEO fundamentals — Google Search still drives 40% of website traffic versus 0.24% from ChatGPT, making traditional search the dominant traffic source
if_condition: If a site is considering deprioritizing traditional SEO in favor of AI search optimization
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 6

name: Citability and Linkability as Content Quality Signal
then_action: Then that content is more likely to be cited, linked, and shared across the industry — functioning as a positive signal for both traditional SEO authority and AI answer sourcing
if_condition: If content contains authentic expertise, proprietary data, or unique industry perspectives that are not replicable by generic AI generation
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 5

name: Multi-Channel Resilience Requirement
then_action: Then SHOULD diversify into multi-channel resilience strategy, since Google dependency creates critical vulnerability given AI-driven search changes reducing traditional organic click volume
if_condition: If an SEO strategy relies solely on Google organic traffic as its primary or only acquisition channel
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 5

name: Target Parent Topics Alongside Specific Keywords for Broader Reach
then_action: Consider both specific keywords and broader parent topics to capture wider audiences and improve coverage across varied user intents and AI prompts
if_condition: If conducting keyword or topic research for content intended to reach wide audiences across both search and AI platforms
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 6

name: AI Tool Adoption Competitive Risk
then_action: Then they risk falling behind competitors who are using AI effectively to reduce time on optimization tasks; AI tool adoption for efficiency is required to remain competitive even when human expertise drives the content itself
if_condition: If an SEO team or content operation is not currently using AI tools for content optimization, topical gap analysis, or competitive benchmarking
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 5

name: GA4 Bounce Classification Rule
then_action: Then the session is classified as 'not engaged' (bounced), and counts toward the bounce rate percentage
if_condition: If a GA4 session has duration of 10 seconds or less, no conversion events triggered, and only one page or screen view recorded
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It 2

name: GA4 Event-Based Bounce Prevention Rule
then_action: Then that session is NOT a bounce in UA; however, in GA4 the same event only prevents a bounce if it is specifically a conversion event — non-conversion interactive events alone do not prevent bounce classification in GA4
if_condition: If a user triggers any interactive event in Universal Analytics after the first pageview (e.g., button click, scroll event)
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It 2

name: Target Gen Z Audiences via TikTok Search Behavior
then_action: Invest in TikTok content optimized for search discovery, treating TikTok as a core search platform rather than a supplementary social channel
if_condition: If the target audience includes Gen Z users who are actively using TikTok as a primary search engine in preference to Google
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 4

name: Prioritize Broader Online Presence for AI Understanding
then_action: Focus on broader online presence signals including reviews, news articles, forums, social media, and blogs — not just owned website content — since AI understanding is built from what others say about your brand
if_condition: If optimizing for AI-generated search result inclusion
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 3

name: Monitor AI Visibility and Gap Topics
then_action: Use AI visibility monitoring tools (e.g., Brand Radar) to track mentions and citations, then analyze topic gaps to create targeted content covering missed subjects
if_condition: If a brand is not tracking where and whether it appears in AI-generated answers across major AI indexes
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 3

name: Pursue Citation Acquisition via Cited Sites
then_action: Contact those cited sites to request inclusion or feature placement, as earning mentions on already-cited sources increases the likelihood of appearing in AI-generated answers
if_condition: If AI-generated answers cite competitor sites or third-party sources but not your brand
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 3

name: Early AI Visibility Action Window Warning
then_action: Act now to establish AI visibility before it becomes a mainstream priority — early movers gain competitive advantage that will be significantly harder to achieve once the market saturates on AI-specific optimization
if_condition: If a brand has not yet prioritized AI visibility optimization while AI search adoption is still growing
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 3

name: AdsBot-Google-Mobile ignores global wildcard in robots.txt
then_action: Then the global wildcard (*) will NOT block AdsBot-Google-Mobile — a specific AdsBot-Google-Mobile user agent directive must be used instead
if_condition: If robots.txt uses only the global user agent wildcard (*) to block crawlers and AdsBot-Google-Mobile access control is needed
source_title: Google Special Case Crawlers Retired special case crawlers

name: Three-Factor AI Overviews Inclusion Optimization
then_action: Then prioritize building these three factors in order: (1) branded web mentions (linked and unlinked), (2) branded anchor texts in inbound links, and (3) branded search volume via keywords containing the brand name
if_condition: If optimizing a website for inclusion in AI Overviews and AI-generated citations
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 7

name: Unlinked Brand Mentions Count as AI Trust Signals
then_action: Then these unlinked branded web mentions contribute to AI system trust signals and should be actively pursued as part of brand authority building, not just traditional link-building
if_condition: If a brand name appears on external web pages without a hyperlink (unlinked mentions) in addition to linked mentions
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 7

name: AI Visibility Requires New Success Measurement
then_action: Then update success metrics to account for AI citation frequency and AI Overview inclusion, since visibility now appears in different places than traditional search rankings
if_condition: If a brand has strong traditional SEO fundamentals and high-quality content but has not adapted measurement frameworks for AI-driven search environments
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same 7

name: AI Overview Click Loss Warning
then_action: Expect the #1 organic result to lose approximately 34% of its historical click-through rate; adjust traffic projections and visibility strategy accordingly
if_condition: If an AI Overview appears above organic results for a target keyword or query
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same

name: Fan-Out Query Coverage Mapping
then_action: Map content to cover up to 10 related sub-queries that Google's fan-out system may generate from that single query, ensuring topical breadth and depth to appear across multiple related searches
if_condition: If a target query is complex or contains multiple constraints (e.g., budget, performance, and portability requirements simultaneously)
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same

name: AI Overview Source Eligibility
then_action: Content is better positioned to be pulled as a source for AI Overviews, which synthesize insights from multiple sources to deliver complete answers at the top of search results
if_condition: If content provides comprehensive, multi-faceted answers that address trade-offs between competing constraints rather than single-dimension responses
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same

name: No Bounce if Page Was Not the Landing Page
then_action: Do NOT count this as a bounce — Bounce Rate only applies to the first page of a session, not subsequent pages visited
if_condition: A visitor leaves from a page they navigated to after initially landing on a different page
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Bounce Rate vs Exit Rate

name: Ecommerce Bounce Rate Benchmark Scoring
then_action: Flag as above-average bounce rate for ecommerce; acceptable range is 20-45%, and scores above this threshold indicate underperformance requiring attention
if_condition: If the site is an ecommerce site and bounce rate exceeds 45%
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It

name: High Bounce Rate on Landing Pages Signals Improvement Needed
then_action: Flag the landing page for improvement — visitors are leaving without any engagement, suggesting content, relevance, or UX issues
if_condition: A landing page shows a high Bounce Rate
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Bounce Rate vs Exit Rate

name: Exit Rate Metric Scope
then_action: Use Exit Rate — it applies to any page in a session and reflects the percentage of exits from that specific page across all visits to it
if_condition: Measuring how often visitors leave the site from a specific page regardless of where they started their session
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Bounce Rate vs Exit Rate

name: Traffic Source Bounce Rate Distortion Warning
then_action: Expect abnormally high bounce rates as a baseline; do not penalize or misdiagnose site quality based on these inflated rates without segmenting by traffic source
if_condition: If a site's traffic is heavily driven by display ads or social media channels
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It

name: Bounce Rate Industry Comparison Requirement
then_action: MUST compare the site's bounce rate against other sites in the same category/industry, not against a universal benchmark
if_condition: If evaluating whether a site's bounce rate is good or bad
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It

name: Email and Referral Traffic Bounce Rate Advantage
then_action: Expect lower bounce rates from these channels; use this as a positive signal of audience intent and content-traffic alignment
if_condition: If a site receives a significant portion of traffic from email campaigns or referral sources
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It

name: High Bounce Rate Issue Diagnosis
then_action: Flag potential issues with content quality, user experience, page layout, or copywriting that require investigation and remediation
if_condition: If a site's bounce rate significantly exceeds the average range for its category (e.g., above 51% for general sites, above 45% for ecommerce)
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It

name: AdsBot-Google-Mobile-Apps obeys AdsBot-Google rules but ignores global wildcard
then_action: Then the restriction will NOT apply — AdsBot-Google-Mobile-Apps follows AdsBot-Google directives but ignores the global (*) wildcard
if_condition: If robots.txt uses only the global user agent wildcard (*) to restrict AdsBot-Google-Mobile-Apps
source_title: Google Special Case Crawlers Retired special case crawlers

name: Keyword-Only Strategy Obsolescence
then_action: Recognize this approach is insufficient, as Google's LLM interprets ambiguous queries and infers unstated intent beyond keyword matching; shift to intent-based and entity-rich content
if_condition: If SEO content strategy relies solely on simple keyword matching and exact-match optimization without addressing user intent or contextual meaning
source_title: AI s Impact on SEO 13 Things That Changed 4 Things That Stayed The Same

name: Retired Google special-case crawlers should not be expected as active
then_action: Then those crawlers should not be expected to be active — they are retired and no longer in use, and any configurations targeting them will have no practical effect
if_condition: If a site's crawl strategy, robots.txt configuration, or traffic analysis references AdsBot-Google-Mobile, AdsBot-Google-Mobile-Apps, Duplex on the web, Web Light, or favicon collection crawlers
source_title: Google Special Case Crawlers Retired special case crawlers

name: Ad Landing Page Relevance Requirement
then_action: Expect high bounce rate as a penalty signal; send users directly to the specific product or landing page that matches the ad content
if_condition: If a user clicks an ad for a specific product or service and is redirected to a homepage or irrelevant page
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Why Do People Bounce

name: BLB Target Selection: Prioritize High External Link Count Pages
then_action: Prioritize pages with many external links, as a page with 187 external links is 38x more likely to have at least one dead link compared to a page with only 5 external links
if_condition: If conducting broken link building outreach and selecting target pages
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Single-Page Satisfaction Bounce Differentiation
then_action: Do not treat a high bounce rate on this page as a negative signal; classify it as a satisfaction bounce indicating successful fulfillment of user intent
if_condition: If a page type is self-contained and fully answers a user query in one visit (e.g., a complete recipe, contact info, or calculator)
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Why Do People Bounce

name: BLB Discovery: Use Google Search Strings to Find Resource Pages
then_action: Use Google search operators: 'Keyword' + inurl:resources, 'Keyword' + intitle:links, 'Keyword' + 'helpful resources', 'Keyword' + 'useful resources' to surface resource pages with many external links
if_condition: If searching for broken link building targets in a specific keyword niche
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: User Expectation Alignment Requirement
then_action: Ensure the page content, headline, and offer directly match what was promised or implied by the referral source to prevent expectation mismatch bounces
if_condition: If a user arrives on a page via a specific referral source (ad, search result, social link) with a defined intent
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It Why Do People Bounce

name: Traffic Conversion Must Follow Trial-to-Customer Funnel
then_action: Then the conversion path must flow: website visitor → free trial signup → closed paying customer, ensuring traffic is not simply informational but actively feeds the sales funnel
if_condition: If data-driven content successfully drives website traffic from media pickup or organic search
source_title: Customer Acquisition Maximizing your Funnel   For Entrepreneurs Example 2 Using Data to drive custom

name: Do Not Rely on Global Wildcard robots.txt Rules for Special-Case Crawlers
then_action: These special-case crawlers will ignore the global (*) rules; access control will not be enforced — specific user agent tokens must be used instead
if_condition: If robots.txt uses only the global user agent wildcard (*) to restrict crawling and the site is crawled by APIs-Google, AdsBot-Google-Mobile, AdsBot-Google, or Mediapartners-Google
source_title: Google Special Case Crawlers

name: GA4 Conversion Event as Bounce Prevention Signal
then_action: Then the session is classified as engaged in GA4 and does not count as a bounce, unlike UA where only non-event single-page sessions were bounced
if_condition: If a user triggers a conversion event during a single-page session lasting under 10 seconds
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It 2

name: Search Intent Alignment to Prevent Pogo-Sticking
then_action: Rewrite or restructure content to satisfy search intent; mismatched content causes users to bounce back to search results, negatively impacting SEO rankings
if_condition: If page content does not match the search intent implied by the target keywords users are using to find the page
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It How to Improve Your Bounce Rate

name: Remove Unused Plugins and Scripts
then_action: Remove unused plugins and scripts to improve page load time; slow loading is a primary driver of high bounce rates
if_condition: If a website has unused plugins or scripts loaded on pages that slow page loading speed
source_title: Bounce Rate What Is It and How to Improve It How to Improve Your Bounce Rate

name: Avoid Pushy Link Requests in Outreach
then_action: MUST NOT directly and forcefully ask for a link; SHOULD gently nudge the recipient toward the replacement content without an explicit or pushy link request
if_condition: If suggesting a replacement link in a broken link building outreach email
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 3

name: Offer Replacement Content for Dead Links
then_action: MUST offer a replacement resource for the dead link to make the decision obvious for the site owner — no replacement offer means significantly lower conversion rates
if_condition: If conducting broken link building outreach and a target page contains a dead/broken link
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 3

name: Content Gap: Skip or Improve Minimally on Rock-Solid Pages
then_action: Recognize this as a highly competitive content landscape; even improving on one or two gaps still gives a viable chance to leapfrog competition, but full gap coverage is the ideal strategy
if_condition: If first page results for a target keyword are comprehensive, well-written, recently updated, and impressive with few detectable gaps
source_title: Content Gap Analysis The Complete Guide

name: Content Gap: Skip or Deprioritize Rock-Solid First Page Results
then_action: Deprioritize that keyword target or significantly raise the quality bar required, as marginal improvements are unlikely to displace well-established content
if_condition: If first page results for a target keyword are comprehensive, up-to-date, highly readable, and impressive with few identifiable gaps
source_title: Content Gap Analysis The Complete Guide

name: Content Gap: Target Results with Identifiable Holes
then_action: Prioritize creating content that addresses those specific gaps to increase the likelihood of outranking existing first page results
if_condition: If first page results for a target keyword show weaknesses in at least one of: recency of publication, depth of detail, readability, or shareability
source_title: Content Gap Analysis The Complete Guide

name: JavaScript Deferral for Interactivity
then_action: Then minimize or defer JavaScript execution and break long browser tasks into smaller chunks to restore page responsiveness
if_condition: If a page has high INP due to heavy JavaScript loading blocking user interaction
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 2

name: Broken Link Outreach: Avoid Generic Contact Addresses
then_action: Do not rely on generic contact points; instead identify the specific individual with authority to replace links, as generic outreach results in emails being discarded or marked as spam
if_condition: If conducting broken link building outreach and considering sending emails to generic 'contact@' addresses or catch-all contact forms on large sites
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 4

name: BLB Tooling: Use Check My Links Extension to Identify Broken Links
then_action: Run the Check My Links Chrome Extension on the page to identify broken (dead) links; note that it does not work 100% of the time so persistence across multiple pages is required
if_condition: If a resource page with many external links has been identified as a BLB candidate
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Broken Link Outreach: Target Decision-Maker for Single-Author Blogs
then_action: Use the site's contact form to reach the author directly, as they are the sole decision-maker for link replacement
if_condition: If the target website for broken link building is a single-author blog
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 4

name: BLB Scale: Use Link Analysis Tools to Find All Sites Linking to Same Dead URL
then_action: Input the dead URL into Semrush or equivalent link analysis tools to discover all other websites linking to that same broken URL, enabling scaled outreach to multiple prospects simultaneously
if_condition: If a dead/broken URL has been identified on a target resource page
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: BLB Scale: Apply Wikipedia Dead Link Technique to Find High-Value 404 Pages
then_action: Use the Wikipedia 'Dead Link' technique to identify 404 pages that many sites are already linking to, as these represent the highest-leverage replacement content opportunities
if_condition: If seeking broken link building opportunities with the highest number of existing inbound links pointing to dead content
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Broken Link Building: Valid Replacement Link Requirement
then_action: Ensure the suggested replacement URL is a working, live page on your target website — not another broken or redirected page
if_condition: If proposing a replacement for a broken link (404 page) on a target website
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide What Is Broken Link Building

name: Broken Link Outreach: Use Staff Page for Large Multi-Author Sites
then_action: Navigate to the site's staff page and identify a specific person listed as 'editor', 'blog manager', 'webmaster', or 'web admin' who has authority to fix broken links, rather than using a generic contact form
if_condition: If the target website has 100+ authors or 50+ departments
source_title: Broken Link Building The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 4

name: Blocking AdsBot Crawlers Impacts Google Ads Quality Checks
then_action: Google Ads will be unable to check web page ad quality, potentially affecting ad serving eligibility or quality scores for that site
if_condition: If AdsBot-Google-Mobile or AdsBot-Google crawlers are restricted or blocked via robots.txt
source_title: Google Special Case Crawlers

name: Blocking Mediapartners-Google Impacts AdSense Ad Relevance
then_action: Google AdSense ad relevance will be negatively affected, as the crawler cannot determine page content to serve contextually relevant ads
if_condition: If the Mediapartners-Google crawler is restricted or blocked via robots.txt
source_title: Google Special Case Crawlers

name: Core Web Vitals Failure Warning
then_action: Use Google Search Console's Experience section, PageSpeed Insights, or Semrush Site Audit to diagnose and remediate the failing metric, as poor scores negatively impact page experience ranking signals
if_condition: If any Core Web Vital metric (LCP ≥ 2.5s, INP > 200ms, or CLS ≥ 0.1) falls outside the good threshold
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance What Are Core Web Vitals

name: Duplex on the web may ignore global wildcard in robots.txt
then_action: Then the restriction may NOT apply — Duplex on the web may ignore the global (*) wildcard, requiring an explicit Duplex-specific directive
if_condition: If robots.txt uses only the global user agent wildcard (*) to restrict the Duplex on the web crawler
source_title: Google Special Case Crawlers Retired special case crawlers

name: Use Updated Google Read Aloud User Agent
then_action: Use the updated Google Read Aloud user agent with newer browser versions to ensure compatibility with sites that don't support old browser versions
if_condition: If a site restricts access based on browser version compatibility and Google Read Aloud bot is crawling the page
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog November 2025

name: Mobile CLS Requires Extra Attention
then_action: Investigate mobile-specific layout shift causes separately, as mobile devices often exhibit worse CLS performance than desktop due to different rendering conditions
if_condition: When CLS scores are acceptable on desktop but CLS performance issues are still reported or suspected on mobile devices
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 3

name: Media Elements Must Have Explicit Size Dimensions
then_action: Set explicit size attribute dimensions for all media elements (images, videos) so the browser reserves the correct amount of space before the element loads, preventing layout shifts
if_condition: When images or videos are included on a page without explicit width and height size attributes defined in HTML
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 3

name: INP De-prioritization for Read-Only Content Pages
then_action: Then INP optimization is low priority since scrolling is not counted in INP measurement and user interaction requirements are minimal
if_condition: If a page is 100% content-based with no interactive elements beyond scrolling (e.g., blog posts, news articles)
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part 2

name: Avoid Lazy Loading Above-the-Fold Images
then_action: Remove lazy loading from above-the-fold images as it delays LCP rendering and increases LCP time, harming Core Web Vitals scores
if_condition: If lazy loading is applied to images that appear above the fold on a page
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part

name: Do Not Prioritize Core Web Vitals Scores Over Content Quality
then_action: Redirect focus to content quality and user experience first, as page experience is one of hundreds of ranking factors and will not compensate for poor content
if_condition: If optimization efforts are focused on improving Core Web Vitals numbers at the expense of content quality or user experience
source_title: Core Web Vitals Essential Guide to Improving Site Performance Why Are Core Web Vitals Important Part

name: Distinct Content Requirement for Indexing
then_action: Google cannot determine the original page, causing all similar pages to struggle to rank; ensure each page has distinct, unique information
if_condition: If multiple pages on the same website contain similar or identical content
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide What Is Duplicate Content

name: Use Search Keywords to Determine LPA Position for Google Ads
then_action: Categorize the niche-ness of search keywords used to locate the audience's position on the Ladder of Product Awareness; more niche keywords indicate higher LPA steps
if_condition: If running Google Ads and need to determine audience awareness level
source_title: Making Ads

name: Prefer Built-in URI Representations Over identifier Property
then_action: Use the built-in URI/URL representation instead of the identifier property; only use identifier property when canonical URI/URL forms are unavailable
if_condition: If a canonical URI or URL representation of an identifier is available (e.g., @id in JSON-LD, itemid in Microdata, resource in RDFa 1.1)
source_title: Data model   Schema org Background notes

name: Recognize New User-Triggered Fetchers
then_action: Recognize these as legitimate user-triggered Google fetchers: Google-Pinpoint (Pinpoint research tool), Google-CWS, and Google-NotebookLM, and apply appropriate access rules accordingly
if_condition: If server logs or robots.txt rules reference or detect crawl requests from Google-Pinpoint, Google-CWS, or Google-NotebookLM user agents
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog November 2025

name: Allow Multi-Type Entity Descriptions
then_action: Then allow and include all relevant types in the entity description, as combining multiple types is valid and useful
if_condition: If an entity description simultaneously uses properties from multiple independent schema.org types (e.g., both Book and Product)
source_title: Data model   Schema org Conformance

name: Use Schema.org Properties with New or External Types
then_action: Then reuse existing schema.org properties with those new types rather than introducing new properties for similar use cases
if_condition: If implementing schema.org markup with new types from schema.org or external extensions
source_title: Data model   Schema org Conformance

name: Warn on Ambiguous Data Without Enforcing Hard Errors
then_action: Then provide a hint or warning about the data plausibility issue but do not treat it as a blocking error
if_condition: If schema.org markup contains unlikely or ambiguous type/property combinations (e.g., volcanoes with fax numbers, countries with opening hours)
source_title: Data model   Schema org Conformance

name: Blocking APIs-Google Crawler Disrupts Push Notification Delivery
then_action: Delivery of push notification messages by Google APIs will be disrupted, as crawling preferences for APIs-Google directly affect this functionality
if_condition: If the APIs-Google crawler is restricted or blocked via robots.txt
source_title: Google Special Case Crawlers

name: Monitor Location-Based Service Pages for Similar Content
then_action: Monitor these pages for substantially similar content, as Google's definition of duplicate content includes near-identical pages, not just word-for-word copies
if_condition: If a site has multiple service pages targeting similar keywords across different geographic locations
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Retrieve Machine-Readable Term Definitions via JSON-LD in HTML
then_action: Retrieve the term's page HTML from Schema.org and extract the embedded JSON-LD, which contains the machine-readable definition for that specific term
if_condition: If a developer needs machine-readable definitions for individual Schema.org terms
source_title: Developers   Schema org

name: Avoid Unique URLs for Product Variations
then_action: Do not create new distinct URLs for each product variation, as this generates thousands of duplicate content pages that can harm search rankings
if_condition: If an ecommerce site has products with multiple variations (e.g., sizes, colors)
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Block Search Result Pages from Indexing
then_action: Prevent search result pages from being indexed by Google, as indexing them can add 1,000+ duplicate content pages
if_condition: If a site has internal search functionality that generates unique URLs for search result pages
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Detect Automatically Generated Duplicate Pages
then_action: Investigate automatically generated pages as a source of duplicate content and apply 301 redirects to resolve duplication
if_condition: If the number of pages indexed in Google Search Console or via site:example.com search significantly exceeds the number of manually created pages
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Use Schema Extension System for Novel Content Types
then_action: Consider using the schema.org extension system to define new custom types rather than omitting markup entirely
if_condition: If the implementation requires content types that do not exist in the current schema.org vocabulary and a less-specific type is insufficient
source_title: FAQ   schema org 3

name: Step-Based Content Must Use H2/H3 Subheaders for Each Step
then_action: Wrap every item or step in an H2 or H3 tag as a subheader, maintaining consistent formatting throughout to signal structure clearly to Google
if_condition: If content presents ordered steps or list items targeting a Featured Snippet
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part 2

name: Table Data Must Use TR Tags for Featured Snippet Eligibility
then_action: Use proper HTML <tr> tags to present data in tables so Google can correctly parse and display the table in a Featured Snippet
if_condition: If content includes tabular data intended to appear as a Featured Snippet
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part 2

name: GEO Content Structure: Self-Contained Paragraphs
then_action: Structure paragraphs so each substantive passage works independently without conversational setup, place answers early in sections, and use clear headings that signal which section answers which question
if_condition: If content is being optimized for AI-generated answer inclusion (GEO), not just traditional search rankings
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions How GEO differs from traditional SEO

name: GEO Measurement: Expand Metrics Beyond Rankings
then_action: Measure AI visibility score, share of voice, sentiment, and context/prompt triggers in addition to traditional rankings, clicks, and traffic metrics
if_condition: If tracking organic search performance for a brand that wants AI visibility alongside traditional SEO
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions How GEO differs from traditional SEO

name: GEO Platform Presence: Multi-Platform Distribution
then_action: Maintain strategic presence on Reddit threads, YouTube videos, industry publications, review sites, and social platforms where AI tools discover and source information
if_condition: If a brand wants to be referenced in AI-generated answers across multiple AI tools
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions How GEO differs from traditional SEO

name: GEO Warning: Passage Extraction Without Context
then_action: Rewrite so each passage delivers its core answer independently, because AI systems extract specific passages and reassemble them without the surrounding conversational context
if_condition: If content sections rely on surrounding conversational setup or prior paragraphs to make sense
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions How GEO differs from traditional SEO

name: Schema.org Universal Ontology Misuse Warning
then_action: Then this usage MUST be avoided — Schema.org is designed for search engine optimization contexts and should not be treated as a global or universal ontology
if_condition: If Schema.org is being used as a complete or universal ontology to model all aspects of the world
source_title: Data model   Schema org

name: Schema.org Schema Extension with domainIncludes and rangeIncludes
then_action: Then domainIncludes and rangeIncludes properties MUST be used to define the extended schema
if_condition: If extending Schema.org with custom Class or Property types for reflection purposes
source_title: Data model   Schema org

name: Googlebot-News Does Not Affect News Tab
then_action: Understand that crawling preferences addressed to Googlebot-News do NOT affect the News tab on Google; adjust targeting strategy accordingly
if_condition: If crawling preferences or robots.txt directives are set targeting Googlebot-News with the intent to control appearance in the Google News tab
source_title: Google s Crawling Documentation Changelog November 2025

name: Use Social Profile Details as LPA Proxy for Non-Search Channels
then_action: Rely on social profile details (e.g., liked pages, job titles, group memberships) as a proxy for the audience's LPA position, since awareness level cannot be directly observed
if_condition: If running ads on non-search channels like Facebook or LinkedIn where search intent is unavailable
source_title: Making Ads

name: Use Staged Releases for Production Schema.org Vocabulary Downloads
then_action: Download period releases from the Schema.org site after they have been staged on staging.schema.org, rather than pulling directly from the live repository
if_condition: If a developer needs to download a stable, versioned release of the Schema.org vocabulary for production use
source_title: Developers   Schema org

name: Use 301 Redirects for Duplicate Pages
then_action: Apply 301 redirects from duplicate pages back to the original content so Googlebot indexes only the original page
if_condition: If duplicate pages are identified across a site
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Verify User-Triggered Fetcher IP Ranges
then_action: Check IP ranges published in user-triggered-fetchers.json, user-triggered-fetchers-google.json, and user-triggered-agents.json objects to confirm the source
if_condition: If you need to identify or validate traffic from Google user-triggered fetchers in server logs
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers

name: Redirect Site Versions to Canonical URL
then_action: Redirect all versions to a single canonical URL using 301 redirects to prevent duplicate content issues
if_condition: If a website serves content on multiple URL versions (WWW vs non-WWW, HTTP vs HTTPS)
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 1

name: Use Siteliner to Identify Duplicate Content
then_action: Use an SEO tool such as Siteliner to scan the website and identify pages with high levels of duplicate content before deciding on a resolution strategy
if_condition: If a site owner suspects multiple pages contain substantial duplicate content but has not audited the site
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 2

name: Internal Duplicate Content Traffic Loss
then_action: Diagnose duplicate content as a likely cause; Google deprioritizes pages using copied content from its index, resulting in reduced organic traffic
if_condition: If organic traffic is declining and the site contains pages that reuse content already present in Google's index, including from its own other pages
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide What Is Duplicate Content

name: In-House Enterprise SEO Team Suitability
then_action: Then in-house implementation is a viable approach for enterprise SEO execution without requiring outsourcing or platform dependency
if_condition: If the organization already has an extensive multi-department team capable of handling technical SEO, content, link building, and reporting
source_title: Enterprise SEO Strategies Tips for Large Scale Success How to Build a Winning Enterprise SEO Strateg

name: Three Resolution Options for Duplicate Content Pages
then_action: Choose one of three actions: delete the duplicate page, redirect it to the canonical version, or apply the rel=canonical tag pointing to the original page
if_condition: If duplicate content pages are identified on a site and a resolution action is required
source_title: Duplicate Content and SEO The Complete Guide Best Practices Part 2

name: User-Triggered Fetcher List Is Non-Exhaustive
then_action: Treat the list as incomplete; it only covers fetchers more likely to appear in log files and does not represent all user-triggered fetchers
if_condition: If relying on the published list of user-triggered fetchers to account for all possible Google user-triggered fetch traffic in log analysis
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers

name: Scale All SEO Activities on Large Sites
then_action: Then expect negligible impact on organic traffic; all SEO activities must be scaled proportionally to the size of the site to drive meaningful results
if_condition: If applying SEO strategies to a site with thousands of pages without scaling the approach
source_title: Enterprise SEO Strategies Tips for Large Scale Success What Is Enterprise SEO

name: Use Schema.org Markup for Search Engine Understanding
then_action: Add schema.org structured data markup to the page to help search engines understand and display the content more prominently in search results
if_condition: If a webpage contains structured content that should be understood and surfaced by search engines
source_title: FAQ   schema org 2

name: Allow Independent Hosting for Third-Party External Extensions
then_action: Allow those external extensions to continue being hosted independently by the third party, without requiring folding into Schema.org core or pending pipeline
if_condition: If a third party maintains a broadly applicable external extension vocabulary (e.g., http://gs1.org/voc) outside of Schema.org
source_title: Extending Schemas   Schema org

name: Do Not Assume All Schema Types Surface in Search Results
then_action: Do not assume the markup will be surfaced or displayed in search results; verify support through engine-specific documentation before relying on it
if_condition: If implementing a schema.org markup type that is not explicitly documented as supported by a specific search engine
source_title: FAQ   schema org 2

name: Do Not Rely on Automated Data Extraction as Schema Alternative
then_action: Do not depend on automated extraction as a substitute for schema.org markup, as it does not work reliably across different site formats
if_condition: If a site owner expects search engines to reliably extract structured data automatically without explicit markup
source_title: FAQ   schema org 2

name: Buffer Start Page Channel Count Rule
then_action: count the Start Page as one channel toward the plan's channel limit, reducing available slots for other social channels
if_condition: if a Buffer user adds a Start Page to their account
source_title: Pricing Support

name: Use Schema.org for Structured Data Markup
then_action: Add schema.org vocabulary markup to the page to help search engines understand the content and provide richer search results
if_condition: If a web page contains structured information about entities, products, events, or other content that search engines should understand
source_title: FAQ   schema org

name: Use Less-Specific Type When Exact Schema Type Is Unavailable
then_action: Use the closest less-specific parent type available in schema.org vocabulary (e.g., use 'Person' type for professors)
if_condition: If the specific content type being marked up does not have a matching schema.org type (e.g., 'Professor' does not exist as a type)
source_title: FAQ   schema org 3

name: Perfect Formatting Cannot Overcome Low Authority
then_action: Do not expect featured snippet placement — Google pulls from trusted first-page content only, so formatting alone cannot compensate for insufficient ranking authority
if_condition: If a page is perfectly formatted for featured snippets but ranks beyond page 1 (e.g., page 11)
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part 3

name: Definition Box Query Matching
then_action: Then structure content as a concise Definition Box with short, direct descriptive text to align with Google's snippet selection behavior for definitional queries
if_condition: If the target query follows a 'what is [X]' pattern
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google Types of Featured Snippets

name: Include Featured Snippet presence in keyword selection
then_action: Factor the Featured Snippet into the keyword selection decision alongside competition and monthly search volume — do not automatically discard the keyword, as Featured Snippets can still boost organic CTR significantly
if_condition: If a keyword is being evaluated for targeting and it returns a Featured Snippet in the SERP
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google What Are Featured Snippets

name: Table Snippets Require Actual HTML Tables
then_action: Convert the tabular data into a proper HTML table, since Google scrapes existing tables rather than assembling table data from disparate page sections
if_condition: If the target SERP displays a Table-type Featured Snippet and your page contains tabular data formatted outside of HTML table elements
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part

name: First Page Visibility Requirement
then_action: Then customers will not find it, effectively making the content invisible and eliminating potential top-of-funnel traffic
if_condition: If a web page or content does not appear on the first page of Google search results for relevant queries
source_title: Customer Acquisition Maximizing your Funnel   For Entrepreneurs Problem Driving Traffic to your Web

name: Add Dedicated Sections for Long-Tail Featured Snippet Variations
then_action: Add additional content sections specifically structured to rank for different long-tail variations of that Featured Snippet query, expanding position zero coverage
if_condition: If a page already targets a primary Featured Snippet keyword
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google How to Optimize For Featured Snippets Part 2

name: Do Not Attempt to Block Google-Safety Crawler via robots.txt
then_action: The Google-Safety crawler completely ignores robots.txt rules as it handles abuse-specific crawling for malware discovery; the rules will have no effect
if_condition: If a site attempts to block or restrict the Google-Safety crawler using robots.txt rules
source_title: Google Special Case Crawlers

name: Warn on Featured Snippet no-click risk for informational queries
then_action: Expect increased no-click searches where users get their answer from the snippet without visiting any page, resulting in fewer clicks compared to SERPs without a Featured Snippet
if_condition: If a target keyword has a Featured Snippet that directly answers the user query (e.g. definition, fact, or simple how-to)
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google What Are Featured Snippets

name: Check SERP for Featured Snippet before finalizing keyword
then_action: Check the live SERP to confirm whether a Featured Snippet is present, and use that data to inform the keyword prioritization and content format decision
if_condition: If a keyword is being finalized for content targeting before content creation begins
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google What Are Featured Snippets

name: Use Domain View Comparison to Assess Ranking Chances
then_action: Use the Domain view to compare your site's authority metrics against competitor sites in the search results to quantify your realistic chances of ranking
if_condition: If comparing your site's competitiveness against pages currently ranking in the SERP for a target keyword
source_title: Free Keyword Difficulty Checker See Who s Ranking Why

name: Apply Location-Specific Keyword Data for Local SEO
then_action: Use location-specific keyword difficulty and search insights to tailor keyword targeting to that local market rather than using nationwide aggregated data
if_condition: If conducting keyword research for a local SEO campaign targeting a specific geographic area
source_title: Free Keyword Difficulty Checker See Who s Ranking Why

name: Table Snippet Data Extractability Requirement
then_action: Then ensure the table structure allows Google to extract and restructure the data effectively; tables that cannot be parsed or restructured by Google will not be selected for Table Snippets
if_condition: If content is formatted as a table intended to capture a Table Featured Snippet
source_title: Featured Snippets How to Capture Position Zero in Google Types of Featured Snippets

name: Cross-Source Brand Description Alignment
then_action: Align all external platform profiles to reinforce the same category, positioning, and value proposition as the primary site, because conflicting signals reduce AI confidence and decrease likelihood of brand mentions in AI-generated answers
if_condition: If brand descriptions across LinkedIn, Crunchbase, review platforms, industry directories, and on-site content describe different categories, positioning, or value propositions
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 2 Entity clarity shapes AI understanding

name: Flag High-Backlink SERPs as High-Barrier Keywords
then_action: Flag the keyword as high-barrier and expect that acquiring comparable backlinks and referring domains will likely be required to rank competitively for that term
if_condition: If many of the top 10 ranking pages for a target keyword have large numbers of backlinks and referring domains
source_title: Free Keyword Difficulty Checker See Who s Ranking Why

name: Schema-Page-Feed Consistency Requirement
then_action: Ensure all three layers — visible page content, schema markup, and downstream feeds — describe the same information in the same way to prevent AI confidence degradation and signal conflicts
if_condition: If page content, schema markup (JSON-LD), and commerce product feeds (e.g. Google Merchant Center) describe the same entity or product in different ways or with conflicting attributes
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 2 Entity clarity shapes AI understanding

name: Combine Volume, Trend, CPC, and Competitive Density for Keyword Evaluation
then_action: Combine search volume, 12-month search trends, CPC data, and competitive density to build a full picture of keyword competitiveness rather than relying on a single metric
if_condition: If evaluating the competitiveness and value of a keyword for an SEO campaign
source_title: Free Keyword Difficulty Checker See Who s Ranking Why

name: Page Performance Impacts AI Source Dependability
then_action: Treat this as a signal that reduces source dependability perception for AI systems — while it does not block inclusion outright, AI systems are less likely to use lower-reliability sources as reference points
if_condition: If a page exhibits slow or unstable load performance
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 1 SEO fundamentals are the foundation

name: Avoid JavaScript-Heavy Implementations for AI Crawlers
then_action: Avoid this approach because many AI crawlers cannot consistently process JavaScript-heavy implementations, making core information harder to extract and reducing likelihood of AI reference
if_condition: If a page relies heavily on JavaScript or client-side rendering for delivering core content
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 1 SEO fundamentals are the foundation

name: AI Content Accessibility Requirement
then_action: Ensure content is accessible, readable, and attributable — with consistent crawling, indexing, and rendering — so AI systems can reliably retrieve, interpret, and reference it
if_condition: If content is being optimized for AI-generated answer inclusion (GEO)
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 1 SEO fundamentals are the foundation

name: YouTube Video Content for AI Reference
then_action: Develop a YouTube channel with substantive product feature demonstrations and educational content, as YouTube is among the top sources cited by leading LLMs
if_condition: If a brand wants AI systems to reference its product features and capabilities in generated answers
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 4 AI visibility extends beyond your websit

name: AI Extraction Uncertainty Reduces Citation Probability
then_action: The probability of that page being used as a reference point in AI-generated answers decreases — audit for client-side rendering issues, crawlability gaps, and unclear authorship
if_condition: If AI systems face uncertainty about whether content can be reliably extracted or interpreted from a page
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 1 SEO fundamentals are the foundation

name: User-Triggered Fetchers Ignore robots.txt
then_action: Do NOT assume robots.txt rules will be followed; user-triggered fetchers generally ignore robots.txt restrictions
if_condition: If a fetch request originates from a Google user-triggered fetcher (e.g., Google Site Verifier, Google Cloud RSS retrieval initiated by a user)
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers

name: Structured Data Implementation Sequence
then_action: Follow the sequence: (1) establish clarity in visible page content first, (2) mirror that structure in JSON-LD schema markup, (3) reflect the same structure in product feeds, (4) cross-reference and align signals across all sources and formats
if_condition: If implementing schema markup and structured data signals for a brand or product entity
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 2 Entity clarity shapes AI understanding

name: Descriptive Headings Signal Section Content to AI
