then_action: Use clear, descriptive headings that explicitly signal what each section covers so LLM chunking and vector retrieval can correctly associate headings with content
if_condition: If page sections contain information targeted for AI answer retrieval
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 3 Content must be easy to extract and reus

name: Consistent Brand Context Description Across Site
then_action: Standardize brand descriptions to be clear and consistent across all on-site pages and supporting sources so AI systems can unambiguously identify brand category, offerings, and authority topics
if_condition: If brand descriptions vary across different pages of the same site or between the site and supporting off-site sources
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 2 Entity clarity shapes AI understanding

name: Avoid Context-Dependent References in AI-Targeted Content
then_action: Rewrite to remove those references so the passage retains full meaning when read in isolation, increasing retrieval accuracy by AI systems
if_condition: If a paragraph uses phrases like 'as mentioned above', 'this is why', or relies on context established elsewhere on the page
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 3 Content must be easy to extract and reus

name: Specific Facts Over Generalizations for AI Retrieval
then_action: Include specific facts and statistics rather than vague generalizations to increase the likelihood that the passage is retrieved and used accurately
if_condition: If content is being optimized for inclusion in AI-generated answers
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 3 Content must be easy to extract and reus

name: Identify Google Messages Fetcher for Link Previews
then_action: Then classify the request as originating from the Google Messages fetcher, triggered by users sharing URLs that require link preview generation
if_condition: If an HTTP request contains the User-Agent string 'GoogleMessages'
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers Chrome Web Store

name: Structure Documents for LLMs Over Human Readability
then_action: Then consider inverting this approach by structuring documents for LLM consumption first, as LLM attention is projected to dominate over human attention and LLM-structured content may yield greater visibility benefits
if_condition: If the content production strategy currently formats documents primarily for human readability following traditional SEO conventions
source_title: GEO LLMO AEO It s All Just SEO 3

name: Reddit Participation for AI Credibility Signals
then_action: Participate actively in relevant subreddit discussions to show expertise in action, as Reddit is among the top cited sources by leading LLMs and contributes to earned mention signals
if_condition: If a brand needs to demonstrate real-world expertise that AI systems can interpret as credibility signals
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 4 AI visibility extends beyond your websit

name: LLM Citation Requires Source Authentication
then_action: Then claims must be supported with proper citations and verifiable sourcing, as LLMs require proof of authenticity to attribute and cite content accurately
if_condition: If content is being optimized for LLM visibility and LLMs decouple information from its original source during output generation
source_title: GEO LLMO AEO It s All Just SEO 3

name: Avoid Relying on Listing Pages for LLM Visibility
then_action: Then revise the strategy, as listings pages receive significantly fewer LLM visits and citations compared to core pages, making them ineffective for LLM optimization
if_condition: If the content strategy depends on listing collections or listings pages to drive LLM visibility
source_title: GEO LLMO AEO It s All Just SEO 3

name: Combine Owned and Earned Mentions for Comprehensive AI Understanding
then_action: AI systems will have weaker credibility signals; build earned mentions from customers and industry sources alongside owned presence so AI systems encounter multiple independent sources discussing the brand in relevant contexts
if_condition: If a brand has owned content but lacks independent third-party mentions, reviews, or citations from customers and industry sources
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 4 AI visibility extends beyond your websit

name: Owned Content Must Be Substantively Helpful
then_action: AI systems will lack sufficient quality content to reference; content MUST address real problems in the industry with enough depth to serve as a reliable reference source for AI-generated answers
if_condition: If owned content created for AI visibility does not address real industry problems with substantive, detailed information
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 4 AI visibility extends beyond your websit

name: Track Citation Frequency as Core GEO Metric
then_action: Then track citation frequency — how often AI platforms mention the brand when answering relevant questions — as a primary visibility KPI
if_condition: If measuring GEO performance for a brand across AI search platforms
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 5 Visibility Is measured differently in AI

name: Long-Form Content Platforms Expand AI Training Surface
then_action: Utilize long-form content formats across additional platforms to increase the volume and diversity of content AI systems can encounter and potentially incorporate into training data for long-term brand understanding
if_condition: If a brand's content strategy focuses only on short-form or website-based content without utilizing podcasts, webinars, conference presentations, or educational platforms
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 4 AI visibility extends beyond your websit

name: Analyze Sentiment of AI Brand Mentions
then_action: Then analyze whether AI mentions are positive, neutral, or negative in sentiment, as mention frequency alone does not indicate favorable brand positioning in AI-generated answers
if_condition: If a brand is frequently cited in AI search responses but conversion or revenue attribution remains unclear
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 5 Visibility Is measured differently in AI

name: Account for AI Citation Volatility in GEO Reporting
then_action: Then do not interpret volatility as optimization failure — AI citation patterns are inherently unstable due to model updates, user context, and conversation history, and even well-optimized brands experience fluctuation
if_condition: If reporting GEO results shows fluctuation in citation frequency despite consistent optimization efforts
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions 5 Visibility Is measured differently in AI

name: Traditional Search Ranking Remains a GEO Prerequisite
then_action: Improve traditional SEO ranking first, since AI systems use Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) that pulls from search index results — strong traditional ranking is a prerequisite for AI citation eligibility
if_condition: If a page ranks poorly in traditional organic search results and is also absent from AI-generated answers
source_title: Generative Engine Optimization Growth Strategies and Metrics For the AI Era

name: GEO Cross-Discipline Coordination Requirement
then_action: Coordinate effort across content strategy, brand presence, technical optimization, and reputation building simultaneously — GEO requires all four pillars to be effective
if_condition: If a GEO strategy is being planned or executed using only a single discipline (e.g., content alone or technical SEO alone)
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions A comprehensive guide to generative engine

name: GEO Entity Clarity and Content Extractability Requirement
then_action: Restructure content to establish entity clarity and extractability — use clear definitions, structured formatting, and direct answers — so AI systems can discover, understand, and reference your content
if_condition: If content lacks clear entity definition, structured information, or is difficult for AI crawlers to parse and extract answers from
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions A comprehensive guide to generative engine

name: Content Type Diversification Required for AI Citation Coverage
then_action: Create a content mix targeting multiple intent categories — 'best' listicles (~7% of AI traffic), how-to guides (~6.35%), 'top' lists (~5.5%), and 'vs' comparisons (~4.88%) — to capture citations across diverse user query types
if_condition: If site content is concentrated in a single format (e.g., only blog posts or only product pages) and AI citation share is low
source_title: Generative Engine Optimization Growth Strategies and Metrics For the AI Era

name: GEO Citation Volatility Monitoring Requirement
then_action: Do not assume citation persistence — between 40-60% of cited sources change monthly, so continuously monitor AI mentions and refresh content authority signals to maintain visibility
if_condition: If AI citation tracking shows your brand was mentioned by AI platforms in a given month
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions A comprehensive guide to generative engine

name: GEO Long-Term Discipline: Avoid Short-Term Tactical Framing
then_action: Then reframe it as a long-term visibility discipline — AI systems rely on stable underlying inputs (authority, clarity, trust) even as individual responses fluctuate, requiring sustained coherence across the entire web presence
if_condition: If GEO strategy is being scoped as a one-time or short-term optimization project rather than an ongoing discipline
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions Frequently asked questions

name: GEO Content Structure: Passage-Level Retrieval Optimization
then_action: Structure content for individual passage retrieval rather than entire pages — create self-contained explanations that make sense without surrounding context, include direct answers to specific questions, and provide concise definitions and fact-based comparisons
if_condition: If content is being optimized for AI-generated answer visibility (GEO)
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions Frequently asked questions

name: GEO Authority Signal: Expertise Clarity Outweighs Brand Size
then_action: Then the smaller publisher can outperform the larger brand in AI-generated answer citations — do not rely on brand size or recognition as an automatic GEO advantage; prioritize clarity, accuracy, and trust signals instead
if_condition: If a smaller publisher has clearer, better-structured expertise than a larger brand competitor in the same topic area
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions Frequently asked questions

name: GEO Cross-Channel Alignment: Full Touchpoint Consistency Required
then_action: Then GEO visibility will be undermined — AI systems evaluate authority and trust across the entire web presence, so all signals must be coherent, accurate, and consistent across every channel
if_condition: If content, SEO, brand, PR, product, or customer touchpoints are misaligned or inconsistent with each other
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions Frequently asked questions

name: GEO Entity Clarity: Multi-Platform Credibility Establishment
then_action: Then establish entity clarity and credible source signals across multiple platforms — AI systems use durable signals like entity recognition, clarity, and cross-platform trust to determine citation worthiness
if_condition: If a brand or publisher is building or auditing its GEO strategy
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions Frequently asked questions

name: Handle Google-Agent Desktop User-Agent
then_action: Then classify the request as a desktop Google-Agent user-triggered fetch and verify the request IP against the user-triggered-agents.json IP ranges to confirm legitimacy
if_condition: If an HTTP request User-Agent contains 'compatible; Google-Agent' alongside an X11 Linux x86_64 desktop string
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers Chrome Web Store

name: GEO Foundation: SEO Trust Signals Remain Required
then_action: Resolve these SEO fundamentals first, as they remain mandatory requirements for GEO — AI systems still depend on the same trust and authority signals as traditional search
if_condition: If a site lacks technical accessibility, authoritative content, or credible trust and expertise signals
source_title: Generative engine optimization GEO How to win AI mentions How GEO differs from traditional SEO

name: Identify Google Site Verifier Fetcher
then_action: Then classify the request as originating from the Google Site Verifier fetcher triggered via Search Console, used to confirm presence of verification tokens on the target page
if_condition: If an HTTP request contains the User-Agent string 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; Google-Site-Verification/1.0)'
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers Chrome Web Store

name: Unconnected Content Publishing Flagged as Reputation Abuse
then_action: AVOID publishing such content as it constitutes site reputation abuse and does not contribute to meaningful entity positioning in AI or traditional search frameworks
if_condition: If content is published on a site with no thematic or business connection to the brand
source_title: GEO LLMO AEO It s All Just SEO 2

name: Track Brand Visibility in AI Outputs
then_action: MUST track brand visibility in AI mentions and outputs to measure and understand GEO performance, since traditional ranking signals do not reflect LLM visibility
if_condition: If a brand is targeting performance in LLM-powered search environments
source_title: GEO LLMO AEO It s All Just SEO 2

name: Avoid Restricting 'Good' Mentions to Linked References for LLMs
then_action: Do NOT restrict qualifying mentions to linked or dofollow references — LLMs read content directly and unlinked mentions contribute meaningfully to entity embeddings and cosine similarity with related concepts
if_condition: If evaluating the quality of brand mentions specifically for LLM optimization
source_title: GEO LLMO AEO It s All Just SEO 2

name: Irrelevant Backlink Building Penalized for GEO
then_action: AVOID this practice — it does not strengthen entity positioning in LLMs and constitutes site reputation abuse; focus on mentions within contextually relevant and credible sources
if_condition: If building backlinks on websites with little or no topical relevance to the brand
source_title: GEO LLMO AEO It s All Just SEO 2

name: Harvest Demand for Type A Customers via Google Search
then_action: Then focus marketing efforts on Google Search to intercept them at the point of active demand, as this is their most likely starting point for finding a solution
if_condition: If a potential customer is aware they have a problem and are actively searching for a solution that fits your product category
source_title: Customer Acquisition Maximizing your Funnel   For Entrepreneurs Top of the Funnel Harvesting Demand

name: Validate Reverse DNS for User-Triggered Fetchers
then_action: Verify reverse DNS: user-owned fetchers should match '***-***-***-***.gae.googleusercontent.com'; Google-owned fetchers should match 'google-proxy-***-***-***-***.google.com'
if_condition: If verifying whether an incoming request is from a user-owned or Google-owned user-triggered fetcher
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers

name: Warn Against Blocking User-Triggered Fetchers
then_action: Then critical user-facing Google features (extension installs, link previews, site verification, text-to-speech) will fail or return errors, negatively impacting user experience and Search Console functionality
if_condition: If server-side rules block or challenge requests from known Google user-triggered fetcher User-Agent strings (e.g., Google-CWS, GoogleMessages, Google-Agent, Google-Site-Verification/1.0)
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers Chrome Web Store

name: Deprecate google-speakr User-Agent for Read Aloud
then_action: Then update configurations to use the current Google-Read-Aloud mobile and desktop User-Agent strings instead, as google-speakr is deprecated and no longer used
if_condition: If server logs or access rules reference the 'google-speakr' User-Agent string for text-to-speech request handling
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers Chrome Web Store

name: logical redirects in robots.txt are not followed
then_action: Google does not follow these logical redirects; serve the robots.txt content directly at the standard URL with a 2xx response
if_condition: if the robots.txt file uses frames, JavaScript, or meta refresh-type redirects to point to the actual robots.txt content
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Handling of errors and HTTP status codes

name: Identify Chrome Web Store Fetcher User-Agent
then_action: Then classify the request as originating from the Chrome Web Store fetcher, used for extension and theme metadata requests on behalf of users
if_condition: If an HTTP request contains the User-Agent string 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; Google-CWS)'
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers Chrome Web Store

name: Handle Google-Agent Mobile User-Agent
then_action: Then classify the request as a mobile Google-Agent user-triggered fetch and verify the request IP against the user-triggered-agents.json IP ranges to confirm legitimacy
if_condition: If an HTTP request User-Agent contains 'compatible; Google-Agent' alongside a Nexus 5X Android mobile device string
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers Chrome Web Store

name: Block AI Bot Access Kills GEO Visibility
then_action: AI systems cannot index or cite the content, eliminating any chance of brand mentions in AI-generated answers — remove AI bot blocks immediately
if_condition: If robots.txt or server configuration blocks AI crawlers (e.g., GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot) from accessing site content
source_title: Generative Engine Optimization Growth Strategies and Metrics For the AI Era

name: Prioritize Document Context to Improve LLM QA Accuracy
then_action: Add global document context (metadata like source, date, entity name) distributed throughout the text to increase QA accuracy to the 72%-75% range
if_condition: If content is being evaluated for LLM question-answering accuracy and currently achieving 50%-60% accuracy on chunk-level queries
source_title: GEO LLMO AEO It s All Just SEO 4

name: Treat Public GitHub Content as LLM Training Signal
then_action: Recognize that public GitHub content is likely included in LLM training data and should be optimized with brand/product context accordingly, even though it has no impact on traditional SEO rankings
if_condition: If a brand publishes content in public GitHub repositories
source_title: GEO LLMO AEO It s All Just SEO 4

name: Place robots.txt at root domain level only
then_action: the robots.txt file will be ignored by crawlers, as crawlers only check for robots.txt at the root domain level
if_condition: if robots.txt is placed in a subdirectory rather than at the root of the domain (e.g., https://example.com/subfolder/robots.txt)
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Examples of valid robots txt URLs

name: Subdomain robots.txt scope is limited to that subdomain only
then_action: it applies only to the www subdomain and does NOT apply to other subdomains such as blog.example.com or the root example.com
if_condition: if a robots.txt file is placed at https://www.example.com/robots.txt
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Examples of valid robots txt URLs

name: Protocol-specific robots.txt does not cross protocols
then_action: it is only valid for FTP protocol crawling and does not apply to HTTP or HTTPS versions of the same domain
if_condition: if a robots.txt file is placed at ftp://example.com/robots.txt
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Examples of valid robots txt URLs

name: Non-standard port requires its own robots.txt
then_action: a separate robots.txt must be placed at that port's root (e.g., https://example.com:8181/robots.txt), as the standard port robots.txt does not apply
if_condition: if a site serves content on a non-standard port (e.g., https://example.com:8181/)
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Examples of valid robots txt URLs

name: IP-based robots.txt does not apply to all hosted sites
then_action: the robots.txt is valid only for crawling that specific IP address as hostname and does not automatically apply to domain-named websites hosted on the same IP
if_condition: if a robots.txt file is placed at an IP address hostname (e.g., http://123.456.789.0/robots.txt) and multiple websites are hosted on that IP
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Examples of valid robots txt URLs

name: robots.txt at root covers all subdirectories on same host/protocol/port
then_action: it applies to all files and subdirectories under that same host, protocol, and port combination
if_condition: if a robots.txt file is validly placed at the root of a host with a specific protocol and port (e.g., https://example.com/robots.txt)
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Examples of valid robots txt URLs

name: Identify FeedFetcher-Google for RSS/Atom Feeds
then_action: Then classify the request as originating from FeedFetcher-Google, which fetches RSS or Atom feeds on behalf of users who have subscribed or submitted feed URLs
if_condition: If an HTTP request contains the User-Agent string 'FeedFetcher-Google; (+http://www.google.com/feedfetcher.html)'
source_title: Google User Triggered Fetchers Chrome Web Store

name: Robots.txt Most Specific User Agent Match
then_action: Select only the group with the most specific user agent that matches the crawler's user agent, and ignore all other groups entirely
if_condition: If a robots.txt file contains multiple user-agent groups and Google's crawler is parsing it
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Order of precedence for user agents

name: Robots.txt Global Wildcard Fallback Only
then_action: Apply the global wildcard group (*) rules as a fallback; do not apply global (*) group when a specific user-agent match exists
if_condition: If no specific user-agent group matches the crawler's user agent in robots.txt
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Order of precedence for user agents

name: Robots.txt Duplicate User Agent Group Merging
then_action: Merge all rules from those duplicate groups into a single combined ruleset internally before applying them
if_condition: If multiple groups in robots.txt share the same specific user-agent declaration (e.g., Googlebot appears in more than one group)
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Order of precedence for user agents

name: Robots.txt Unsupported Directives Break Group Parsing
then_action: The parser treats subsequent user-agent lines as part of the same group rather than a new group, potentially causing unintended rule grouping
if_condition: If unsupported directives (anything other than allow, disallow, or user-agent) appear between user-agent declarations in robots.txt
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Order of precedence for user agents

name: Sequence PLG Scaling Steps Correctly
then_action: Then follow the implementation sequence: (1) validate paid channels and scale SEO, (2) set up B2B sales motion for enterprise, (3) scale specialized programs like education, (4) amplify virality through Product Growth strategy, (5) align product changes with top-of-funnel optimization
if_condition: If a PLG company is ready to scale beyond initial product-market fit
source_title: The Ultimate Product Led Growth Resources Guide   OpenView Acquisition strategies and product virali

name: robots.txt HTML Content Parsing Degradation
then_action: Google will attempt to parse and extract any valid rules while ignoring all HTML content, risking incomplete or incorrect directive interpretation
if_condition: if robots.txt file contains HTML markup instead of plain-text robots.txt directives
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification File format

name: robots.txt Line Separator Compliance
then_action: Google may fail to correctly parse individual directives, causing crawling rules to be ignored or misinterpreted
if_condition: if robots.txt lines are not separated by CR, CR/LF, or LF line separators
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification File format

name: robots.txt BOM and Invalid Line Handling
then_action: Google ignores those invalid lines entirely, so BOM presence or malformed directives will not block crawling but indicate a formatting issue to resolve
if_condition: if robots.txt file begins with a Unicode Byte Order Mark (BOM) or contains otherwise invalid lines
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification File format

name: robots.txt must return 2xx status for Google to process it
then_action: Google will not process the robots.txt as intended; ensure the robots.txt endpoint returns a 2xx success status code for crawlers to respect its directives
if_condition: if a robots.txt file is served with a non-2xx HTTP status code
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Handling of errors and HTTP status codes

name: Robots.txt Group Order Is Irrelevant
then_action: This assumption is incorrect — group order within robots.txt is irrelevant; only specificity of user-agent match determines which group is applied
if_condition: If a site owner structures robots.txt assuming group order (top-to-bottom placement) determines which rules take precedence for a given crawler
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Order of precedence for user agents

name: robots.txt 500 KiB File Size Limit
then_action: Google ignores all content after the 500 KiB limit, meaning crawling directives beyond that point will not be enforced; consolidate rules or move excluded content to a separate directory to reduce file size
if_condition: if robots.txt file size exceeds 500 kibibytes (KiB)
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification File format

name: Buffer Unsupported Payment Methods
then_action: Payment will not be accepted; user must use a supported payment method (e.g., credit/debit card)
if_condition: If a user attempts to pay for Buffer using PayPal, checks, or bank transfers
source_title: Pricing What features are supported for each specific channel 2

name: 4xx on robots.txt allows unrestricted crawling
then_action: Google treats the situation as if no robots.txt file exists and crawls the site without restrictions; fix the 4xx error or explicitly serve a permissive robots.txt if unrestricted crawling is intended
if_condition: if the robots.txt file returns any 4xx client error HTTP status code
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Handling of errors and HTTP status codes

name: 5xx on robots.txt blocks all crawling
then_action: Google treats robots.txt as completely disallowing all crawling; resolve server errors promptly to restore normal crawl access
if_condition: if the robots.txt file returns a 5xx server error or cannot be fetched due to DNS/network issues
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Handling of errors and HTTP status codes

name: robots.txt redirect chains exceeding five hops treated as 404
then_action: Google stops following redirects after five hops and treats the result as a 404 error, effectively allowing unrestricted crawling; reduce redirect chains to five or fewer hops
if_condition: if the robots.txt URL has a chain of more than five 3xx HTTP redirects before reaching the final destination
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Handling of errors and HTTP status codes

name: robots.txt cache extended during 5xx or timeout conditions
then_action: Google may cache the previous robots.txt response longer than the standard 24-hour window, meaning crawl behavior may not update immediately after fixing server issues; monitor cache-control headers and resolve errors promptly
if_condition: if Google cannot refresh the robots.txt file due to 5xx errors or timeouts
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Handling of errors and HTTP status codes

name: Cache-Control max-age header modifies robots.txt cache lifetime
then_action: Google adjusts the robots.txt cache lifetime according to the specified max-age value, which can shorten or extend the default 24-hour cache period; set max-age appropriately when robots.txt updates need to propagate faster or slower
if_condition: if the robots.txt HTTP response includes a Cache-Control header with a max-age directive
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Handling of errors and HTTP status codes

name: sitemap field requires fully qualified URL
then_action: the sitemap directive is invalid; use a fully qualified URL including protocol and host (e.g., https://example.com/sitemap.xml)
if_condition: if the sitemap field in robots.txt is specified without a fully qualified URL (missing protocol or host)
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Syntax

name: crawl-delay field not supported by Google
then_action: Google does not support the crawl-delay field and will ignore it; remove it to avoid confusion and manage crawl rate through Google Search Console instead
if_condition: if crawl-delay is specified in robots.txt targeting Googlebot
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Syntax

name: rules without path value are ignored
then_action: crawlers ignore these rules entirely; always specify a path value for allow/disallow directives
if_condition: if a robots.txt allow or disallow rule is written without a path value (e.g., 'Disallow:' with no value after the colon)
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Syntax

name: Buffer Trial-to-Paid Conversion Requirement
then_action: The account will automatically downgrade to the Free plan with associated restrictions (3-channel limit, 10 posts per channel)
if_condition: If a user does not enter payment details in the Billing Method section before the 14-day free trial ends
source_title: Pricing What features are supported for each specific channel 2

name: sitemap field applies to all crawlers regardless of user-agent grouping
then_action: the sitemap directive is not tied to that specific user-agent and may be followed by all crawlers whose access to the sitemap URL is not otherwise disallowed
if_condition: if a sitemap field is declared in robots.txt under a specific user-agent group
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Syntax

name: path values in robots.txt are case-sensitive
then_action: path values are case-sensitive and will not match URLs with different casing; ensure exact case match with the actual URL paths to be controlled
if_condition: if allow or disallow path values in robots.txt use inconsistent or wrong casing compared to actual URL paths
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Syntax

name: Buffer Advanced Analytics Channel Exclusion
then_action: Advanced Analytics will not be available for those channel types regardless of plan tier
if_condition: If a user connects Bluesky, Facebook groups, Instagram personal profiles, LinkedIn profiles, Mastodon, Pinterest accounts, Start Page, Threads, TikTok, or YouTube to Buffer
source_title: Pricing What features are supported for each specific channel 2

name: robots.txt URL is case-sensitive
then_action: ensure the URL paths in robots.txt match the exact case of the actual URLs, as the robots.txt URL and its directives are case-sensitive
if_condition: if Disallow or Allow directives reference URLs with uppercase or mixed-case characters
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification What is a robots txt file

name: REP does not apply to user-controlled or safety crawlers
then_action: recognize that the Robots Exclusion Protocol does not apply to those crawlers — robots.txt will not restrict their access
if_condition: if robots.txt Disallow rules are expected to block user-controlled crawlers or Google safety crawlers (e.g., malware analysis)
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification What is a robots txt file

name: Buffer Volume Discount Pricing Threshold
then_action: Channels 1–10 are billed at the standard per-channel rate; channels above 10 are billed at a discounted per-channel rate
if_condition: If a user on a paid Buffer plan adds more than 10 channels to their account
source_title: Pricing What features are supported for each specific channel 2

name: robots.txt path values must start with slash
then_action: the rule is invalid; path values must start with '/' to designate the root of the website
if_condition: if a robots.txt allow or disallow field path value does not start with '/'
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Syntax

name: Robots.txt Most Specific Rule Selection
then_action: Apply the most specific rule based on the length of the rule path (longer path = more specific = takes precedence)
if_condition: If multiple robots.txt rules match the same URL path
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification URL matching based on path values

name: Robots.txt Conflicting Rules Least Restrictive Resolution
then_action: Apply the least restrictive rule, meaning prefer the allow directive over the disallow directive
if_condition: If two or more robots.txt rules of equal specificity conflict (e.g., one allow and one disallow match the same URL, including wildcard rules)
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification URL matching based on path values

name: Robots.txt Trailing Wildcard Equivalence
then_action: Treat it as equivalent to the path without the trailing wildcard (e.g., `/path/`), since trailing wildcards are ignored by Google
if_condition: If a robots.txt path rule ends with `/*` (e.g., `/path/*`)
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification URL matching based on path values

name: Robots.txt Dollar Sign End-of-URL Anchor
then_action: Match only URLs that end exactly at that point — `/$` matches only the root URL, while `/*.php$` matches only URLs ending with `.php`
if_condition: If a robots.txt rule uses `$` at the end of the path (e.g., `/$` or `/*.php$`)
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification URL matching based on path values

name: Robots.txt Non-ASCII Character Encoding Requirement
then_action: Encode them as UTF-8 characters or as percent-escaped UTF-8 encoded characters per RFC 3986 to ensure correct matching by Googlebot
if_condition: If a robots.txt path rule contains non-7-bit ASCII characters
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification URL matching based on path values

name: Use Sitemap directive in robots.txt to declare sitemap location
then_action: add a Sitemap directive in robots.txt pointing to the sitemap URL to help Google crawlers discover and index the site's content more efficiently
if_condition: if a sitemap exists for the site and robots.txt is already in use
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification What is a robots txt file

name: disallowed URLs can still be indexed as URL-only results
then_action: Google cannot crawl or index the page content but may still index and show the URL in search results without a snippet; use noindex meta tag (accessible to crawlers) to prevent URL indexing entirely
if_condition: if a page is blocked by a disallow rule in robots.txt and it appears in search results without a snippet
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification Syntax

name: robots.txt must be placed at top-level directory
then_action: place the robots.txt file in the top-level directory of the site on a supported protocol (HTTP, HTTPS, or FTP), otherwise Google crawlers will not find or honor it
if_condition: if a robots.txt file is created to control crawler access
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification What is a robots txt file

name: robots.txt rules are scoped to host, protocol, and port only
then_action: note that rules apply only to the specific host, protocol, and port number where the robots.txt file is hosted — separate robots.txt files are needed for each distinct host/protocol/port combination
if_condition: if robots.txt rules are intended to apply across subdomains, different protocols, or different ports
source_title: How Google Interprets the robots txt Specification What is a robots txt file

name: Avoid crawl-delay directive for Google crawlers
then_action: Do not use crawl-delay; it is not recognized by Google's crawlers and will not control crawl rate
if_condition: If a site uses the 'crawl-delay' directive in robots.txt to manage Google's crawling behavior
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling 2

name: Server errors reduce crawl rate
then_action: Crawl rate will slow down; keep server errors low to maintain healthy crawl rates
if_condition: If a site has a significant number of 5xx HTTP response status codes or connection timeouts
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling 2

name: All crawled URLs count toward crawl budget
then_action: All such URLs count toward the site's crawl budget, not just canonical HTML pages
if_condition: If a site has alternate URLs (e.g., AMP, hreflang) or embedded resources (CSS, JavaScript, XHR fetches) that Googlebot accesses
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling 2

name: Nofollow links do not guarantee uncrawled URLs
then_action: Googlebot may still crawl that URL; nofollow does not reliably prevent crawling
if_condition: If a link on your site is marked as nofollow but the same URL is linked without nofollow on another page on your site or anywhere on the web
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling 2

name: Noindex can free up crawl budget indirectly
then_action: Noindex can indirectly free up crawl budget over time by allowing crawlers to focus on other URLs, though Google must crawl pages first to discover the noindex rule
if_condition: If pages are tagged with noindex and are being crawled by Googlebot
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling 2

name: Crawling is not a ranking signal
then_action: Do not optimize crawl rate with the expectation of ranking improvement; crawling is necessary for indexation but is not a ranking factor in Google Search
if_condition: If a site owner assumes that increasing crawl rate or crawl frequency will improve search rankings
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling 2

name: 4xx errors (except 429) do not waste crawl budget
then_action: Do not assume these pages waste crawl budget; only 429 (Too Many Requests) responses are treated differently and can affect crawl rate
if_condition: If pages on a site return 4xx HTTP status codes other than 429
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling 2

name: Site speed signals healthy servers and improves crawl rate
then_action: Google's crawlers interpret faster responses as a signal of healthy servers and will increase crawl rate accordingly
if_condition: If a site improves its server response speed and overall page load performance
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling 2

name: Sitemap Compression Does Not Save Crawl Budget
then_action: Avoid compressing sitemaps to game crawl budget — zipped sitemaps still require a server fetch, so no meaningful crawl time or effort is saved
if_condition: If compressing sitemaps (e.g., using gzip/zip) with the intent to reduce crawl budget usage
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling

name: Buffer Free Plan Channel Lock Rule
then_action: Additional channels will be locked and unavailable; user must upgrade to a paid plan to access more than three channels
if_condition: If a user attempts to connect more than three channels on Buffer's Free plan
source_title: Pricing What features are supported for each specific channel 2

name: Versioned URL Changes Must Reflect Meaningful Content Updates
then_action: Avoid unnecessary versioned URL updates — this wastes crawl resources without benefit; only update versioned URLs when page content has changed meaningfully
if_condition: If updating versioned URL parameters on pages that have not meaningfully changed in content, with the intent to trigger re-crawling sooner
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling

name: Crawl Frequency Tied to Content Change Rate
then_action: Google will crawl the site more often regardless of site size — prioritize ensuring high-value, frequently updated content is accessible and linked clearly
if_condition: If a site contains important content that changes frequently
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling

name: Home Page Links Increase Crawl Priority But Not Rankings
then_action: These pages may be crawled more often as they appear more important to Googlebot, but higher crawl frequency does not translate to higher rankings
if_condition: If pages are linked directly from the home page
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling

name: Qualify Paid/Sponsored External Links
then_action: Then you MUST add the `sponsored` or `nofollow` attribute to that link to comply with Google's requirements
if_condition: If an external link on your site is paid for or part of a sponsorship arrangement
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google External links link to other sites

name: Tag User-Generated Content Links
then_action: Then you MUST apply the `ugc` or `nofollow` attribute to those links to signal their user-generated origin to Google
if_condition: If external links appear in user-generated content sections such as forums, comment sections, or Q&A sites
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google External links link to other sites

name: Avoid Blanket Nofollow on All External Links
then_action: Then you should stop this practice, as Google advises against using `nofollow` as a blanket policy for all external links
if_condition: If your site applies `nofollow` to every external link regardless of link type or trust level
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google External links link to other sites

name: Page Render Speed Increases Crawl Rate
then_action: Improve page load and render speed to increase how much Google can crawl, since crawl resources are limited by both time and number of bots, and rendering time counts equally to request time
if_condition: If pages are slow to load or render, consuming significant crawl time budget
source_title: Myths and facts about crawling

name: Buffer Automatic Publishing Exclusion
then_action: Automatic publishing is not supported for those profile types; only Notification Publishing is available for applicable channels
if_condition: If a user attempts to set up automatic publishing for Facebook groups or Instagram personal profiles in Buffer
source_title: Pricing What features are supported for each specific channel 2

name: Descriptive Anchor Text Between <a> Elements
then_action: add descriptive text between <a> elements that indicates what the linked page contains, so Google and users understand the link destination
if_condition: if a hyperlink is created using <a> elements with no descriptive text placed between the opening and closing tags
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Anchor text placement

name: Alt Text Required for Image Links
then_action: add descriptive alt text to the element, as Google uses the alt attribute as the anchor text for image links
if_condition: if an element is used as a hyperlink (wrapped in an tag) and the alt attribute is empty or missing
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Anchor text placement

name: JavaScript-Inserted Anchor Text Verification
then_action: use the URL Inspection Tool to verify the anchor text appears correctly in the rendered HTML, ensuring Google can crawl and interpret the link
if_condition: if JavaScript is used to dynamically insert anchor text into <a> elements on the page
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Anchor text placement

name: Title Attribute Fallback for Empty <a> Elements
then_action: Google may use the title attribute as a fallback for anchor text, but this is not a reliable substitute — descriptive inner text or alt text should still be implemented
if_condition: if an <a> element has no inner text and no associated image alt text but has a title attribute
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Anchor text placement

name: File Special Request for Sustained Crawl Rate Reduction
then_action: File a special request to Google reporting the unusually high crawl rate and specify the optimal crawl rate for the site; note this may take several days to be evaluated and cannot be used to increase crawl rate
if_condition: If unusually high crawl rate persists and cannot be resolved via HTTP error codes alone, and the site owner knows the optimal crawl rate
source_title: Reduce Google Crawl Rate

name: Audit Access Logs When Crawl Rate Spikes Sharply
then_action: Check with the hosting company and review recent access logs to identify the traffic source and root cause; also review guides on managing faceted navigation URLs and optimizing crawl efficiency
if_condition: If a sharp increase in Google crawling activity is detected on the site
source_title: Reduce Google Crawl Rate

name: Buffer Advanced Analytics Channel Eligibility
then_action: Advanced analytics are ONLY available for Facebook Pages, Instagram accounts, LinkedIn Pages, and X/Twitter premium profiles — other channel types do not support this feature
if_condition: If a Buffer user wants to access advanced analytics for a specific channel
source_title: Pricing What features are supported for each specific channel

name: Anticipate Hostname-Wide Impact When Reducing Crawl Rate
then_action: Expect the reduced crawl rate to affect the entire hostname, impacting both URLs returning errors and URLs returning valid content
if_condition: If crawl rate reduction measures (error status codes or special requests) are applied to any URLs on a hostname such as subdomain.example.com
source_title: Reduce Google Crawl Rate

name: Identify Faceted Navigation as Common Crawl Spike Cause
then_action: Investigate these URL configurations as likely causes of the crawl spike and prioritize optimizing or managing these URL patterns to reduce inefficient crawl demand
if_condition: If a site uses faceted navigation, sorting/filtering functionality, date-specific calendar URLs, or Dynamic Search Ad targets and experiences elevated crawl rates
source_title: Reduce Google Crawl Rate

name: Images and Videos Cannot Be Indexed Without Crawling
then_action: remove the crawl disallow rule for those resources — Google cannot index images and videos without crawling them, unlike text-based URLs which may still be indexed via links
if_condition: if robots.txt disallows crawling of pages or directories that contain images or videos intended to appear in search results
source_title: Useful robots txt Rules

name: Use Error Status Codes for Urgent Crawl Rate Reduction
then_action: Return HTTP 500, 503, or 429 status codes instead of 200 for crawl requests to signal Google to reduce crawl rate urgently
if_condition: If Google's crawl rate is causing critical server load or infrastructure costs and immediate reduction is needed within hours to 1-2 days
source_title: Reduce Google Crawl Rate

name: Use Nofollow Selectively for Untrusted Sources
then_action: Then apply the `nofollow` attribute to that specific link rather than using it globally
if_condition: If you are linking to an external site you do not trust or do not want to pass reputation to
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google External links link to other sites

name: Demand Conversion Path Must Be Frictionless for Ready Buyers
then_action: Then conversion rates will suffer — make it dead simple for ready buyers to take the next step by removing friction from demand program touchpoints
if_condition: If a B2B demand program targets buyers who are already in-market or ready to evaluate but the conversion path (CTA, demo request, pricing page) requires multiple steps or unclear next actions
source_title: Brand Demand Expand A Better Way to Structure B2B Marketing

name: Brand Motion Validation via Branded Search and Sales Attribution
then_action: Then the company does not have an active brand motion and must reassess brand investment, consistency, and distribution strategy before expecting pipeline impact
if_condition: If branded search volume is not trending up over time AND sales conversations are not beginning with references to company content or prior brand familiarity
source_title: Brand The Most Misunderstood Motion in B2B Marketing

name: Demand-Heavy Plans Signal Missing Long-Term Leverage
then_action: Then the team is not building long-term leverage — rebalance with intentional brand and expand strategies even if weight is not equal across motions
if_condition: If the majority of a B2B marketing team's programs fall under the demand motion (lead generation, conversion, direct response) with minimal brand or expand investment
source_title: Brand Demand Expand A Better Way to Structure B2B Marketing

name: Crawlable Link Anchor Tag Requirement
then_action: The link will not be reliably crawlable by Google; switch to <a href='[valid URL]'> format to ensure Googlebot can extract and follow the link
if_condition: If a link is implemented using any HTML element other than <a> with a valid href attribute (e.g., <span href>, <a routerLink>, <a onclick>, or script-based navigation)
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Make your links crawlable

name: JavaScript href Link Crawlability Warning
then_action: Google may attempt to resolve the URL but crawlability is not guaranteed; replace with a standard <a href='[resolved URL]'> to ensure reliable crawling
if_condition: If a link uses <a href='javascript:...'> format (e.g., javascript:goTo('products') or javascript:window.location.href='/products')
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Make your links crawlable

name: Anchor Without href Attribute Warning
then_action: Google cannot reliably extract URLs from hrefless anchor elements; add a valid href attribute containing the destination URL to make the link crawlable
if_condition: If an <a> element is used without an href attribute to create a navigable link
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Make your links crawlable

name: Valid URL in href Requirement
then_action: Google crawlers cannot send requests to that URL; ensure the href value is a fully resolvable URI (absolute or relative) pointing to a real web resource
if_condition: If the URL value inside an <a href> attribute does not resolve to an actual web address resembling a valid URI
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Make your links crawlable

name: Anchor Text Length Should Be Concise — Avoid Unnecessary Detail
then_action: Shorten anchor text to a concise phrase that describes the linked page without extraneous information
if_condition: If anchor text is unusually long and includes unnecessary descriptive details beyond what is needed to identify the linked page
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Write good anchor text

name: Anchor Text Must Be Descriptive and Contextually Relevant
then_action: Replace with descriptive, concise anchor text that reflects the content of the linked page and fits naturally within the surrounding sentence context
if_condition: If anchor text used for internal or external links is generic (e.g., 'Click here', 'Read more', 'website', 'article') or lacks relevance to the linked page
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Write good anchor text

name: Avoid Keyword Stuffing in Anchor Text
then_action: Trim anchor text to only include keywords the reader genuinely needs to understand the next page; keyword stuffing in anchors violates Google spam policies
if_condition: If anchor text contains multiple keywords crammed together or feels forced beyond what is needed to describe the linked page
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Write good anchor text

name: Avoid Chaining Multiple Links Together
then_action: Separate links with meaningful surrounding text to help readers distinguish between links and preserve contextual signals for each link
if_condition: If multiple hyperlinks are placed consecutively next to each other with little or no surrounding text separating them
source_title: SEO Link Best Practices for Google Write good anchor text

name: Control AI Training Data Access via Google-Extended robots.txt Token
then_action: Use the Google-Extended token in robots.txt to set allow/disallow rules — Google-Extended controls AI training crawl permissions using existing Google user agent strings for HTTP requests
if_condition: If a site owner wants to control whether their content is used for Google AI model training (e.g., Gemini) without a separate crawler HTTP user agent string being available
source_title: Google s common crawlers A note about Chrome W X Y Z in user agents Part 2

name: Use Google-Extended to Control AI Training Data
then_action: Add Google-Extended directive to robots.txt to block this usage without affecting Search inclusion or rankings
if_condition: If a site owner wants to prevent their content from being used to train future versions of Gemini models
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling Our standard crawlers always respect websites choices abo

name: Use Search Console to Diagnose Crawling Issues
then_action: Access Google Search Console (free) to diagnose problems such as server downtime or speed issues and review crawling activity reports
if_condition: If a site is experiencing server downtime, slow response times, or unexpected drops in crawling activity
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling Our standard crawlers always respect websites choices abo

name: Google-Extended Has No Ranking Impact
then_action: Proceed with using Google-Extended freely — Google explicitly does not use it as a ranking signal and it does not affect a site's inclusion in Search
if_condition: If a site owner is hesitant to use Google-Extended in robots.txt because they fear it will negatively affect their Search rankings or inclusion
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling Our standard crawlers always respect websites choices abo

name: Robots.txt Controls Must Be Respected by Googlebot
then_action: Google's standard crawlers must honor those preferences, including when reusing previously crawled data across multiple uses
if_condition: If a site owner specifies content access restrictions via robots.txt or other open web protocol controls
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling Our standard crawlers always respect websites choices abo

name: Use Sitemap Files to Influence Recrawl Frequency
then_action: Submit sitemap files to Google to signal new and updated pages, which influences recrawling frequency and helps Google discover content faster
if_condition: If a website has new or updated pages that need to be discovered or recrawled by Google
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the

name: Robots.txt File Naming and Location Requirement
then_action: Then the file MUST be saved as a text file named exactly 'robots.txt' and placed in the root directory of the site (e.g., https://example.com/robots.txt); any other name or location will not be recognized by Google's crawlers
if_condition: If uploading a new or updated robots.txt file to a website
source_title: Updating Your Robots txt File

name: Robots.txt UTF-8 Encoding Requirement
then_action: Then the file MUST be saved with UTF-8 encoding to ensure Google's crawlers correctly parse the rules
if_condition: If creating or editing a robots.txt file in a text editor before uploading
source_title: Updating Your Robots txt File

name: Robots.txt Cache Refresh via Search Console
then_action: Then use the 'Request a recrawl' function within the robots.txt report in Google Search Console to trigger a faster cache update
if_condition: If robots.txt changes need to be reflected in Google's cache faster than the automatic 24-hour refresh cycle
source_title: Updating Your Robots txt File

name: Robots.txt Syntax Compliance Requirement
then_action: Then correct syntax MUST be used and Google's robots.txt file format guidelines MUST be followed; incorrect syntax may cause crawlers to misinterpret or ignore the rules
if_condition: If editing rules within a robots.txt file
source_title: Updating Your Robots txt File

name: Discover Stage Should Prioritize Low-CAC High-Intent Channels
then_action: Then prioritize word-of-mouth referrals, product invitations, and organic search (users Googling solutions) over paid channels, as these deliver higher-intent users at lower customer acquisition cost (CAC)
if_condition: If evaluating or prioritizing traffic acquisition channels for a PLG product
source_title: Your Guide to Product Led Growth Benchmarks

name: Recrawl Frequency Based on Content Freshness
then_action: Google may wait up to a month before recrawling the page; to increase crawl frequency, ensure pages have fresh, regularly updated, or highly relevant content
if_condition: If a page has not changed for years and shows no signals of fresh or highly relevant content
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the

name: News Homepage Crawl Frequency Priority
then_action: Google may recrawl the page every few minutes to surface breaking news in search results; prioritize keeping news homepages lightweight and crawlable
if_condition: If a page is a news homepage or contains frequently updated breaking news content
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the

name: Full Rendering Required for Page Understanding
then_action: Google uses a full rendering technique to load the page completely; the same page may need to be crawled multiple times to capture a representative snapshot as new elements are added
if_condition: If a web page relies on JavaScript, dynamic content, or lazy-loaded elements to render its full content
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the

name: Subscription Content Structured Data Requirement
then_action: Use structured data to signal subscription access to crawlers, ensuring compliance with Google's guidelines and avoiding spam rule violations
if_condition: If a site provides subscription or paywall content and wants to show human visitors a login screen while granting crawlers access
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the 2

name: Page Size Impact on Crawl Efficiency
then_action: Crawl efficiency may be negatively impacted; optimize page size and reduce the number of resource files to improve crawlability and rendering speed
if_condition: If a mobile page exceeds 2.3 megabytes or loads more than 60 different files
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the

name: Product-Led Organic Acquisition Channel Priority
then_action: Then prioritize organic sources including SEO and direct traffic (53% share) and product-driven acquisition (13%); invest in product-led marketing such as template galleries, product-related content hubs, and product education
if_condition: If a product-led company is allocating resources to user discovery and acquisition channels
source_title: Your Guide to Product Led Growth Benchmarks Product led growth benchmarks

name: Crawler User-Agent Identifiability Requirement
then_action: The crawler MUST use easily identifiable user-agent names and known internet addresses to ensure transparency and allow site owners to distinguish legitimate Googlebot traffic
if_condition: If deploying any automated web crawler or bot that accesses web pages on behalf of Google services
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the

name: Paywall Access Permission Requirement
then_action: Do NOT access the content unless explicit permission has been granted by the site owner; accessing paywall content without permission violates Google's crawling policies
if_condition: If a crawler or automated agent attempts to access pages behind a paywall or subscription gate
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the 2

name: Login Page Crawling Restriction
then_action: Avoid crawling content behind login pages by default; use robots.txt or robots meta tags to explicitly declare crawler interaction preferences for those pages
if_condition: If a site has pages that require user login to access content
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the 2

name: Robots.txt and Meta Tags for Crawl Control
then_action: Implement robots.txt to declare crawler interaction rules and use robots meta tags to communicate content access preferences per page
if_condition: If a site owner wants to control which pages are crawled or how crawlers interact with specific content
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the 2

name: Sitemap Submission for New Content Discovery
then_action: Submit the new content URLs via sitemaps to signal to Google's crawlers that the content is ready for crawling and indexing
if_condition: If a site publishes new content that needs to be indexed by Google quickly
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the 2

name: Preview Controls for Subscription Content
then_action: Use Google's preview controls to prevent subscription content from being displayed in search result page previews
if_condition: If a site has subscription content that should not appear in Google Search page previews or snippets
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the 2

name: Automatic Crawl Rate Adjustment on Server Errors
then_action: Google's crawlers will automatically reduce crawl rate to avoid overloading the server; site owners should monitor server health and error rates to ensure optimal crawl frequency
if_condition: If a site returns errors or experiences slowdowns during a crawl session
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the 2

name: Crawl Budget Optimization for Non-Essential Pages
then_action: Identify and explicitly exclude non-essential content from crawling via robots.txt or crawl budget settings to lower server load and infrastructure costs
if_condition: If a site has pages that do not need to appear in Search results (e.g., infinite calendar pages, duplicate utility pages) and wants to reduce infrastructure costs
source_title: Things to Know about Google s Web Crawling What is crawling In short crawling is how Google sees the 2

name: Google Crawler Verification: Forward DNS Confirmation
then_action: Run a forward DNS lookup on that domain name and confirm it resolves back to the original accessing IP address from your logs; only if both reverse and forward DNS match is the request confirmed as genuine Google traffic
if_condition: If the reverse DNS lookup of an accessing IP resolves to googlebot.com, google.com, or googleusercontent.com
source_title: Verify Requests from Google Crawlers and Fetchers

name: Large-Scale Google IP Verification: Use Automated CIDR Matching
then_action: Use an automated solution to match crawler IP addresses against Google's published list of IP addresses, which are represented in CIDR format, rather than performing individual DNS lookups
if_condition: If performing large-scale lookups to verify multiple Google crawler IP addresses at once
source_title: Verify Requests from Google Crawlers and Fetchers

name: Special-Case Crawlers May Not Respect robots.txt
then_action: Do not assume robots.txt rules will be respected, as special-case crawlers may or may not honor robots.txt; verify crawler type and review Google documentation for that specific crawler's behavior
if_condition: If a special-case Google crawler (not a common crawler) accesses your site and you have robots.txt rules in place
source_title: Verify Requests from Google Crawlers and Fetchers

name: Disallow Directory Crawling Requires Trailing Slash
then_action: append a trailing forward slash to the directory name to correctly disallow crawling of the whole directory (e.g., use 'Disallow: /calendar/' not 'Disallow: /calendar')
if_condition: if a robots.txt Disallow rule targets a directory path without a trailing forward slash
source_title: Useful robots txt Rules

name: Disallowed Crawling Does Not Guarantee Non-Indexing
then_action: do not assume the URL will be excluded from indexing — Google may still index URLs it cannot crawl if they are linked from other pages; use noindex or other methods to prevent indexing
if_condition: if robots.txt disallows crawling of a URL and the site owner assumes that URL will not appear in search results
source_title: Useful robots txt Rules

name: Block Entire Site from All Crawlers
then_action: use 'User-agent: *' with 'Disallow: /' in robots.txt to block all crawlers from the entire site
if_condition: if the goal is to prevent all web crawlers from accessing any part of the site
source_title: Useful robots txt Rules

name: Allow Only One Specific Crawler While Blocking All Others
then_action: create a robots.txt group for the specific crawler with 'Allow: /' and a separate group for 'User-agent: *' with 'Disallow: /'
if_condition: if the site should be accessible to only one specific crawler (e.g., Googlebot-News) and all other crawlers should be blocked
source_title: Useful robots txt Rules

name: Block Specific File Types Using Wildcard Pattern
then_action: use the '$' wildcard in the Disallow rule to match URLs ending with that extension (e.g., 'Disallow: /*.gif$') combined with the '*' wildcard for path segments
if_condition: if the goal is to prevent a crawler from accessing files of a specific extension (e.g., .gif images)
source_title: Useful robots txt Rules

name: Consolidate Multiple Crawlers Into One robots.txt Group
then_action: list all applicable user agents in a single group in robots.txt so all rules apply to every listed agent, reducing file length and management complexity
if_condition: if multiple crawlers (user agents) share the same robots.txt rules
source_title: Useful robots txt Rules

name: Google Crawler Verification: Reverse DNS Domain Check
then_action: Run a reverse DNS lookup on the accessing IP address, then verify the resulting domain name is either googlebot.com, google.com, or googleusercontent.com; if it is not one of these domains, the request is not from Google
if_condition: If an IP address in server logs claims to be a Google crawler or fetcher and you need to verify authenticity
source_title: Verify Requests from Google Crawlers and Fetchers

name: LinkedIn Personal Profiles Outperform Company Pages for Organic Reach
then_action: Shift strategy to treat employee profiles as the primary distribution system, since personal profiles consistently outperform company pages even when normalized per 1,000 followers — and the engagement gap has expanded over time, not closed
if_condition: If a B2B company is relying primarily on company pages as the main organic growth engine on LinkedIn
source_title: Employee Profiles Outperform Brand Pages on LinkedIn Why we ran this analysis again and what changed

name: Branded Search Tracking as Brand Health Signal
then_action: Then monitor branded search volume trends over time, direct traffic to educational content, self-reported attribution on forms, and mentions in sales calls via Gong or rep notes as correlated signals of brand health — no single metric is sufficient alone
if_condition: If a B2B company is running a brand motion and tracking effectiveness metrics
source_title: Brand The Most Misunderstood Motion in B2B Marketing

name: Buffer Annual Billing Discount
then_action: A 20% discount is applied to the total subscription cost compared to the monthly billing rate
if_condition: If a user selects annual billing instead of monthly billing for a Buffer paid plan
source_title: Pricing What features are supported for each specific channel 2

name: Inbound Marketing Over Paid Traffic Rule
then_action: Then prioritize inbound marketing tactics (content, SEO, social presence) to build organic traffic instead of paying for traffic via search ads, thereby reducing cost per lead
if_condition: If a startup needs to drive lead flow at low cost to improve CAC metrics
source_title: Startup Killer the Cost of Customer Acquisition   For Entrepreneurs Lessons Learned Business Plannin

name: Structure Content for LLM Retrieval Over SEO Signals
then_action: Structure content for LLM retrieval rather than traditional search engine ranking signals — prioritize topical authority, extraction-friendly formatting, and precise answer delivery over keyword density or backlink optimization
if_condition: If content goal includes being cited or referenced by AI language models (ChatGPT, Perplexity, etc.)
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs The Data First

name: Build Comprehensive Reference Documents Instead of Blog Posts
then_action: Publish long-form deep dives structured as comprehensive reference guides rather than standard blog posts, so LLMs can index, cite, and reinforce the content as an authoritative source
if_condition: If publishing new content intended to attract AI-referred traffic
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs The Data First

name: H-Tags as Answer Containers for LLM Citation
then_action: Rewrite H2s so each functions as a standalone question with its section serving as the direct answer, enabling language models to retrieve and cite discrete content units
if_condition: If content pages use H-tags as navigational labels rather than question-framed answer containers
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs Why This Worked

name: Establish Topic Cluster Authority Before Expanding to New Areas
then_action: Delay expansion — establish dominance in specific topic clusters first, then scale, because later content compounds faster when foundational authority from earlier content is already established
if_condition: If planning to scale content into new topic areas before achieving authority in current ones
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs The Data First

name: Allow 3-Month Minimum Foundation Period Before Expecting AI Traffic Lift
then_action: Do not conclude the strategy is failing — visible AI traffic growth requires approximately 3 months of foundational content work before an inflection point appears; continue building authority consistently
if_condition: If AI-optimized content has been published for fewer than 3 months and AI-referred traffic has not yet increased
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs The Data First

name: Maintain Traditional SEO Signals Alongside LLM Optimization
then_action: Retain traditional SEO signals (keyword relevance, backlinks, domain authority, crawl budget) as they still contribute to overall visibility — LLM optimization should supplement, not replace, SEO fundamentals
if_condition: If pivoting content strategy fully toward LLM optimization while abandoning traditional SEO practices
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs The Data First

name: Metric Monitoring Across All Platforms
then_action: Then implement cross-platform analytics monitoring to collect and interpret key performance numbers, enabling data-driven strategy adjustments
if_condition: If social media content is being published across multiple platforms without tracking platform-specific performance metrics
source_title: Rochi Zalani   Buffer Blog Posts

name: Flat AI Referral Traffic Indicates Structural Problem
then_action: Diagnose the issue as a structural problem (messy URL structure, broken H-tag hierarchy, poor internal linking) rather than a creative or topical problem, and audit technical page structure first
if_condition: If AI-referred traffic is flat or declining despite regular content publication
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs Why This Worked

name: Topic Cluster Depth Over Volume for LLM Authority
then_action: Consolidate into comprehensive 2,500-word deep dives per core topic to establish topic ownership that LLMs recognize and cite consistently
if_condition: If a site publishes multiple short pieces (under 1,500 words) scattered across a topic area rather than three to four deeply structured pages per cluster
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs Why This Worked

name: Technical Structure Multiplier Effect on Content Quality
then_action: Fix technical structure immediately, as messy page structure actively discounts all content work and prevents LLM citation regardless of content depth
if_condition: If content quality is high but page structure has messy URLs, improper H-tag hierarchy, or illogical internal linking
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs Why This Worked

name: Minimum Time Horizon for LLM Citation Authority
then_action: Allow at least 9 months for compounding citation authority effects to materialize, as LLM topic ownership builds gradually and accelerates once established
if_condition: If evaluating AI referral traffic growth results before 9 months of consistent topic cluster publishing
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs Why This Worked

name: Connect CRM and Import High-Intent Revenue Outcomes
then_action: Connect CRM to Google Ads and import HIROs (High-Intent Revenue Outcomes) and Closed-Won deals as primary conversion actions so the algorithm optimizes for real buying signals
if_condition: When setting up conversion tracking for a B2B Google Ads account
source_title: Paid Search Playbook 2

name: Bidding Strategy Progression Based on Conversion Volume
then_action: Transition from Manual CPC or Enhanced CPC to Max Conversions, and when stable, move to Target CPA while gradually lowering the target
if_condition: When a B2B Google Ads account reaches 30 or more high-intent conversions per month
source_title: Paid Search Playbook 2

name: Prioritize User Experience Over Supplier Relationships
then_action: Then prioritize user experience optimization over exclusive supplier relationships, as the competitive plane has shifted from supplier control to consumer acquisition and retention
if_condition: If a digital business is competing in an Internet-era market where distribution costs are effectively zero and transaction costs are negligible
source_title: Aggregation Theory 2

name: Aggregation Theory Monopoly Endgame Warning
then_action: Then recognize that the end game of Aggregation Theory is monopoly, and evaluate antitrust implications for that aggregator company
if_condition: If a company achieves aggregator status by eliminating distribution costs, marginal costs, and transaction costs simultaneously across its business model
source_title: Aggregation Theory

name: Super-Aggregator Identification Rule
then_action: Then classify it as a Super-Aggregator and apply heightened antitrust scrutiny, as its competitive moat is structurally stronger than standard aggregators
if_condition: If a company (e.g., Facebook, Google) faces zero transaction costs across all parts of its business — including supply, distribution, and demand — not just one segment
source_title: Aggregation Theory

name: Aggregator Competitive Moat via Customer Relationship Integration
then_action: Then the platform achieves aggregator status with a durable competitive moat, shifting value away from traditional incumbents (newspapers, publishers, taxi companies, hotels) toward itself
if_condition: If a digital platform controls exclusive customer relationships and user data at scale while suppliers remain modularized and interchangeable
source_title: Aggregation Theory 3

name: Avoid Lead Form Extensions in B2B Paid Search
then_action: Do not use lead form extensions; instead use sitelinks, callouts, and image assets to support ad performance without reducing lead quality
if_condition: When configuring ad extensions for a B2B Google Ads campaign
source_title: Paid Search Playbook 2

name: Social Platform Validation Displaces Legacy Media Recognition
then_action: Then the legacy media outlet has lost its monopoly on cultural validation and must adapt its content strategy to reclaim relevance or accept a reduced role in talent-driven culture
if_condition: If athletes or talent treat appearances on social platforms (e.g., HouseofHighlights on Instagram) as equivalent to or more desirable than legacy media features (e.g., SportsCenter Top 10)
source_title: Beware the lessons of growing up Galapagos 5

name: Group Keywords into Single Theme Ad Groups (STAGs)
then_action: Group related keywords into Single Theme Ad Groups (STAGs) to improve relevance and quality score
if_condition: When structuring or restructuring a Google Ads account for B2B paid search
source_title: Paid Search Playbook 2

name: Exclude Low-Intent and Irrelevant Traffic
then_action: Add negative keywords to exclude irrelevant traffic including queries related to free, careers, templates, and examples to prevent low-value spend
if_condition: When building or auditing negative keyword lists for a B2B Google Ads account
source_title: Paid Search Playbook 2

name: Expect Volume Reduction After Account Cleanup
then_action: Expect total lead volume to decrease but anticipate 20–40% budget recovery from eliminated low-value spend, resulting in higher lead quality and lower customer acquisition cost (CAC)
if_condition: When restructuring an existing B2B Google Ads account by applying negatives and reallocating budget to high-intent campaigns
source_title: Paid Search Playbook 2

name: AI Undermines Internet as Authoritative Truth Source
then_action: Then the internet's reliability as a source of truth is further eroded, requiring brands and publishers to establish alternative trust signals and authoritative entity presence
if_condition: If AI-generated content becomes widespread and indistinguishable from human-authored content across the internet
source_title: Concepts AI Part 1

name: Publishers Lose Discovery Power to Platform Aggregators
