name: Unlisted Visibility Access Rule
then_action: Then the video will only be accessible to users who have the direct link — it will not appear in public search results or on the channel page
if_condition: If a YouTube video is set to 'Unlisted' visibility during the upload Visibility step
source_title: How to Use YouTube Studio to Grow Your Channel How to use YouTube Studio

name: H-Tags Must Function as Answer Containers for AEO
then_action: Rewrite all H2 tags to function as standalone questions where the section beneath directly answers that question, treating H-tags as answer containers rather than navigational labels
if_condition: If content is being optimized for AI citation and LLM retrieval
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs Why This Worked

name: Filter Keywords Through Intent Tokens
then_action: Do not bid on that keyword; exclude it from campaigns as it likely captures low-intent traffic that wastes budget
if_condition: If a B2B paid search keyword lacks intent signals such as 'software', 'solution', 'price', 'best', or similar buying-language qualifiers
source_title: Paid Search Playbook

name: Establish Topic Cluster Authority Before Expanding
then_action: Then first establish deep authority in a specific topic cluster before expanding — new content published within an established authority cluster gains traction significantly faster than content in unestablished areas
if_condition: If planning to scale AI-optimized content across multiple topic areas simultaneously
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs The Data First

name: Expect 3-Month Lag Before AI Citation Growth Becomes Visible
then_action: Then continue building foundational content — visible AI citation growth typically requires approximately 3 months of consistent topical authority-building before compound growth becomes observable
if_condition: If AI-referred traffic shows no measurable inflection point in the first 1–2 months after publishing LLM-optimized content
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs The Data First

name: Segment Campaigns by Intent Level
then_action: Segment campaigns by intent level so that spend follows value — higher budget allocation goes to higher-intent segments
if_condition: When allocating budget across Google Ads campaigns for B2B demand generation
source_title: Paid Search Playbook 2

name: Track Only High-Intent Conversion Actions
then_action: Only track actions that demonstrate real buying intent (e.g., HIROs, Closed-Won); set these as primary conversion actions so the algorithm optimizes for pipeline value, not volume
if_condition: If configuring conversion tracking in Google Ads for a B2B account
source_title: Paid Search Playbook 2

name: Use SEO Research Tools Before Writing Blog Content
then_action: Conduct keyword research using Ahrefs, SurferSEO, or Clearscope before creating the outline or draft
if_condition: If a blog post is being planned and requires organic search visibility
source_title: How to Write a Blog Post According to How We Do it at Buffer Writing toolkit

name: Allow 9+ Month Timeline for AI Citation Authority to Compound
then_action: Do not conclude the strategy is failing — AI citation authority compounds over time, and topic ownership in LLM models builds progressively, making early results an unreliable signal of long-term performance
if_condition: If evaluating AEO campaign results within fewer than 9 months of implementation
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs Why This Worked

name: Messy Page Structure Discounts Content Quality for AI Retrieval
then_action: Flag the structural issues as a multiplier problem — poor page structure will discount the value of all content work on that page for LLM citation purposes, even if the content itself is high quality
if_condition: If a page has disorganized H-tag hierarchy, unclean URLs, or illogical internal linking
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs Why This Worked

name: Use Keyword Research Regardless of Ranking Goals
then_action: Still conduct keyword research using tools like Ahrefs or Semrush to inform topic framing and content relevance
if_condition: If a blog post is being written even when the primary goal is not to rank high in search engines
source_title: How to Write a Blog Post According to How We Do it at Buffer How to write a blog post in 6 steps

name: Structure Content for LLM Retrieval Over SEO Signals
then_action: Then restructure content specifically for how language models retrieve and cite information — prioritize topical authority, extraction-friendly formatting, and comprehensive reference documents over keyword density, backlinks, and crawl budget optimization
if_condition: If the goal is to grow AI-referred traffic (e.g., from ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude) rather than traditional organic search traffic
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs The Data First

name: Track HIRO and CAC Instead of CPL
then_action: Replace CPL and raw conversions with cost per HIRO (High-Intent Revenue Opportunity) and CAC to accurately reflect pipeline impact and avoid optimizing toward low-value conversions
if_condition: If B2B paid search performance is being reported using CPL or raw conversion volume as primary KPIs
source_title: Paid Search Playbook

name: Start Keyword Research Before Content Creation
then_action: Then conduct thorough keyword research using Ahrefs first to identify primary and secondary keywords before any writing begins
if_condition: If a how-to or tutorial blog post is being planned for publication
source_title: How to Write a Blog Post According to How We Do it at Buffer Keyword research with Ahrefs

name: Evaluate Keyword Viability Using Three-Factor Analysis
then_action: Then assess viability by analyzing all three factors together: search intent alignment, competition level, and the combination of search volume with keyword difficulty — only proceed if all three factors support targeting that keyword
if_condition: If a candidate keyword has been identified via Ahrefs
source_title: How to Write a Blog Post According to How We Do it at Buffer Keyword research with Ahrefs

name: SEO Content Score Check Before Publishing
then_action: Revisit Surfer SEO to check the content score and make necessary adjustments to improve search performance before scheduling
if_condition: If a blog post is SEO-focused and is in the drafting or pre-publish stage
source_title: How to Write a Blog Post According to How We Do it at Buffer Turn outline to fleshed out draft

name: Use SurferSEO to Guide Content Optimization
then_action: Then use SurferSEO to generate content optimization recommendations based on top-performing pages for those keywords, and use Surfer's Chrome extension to build out article headers aligned with those recommendations
if_condition: If target keywords have been confirmed and content drafting is about to begin
source_title: How to Write a Blog Post According to How We Do it at Buffer Keyword research with Ahrefs

name: Internal Link Intent Alignment Requirement
then_action: Only add internal links that match the search intent of the article; for listicles, prioritize internal links that are idea lists or platform-related content
if_condition: If internal links are being added to a blog post draft
source_title: How to Write a Blog Post According to How We Do it at Buffer Turn outline to fleshed out draft

name: Target Surfer's Recommended Word Count and Content Score
then_action: Then aim to meet Surfer's recommended ideal word count and achieve Surfer's recommended overall content score to align with top-performing pages for that keyword
if_condition: If writing a blog post that has been set up in SurferSEO with a target keyword
source_title: How to Write a Blog Post According to How We Do it at Buffer Keyword research with Ahrefs

name: Topic Cluster Depth Over Volume for LLM Citation
then_action: Build 3–4 deeply structured pages per topic cluster with 2,500+ word comprehensive deep dives rather than publishing multiple shorter pieces, as depth signals topic ownership to LLMs
if_condition: If planning content strategy to increase LLM citation rates on a core topic area
source_title: How We Grew AI Referred Traffic 5 6 in 9 Months   Refine Labs Why This Worked

name: Build Brand Before Demand Capture
then_action: Then prioritize Brand-building activities first to create awareness and preference upstream, so that when buyers enter an active search, the company is already on their shortlist — skipping Brand means competing only on captured demand where trust is already formed elsewhere
if_condition: If a B2B company is investing primarily in demand capture channels (paid search, retargeting, outbound) without a Brand investment that establishes preference before buyers begin searching
source_title: Megan Bowen on National TV The Future of B2B Marketing Starts Here

name: Do Not Count Content Downloads as Meaningful Conversions
then_action: Remove content downloads from primary conversion reporting when their pipeline impact is low; in audits, over 30% of reported conversions were content downloads with negligible pipeline contribution
if_condition: If ebook or whitepaper downloads are being reported as conversions and used to evaluate campaign performance or justify spend
source_title: Paid Search Playbook

name: Apply Negative Keywords for Irrelevant Traffic
then_action: Add these terms as negative keywords to eliminate low-intent, irrelevant traffic and reclaim 20–40% of wasted budget for high-intent campaigns
if_condition: If a B2B paid search account is receiving traffic from queries containing terms like 'free', 'careers', 'templates', or 'examples'
source_title: Paid Search Playbook 2

name: Apply Negative Keyword Lists to Cut Waste
then_action: Then apply structured negative keyword lists (at minimum five categories) to eliminate irrelevant traffic, as accounts without this control can waste over $330K on zero-conversion campaigns
if_condition: If a B2B paid search account shows campaigns generating spend with zero high-intent conversions such as demos, trials, quotes, or sales consults
source_title: Paid Search Playbook

name: Do Not Report Content Downloads as Meaningful Conversions
then_action: Then remove or reclassify these as low-value signals, since audits show over 30% of reported conversions can be content downloads with near-zero pipeline contribution
if_condition: If ebook or whitepaper downloads are being counted as conversions in B2B paid search reporting without verifying their pipeline impact
source_title: Paid Search Playbook

name: AI-Powered IP Roadmap Generation
then_action: Then the system integrates with ChatGPT to generate a personalized IP roadmap and UI navigation path supported by Vault resources derived from 200+ B2B customer engagements
if_condition: If a B2B demand generation marketer submits a query to DemandGPT about specific marketing concepts or strategy needs
source_title: Refine Labs Announces DemandGPT Custom AI Assistant for Demand Gen Marketing

name: Connect CRM and Import HIROs and Closed-Won
then_action: Connect the CRM to Google Ads and import High-Intent Revenue Opportunities (HIROs) and Closed-Won deals as offline conversions to enable value-based optimization
if_condition: If a B2B Google Ads account is not connected to a CRM or is missing offline conversion imports
source_title: Paid Search Playbook 2

name: LinkedIn Analytics Tracking for Social Media Performance
then_action: Track total posts, clicks per post, interactions per post, and total traffic from LinkedIn using both Google Analytics and LinkedIn Page Analytics to measure performance impact
if_condition: If LinkedIn is scaled to increased posting frequency (three times per day weekdays, once per day weekends)
source_title: Inside Buffer s Social Media Strategy Stats Snapchat buffersnaps

name: Social Channel Traffic Attribution Measurement
then_action: Then measure traffic to the blog from each social channel separately and track weekly follower growth counts alongside average likes and comments per post to build a complete performance picture
if_condition: If evaluating the effectiveness of social media channels in driving website or blog traffic
source_title: Inside Buffer s Social Media Strategy Stats What we ve learned and our strategy for the future 2

name: Instagram Comment Reply Requirement
then_action: Reply to comments on Instagram posts to improve performance relative to baseline; failing to do so reduces algorithmic reach and engagement signals
if_condition: If an Instagram post receives comments and the account owner has not replied to those comments
source_title: Instagram   Buffer Blog Posts

name: Instagram Analytics Monitoring Requirement
then_action: Monitor specific Instagram analytics metrics (reach, impressions, engagement rate, follower growth) using recommended tools to track growth effectively
if_condition: If managing an Instagram account for growth or marketing purposes
source_title: Instagram   Buffer Blog Posts

name: Separate Organic vs Paid Reach Tracking
then_action: Then track organic reach and paid reach separately to avoid inflated or misleading performance conclusions — combined reach figures obscure true organic content performance
if_condition: If reporting on Facebook reach metrics that include both boosted posts and organic content
source_title: Inside Buffer s Social Media Strategy Stats 2

name: Data-Driven Posting Time Optimization
then_action: Optimize posting times based on data analysis of millions of posts and account-specific analytics to maximize initial engagement velocity and algorithmic distribution
if_condition: If scheduling Instagram posts and posting time has not been optimized based on audience activity data
source_title: Instagram   Buffer Blog Posts

name: Post Stories at Followers' Most Active Times
then_action: Then post Stories during those identified peak active times to maximize reach and engagement rewards from the Instagram algorithm
if_condition: If you are scheduling Instagram Stories and have access to the Professional Dashboard showing follower activity peaks by day and time
source_title: Instagram Stories The Complete Guide to Using Stories to Boost Engagement Reach Instagram Story anal 2

name: Podcast Attribution Blind Spot in Software Tracking
then_action: Then treat this as a signal of attribution failure rather than poor podcast performance — self-reported attribution data may reveal podcast is actually driving a substantial share of closed won revenue (e.g., 53% in the Refine Labs study) that software tools cannot capture
if_condition: If software-based attribution reports 0% or near-zero contribution from podcast channels while podcasts are an active demand creation investment
source_title: Refine Labs Study Confirms Measurement Gap in Software Based Attribution Releases Hybrid Attribution

name: Implement Hybrid Attribution for Dark Social Channels
then_action: Then implement a Hybrid Attribution Framework that combines software-based tracking with first-party customer-declared intent data, as software-based attribution alone will produce a ~90% measurement gap and attribute 0% to channels like podcasts that may actually drive the majority of closed won revenue
if_condition: If a B2B revenue team is measuring demand creation programs that include dark social channels such as podcasts, social media, word of mouth, or communities using only software-based attribution
source_title: Refine Labs Study Confirms Measurement Gap in Software Based Attribution Releases Hybrid Attribution

name: Post-Takeover Metrics Comparison Required
then_action: Compare post-takeover metrics against the baseline metrics established in the goal-setting phase to measure success of the collaboration
if_condition: If an Instagram takeover campaign has been completed
source_title: Instagram Takeover How to Host a Takeover in 6 Easy Steps 6 Wrap up

name: Flag Dark Social as Primary Conversion Source
then_action: Classify these as dark social conversions; recognize they represent the highest-quality pipeline opportunities and prioritize budget toward these channels even though software attribution cannot track them
if_condition: If self-reported attribution data shows high mentions of social networks, communities, podcasts, word-of-mouth, DMs, or events as conversion sources
source_title: The Attribution Mirage What s the Impact of The Attribution Mirage Part 1

name: Optimize LinkedIn Profile for Visibility and Trust
then_action: Create and optimize a compelling LinkedIn profile to enhance online visibility and build audience trust
if_condition: If a LinkedIn profile lacks compelling content such as a strong headline, summary, or relevant experience details
source_title: LinkedIn   Buffer Blog Posts

name: Respond to Google Reviews for Consumer Trust
then_action: Respond to all Google reviews, as 88% of consumers choose businesses that respond to reviews over those that do not
if_condition: If a business receives Google reviews and has not responded to them
source_title: News and Trends   Buffer Blog Posts

name: Peer-Level Relationship Building for Twitter Growth
then_action: Prioritize establishing relationships with accounts of similar follower count, as peers are less likely to ignore outreach and these early connections become long-term partners as both accounts grow
if_condition: If a Twitter account is in early growth phase and attempting to build relationships with larger accounts but receiving no engagement back
source_title: How I Added 44 716 Twitter Followers in 18 Weeks You cannot grow predictably on Twitter without a pr 2

name: Secure Personal Domain for Personal Professional Brand
then_action: Then they MUST secure yourname.com (or a very similar domain) as the home base for their personal professional brand to establish a distinct online identity
if_condition: If a small business owner is building a personal professional brand separate from their business brand
source_title: Personal Brand Meet Business Brand How Small Business Owners Can Make Them Work Together Creating Yo

name: Add List to Idea Pin to Increase Accessibility and Context
then_action: Add a list even though it is optional, as it increases accessibility for users and provides Pinterest with additional context signals that improve content discoverability
if_condition: If publishing a Pinterest Idea Pin and deciding whether to include a list
source_title: Pinterest Idea Pins The complete guide to inspiring your followers Step 7 Add a list to your Idea Pi

name: Use Keywords in Pinterest Idea Pin Description for Searchability
then_action: Include relevant keywords that clearly describe what the Pin is about to improve searchability and help Pinterest understand the content context
if_condition: If creating a Pinterest Idea Pin and writing the caption or description
source_title: Pinterest Idea Pins The complete guide to inspiring your followers Step 7 Add a list to your Idea Pi

name: Match List Type to Idea Pin Content Category
then_action: Select 'Ingredients' for cooking content, 'Supplies' for craft/DIY content, or 'Notes' for all other categories to maximize relevance and Pinterest context signals
if_condition: If adding a list to a Pinterest Idea Pin and the content is cooking, crafts, or another category
source_title: Pinterest Idea Pins The complete guide to inspiring your followers Step 7 Add a list to your Idea Pi

name: Account for Multi-Source Attribution in Self-Reported Responses
then_action: Record all mentioned sources rather than forcing single-touch attribution; multi-source answers reflect the actual non-linear buyer journey and should inform multi-channel investment decisions
if_condition: If self-reported attribution responses mention multiple channels or touchpoints in a single answer
source_title: The Attribution Mirage What s the Impact of The Attribution Mirage Part 1

name: Assign Self-Reported Attribution to Early-Stage Awareness Signals
then_action: Then this first-touch signal should be captured via self-reported attribution methods, as software-based tools cannot detect or record these dark social interactions that influence the Awareness and Interest stages of the Buyer's Journey
if_condition: If a buyer first learns about a vendor solution through peers, social connections, podcasts, or professional communities rather than a tracked digital touchpoint
source_title: The Attribution Mirage What s the Impact of The Attribution Mirage Part 3

name: Expand LinkedIn Title Beyond Business Owner Label
then_action: Then they SHOULD add descriptors beyond 'Owner of Company' that communicate who they are and what they are best at, to strengthen their personal professional brand signal
if_condition: If a small business owner's LinkedIn title only states their role as 'Owner of [Company]'
source_title: Personal Brand Meet Business Brand How Small Business Owners Can Make Them Work Together Creating Yo

name: Unified Brand Exception for Thought Leaders
then_action: Then maintaining a single unified personal-business brand may be more appropriate than keeping them separate
if_condition: If the small business owner is an author, speaker, coach, or thought leader where their personal identity IS the product or service
source_title: Personal Brand Meet Business Brand How Small Business Owners Can Make Them Work Together Creating Yo

name: Pinterest Idea Pin Title Must Inspire Curiosity and Match Cover Photo
then_action: The title must inspire curiosity in the target market AND align with the text displayed on the cover photo — a mismatched or generic title reduces discoverability and click-through intent
if_condition: When publishing a Pinterest Idea Pin and adding a title
source_title: Pinterest Idea Pins The complete guide to inspiring your followers Step 6 Add a title choose a Pinte

name: Open Text Field for Self-Reported Attribution
then_action: Then use a mandatory open text-field with no drop-down menus or suggestions, asking prospects to describe in their own words how they became aware of your company (e.g. 'How did you hear about us?')
if_condition: If adding a self-reported attribution question to intake forms or lead capture forms
source_title: The Attribution Mirage What s the Impact of The Attribution Mirage Part 2

name: Avoid Sole Reliance on Software Attribution
then_action: Then attribution data will be severely misleading — software may show search as the primary conversion source (e.g. 79%) while self-reported data reveals Dark Social or awareness channels drove the majority of closed revenue (e.g. 98%), causing misallocation of budget and strategy
if_condition: If software-based attribution is the only method used to measure marketing performance and revenue sourcing
source_title: The Attribution Mirage What s the Impact of The Attribution Mirage Part 2

name: Claim Website to Enable Link Attribution on Pinterest Pins
then_action: Then they must claim their website through Pinterest account settings to activate link attribution across all Pins
if_condition: If a Pinterest business account owner wants their website link to appear on all their Pins and under their name
source_title: Pinterest Idea Pins The complete guide to inspiring your followers Step 1 Create or convert to a Pin

name: Avoid Biasing Self-Reported Responses
then_action: Then self-reported attribution data will be biased and inaccurate, failing to capture true unaided recall of how prospects became aware of the company
if_condition: If intake forms use drop-down menus or suggested options for the 'How did you hear about us?' attribution question
source_title: The Attribution Mirage What s the Impact of The Attribution Mirage Part 2

name: Dual Attribution Measurement Requirement
then_action: Then implement two parallel attribution layers: (1) Capture Demand Attribution using software-based tools to track digital interactions, and (2) Create Demand Attribution using self-reported data to track true awareness origins
if_condition: If a B2B company is measuring marketing attribution using only software-based tracking tools
source_title: The Attribution Mirage What s the Impact of The Attribution Mirage Part 2

name: Classify Freeform Attribution Responses
then_action: Then automatically classify freeform answers into categories for analysis, then combine the classified self-reported data with software-based attribution data to produce a hybrid attribution view
if_condition: If collecting freeform open-text self-reported attribution responses at scale
source_title: The Attribution Mirage What s the Impact of The Attribution Mirage Part 2

name: LinkedIn Link Post Click Tracking Requirement
then_action: Track clicks and click-through rates using a URL shortener tool such as Short.io in addition to standard engagement metrics, to accurately measure post performance beyond platform-native analytics
if_condition: If a LinkedIn post contains an external link
source_title: Reverse engineering LinkedIn success My Reverse Engineering Strategy

name: Optimize Site Architecture for SEO During Rebrand
then_action: Include site architecture optimization for SEO as part of the redesign process to improve user experience and organic search performance simultaneously
if_condition: If a site rebrand or redesign is being undertaken
source_title: Why I Changed My Entire Brand and what you can learn from it Conclusion

name: Implement Pillar Page Architecture for SEO
then_action: Then create high-authority pillar pages for each core topic that act as hubs with internal links connecting to all related articles on that subject, mirroring structures used by high-authority sites like James Clear and Mark Manson
if_condition: If a website covers multiple core topics and is being structured or restructured for SEO performance
source_title: Why I Changed My Entire Brand and what you can learn from it Why make the effort And what can you le

name: Use Architecture-Knowledgeable Web Development Team
then_action: Then require that the team demonstrates understanding of SEO-friendly site architecture and internal linking structures, not just visual design capabilities, as architecture is a critical layer beyond website appearance
if_condition: If selecting a web development team for an SEO-driven website build or redesign
source_title: Why I Changed My Entire Brand and what you can learn from it Why make the effort And what can you le

name: Build SEO-Focused Platform as Social Media Hedge
then_action: Then invest in building an SEO-focused website platform that goes deep on a handful of core topics, to protect the business against the risk of social media platforms changing algorithms or disappearing
if_condition: If a creator's primary audience and revenue are dependent on social media platforms
source_title: Why I Changed My Entire Brand and what you can learn from it Why make the effort And what can you le

name: Author Page Content Depth Rule
then_action: Flag the page as low-information for entity or expertise extraction; do not use it as a primary source for topical authority mapping or AEO signals
if_condition: If a page is an author archive page containing only metadata about the author (name, experience duration, topic tags) without actual article content or substantive expertise signals
source_title: Elizabeth Author at OpenView

name: Author Entity Association Rule
then_action: Map the author entity to those topic clusters as topical signals for the parent domain, and associate the contributor with the organization's content authority in those areas
if_condition: If an author page explicitly lists recurring topic clusters (e.g. marketing, customer success, churn management, platform strategies) alongside a named contributor and a specific domain
source_title: Elizabeth Author at OpenView

name: SaaS Contributor Expertise Scoring Rule
then_action: Assign moderate-to-high topical authority score for SaaS business content; treat authored articles as credible secondary sources for AEO and content gap analysis
if_condition: If an author at a SaaS-focused domain (e.g. openviewpartners.com) has approximately 3 or more years of documented contribution covering startup strategy, sales, and customer success topics
source_title: Elizabeth Author at OpenView

name: Periodic Website Infrastructure Renovation for Growth Stage Alignment
then_action: Renovate website content and infrastructure to align with the new lifecycle stage, building a scalable web information structure capable of handling continued rapid growth
if_condition: When an expansion stage software company has recently completed a venture capital investment round or is experiencing rapidly increasing customer acquisition and market buzz
source_title: How expansion stage software companies can scale their websites successfully   OpenView

name: Vanity Metrics Context Requirement
then_action: Flag these as vanity metrics that lack actionable insight; require additional contextual metrics to be included alongside them to make the data meaningful for business decisions
if_condition: If a dashboard or report includes metrics like 'website hits', 'page views', 'sign-ups', 'visitors', or 'downloads' without accompanying context such as cancellation rates, bounce rates, or conversion data
source_title: Metrics   Useful tools or Vanity traps   OpenView

name: Prioritize Owned and Earned Channels Over Paid Channels
then_action: Prioritize building owned and earned channels such as virality, word of mouth, and user-generated content before investing in SEO, SEM, or paid social channels
if_condition: If a growth team is selecting which acquisition channels to build or invest in
source_title: 10 growth tactics that never work

name: Newsletter Subscriber-Gated Content Signal
then_action: Flag that the content may not be fully indexable by search engines due to access restrictions, reducing organic SEO value and requiring alternative distribution strategies
if_condition: If a webpage is a members-only or subscriber-gated newsletter edition aggregating community Slack discussions
source_title: Community Wisdom Communicating pricing increases to customers explaining AI limitations to new hires

name: Treat Growth as an Interconnected System
then_action: Evaluate the downstream impact on all three levers (acquisition, retention, monetization) before implementing, since changing one component affects the others; model growth as a system, not independent channels
if_condition: When a growth team changes acquisition, retention, or monetization strategy in isolation
source_title: Brian Balfour 10 lessons on career growth and life

name: Avoid Universal Audience Targeting in Growth Strategy
then_action: Reject the approach; solving for everyone means solving for no one. Narrow the target audience to a specific use case and user segment to enable effective product and growth decisions
if_condition: When a growth or product strategy attempts to address all user segments or solve for every potential customer simultaneously
source_title: Brian Balfour 10 lessons on career growth and life

name: Ecosystem Strategy: Use Intermediaries Instead of Direct Outreach
then_action: Then go through intermediaries (influencers, channel partners, developers, communities) who already have established access and trust with your target audience, rather than approaching prospects directly
if_condition: If traditional B2B growth channels (organic search, email outreach, paid ads) are showing declining returns or saturated engagement metrics
source_title: Ecosystem is the next big growth channel   by Emily Kramer

name: Enterprise AI Pilot Failure Risk: Avoiding Friction
then_action: Then the pilot is at high risk of failure (95% failure rate per MIT research); redesign the implementation to confront and resolve the friction points instead
if_condition: If an enterprise AI pilot is designed to route around existing organizational friction or workflow resistance rather than addressing it directly
source_title: First interview with Scale AI s CEO 14B Meta deal what s working in enterprise AI and what frontier

name: AI Model Improvement Requires Ongoing Human Expert Collaboration
then_action: Then the model will degrade in usefulness over time; implement ongoing human expert feedback loops to sustain and improve model performance
if_condition: If an AI model is deployed in an enterprise context without a structured process for domain experts to review, correct, and continuously improve model outputs
source_title: First interview with Scale AI s CEO 14B Meta deal what s working in enterprise AI and what frontier

name: Enterprise Data Readiness Assessment Before AI Training
then_action: Then flag this as a critical risk; enterprise data requires significant cleaning, structuring, and preparation before it is useful for AI training, and skipping this step will produce poor model outcomes
if_condition: If an organization assumes its existing enterprise data can be fed directly into AI model training pipelines without preparation or curation
source_title: First interview with Scale AI s CEO 14B Meta deal what s working in enterprise AI and what frontier

name: Product Hunt Live Upvote Embed Requirement
then_action: MUST embed a live upvote counter widget on your webpage during launch day — this drives real-time engagement signals visible to both visitors and the algorithm
if_condition: If running a Product Hunt launch campaign on launch day
source_title: How to successfully launch on Product Hunt when it s right for your startup The insider s guide to w 3

name: Product Hunt Day Selection for Traffic vs Rank Tradeoff
then_action: Schedule launch on Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday — these days have the highest traffic metrics despite having the most competition and lower ranking potential
if_condition: If launching on Product Hunt and primary goal is maximum incoming traffic (accepting lower rank placement)
source_title: How to successfully launch on Product Hunt when it s right for your startup The insider s guide to w 3

name: Product Hunt Weekend Launch for #1 Rank Probability
then_action: Schedule launch on Saturday or Sunday — weekends offer the absolute best chance to rank #1 due to lowest competition, despite generating less traffic than weekdays
if_condition: If launching on Product Hunt and primary goal is achieving #1 rank for brand awareness, social proof, or credibility (accepting lower traffic volume)
source_title: How to successfully launch on Product Hunt when it s right for your startup The insider s guide to w 3

name: Product Hunt Monday/Friday Launch for Balanced Rank and Engagement
then_action: Schedule launch on Monday or Friday — these days offer significantly lower competition than Tue-Thu while maintaining strong engagement metrics
if_condition: If launching on Product Hunt and goal is a higher rank than mid-week allows but more traffic than weekends provide
source_title: How to successfully launch on Product Hunt when it s right for your startup The insider s guide to w 3

name: Product Hunt Launch Date Lock-In Requirement
then_action: MUST commit to that launch date immediately — the date cannot be changed once the page is live, so ranking goals must be set and finalized before this moment
if_condition: If the Product Hunt launch page goes live at 12:01 a.m. on launch day
source_title: How to successfully launch on Product Hunt when it s right for your startup The insider s guide to w 3

name: Product Hunt High-Karma Upvoter Prioritization
then_action: Prioritize users with high karma scores — their upvotes, reviews, and comments carry significantly more weight in Product Hunt's ranking algorithm than low-karma users
if_condition: If building a pre-launch outreach list of Product Hunt community members to notify on launch day
source_title: How to successfully launch on Product Hunt when it s right for your startup The insider s guide to w 3

name: Product Hunt Vote Spike Algorithm Flag Risk
then_action: AVOID sudden vote spikes — the ranking algorithm flags unnatural engagement velocity, which can suppress ranking; distribute vote outreach gradually throughout the day
if_condition: If coordinating upvote campaigns for a Product Hunt launch and votes arrive in sudden concentrated bursts
source_title: How to successfully launch on Product Hunt when it s right for your startup The insider s guide to w 3

name: MMM Requires Aggregate Data Only
then_action: Switch to aggregate data only; MMM must work independently of platform analytics tools and individual user data to avoid privacy concerns and fill tracking gaps caused by limitations like iOS 14
if_condition: If building a Marketing Mix Model (MMM) and individual user-level or platform-specific tracking data is being used as primary input
source_title: How today s top consumer brands measure marketing s impact 2 MMM Marketing mix modeling

name: Channel Randomization Limitation for Non-Digital Channels
then_action: Then recognize that individual-level randomization is technically not possible for these channels; switch to geo-level experiment design instead
if_condition: If attempting to run individual-level randomization for channels such as billboards or organic search rankings
source_title: How today s top consumer brands measure marketing s impact 3 CLS Conversion lift studies

name: Conduct Willingness-to-Pay Conversations Before Setting Pricing
then_action: Conduct willingness-to-pay conversations and pricing surveys with customers before finalizing pricing features to ensure alignment with actual customer value perception
if_condition: When determining pricing tiers or deciding which features to include in paid vs. free plans
source_title: M A competition pricing and investing

name: Evaluate Companies on Four Key Dimensions Before M&A Assessment
then_action: Evaluate the company on four key dimensions (product-market fit, distribution strategy, market position, and operational maturity) before proceeding with acquisition or investment decisions
if_condition: When assessing a company for M&A potential or investment consideration
source_title: M A competition pricing and investing

name: Communicate Transparently When Selling Your Company
then_action: Communicate transparently with all stakeholders about the sale process, and consider being public about selling to leverage network effects and existing connections during the acquisition process
if_condition: When a startup founder or executive is in the process of selling their company to stakeholders including employees, investors, and acquirers
source_title: M A competition pricing and investing

name: Confirm Product-Market Fit Before Pursuing M&A
then_action: First identify whether the company has achieved product-market fit before pursuing M&A, then develop distribution strategies aligned with your market position
if_condition: When a company is considering pursuing M&A activity either as acquirer or acquisition target
source_title: M A competition pricing and investing

name: Prioritize Functionality Over Perfection for Infrastructure Products
then_action: Apply the 'worse is better' philosophy by prioritizing functionality and shipping over perfection — focus on shipping, learning, and iterating rather than pursuing perfect solutions; treat tech debt as a 'champagne problem' secondary to core functionality
if_condition: When building data and infrastructure products and facing a choice between shipping a functional solution versus delaying for a perfect solution
source_title: M A competition pricing and investing

name: Use Offsite Exercises to Help Teams Internalize Organizational Changes
then_action: Use offsite exercises designed to help teams internalize and process upcoming changes, particularly when working with strongly opinionated users or teams that require different communication approaches
if_condition: When a team is facing significant upcoming changes such as M&A transitions, pricing model shifts, or strategic pivots
source_title: M A competition pricing and investing

name: Define Open Source vs. Paid Feature Boundaries Strategically
then_action: Strategically determine open source vs. paid feature boundaries based on customer willingness-to-pay data and distribution strategy, ensuring core functionality remains accessible while monetizing enterprise or advanced capabilities
if_condition: When a data or infrastructure company with an open source product needs to determine which features to keep free versus monetize

name: B2B Organic Growth as PMF Signal
then_action: Treat it as a signal of strong product-market fit and prioritize scaling this channel before investing heavily in outbound or paid channels
if_condition: If a B2B startup is experiencing strong organic inbound growth without paid acquisition

name: New Channel Exploration for Scaling B2B Companies
then_action: Evaluate the remaining channels (e.g., Content/SEO, partnerships) as potential new growth opportunities rather than attempting to run all channels simultaneously
if_condition: If a B2B company is already growing primarily through one of the first three channels (self-serve PLG, sales-assist inbound, or outbound sales)
source_title: Scaling your growth engines   by Lenny Rachitsky How B2B startups grow as they scale Part 1

name: B2B Growth Channel Identification and Prioritization
then_action: Identify and evaluate the most effective growth channels specific to B2B startups (e.g., outbound sales, content, product-led growth, partnerships) before committing resources, using frameworks validated by successful B2B companies
if_condition: If a B2B startup is evaluating which growth channels to invest in during early scaling stages
source_title: Scaling your growth engines   by Lenny Rachitsky

name: Run Multi-Platform Paid Ads for B2B Scalability
then_action: Then run paid advertising across Google, Facebook, and LinkedIn as these platforms are validated B2B growth channels
if_condition: If a B2B company is looking to add scalable paid acquisition channels to complement organic or outbound growth
source_title: Scaling your growth engines   by Lenny Rachitsky How B2B startups grow as they scale Part 2

name: Focus Optimization on Top-Performing B2B Channel
then_action: Then concentrate most optimization efforts on that top-performing channel, as most growth will concentrate in one channel rather than being evenly distributed
if_condition: If a B2B company has identified which of the three primary channels (inbound self-service, inbound sales-assist, or outbound sales) is generating the most growth
source_title: Scaling your growth engines   by Lenny Rachitsky How B2B startups grow as they scale Part 2

name: Early-Stage Growth Channel Rule
then_action: Leverage partnerships and SEO as primary growth channels before investing in higher-cost acquisition methods
if_condition: If a product is in early-stage growth and needs to accelerate user or revenue acquisition with limited resources
source_title: Unorthodox frameworks for growing your product career and impact 2

name: AEO ChatGPT Distribution Channel Rule
then_action: Then prioritize ChatGPT as a primary distribution channel investment, positioning content and integrations to gain visibility and referral traffic from AI-driven recommendations
if_condition: If a B2B company is evaluating new growth channels and ChatGPT is demonstrably entering a third-party platform phase based on platform cycle signals
source_title: Why ChatGPT will be the next big growth channel and how to capitalize on it

name: ChatGPT Platform Integration Urgency Rule
then_action: Then the company must commit to a ChatGPT platform bet within six months or risk losing competitive positioning to early movers, as platform shifts create winner-take-all dynamics
if_condition: If a company has not yet integrated with ChatGPT and competitors are beginning to establish ChatGPT-based distribution channels
source_title: Why ChatGPT will be the next big growth channel and how to capitalize on it

name: Prioritize ChatGPT/AEO as Emerging Distribution Channel
then_action: Invest in AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) and ChatGPT discoverability as the next major distribution opportunity, positioning content and products to appear in AI-generated responses before the channel matures and closes
if_condition: If you are evaluating new distribution channels for the AI era and most existing platforms are in late-stage monetization phases

name: Prioritize ChatGPT/AEO as Emerging Distribution Opportunity
then_action: Prioritize investment in AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) and ChatGPT discovery presence, and monitor for a potential developer platform opportunity — this is identified as the next major distribution channel before Phase 3 monetization closes it
if_condition: If a product is evaluating next-generation distribution channels and ChatGPT is still in its open ecosystem phase (Phase 2)
source_title: The Four Fits A Growth Framework for the AI Era Channel

name: Low Engagement Rate Ranking: Refine Targeting
then_action: Refine targeting to reach people who are more likely to engage with the ad content
if_condition: If a Facebook ad receives a low engagement rate ranking compared to other ads targeting the same audience

name: Regular Targeting Updates Required for Sustained Relevance

name: Low Conversion Rate Ranking: Target Higher-Intent Audience
then_action: Target a higher-intent audience, such as selecting 'engaged shoppers' under purchase behavior to reach users who clicked Shop Now within the last week
if_condition: If a Facebook ad receives a low conversion rate ranking compared to other ads targeting the same audience
source_title: 10 Facebook ad targeting tips to improve conversions 4

name: Lookalike Audience Size: Smaller Is More Precise
then_action: Select a smaller audience size percentage to more precisely match the characteristics of the source audience, improving targeting relevance

name: A/B Test Advantage+ Against Other Audience Selections
then_action: SHOULD run A/B tests comparing Advantage+ with other audience selections to measure relative effectiveness
if_condition: If deploying Advantage+ audience targeting in a new or existing campaign
source_title: 10 Facebook ad targeting tips to improve conversions 7

name: Target Life Event Audiences for Relevant Offers
then_action: Add life event targeting layers (recently moved, new job, engaged, married, birthday month, anniversary) to prioritize users whose current circumstances make them most likely to convert
if_condition: If a Facebook ad campaign promotes products or services closely tied to personal milestones (e.g., home goods, gifts, financial products, travel)

name: Layer Audience Targeting with Narrow Audience Feature
then_action: Use the Narrow Audience feature under Detailed Targeting to add additional targeting layers combining demographics, interests, and behaviors, starting with 'Engaged Shoppers' as a base behavior segment
if_condition: If a Facebook ad set is targeting a broad audience using a single demographic, interest, or behavior criterion and conversion rates are below target
source_title: 10 Facebook ad targeting tips to improve conversions 5

name: Advantage+ Age Restriction Compliance
then_action: MUST NOT target users under 18 globally, under 20 in Thailand, or under 21 in Indonesia
if_condition: If using Meta Advantage+ audience targeting in a campaign
source_title: 10 Facebook ad targeting tips to improve conversions 7

name: Avoid Advantage+ for Remarketing Campaigns
then_action: AVOID using Advantage+ audience targeting; use manual audience targeting instead
if_condition: If the campaign objective is remarketing to existing customers or past website visitors
source_title: 10 Facebook ad targeting tips to improve conversions 7

name: Use Advantage+ Shopping or App Campaigns for Sales/App Promotion
then_action: SHOULD use Advantage+ shopping campaigns or Advantage+ app campaigns specifically
if_condition: If the campaign objective is driving sales or promoting an app
source_title: 10 Facebook ad targeting tips to improve conversions 7

name: Provide Broad Audience for Advantage+ Optimization
then_action: Start with a broad audience to allow the AI to find best matches; narrow targeting reduces Advantage+ effectiveness
if_condition: If configuring audience parameters for an Advantage+ campaign
source_title: 10 Facebook ad targeting tips to improve conversions 7

name: UTM Parameters Required on All Social Links
then_action: Add UTM parameters to all social links to enable accurate attribution and bring more useful data into Google Analytics
if_condition: If sharing any link to external content or landing pages via social media posts

name: Follower Accumulation Is No Longer a Valid Social Media Goal
then_action: Avoid follower accumulation as a primary metric since current social algorithms no longer reward high follower counts; shift focus to consistent, relevant content performance instead
if_condition: If setting social media KPIs or strategy goals focused primarily on growing follower count

name: Tag Physical Location for Local Discovery
then_action: Tag the physical location on posts and Stories so content appears in location-based searches and exploration, functioning as the Instagram equivalent of local SEO
if_condition: If the Instagram account represents a local business or is promoting a physical event with a specific location

name: Verified Account Commenting Amplification
then_action: Then the verified account's comments appear at the top of comment sections, increasing profile click likelihood and amplifying engagement; pair verification with proactive commenting on high-traffic creators for maximum reach effect
if_condition: If a brand has Instagram verification and comments on popular creators' posts
source_title: 21 Instagram best practices for ads and organic content 2025 3

name: Buffer Tool Selection for Simple Scheduling Teams
then_action: Then recommend Buffer as it is purpose-built for quick content scheduling with simple post performance tracking including Stories analytics, starting at $6/month per channel
if_condition: If a small business owner, solo social media manager, or agency needs to schedule and publish content across Instagram, Facebook, X, TikTok, Pinterest, or LinkedIn without complex workflows

name: LinkedIn Page Analytics Access Requirement
then_action: Then the user must be a LinkedIn Page admin; non-admins cannot access the analytics tool and will be blocked from all tracking features
if_condition: If a user attempts to access LinkedIn Page Analytics to track content, followers, visitors, leads, or competitors

name: LinkedIn Analytics Competitor Monitoring
then_action: Then use LinkedIn Page Analytics competitor tracking feature to monitor competitor activity and inform strategic positioning decisions
if_condition: If a LinkedIn Page admin needs to benchmark performance against competitors on LinkedIn
source_title: 21 social media analytics tools to boost your strategy in 2026 21 LinkedIn Page Analytics

name: Instagram Reels Algorithmic Expansion Logic
then_action: Instagram algorithm will progressively expand the Reel's reach to larger audiences; optimize content specifically to maximize these three engagement signals
if_condition: If a newly published Instagram Reel receives high watch time, likes, and shares from its initial small audience group
source_title: 21 Instagram best practices for ads and organic content 2025

name: Select Metrics Aligned to Specific Social Goals
then_action: Then track only the metrics relevant to those goals — such as reach and impressions for awareness goals, hashtag performance for campaign tracking, or video views for video content objectives
if_condition: If specific social media goals have been defined (e.g., brand awareness, engagement, lead generation)
source_title: How to Build Your Social Media Marketing Strategy None Part 6

name: Aucune pénalité Instagram pour les Reels créés avec des outils externes
then_action: Alors savoir qu'Instagram ne pénalise pas la portée ou la distribution des Reels réalisés avec des outils externes, donc utiliser librement ces outils sans crainte d'impact algorithmique négatif
if_condition: Si vous créez des Reels Instagram avec des applications tierces comme CapCut ou Canva

name: Utiliser des outils de suivi de liens pour identifier les sources de trafic social
then_action: Utilisez des outils dédiés aux liens de suivi (ex: UTM parameters) pour identifier les sources de trafic provenant des réseaux sociaux
if_condition: Si vous souhaitez relier précisément vos efforts sur les médias sociaux à vos résultats commerciaux (ventes, conversions)
source_title: Comment démontrer et améliorer votre ROI des médias sociaux 4

name: Avertissement sur la difficulté d'attribution précise des ventes à des publications individuelles
then_action: Ne pas conclure à l'inefficacité : la présence globale sur les réseaux sociaux peut avoir un impact supérieur à celui de publications isolées, et l'attribution directe reste difficile
if_condition: Si une publication ou campagne sociale individuelle ne génère pas de conversions directement mesurables

name: Suivi des conversions par publication
then_action: Alors mettre en place un tracking par publication pour identifier le contenu convertissant et créer davantage de contenu sur ce modèle
if_condition: Si des ventes, inscriptions ou conversions sont générées sans être attribuées à des publications précises
source_title: Comment démontrer et améliorer votre ROI des médias sociaux 3

name: Associer un identifiant unique par publication pour tracer les conversions
then_action: Associez un identifiant unique à chaque publication pour mettre en relation les données de conversion avec des activités spécifiques
if_condition: Si vous publiez du contenu sur les réseaux sociaux et souhaitez relier chaque publication à des données de conversion précises
source_title: Comment démontrer et améliorer votre ROI des médias sociaux 4

name: Comparer plusieurs campagnes pour identifier les meilleures performances ROI
then_action: Comparez les campagnes en saisissant leurs coûts et résultats respectifs pour identifier celles qui offrent le meilleur ROI
if_condition: Si vous gérez plusieurs campagnes sociales simultanément et cherchez à prioriser l'allocation de budget
source_title: Comment démontrer et améliorer votre ROI des médias sociaux 4

name: Avertissement sur le périmètre du ROI social
then_action: Alors risque de sous-estimation du ROI réel — intégrer toutes les sources de valeur générées (trafic web, SEO, notoriété de marque, conversions offline) car le ROI social peut provenir de n'importe quel canal
if_condition: Si le ROI des médias sociaux est mesuré uniquement via les métriques natives des plateformes sociales
source_title: Comment démontrer et améliorer votre ROI des médias sociaux 3

name: Intégrer Analytics pour relier réseaux sociaux aux objectifs commerciaux
then_action: Intégrez des plateformes comme Google Analytics ou Adobe Analytics pour révéler la contribution des réseaux sociaux aux résultats business
if_condition: Si vous cherchez à mesurer comment vos efforts sur les réseaux sociaux contribuent à vos objectifs commerciaux
source_title: Comment démontrer et améliorer votre ROI des médias sociaux 4

name: No penalización de Reels de Instagram por herramientas externas
then_action: Instagram NO penaliza el contenido por haber sido creado con aplicaciones externas, por lo que se pueden usar libremente sin afectar el alcance orgánico
if_condition: Si se crean Reels de Instagram utilizando herramientas externas como CapCut u otras aplicaciones de edición de video
source_title: Cómo crear un calendario de redes sociales plantilla gratuita 4

name: Integrate Social Analytics with Web Attribution Tools to Capture Full ROI
then_action: Then integrate with platforms like Google Analytics or Adobe Analytics and use link monitoring and web attribution tools with unique post identifiers to connect social efforts directly to broader business results, since ROI can originate from outside social networks
if_condition: If social media ROI measurement relies solely on native platform metrics (impressions, reach, followers, engagement rates) without connecting to business outcomes
source_title: Cómo monitorear calcular y mejorar el ROI en redes sociales 3

name: Benchmark Against Industry and Competitors for Strategic Adjustment
then_action: Then monitor up to 20 competitors per social network and compare your performance against industry benchmarks to adjust strategy based on what is already working for similar clients
if_condition: If you need to adjust your social media strategy and want external reference points beyond your own historical data
source_title: Cómo monitorear calcular y mejorar el ROI en redes sociales 3

name: UTM Parameters for Social ROI Tracking
then_action: Add UTM tags to the end of URLs to monitor exactly how much traffic a specific URL receives and attribute conversions to the correct social channel
if_condition: If a social media post or campaign URL lacks UTM parameters and you need to track traffic source attribution
source_title: Cómo monitorear calcular y mejorar el ROI en redes sociales 5

name: Instagram Hashtag Placement Rule
then_action: Move hashtags to the caption, as hiding them in comments is no longer effective and does not contribute to SEO or search visibility
if_condition: If hashtags are hidden in Instagram comments rather than included in the post caption
source_title: Social SEO Guide to more reach on social media free tool 5

name: TikTok Spoken Keyword Signal Rule
then_action: Say the target keywords out loud in the video to generate two search signals: the spoken keyword and its appearance in auto-generated captions
if_condition: If creating a TikTok video targeting specific keywords
source_title: Social SEO Guide to more reach on social media free tool 5

name: LinkedIn Page Completion Rule
then_action: Complete all available and relevant sections of the LinkedIn Page to maximize keyword opportunities and improve search discoverability
if_condition: If managing a LinkedIn Page with incomplete sections
source_title: Social SEO Guide to more reach on social media free tool 5

name: Pinterest Long-Tail Keyword Pin Rule
then_action: Build Pins around long-tail keyword variations instead (e.g., 'How to build a DIY bookcase') to better match specific user search queries and improve discoverability
if_condition: If creating Pinterest Pins targeting a broad keyword topic (e.g., 'DIY Bookcase')
source_title: Social SEO Guide to more reach on social media free tool 5

name: Use Short Highly Relevant Keywords in X Posts
then_action: Focus on short but highly relevant keywords that fit naturally into the post content rather than forced long-tail phrases
if_condition: If optimizing posts on X (Twitter) for social SEO
source_title: Social SEO Guide to more reach on social media free tool 6

name: Account for Algorithm Limitations in Reach Estimates
then_action: Then adjust estimates downward — constant algorithm and trend changes mean not all posts will reach the intended audience, affecting real-world ROI outcomes
if_condition: If social media ROI calculations assume all published posts will reach the full target audience
source_title: Cómo monitorear calcular y mejorar el ROI en redes sociales 4

name: ER Post Calculation Rule
then_action: Use formula: ER post = (Total engagements on a post / Total followers) * 100
if_condition: If measuring engagement rate for an individual social media post against total follower count
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 2 Engagement rate by post ER post Best for speci

name: Video File Name Must Use Primary Keyword
then_action: Name the video file using the primary keyword phrase (e.g., DIY-bookcase.mov) before uploading to social platforms
if_condition: If uploading a video as part of a social SEO strategy
source_title: Social SEO Guide to more reach on social media free tool 6

name: Voice Search Content Optimization
then_action: Then create content that answers specific questions and build out conversational, long-tail keyword targeting to match natural spoken query patterns
if_condition: If optimizing social content for voice search (given 20% of Google App searches and 31.5% of users using voice assistants weekly)
source_title: Social SEO Guide to more reach on social media free tool 7

name: Use ER Impressions for Paid Content with CPM Structure
then_action: Use Engagement Rate by Impressions (ER impressions = Total engagements / Total impressions * 100) as the primary metric to measure content success
if_condition: If content is paid and uses a CPM (cost per thousand impressions) cost structure
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 3 Engagement rate by impressions ER impressions

name: Calculate Average ER Impressions Across Multiple Posts
then_action: Calculate Average ER impressions = Total ER impressions / Number of posts to normalize performance comparison
if_condition: If evaluating paid content performance across more than one post using impressions-based engagement
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 3 Engagement rate by impressions ER impressions

name: Account for Impression Inflation from Repeat Views
then_action: Recognize that impressions will count each view separately (e.g., 3 views = 3 impressions, 1 reach), making it unlikely the user engages multiple times, which depresses the ER impressions metric
if_condition: If a single user sees the same post or ad multiple times within the measurement period
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 3 Engagement rate by impressions ER impressions

name: Expect Lower ER Impressions vs ERR and ER Post
then_action: Expect ER impressions values to be lower, because impressions count repeat views of the same content by the same user, inflating the denominator without proportionally increasing engagements
if_condition: If comparing ER impressions results against ERR (engagement rate by reach) or ER post metrics
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 3 Engagement rate by impressions ER impressions

name: Warn Against Misinterpreting Daily ER for Individual Engagement Frequency
then_action: Do not use Daily ER for this distinction — the formula aggregates total engagements and cannot differentiate individual vs. repeated engagement from the same follower
if_condition: If using Daily ER to distinguish whether a single follower engaged multiple times versus multiple followers each engaging once
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 4 Daily engagement rate Daily ER Best for long t

name: Adjust Engagement Rate Expectations Based on Follower Count
then_action: Flag this as expected behavior rather than a performance problem, since smaller accounts have more focused audiences and naturally generate higher engagement rates proportionally
if_condition: If an account's engagement rate declines as its follower count grows
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 6 Cost per engagement CPE Best for influencer ma

name: Include Valid Engagement Actions in Rate Calculation
then_action: Include only qualifying interaction actions: likes, comments, shares, saves, poll responses, messages, or link clicks — exclude views, as they do not count as engagement
if_condition: When calculating engagement rate for social media content
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update Key takeaways

name: Calculate CPE Using Standard Formula
then_action: Calculate CPE as: Total amount spent divided by Total engagements; a good engagement rate benchmark is 1%–5% though this varies by platform and industry
if_condition: If measuring the cost efficiency of audience interactions in an influencer marketing or conversion-based campaign
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 6 Cost per engagement CPE Best for influencer ma

name: Average ER Post Calculation Rule
then_action: Sum the individual ER post values for each post, then divide by the total number of posts: Average ER by post = Total ER by post / Number of posts
if_condition: If calculating average engagement rate across multiple posts
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 2 Engagement rate by post ER post Best for speci

name: Plot Daily Engagement Alongside Post Volume for Accurate Trend Analysis
then_action: Plot daily engagement data together with the number of posts published on the same days to contextualise fluctuations and avoid misattributing volume changes to audience behaviour
if_condition: If daily engagement volume fluctuates and the number of posts published varies within the reporting period
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 4 Daily engagement rate Daily ER Best for long t

name: Warn Against Cross-Channel Engagement Rate Comparisons
then_action: Flag that engagement metrics vary by channel; direct cross-platform comparisons are unreliable and benchmarks must be evaluated per-platform
if_condition: When comparing engagement rates across different social media platforms (e.g., Instagram vs. LinkedIn vs. TikTok)
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update Key takeaways

name: Normalize Engagement Rate Against Reach or Audience Size
then_action: Express engagement rate as interactions relative to reach or audience size (not absolute interaction counts), to ensure meaningful cross-content and cross-channel comparability
if_condition: When reporting or interpreting an engagement rate metric
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update Key takeaways

name: Use Industry-Specific Benchmarks for Engagement Rate Evaluation
then_action: Compare engagement rates only against other accounts in the same industry, not generic benchmarks, since fashion/entertainment inherently outperform finance except on LinkedIn
if_condition: If evaluating whether a campaign's engagement rate is performing well or poorly
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 6 Cost per engagement CPE Best for influencer ma

name: Calculate Engagement Rate Using Reach-Based Formula
then_action: Then use the Engagement Rate by Reach (ERR) formula: (Total Engagements ÷ Reach) × 100, as this is the most common and comparable formula across platforms
if_condition: If calculating a standardized engagement rate metric for social media content performance reporting
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update More industries

name: Use Daily ER Formula for Single-Day Follower Engagement
then_action: Use formula: Daily ER = (Total engagements in a day / Total followers) * 100
if_condition: If calculating engagement rate for a single day relative to total followers
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 4 Daily engagement rate Daily ER Best for long t

name: Use Average Daily ER Formula for Multi-Day Analysis
then_action: Use formula: Average Daily ER = Total engagements for X days / (X * followers) * 100
if_condition: If calculating engagement rate across multiple days (X days) relative to followers
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 4 Daily engagement rate Daily ER Best for long t

name: Verify Engagement Definition Before Cross-Campaign CPE Comparison
then_action: Check which specific interactions count as 'engagements' on each platform before comparing results, as definitions vary and can invalidate direct comparisons
if_condition: If comparing CPE or engagement rates across different campaigns, platforms, or accounts
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 6 Cost per engagement CPE Best for influencer ma

name: High Engagement Rate Signals Algorithmic Amplification
then_action: Social platform algorithms are more likely to surface that content to new audiences, increasing organic visibility and reach
if_condition: If a post or account achieves above-average engagement rates (likes, comments, shares, saves relative to reach or followers)
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update Why does engagement rate matter

name: Use Engagement Rate Drop as Algorithm/Audience Shift Signal
then_action: Then investigate whether the change reflects an algorithm update or shifting audience preferences rather than assuming brand-specific content failure
if_condition: If engagement rate changes significantly without a corresponding change in content quality or posting frequency
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update More industries

name: Overall Engagement Rate Calculation Requirement
then_action: Include total number of posts published AND total number of likes and comments across all posts in the calculation
if_condition: If calculating overall account engagement rate across all content
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update Why does engagement rate matter

name: Apply Platform-Specific Engagement Rate Norms
then_action: Apply platform-specific norms rather than a single cross-platform benchmark, as TikTok generally yields higher engagement rates than Instagram
if_condition: If benchmarking engagement rates across TikTok and Instagram simultaneously
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 6 Cost per engagement CPE Best for influencer ma

name: Calculate Engagement Rate with Standard Formula
then_action: Calculate engagement rate as: (total engagements ÷ followers, reach, impressions, or views) × 100; benchmark against industry average of 1.4%–2.8% across platforms
if_condition: If measuring social media performance and engagement rate needs to be quantified
source_title: How to measure and increase social media engagement in 2025

name: Complete Profile Setup Before Marketing
then_action: Fill out all contact details, verify the URL is working, and upload a high-res professional profile image before launching any marketing activity
if_condition: If an Instagram business profile is missing contact details, has a broken URL, or lacks a high-resolution profile image aligned with brand identity
source_title: How to create an Instagram marketing strategy 2025 guide Key Takeaways

name: Avoid Vanity Metrics in Instagram Reporting
then_action: Replace or supplement vanity metrics with a balanced mix of reach, engagement rate, retention (video watch time), and conversion metrics to accurately assess business impact
if_condition: If measuring Instagram marketing performance using only likes or follower count as primary KPIs
source_title: How to create an Instagram marketing strategy 2025 guide 1 Hootsuite

name: Pack Instagram Bio with SEO Keywords
then_action: Include as many relevant SEO keywords as possible in the bio to improve discoverability and search relevance on the platform
if_condition: If writing or optimizing an Instagram business bio
source_title: How to create an Instagram marketing strategy 2025 guide Key Takeaways

name: Switch to Professional Account for Business Tools
then_action: Switch to a professional account immediately to unlock Insights (analytics), advertising tools, and account controls required for effective Instagram marketing
if_condition: If an Instagram account is being used for business purposes but has not been switched to a professional account
source_title: How to create an Instagram marketing strategy 2025 guide Key Takeaways

name: Track Beyond Social Metrics for Business Goal Alignment

name: Expect Lower ER When Using Impressions as Base
then_action: Expect the resulting ER to be lower than ERR and ER post equations, because the same user seeing a post multiple times inflates impressions without proportionally increasing engagements

source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 3 Engagement rate by impressions ER impressions

name: Expect ER Post Decline as Follower Count Grows

name: Do Not Conflate Reach and Impressions in Engagement Calculations
then_action: Distinguish reach (counts as 1 regardless of repeat views) from impressions (counts each individual view separately); choose impressions only when repeated exposure measurement is intentional and relevant to the campaign goal
if_condition: If a single user sees the same post or ad multiple times and you are selecting a base metric for engagement rate
source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 3 Engagement rate by impressions ER impressions

source_title: Engagement rate benchmarks and formulas 2026 update 2 Engagement rate by post ER post Best for speci

name: Daily ER Cannot Distinguish Individual vs. Repeat Engager
then_action: Avoid this interpretation — Daily ER formula cannot distinguish between one follower engaging 10 times and 10 followers engaging once each

name: Apply Platform-Specific Engagement Rate Norms for TikTok vs Instagram

name: Use CPE for Influencer Marketing and Conversion-Based Campaign Evaluation

name: Calculate Engagement Rate Using ERR Formula
then_action: Use Engagement Rate by Reach (ERR): divide total engagements by total reach and multiply by 100 to get a percentage

name: utm_medium vs utm_source Role Separation
then_action: Set utm_medium to the channel category (e.g., 'social', 'email', 'cpc') and utm_source to the exact traffic origin (e.g., 'instagram', 'newsletter', 'partner_name') — never swap these roles
if_condition: If assigning values to utm_medium and utm_source parameters

name: Descriptive Campaign Names for Long-Term Readability
then_action: Rename to descriptive values that will still be interpretable months later without context (e.g., 'spring_sale_2025', 'product_launch_june')
if_condition: If utm_campaign names are vague, abbreviated, or cryptic (e.g., 'camp1', 'q3promo')

name: Keep utm_medium List Short and Consistent
