source_title: News in the Age of Abundance   David Perell None Part 7

name: Treat the Individual Article as the Core Content Unit
then_action: Treat each individual article as a standalone shareable unit — optimizing its headline, format, and distribution independently — rather than relying on the overall brand or publication bundle to drive traffic
if_condition: If structuring a digital content or SEO strategy for a news site or content creator
source_title: News in the Age of Abundance   David Perell None Part 6

name: Independent Writers Outcompete Large Newspapers on Local Knowledge Topics
then_action: Favor independent local writers over large national newspapers as authoritative sources, since traditional outlets cannot scale local expertise and independent writers increasingly outcompete them on locally-grounded subjects
if_condition: If content requires deep local or community-specific knowledge to be authoritative
source_title: News in the Age of Abundance   David Perell None Part 7

name: Citizen Blogger Competitive Relevance at Scale
then_action: Assign high competitive potential to the independent blog; collective armies of specialized bloggers can out-compete multinational newspapers because the Internet's scale favors distributed specialist knowledge over centralized generalist reporting
if_condition: If evaluating whether a niche independent blog can compete in authority or reach against a large multinational news organization
source_title: News in the Age of Abundance   David Perell None Part 7

name: Fast News Cycle Amplification Opportunity
then_action: Then bloggers and creators who have already published validated narratives on that topic are more likely to be sourced or amplified by journalists, making timely blog publishing on trending topics a high-leverage SEO and authority-building strategy
if_condition: If the news cycle for a topic is accelerating and journalists are publishing rapidly on that subject
source_title: News in the Age of Abundance   David Perell None Part 18

name: Avoid Misleading Headlines That Game Social Algorithms
then_action: Do not spin stories or write misleading headlines to game algorithms, as this pollutes the information ecosystem and reduces long-term content credibility
if_condition: If a content creator is optimizing a headline or story framing to exploit social media algorithm ranking signals
source_title: News in the Age of Abundance   David Perell None Part 29

name: Build Domain Encyclopedia to Trigger Data Network Effects
then_action: Structure and publish that information as a comprehensive encyclopedia to trigger data network effects, enabling low-cost acquisition via Google search and word-of-mouth
if_condition: If a creator or company is accumulating domain-specific information and has reached a critical mass of content in a niche
source_title: One Big Idea   David Perell 2

name: LinkedIn About Section Must Maximize Brand Information Opportunity
then_action: Treat the About section as the primary space to share brand or business information and invest time to fully populate it with story, results, and brand context
if_condition: If a LinkedIn About section is sparse, vague, or left partially empty despite available character space
source_title: Optimize Your LinkedIn for 2024 About Section

name: Use Empirical Data Over Intuition for Performance Analysis
then_action: Use empirical data and measurable evidence rather than intuitive feelings or conventional wisdom; quantify performance metrics to identify specific improvement areas
if_condition: When analyzing performance metrics or making technical decisions about content, SEO strategy, or creative output
source_title: Practice Analytically Perform Intuitively   David Perell Write Online Build Your Audience Part 2

name: Specificity Increases Discoverability of Demand
then_action: Then prioritize the more specific niche — greater specificity correlates with higher likelihood of finding people actively searching for solutions, improving both content relevance and conversion potential
if_condition: If a creator is choosing between a broad niche and a highly specific niche with active problem-seeking audiences
source_title: The Counterintuitive Approach to Uncovering Your Profitable Niche A Smarter Approach to Idea Generat

name: Manufacturer Consolidation Signals Convergence Risk
then_action: Then assume product homogenization is accelerating in that category — content strategies or brand narratives emphasizing uniqueness in that space will lack credibility and should be repositioned
if_condition: If a product category shows signs of manufacturer and distributor consolidation with only a few surviving brands carried at scale
source_title: The Microwave Economy   David Perell 7

name: Algorithm Gaming via Story Spinning
then_action: Spin stories with misleading but high-engagement headlines and choose framing that maximizes platform-specific emotional triggers rather than informational accuracy
if_condition: If a content creator is optimizing for social media algorithmic reach on platforms that reward engagement over accuracy
source_title: The Paradox of Abundance   David Perell 2

name: Native Content Over External Links on Twitter
then_action: Write a thread instead of linking to the article, because Twitter's algorithm downranks posts that direct users off-platform, reducing reach significantly
if_condition: If publishing content on Twitter and considering linking to an external article instead of writing a native thread
source_title: The Ultimate Guide to Writing Online   David Perell 7 Engage the Public

name: Bio Must Include Topic, Audience, and Owned Property Link
then_action: Ensure the bio explicitly states what you write about, who you serve, and includes a direct link to your website or newsletter to capture engaged readers into owned channels
if_condition: If setting up or auditing a social media bio on any platform
source_title: The Ultimate Guide to Writing Online   David Perell 7 Engage the Public

name: Evergreen Content Platform Selection Rule
then_action: Choose a personal website over a newsletter platform like Substack to better support navigation, customization, and long-term content discovery
if_condition: If a writer primarily produces timeless, evergreen articles rather than time-sensitive newsletter content
source_title: The Ultimate Guide to Writing Online   David Perell 8 Build your Online Home

name: Avoid Cynical/Pessimistic Beliefs to Preserve Agency
then_action: Then recognize these beliefs are self-fulfilling and will cause focus on obstacles rather than solutions, reducing actual agency and long-term success probability
if_condition: If a creator or SEO professional adopts cynical or pessimistic beliefs about their ability to influence outcomes (e.g., 'the algorithm is unfair', 'the playing field is rigged')
source_title: Blame Yourself for Everything and Preserve Your Agency 2

name: Apply Iteration to Reduce Luck Dependency
then_action: Then increase the frequency of thinking, deliberate choice-making, and iteration cycles — more active iteration statistically reduces the impact of luck on long-term results
if_condition: If outcomes feel heavily luck-dependent or inconsistent despite sustained effort in content or SEO work
source_title: Blame Yourself for Everything and Preserve Your Agency 2

name: Engage Unconventional Candidates Through Genuine Work Curiosity
then_action: Invest time studying their actual work deeply, then reach out with specific, thoughtful questions or modifications about their approach — authentic curiosity-based outreach builds relationships more effectively than direct recruiting pitches
if_condition: If a candidate is found working on a non-mainstream or unusual project adjacent to your domain (e.g., niche GitHub repos, unpublished papers, obscure side projects)
source_title: Curate People 8

name: Avoid Competing for Obvious Talent Pools
then_action: Avoid pursuing that candidate through standard channels — competing for obvious talent raises costs and reduces differentiation; instead redirect sourcing effort toward undiscovered individuals working on adjacent or unconventional projects
if_condition: If a candidate is widely recognized, heavily recruited, or appearing in obvious talent pipelines that all competitors also access
source_title: Curate People 8

name: Domain Expertise Required for Quality Talent Identification
then_action: Do not outsource recruiting to generalists without domain taste — they cannot identify unconventional high-quality candidates and will default to safe, obvious picks; only domain experts (makers finding makers, engineers finding engineers) can accurately assess talent quality
if_condition: If recruiting or hiring for a specialized role (engineering, building, creative) and the recruiter lacks direct domain experience in that field
source_title: Curate People 8

name: Heretical Opinion Scoring Is Non-Linear
then_action: Do not average it against other opinions — treat it as qualitatively disqualifying, like a uranium chunk on a scale that overrides all other contributions and prior reputation
if_condition: If an opinion expressed by a creator or entity is classified as 'heretical' by the prevailing ideological consensus (i.e., triggers punitive social response regardless of truth value)
source_title: Heresy

name: Multi-Factor Evaluation Before Channel Spin-Off
then_action: Evaluate all four factors before deciding: current views, subscriber count, audience retention rates, and engagement signals — do not rely on any single metric alone
if_condition: If a content creator is evaluating whether to spin off a separate YouTube channel for a different content format or topic
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 6

name: Early Platform Feature Adoption Reward
then_action: Then the platform will algorithmically promote that creator's content to drive adoption metrics for the feature, resulting in increased reach and visibility
if_condition: If a creator adopts a newly launched YouTube feature immediately after release before widespread adoption occurs
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 9

name: YouTube Posts Algorithmic Boost
then_action: Then YouTube's algorithm will preferentially surface those posts in subscriber feeds and homepage feeds, increasing visibility and channel growth
if_condition: If a YouTube creator publishes Posts (the community/social tab feature) regularly while most competitors do not use it
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 9

name: Content Gap Days Missed Opportunity Warning
then_action: Then the creator is missing algorithmic touchpoints on those empty days and should fill gaps using Posts or other lightweight content formats to maintain algorithmic presence
if_condition: If a creator only posts long-form content and short clips but leaves multiple days per week with no content published in any format
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 9

name: YouTube Translation Feature for Audience Expansion
then_action: Then the creator should add translated audio tracks to existing videos to expand reach into non-English speaking audiences and gain early-mover algorithmic advantage
if_condition: If a YouTube creator produces content in one language and has not utilized YouTube's audio track translation feature now available to broader creators
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 9

name: YouTube Same-Channel Daily Multi-Post Cannibalization
then_action: Content will cannibalize itself because the YouTube homepage feed algorithm rarely shows multiple videos from the same creator simultaneously, reducing overall reach
if_condition: If a creator posts multiple videos per day on the same YouTube channel
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 5

name: Topic-Based Multi-Channel Strategy for YouTube Reach
then_action: Create separate channels for each distinct content topic rather than consolidating all content on one channel, to avoid algorithmic suppression and maintain audience expectation alignment
if_condition: If a YouTube creator produces content across multiple distinct topic categories (e.g., gaming, lifestyle, education)
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 5

name: Subscriptions Feed vs Homepage Feed Placement Difference
then_action: This is expected YouTube algorithm behavior — homepage feed limits same-creator exposure while subscriptions feed does not; optimize homepage reach by splitting content across channels
if_condition: If same-channel videos appear multiple times in the subscriptions feed but not on the homepage feed
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 5

name: Volume-Based vs Topic-Based Channel Splitting Effectiveness
then_action: Prioritize topic-based channel separation over volume-based separation, as topic differentiation better serves audience expectations and algorithmic categorization
if_condition: If a creator is deciding whether to split channels based on upload volume alone versus splitting by content topic
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 5

name: Engagement Posts Trigger Algorithm Distribution
then_action: Create polls or open questions as community posts to generate active comments and interactions, which signals high engagement to the algorithm and causes it to serve more content from the channel
if_condition: If a YouTube channel needs increased content distribution from the algorithm
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 10

name: Separate Channels Can Reach Non-Overlapping Audiences at Scale
then_action: Expect minimal audience overlap between channels; treat each channel as reaching a distinct viewer segment, as demonstrated by cases like Pat Flynn's Pokemon channels each reaching 1.5–2M subscribers independently
if_condition: If a creator operates multiple YouTube channels targeting different content formats or niches (e.g., a main channel and a clips or topic-specific channel)
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 6

name: Use Multi-Channel Testing to Validate YouTube Strategy
then_action: Test the strategy across multiple channels simultaneously to create control groups and produce more reliable, comparable results
if_condition: If a YouTube growth strategy is being evaluated and the creator manages or has access to multiple channels
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 8

name: Keep Long-Form and Shorts on Same Channel
then_action: Keep both content formats on the same channel, as adding Shorts does not hurt long-form performance — long-form views remain unchanged when Shorts are added
if_condition: If a YouTube creator is considering separating long-form content and Shorts into different channels to avoid performance interference
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 8

name: Use YouTube Posts as Growth Hack on Content-Free Days
then_action: Publish a YouTube Post (formerly Community post) on those days to maintain audience engagement and leverage the feature as a growth mechanism
if_condition: If a YouTube channel has days in the publishing schedule with no video content going live
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 8

name: Use Platform Analytics to Segment Localized Audience by Geography
then_action: Use YouTube analytics to track views, retention, and geographic data by language, then identify whether Spanish viewers originate from Spain or Latin America to inform targeting and content decisions
if_condition: When a localized content channel (e.g., Spanish-language podcast) has been live and is generating views
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 17

name: Engage Platform PMs via Public Feature Commentary
then_action: Engage in calls with the PM to provide feedback, and use the opportunity to ask for metrics or feedback on your own content performance
if_condition: If a creator posts positive public commentary about platform features (e.g., on LinkedIn) and a platform product manager reaches out
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 21

name: Target Long-Tail Creator Status to Attract Platform PM Feedback
then_action: Actively seek engagement with platform product managers, as PMs specifically seek long-tail creator feedback to understand what works beyond already-successful large shows
if_condition: If a creator is not yet at the top tier of platform popularity (i.e., is a long-tail creator with moderate but growing audience)
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 21

name: YouTube Playlist Naming: Use Engaging Searchable Titles Over Generic Labels
then_action: Replace it with an engaging, benefit-driven or curiosity-driven title (e.g., 'the best ways to make money' or 'how to build wealth') to improve algorithmic performance and viewer click-through
if_condition: If a YouTube playlist is named with a generic category label (e.g., 'finance', 'health', 'business')
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 20

name: YouTube Playlist Testing: Allow Three-Month Window Before Evaluating Results
then_action: Do not conclude the test is ineffective; continue monitoring view changes and algorithmic response for the full three-month period before drawing conclusions or making further changes
if_condition: If a YouTube playlist name or structure has been changed and fewer than three months have elapsed since the change
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 20

name: YouTube Playlist Structure: Segment by Specific Audience Rather Than Broad Topic
then_action: Restructure playlists to target each specific audience segment separately so the algorithm can more accurately serve content to relevant viewers and so viewers self-identify and find content more effectively
if_condition: If a YouTube channel serves multiple distinct audience segments (e.g., finance professionals, content creators, entrepreneurs) and playlists are organized by broad topic category
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 20

name: YouTube Algorithm: Implicit Playlist Signals Drive Content Distribution Even Without Direct Clicks
then_action: Recognize that YouTube's algorithm will serve more content from playlists where a viewer has watched multiple videos, even if the viewer never directly navigated the playlist — so playlist organization and naming affect algorithmic distribution broadly
if_condition: If a creator assumes playlist optimization only matters when viewers explicitly click into and browse playlists
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 20

name: Automated Social Media Platform Monitoring
then_action: Set up AI agents or automated digests to monitor and surface social media platform changes, saving approximately 10 minutes per day of manual searching and ensuring no major updates are missed
if_condition: If a media or content team risks missing platform feature updates (e.g., Instagram, YouTube algorithm changes) due to manual monitoring
source_title: How I Helped Grow Diary of a CEO to 14M Subscribers 120 35

name: Sourcing from Unconventional Signals
then_action: Prioritize these candidates over conventional applicants — spend time reviewing their GitHub or papers, formulate thoughtful questions about their specific approach, then reach out with genuine curiosity before any recruiting intent
if_condition: If searching for high-quality specialized talent and candidates are working on weird or niche projects adjacent to your field (e.g., obscure GitHub repos, unpublished papers, side experiments)
source_title: Curate People 8

name: Like-Recognizes-Like Talent Evaluation
then_action: Flag the evaluation as low-confidence — assign domain-matched evaluators (makers evaluate makers, engineers evaluate engineers) to ensure accurate quality assessment of unconventional candidates
if_condition: If a hiring manager or evaluator does not share the same craft domain as the candidate being assessed (e.g., a non-engineer evaluating engineers)
source_title: Curate People 8
